Theme access pattern: keeps the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention. Parity catalog declares 8 stories total (4 happy + 4 negative ŌĆö meets the "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor) using only the allowed assertion vocabulary; 7 catalog-shape vitest guards pass (7/7) including a render-shape-vocabulary pin on happy-path stories that fails the suite if a future story sneaks in an interaction verb on a happy-path. Happy-path stories cover the outer vertical-flex `h-full` container, header child mounting with the canonical "Group Chat: {name}" headline + pencil-icon Rename button reachable through prop threading, messages child mounting with the Beta empty-state surface reachable, and input child mounting with a `<textarea>` reachable. Negative-path stories pin: the panel itself emits no `[role="dialog"]` wrapper (it is a layout shell, not a modal); Stop All is suppressed under `state === 'idle'` (the header's `{state !== 'idle' && ...}` guard reaches through prop threading); the panel itself emits no `[role="alert"]` banner (that affordance belongs to `ContextWarningSash`, not the panel shell); and a no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle pin on a pure render. Renderer-side consumers (`MainPanel`-style host that swaps in the Group Chat view when a group chat is active) are NOT touched ŌĆö feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `GroupChatPanel` lift").
- [x] ISC-44.layer-2.5.git_status_widget: `GitStatusWidget` (renderer `src/renderer/components/GitStatusWidget.tsx`, 244 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API per pre-flight grep) lifted into `src/webFull/components/GitStatusWidget.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/GitStatusWidget.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-git-status-widget` (leaf-parade lift wave, header widget). Pre-flight `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]" src/renderer/components/GitStatusWidget.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (exit 1). The widget itself touches none of the banned surface. **One adapt decision worth naming (follows established precedent):** the renderer source self-sources git-status data from two focused contexts via `useGitFileStatus()` + `useGitDetail()`. The provider behind those contexts (`GitStatusProvider` in `src/renderer/contexts/GitStatusContext.tsx`) is built on `useGitStatusPolling`, which calls `window.maestro.git.getStatus(...)` at module-instantiation ŌĆö lifting the context as-is would drag the entire git-status polling hook + the IPC site through the webFull bundle entry point (out of scope per the brief's "needs a server-side route which is out of scope" guard). Matching webFull pattern (per `AppOverlays` and `SessionList` L4.1 precedents): **promote the self-sourced values to props.** Two new inputs (`fileCount: number`, `fileDetails?: GitFileDetails`) replace `getFileCount(sessionId)` and `getFileDetails(sessionId)`. The widget contract no longer carries `sessionId` (the renderer source uses it only to key into the two hooks; with the hooks gone, there is no key). The host (a downstream layer-wiring pass that ports the git-status substrate as a REST + WS surface) decides where those values come from ŌĆö a future webFull `useGitFileStatus()` hook reading `/api/git/status`, a global store, or prop-drilling from the App root. **Type-resolution adapts (matching the L2.5 precedent):** `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` (standard L2.5 swap). `GitFileChange` is NOT pulled from `'../contexts/GitStatusContext'` (which re-exports it from `useGitStatusPolling`) ŌĆö instead the lifted module defines it locally (5 primitive fields: `path`, `status`, `additions`, `deletions`, `modified`). A new `GitFileDetails` aggregate captures the shape the renderer hook's `getFileDetails(sessionId)` returns (`{ fileChanges?, totalAdditions, totalDeletions, modifiedCount }`); the renderer declares this shape inline inside `GitDetailContextValue`, lifting it to a named type here keeps the prop contract greppable. Matches the `ExecutionQueueIndicator` / `DeleteWorktreeModal` precedent of pulling only the specific data shape each lifted module consumes, rather than copying the entire renderer aggregator into `src/shared/` (which would create the silent-drift surface audit risk A explicitly warns against). `lucide-react` icons (`GitBranch`, `Plus`, `Minus`, `FileEdit`, `FileDiff`, `History`) kept verbatim ŌĆö already a webFull-tree dep. Theme access pattern: kept the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention. **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/GitStatusWidget.parity.test.ts`, 9 stories: 5 happy + 4 negative ŌĆö meets the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative" floor; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards):** happy-path stories cover compact-pill rendering with file count + breakdown title, full-breakdown rendering with additions/deletions/modified counts, tooltip listing changed files with per-file counts on hover, "View Full Diff" affordance presence, and "View Git Log" affordance presence when `onViewLog` is supplied. Negative-path stories cover suppression when `!isGitRepo`, suppression when `fileCount === 0`, hidden "View Git Log" when callback absent, and a no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle pin. 6 catalog-shape vitest guards including a presentational-contract pin that asserts the catalog never drifts toward `role="dialog"` assertions (the widget is a header pill + hover tooltip, not a modal ŌĆö a future refactor wrapping in a modal is a behavior change and should fail the catalog rather than silently track it). Uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary; passes the IPC-leakage guard. **What this lift is NOT:** not a lift of `GitStatusContext` / `GitStatusProvider` / the `useGitStatusPolling` hook (IPC-bound; needs a server-side `/api/git/*` port ŌĆö explicitly out of scope); not a lift of `git:getStatus` IPC handler; not a wiring change inside webFull `App.tsx`. Barrel export added at `src/webFull/components/index.ts`. `src/web/` + `src/renderer/` + `src/main/` + `src/server/` UNTOUCHED (`git diff main..HEAD -- src/web/ src/renderer/ src/main/ src/server/ | wc -l` ŌåÆ 0). `npm run lint` clean (after `npm run build:prompts` to materialize the pre-existing `src/generated/prompts.ts`). See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `GitStatusWidget` lift").
- [x] ISC-44.layer-2.5.maestro_silhouette: `MaestroSilhouette` (renderer `src/renderer/components/MaestroSilhouette.tsx`, 88 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API per pre-flight grep) lifted verbatim into `src/webFull/components/MaestroSilhouette.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/MaestroSilhouette.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-silhouette-and-autocomplete` (leaf-parade lift wave, paired with `TemplateAutocompleteDropdown` in one semantic atomic commit per the brief). Pre-flight `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]" src/renderer/components/MaestroSilhouette.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (exit 1). The component is presentational-only ŌĆö two named exports (`MaestroSilhouette` static + `AnimatedMaestro` with `conductingMotion` CSS-keyframe animation) plus a module-load-time `document.head.appendChild` of the keyframes guarded by both `typeof document !== 'undefined'` and an `#maestro-animation-styles` id-check so it is idempotent and SSR-safe. All input is via the `MaestroSilhouetteProps` prop bag (`className?`, `style?`, `variant?: 'dark' | 'light'`, `size?`); all output is a single `<img>` element. Import-path adapts (one): PNG asset imports `'../assets/conductor-{dark,light}.png'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/assets/conductor-{dark,light}.png'`. The renderer assets are pure binaries with zero runtime behavior; re-using them directly follows the L2.5 `WelcomeContent` precedent (`maestroWandIcon` from `'../../renderer/assets/icon-wand.png'`). Duplicating the PNGs into `src/webFull/assets/` would create silent drift for the load-bearing conductor glyph used by the `AnimatedMaestro` Standing Ovation overlay and any future host. Parity catalog declares 6 stories (4 happy + 2 negative ŌĆö meets the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative" floor) using only the allowed assertion vocabulary; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards pass (6/6). Happy-path stories cover the static export's canonical alt text, the animated export's distinct alt text, className pass-through preserving the thin-wrapper contract (no extra wrappers around the `<img>`), and a both-exports-coexist story pinning the two-alt-text discriminator. Negative-path stories pin the no-wrapper-div / no-role / no-aria-label contract (refactor guard against wrapping the `<img>` in a `role="img"` container) and the no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle pin on a pure render (mount ŌåÆ prop-flip ŌåÆ unmount). Renderer-side consumers (the Standing Ovation overlay and brand surfaces) are NOT touched ŌĆö feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `MaestroSilhouette` + `TemplateAutocompleteDropdown` paired lift").
- [x] ISC-44.layer-2.5.template_autocomplete_dropdown: `TemplateAutocompleteDropdown` (renderer `src/renderer/components/TemplateAutocompleteDropdown.tsx`, 108 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API per pre-flight grep) lifted verbatim into `src/webFull/components/TemplateAutocompleteDropdown.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/TemplateAutocompleteDropdown.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-silhouette-and-autocomplete` (leaf-parade lift wave, paired with `MaestroSilhouette` in one semantic atomic commit per the brief). Pre-flight `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]" src/renderer/components/TemplateAutocompleteDropdown.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (exit 1). The component is a `forwardRef<HTMLDivElement>` presentational dropdown that renders the absolute-positioned template-variable picker used by both the AI tab prompt composer (`AgentPromptComposerModal`) and the Auto Run document editor. Behavior is gated entirely on the `state` prop: returns `null` when `!state.isOpen` OR `state.filteredVariables.length === 0`; when rendered, emits a scrollable list of rows (each carrying `data-index={index}`, a `<code>` chip with the variable name, and a `<span>` with the description) plus a footer with three `<kbd>` chips (ŌåæŌåō navigate, Tab select, Esc close). Selection state lives entirely in the prop bag ŌĆö the host owns the autocomplete reducer; this component is pure render. All side effects are delivered via the `onSelect(variable)` callback the host wires; hover effects directly mutate `currentTarget.style.backgroundColor` to preserve the renderer's exact behavior (no setState on hover). Import-path adapts (two): `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` (standard L2.5 swap ŌĆö webFull has no `types/` aggregator); `AutocompleteState` from `'../hooks'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/hooks'` directly (type-only import). The hook barrel at `src/renderer/hooks/index.ts` re-exports the interface from `useTemplateAutocomplete`, and the interface itself is a pure data shape (`{ isOpen, position: {top, left}, selectedIndex, searchText, filteredVariables }`) with no transitive `window.maestro` references at module-load time ŌĆö pre-flight grep on `useTemplateAutocomplete.ts` + `templateVariables.ts` returned only a doc-comment mention in the shared module (no live code reference). Matches the L2.5 `GroupChatHeader` / `GroupChatPanel` precedent of pulling specific type-only re-exports from the canonical renderer aggregator rather than copying them into `src/shared/` (which would create the silent-drift surface audit risk A explicitly warns against). Parity catalog declares 7 stories (4 happy + 3 negative ŌĆö meets the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative" floor) using only the allowed assertion vocabulary; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards pass (6/6). Happy-path stories cover the row + footer chrome when open with variables (data-index attributes + code chip text + span description text + three kbd chips), the selectedIndex=1 case pinning per-row data-index discrimination, the arbitrary-count case proving the `.map()` rendering through all rows, and the description-text surfacing for each variable. Negative-path stories pin the two halves of the `!state.isOpen || filteredVariables.length === 0` early-return (closed-but-non-empty AND open-but-empty cases both suppress the row + footer surface) plus the no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle pin on mount ŌåÆ hover ŌåÆ click. Renderer-side consumers (the AI tab `AgentPromptComposerModal` and the Auto Run document editor) are NOT touched ŌĆö feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `MaestroSilhouette` + `TemplateAutocompleteDropdown` paired lift").
- [x] ISC-44.layer-2.5.groupchat_header: `GroupChatHeader` (renderer `src/renderer/components/GroupChatHeader.tsx`, 143 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API per pre-flight grep) lifted verbatim into `src/webFull/components/GroupChatHeader.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/GroupChatHeader.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-groupchat-header` (leaf-parade lift wave, direct sibling of the just-landed `GroupChatMessages` lift ŌĆö both ports continue the GroupChat module surface, ultimately composed by a downstream `GroupChatPanel`). Pre-flight `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|ipcRenderer" src/renderer/components/GroupChatHeader.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (exit 1). The component touches none of the banned surface; all side effects are delivered via prop callbacks (`onStopAll`, `onRename`, `onShowInfo`, `onToggleRightPanel`) which the host wires to its own runtime. Import-path adapts (four standard L2.5 swaps): `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` (standard L2.5 swap); `GroupChatState` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/group-chat-types'` (canonical source ŌĆö the renderer barrel re-exports from there anyway; same swap the L2.5 `GroupChatMessages` sibling lift made); `Shortcut` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/types'` directly (the interface lives in the renderer types barrel only ŌĆö `src/renderer/types/index.ts` line 165 ŌĆö and is not yet replicated to `src/shared/`; matches the L2.5 `ShortcutsHelpModal` precedent of pulling specific types from the canonical aggregator rather than copying into `src/shared/` which would create the silent-drift surface audit risk A explicitly warns against; `Shortcut` is a pure data shape with no transitive `window.maestro` references); `formatShortcutKeys` from `'../utils/shortcutFormatter'` resolves to the webFull-side shim at `src/webFull/utils/shortcutFormatter.ts` (precursor infrastructure landed in the `leaf-autorunner-help` lift @ `a82d4012e`, which swaps the renderer formatter's transitive `window.maestro.platform` dependency for `navigator.userAgent`-based `isMacOSPlatform()`). This lift is the third consumer of that shim (after `AutoRunnerHelpModal` and `GroupChatMessages`). `lucide-react` icons (`Info`, `Edit2`, `Columns`, `DollarSign`, `StopCircle`) are already webFull-tree deps. Theme access pattern: keeps the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention. Parity catalog declares 10 stories total (5 happy + 5 negative ŌĆö exceeds the "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor) using only the allowed assertion vocabulary; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards pass (6/6). Happy-path stories cover the baseline headline + Rename pencil + Info button rendering, participant pill pluralization for multiple participants, Stop All button surfacing under non-idle state, cost pill two-decimal formatting under positive `totalCost`, and the right-panel toggle button surfacing when the panel is closed (with the formatted shortcut hint in the tooltip). Negative-path stories pin the Stop All suppression under idle state, participant pill singular form at `participantCount === 1`, cost pill suppression when `totalCost === 0` / undefined, the incomplete-cost asterisk + tooltip variant under `costIncomplete === true`, and the right-panel toggle suppression when `rightPanelOpen === true`. Renderer-side consumers (`GroupChatPanel` and wherever the header mounts) are NOT touched ŌĆö feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `GroupChatHeader` lift").
- [x] ISC-44.layer-2.5.app_overlays: `AppOverlays` (renderer `src/renderer/components/AppOverlays.tsx`, 129 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API per pre-flight grep) lifted into `src/webFull/components/AppOverlays.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/AppOverlays.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-app-overlays` (leaf-parade lift wave, mid-size dispatcher leaf). Pre-flight `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|ipcRenderer" src/renderer/components/AppOverlays.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (exit 1). The dispatcher itself touches none of the banned surface. **Two adapt decisions worth naming (both follow established precedent):** (1) **Store self-source stripped ŌåÆ promoted to props.** The renderer source uses the "Tier 1A" self-source pattern, reaching into `useModalStore` for three modal-data values (`standingOvationData`, `firstRunCelebrationData`, `keyboardMasteryData`) plus `useSettingsStore` for `shortcuts` + `disableConfetti`. Those two stores are renderer-only architecture as of this lift ŌĆö `src/renderer/stores/settingsStore.ts` carries 20+ `window.maestro.settings.set` callsites and there is no `src/webFull/stores/` directory yet. Lifting the stores would explode this leaf into a multi-file wave that pulls in the entire IPC-persisted settings layer (and the modal-registry store with it). The matching webFull pattern: promote the self-sourced values to props (`standingOvationData`, `firstRunCelebrationData`, `pendingKeyboardMasteryLevel`). The host decides whether those values come from a future webFull-side modal store, REST-backed `useSettings()`, or prop drilling at the App root. This matches the L4.1 `SessionList` precedent of stripping renderer-specific store hooks at the lift boundary and letting the consumer wire the data source. (2) **Overlay children passed as render-prop slots, not imported.** The renderer dispatcher imports `StandingOvationOverlay`, `FirstRunCelebration`, and `KeyboardMasteryCelebration` directly. Of those three, only `FirstRunCelebration` is in webFull (lifted in `leaf-first-run-celebration` @ `fd9bc3cf9`). The other two are not yet lifted. Importing them from `'../../renderer/components/...'` was rejected for two reasons: `StandingOvationOverlay` carries a `window.maestro.shell.openExternal` callsite (pre-flight grep confirmed 1 hit), and cross-fork imports are the audit-risk-A silent-drift surface this fork-hygiene rule exists to prevent. Instead, the three overlays become render-prop `ReactNode` slots (`standingOvationSlot`, `firstRunCelebrationSlot`, `keyboardMasterySlot`). The dispatcher becomes a pure render-gate: when the data prop is non-null, render the matching slot; otherwise render nothing. The gate logic ŌĆö the actual lift surface ŌĆö is verbatim from the renderer source, including the render order (FirstRunCelebration ŌåÆ KeyboardMastery ŌåÆ StandingOvation). When the two unlifted overlays land in future leaf parades, the host wiring layer can keep the render-slot pattern (the dispatcher stays decoupled) or inline the three component imports at the host call site ŌĆö either choice is host-local and the dispatcher contract is stable. **Import-path adapts (two, both matching L2.5 precedent):** `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'`; `ConductorBadge` from `'../constants/conductorBadges'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/constants/conductorBadges'` (the constants module is pure data with zero IPC, zero Electron API surface ŌĆö matches the L2.5 `ShortcutsHelpModal` / `MarkdownRenderer` precedent of pulling non-divergent renderer modules directly by relative path rather than duplicating into `src/shared/`). Theme is accepted as a prop per the L2.1 / L2.3 / L2.4 / L2.5 convention; the dispatcher itself does not render any theme-styled DOM but keeps the prop on the surface for host convenience. **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/AppOverlays.parity.test.ts`, 10 stories: 5 happy + 5 negative ŌĆö meets the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 8 catalog-shape vitest guards):** render-shape oriented (per the L2.5 SessionListItem / ToggleButtonGroup precedent ŌĆö `hasElement` only since the dispatcher is a pure visibility gate with no internal interaction surface). Happy-path stories cover the first-run slot rendering when data is present, keyboard-mastery slot rendering when level is non-null, standing-ovation slot rendering when data is present, keyboard-mastery slot rendering at `level=0` (boundary pin ŌĆö a future refactor that uses `if (level)` instead of `if (level !== null)` would suppress level=0 and fail this story), and all three slots rendering simultaneously when all three data gates fire (with the render-order assertion pinning FirstRunCelebration ŌåÆ KeyboardMastery ŌåÆ StandingOvation). Negative-path stories cover suppression when each data gate is null (slot supplied but data gate null does NOT render ŌĆö the dispatcher is data-driven, not slot-driven), the all-three-null steady state (none of the three markers present), and a lifecycle no-IPC / no-WS pin. Includes a **render-shape-only guard** (asserts the catalog uses only `hasElement` / `hasText` ŌĆö no interaction or wire-frame verbs since the slots own all click / keyboard semantics) and a **render-order pin** (verifies the all-three-slots story declares its markers in the canonical FirstRunCelebration ŌåÆ KeyboardMastery ŌåÆ StandingOvation order so a future refactor that flips the order accidentally would fail the suite). Uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary; passes the IPC-leakage guard. **Renderer-side consumers UNTOUCHED:** `src/renderer/App.tsx` continues to render the renderer `AppOverlays` as part of the Electron entrypoint. Feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern ŌĆö and it has additional prerequisites (a webFull-side modal store and/or REST-backed source for the three data gates; lifts of `StandingOvationOverlay` and `KeyboardMasteryCelebration`). See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `AppOverlays` lift").
- [x] ISC-44.layer-2.5.shortcuts_help: `ShortcutsHelpModal` (renderer `src/renderer/components/ShortcutsHelpModal.tsx`, 216 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API per audit item #5) lifted verbatim into `src/webFull/components/ShortcutsHelpModal.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/ShortcutsHelpModal.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-shortcuts-help` (leaf-parade lift wave). Pre-flight grep flagged a single transitive dependency surface: the renderer's `formatShortcutKeys` (`src/renderer/utils/shortcutFormatter.ts`) leans on `isMacOSPlatform()` from `src/renderer/utils/platformUtils.ts`, which reads `window.maestro.platform` (Electron preload bridge). In a browser host that returns `''` and `isMacOSPlatform()` returns false, which would always render the non-Mac key map (`Ctrl+Shift+K` instead of `Ōīś Ōć¦ K`). The platform-correct shim already exists at `src/webFull/utils/platformUtils.ts` (user-agent sniff, mirror API). To keep `src/renderer/` read-only AND get the browser-correct rendering, the lift inlines a thin `formatShortcutKeysForBrowser` wrapper inside the webFull component that mirrors the renderer formatter's key maps verbatim and routes platform detection through `webFull/utils/platformUtils`. The renderer's `formatShortcutKeys` is left untouched. The remaining transitive deps (`FIXED_SHORTCUTS`, `KEYBOARD_MASTERY_LEVELS`, `getLevelForPercentage`, `fuzzyMatch`) are pure type-only / string-op modules and are imported directly from the renderer source by relative path (`../../renderer/...`). Type imports: `Theme` from `'../../shared/theme-types'` (L2.5 precedent); `Shortcut` + `KeyboardMasteryStats` from `'../../renderer/types'` directly ŌĆö both are pure data shapes not present in `src/shared/` and matching the DeleteWorktreeModal precedent of pulling specific types from the canonical aggregator rather than copying into `src/shared/` (which would create the silent-drift surface audit risk A explicitly warns against). `MODAL_PRIORITIES` resolves via the existing `src/webFull/constants/modalPriorities.ts` re-export (uses `MODAL_PRIORITIES.SHORTCUTS_HELP`). Composes the L2.1 `Modal` primitive directly with `customHeader` + `footer` slots (matches renderer source); no `ModalFooter` ŌĆö the modal's header is a custom three-row block (title + count badge + close X / no-agents banner / search input + hint) and the footer is the mastery progress bar, neither of which fits the two-button `ModalFooter` idiom. Threads `theme` as a prop per the L2.1 / L2.3 / L2.4 / L2.5 convention. Parity catalog ships 10 stories total (6 happy + 4 negative ŌĆö exceeds the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor) covering: header chrome rendering, list rendering with formatted keys, no-agents banner trigger, mastery footer rendering with stats, search filter behavior, close-button onClose; negative paths cover empty-search "No shortcuts found" message, mastery footer omitted without stats, no-agents banner omitted without flag, Escape closes via layer stack, 100% trophy hidden when not fully mastered, and empty shortcut maps still render FIXED_SHORTCUTS. Uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary; 5 catalog-shape vitest guards pass. Renderer-side consumers (`AppModals`, `useModalProps`, the `?` / `Meta+/` shortcut handler) are NOT touched ŌĆö feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `ShortcutsHelpModal` lift").
- [x] ISC-44.layer-2.5.context_warning_sash: `ContextWarningSash` (renderer `src/renderer/components/ContextWarningSash.tsx`, 192 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API per Architect audit #6) lifted verbatim into `src/webFull/components/ContextWarningSash.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/ContextWarningSash.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-context-warning-sash` (leaf-parade lift wave). **Banner-shape, NOT modal-shape:** this is the first L2.5 lift that is not a modal ŌĆö it is a `role="alert"` `aria-live="polite"` banner that warns when the AI agent's context-window usage crosses a configurable yellow (warn) or red (urgent) threshold. Composition: no `Modal` / `ModalFooter` / layer-stack registration. Self-contained: ships its own `<style>` block with the `slideDown` / `pulse` keyframes the renderer source carries (the `slideDown` keyframe is defined but unused in the source ŌĆö preserved verbatim per the verbatim-lift rule). Pre-flight grep against `window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|ipcRenderer` returned empty (confirms audit #6). One import-path adjustment matching the L2.5 precedent: `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'`. `lucide-react` icons (`AlertTriangle`, `X`) are already a webFull-tree dep used by the L2.1 Modal / ConfirmModal / Settings primitives. Threads `theme` as a prop per the L2.1 / L2.3 / L2.4 / L2.5 convention. Internal state-machine preserved verbatim: per-tab dismissal Map keyed by `tabId ?? '__default__'`, re-show triggers on `+10%` usage bump OR yellowŌåÆred escalation since dismissal. Action surface: `onSummarizeClick` callback wired to the "Compact & Continue" button (renderer label is "Compact & Continue" despite the prop name ŌĆö preserved verbatim) plus an internally-handled Dismiss button (`X` icon, `aria-label="Dismiss warning"`, `title="Dismiss"`). **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/ContextWarningSash.parity.test.ts`, 8 stories: 4 happy + 4 negative ŌĆö meets brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards):** happy-path stories cover yellow-threshold banner render with "reaching X% capacity" copy + `aria-label="Context window at 65% capacity"`, red-threshold banner render with "consider compacting to continue" copy at 85%, action-row affordances (Compact & Continue button + dismiss `aria-label` / `title`), and yellowŌåÆred escalation re-show after dismissal at 65% climbing to 82%. Negative-path stories cover hidden-below-yellow-threshold (40%), hidden-when-`enabled=false` even at 95%, hidden after dismissal without meaningful usage bump (+3% drift stays within both gates), and the no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle guard pinning the presentational contract. 6 catalog-shape vitest guards including a banner-shape pin that asserts the catalog never drifts toward `role="dialog"` assertions (if a future refactor wraps the sash in a modal, that's a behavior change and the catalog should fail rather than silently track it). Uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary; passes the IPC-leakage guard. Renderer-side consumers (the AI tab chrome that mounts the sash above the prompt input) are NOT touched ŌĆö feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern (the consumer needs the context-usage subscription wired against the webFull WS bridge rather than the renderer's `window.maestro.processManager` callbacks, which is a separate lift). See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `ContextWarningSash` lift").
- [x] ISC-44.layer-2.5.execution_queue_indicator: `ExecutionQueueIndicator` (renderer `src/renderer/components/ExecutionQueueIndicator.tsx`, 191 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API per Architect audit) lifted verbatim into `src/webFull/components/ExecutionQueueIndicator.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/ExecutionQueueIndicator.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-execution-queue` (leaf-parade lift wave). Pure presentational compact indicator that surfaces the number of items queued for sequential AI-mode execution within a session (above the input area), with a click handler that opens the downstream `ExecutionQueueBrowser` modal (out-of-scope for this lift; the indicator's contract ends at `onClick`). Dynamic pill-row sizing via `ResizeObserver` collapses gracefully to a `+N` overflow token when the available width can't fit all tab pills. **Import-path adapts (matching the L2.5 precedent):** `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` (L2.5 precedent ŌĆö webFull has no `types/` aggregator). **Type-shape adapt:** the renderer source imports `Session` from `'../types'` which carries `executionQueue: QueuedItem[]` alongside ~80 other fields including Electron-bound surfaces (`claudeSession`, `agentSessionId` plumbing) that webFull does not model in its wire-protocol `SessionData`. Importing the renderer `Session` would drag the renderer types graph into the webFull tree ŌĆö the opposite of the lift's goal. The lift instead defines a narrow `ExecutionQueueSession` (one field: optional `executionQueue`) and `ExecutionQueueItem` (two fields: `type`, optional `tabName`) directly in the lifted module. This matches the DeleteWorktreeModal / ShortcutsHelpModal precedent of pulling only the specific data shape each lifted module consumes, rather than copying the entire renderer aggregator into `src/shared/` (which would create the silent-drift surface audit risk A explicitly warns against). When/if webFull's `SessionData` grows an `executionQueue` field over the WebSocket protocol, the narrow type becomes a structural-subtype of the wider Session and callers can pass the real Session directly without changes. **Composition shape:** no Modal / ModalFooter / layer-stack ŌĆö the indicator is a single `<button>` with inline tab pills + type-breakdown icons + "Click to view" affordance. `lucide-react` icons (`ListOrdered`, `Command`, `MessageSquare`) kept verbatim ŌĆö already a webFull-tree dep. **Theme access pattern:** kept the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention. Parity catalog ships 10 stories total (5 happy + 5 negative ŌĆö meets the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor) covering: plural-count phrase + click-to-view affordance, singular-count pivot at queue.length === 1, message + command type-breakdown counts, tab pill with `(N)` count suffix when a tab has multiple items, `onClick` activation; negative paths cover empty-queue null-render, message-icon suppression when only commands queued, command-icon suppression when only messages queued, "Unknown" pill fallback for items missing tabName, and no `(1)` suffix when each tab has exactly one item. Uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards pass (6/6). Renderer-side consumers (`InputArea` host that gates rendering on AI mode + queue non-empty, the `ExecutionQueueBrowser` modal the click opens) are NOT touched ŌĆö feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `ExecutionQueueIndicator` lift").
- [x] ISC-44.layer-2.5.theme_picker: `ThemePicker` (renderer `src/renderer/components/ThemePicker.tsx`, 70 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API per pre-flight grep) lifted verbatim into `src/webFull/components/ThemePicker.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/ThemePicker.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-theme-picker` (leaf-parade lift wave). Tiny presentational primitive: renders a two-column swatch grid grouped by `mode` (dark / light) with Moon / Sun icons above each section heading, active swatch ringed in the current accent colour, click handler is a plain `(id) => void` callback. **Import-path adapts:** single swap ŌĆö `Theme` and `ThemeId` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` (L2.5 precedent; webFull has no `types/` aggregator that re-exports these). **Composition shape:** no Modal / ModalFooter / layer-stack / `useLayerStack` / `MODAL_PRIORITIES` / `formatShortcutKeys` ŌĆö just `react` + `lucide-react` (`Moon`, `Sun`, already webFull-tree deps used by sibling L2.5 lifts) + the two pure types. **Theme access pattern:** kept the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention, plus the rich `themes: Record<ThemeId, Theme>` map prop that lets the picker render any registered theme's swatch using that theme's own colour palette for the swatch preview strip. **Settings plumbing intentionally out-of-scope:** the `setActiveThemeId` callback is a plain function ŌĆö the actual settings-store write + cross-window broadcast lives upstream of this component (renderer-side `setActiveTheme` ŌåÆ `window.maestro.settings.set` ŌåÆ settings-broadcast, already covered by `ISC-44.global.settings_broadcast`) and stays in the renderer / will be lifted in its own brief when the webFull host wires this primitive into a Settings panel. Parity catalog ships 8 stories total (4 happy + 4 negative ŌĆö meets the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor): happy paths cover both mode-section headings rendering, swatch buttons rendering per theme with names visible, active-swatch ring-2 indicator, and click-fires-callback; negative paths cover absent-mode-themes section rendering with no swatches, non-active swatches lacking the ring-2 class, empty-themes-map rendering no buttons, and the no-guard-on-active-click semantics (clicking the active swatch still fires the callback ŌĆö the parent decides whether to no-op). Uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards pass (6/6). `src/web/` + `src/renderer/` + `src/main/` + `src/server/` untouched. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `ThemePicker` lift").
- [x] ISC-44.layer-2.5.collapsible_json_viewer: `CollapsibleJsonViewer` (renderer `src/renderer/components/CollapsibleJsonViewer.tsx`, 303 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API) lifted verbatim into `src/webFull/components/CollapsibleJsonViewer.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/CollapsibleJsonViewer.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-collapsible-json-viewer` (leaf-parade lift wave ŌĆö chain-merge #8). **Cross-fork neutralization:** this lift **NEUTRALIZES the webFullŌåÆrenderer cross-fork import introduced by the just-merged `leaf-agent-error` wave** (chain-merge #7, commit `dc436bffe`): `src/webFull/components/AgentErrorModal.tsx` currently imports the viewer from `'../../renderer/components/CollapsibleJsonViewer'`. That direct webFullŌåÆrenderer reach was documented at the time as audit-risk-A silent-drift surface (the renderer is the upstream-mirror in this fork ŌĆö every webFullŌåÆrenderer reach is a future rebase conflict source). With the viewer landed in webFull, the cleanup wave that follows can retarget `AgentErrorModal`'s import from `'../../renderer/components/CollapsibleJsonViewer'` ŌåÆ `'./CollapsibleJsonViewer'` and fully close that audit-risk-A surface around the JSON-details panel ŌĆö explicitly out of scope for THIS brief by instruction ("After this lands, AgentErrorModal can be retargeted to use the webFull copy in a follow-up"). Pre-flight grep against `window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|ipcRenderer` on the renderer source returned empty (exit 1). Transitive dep audit: only impure import is `safeClipboardWrite` from `src/renderer/utils/clipboard.ts`, which routes through `navigator.clipboard.writeText` only ŌĆö pure browser API; the Electron-only `window.maestro.shell.copyImageToClipboard` surface is exclusively inside `safeClipboardWriteImage`, a sibling function this viewer does NOT call (confirmed by Read: only `safeClipboardWrite` is imported). The dep chain is browser-safe end-to-end. Two import-path adjustments matching the L2.5 precedent: `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'`; `safeClipboardWrite` from `'../utils/clipboard'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/utils/clipboard'` (pure browser-API utility pulled by relative path ŌĆö L2.5 precedent set by the `leaf-shortcuts-help` lift for `FIXED_SHORTCUTS` etc., and by the prior `leaf-agent-error` lift for the viewer itself; duplicating a tiny browser-safe utility into webFull would create a silent-drift surface, which is the audit-risk-A pattern THIS lift is closing at the component level). `lucide-react` icons (`ChevronRight`, `ChevronDown`, `Copy`, `Check`) are already a webFull-tree dep used by Settings / ConfirmModal / L2.1 Modal. Threads `theme` as a prop per the L2.1 / L2.3 / L2.4 / L2.5 convention. Composition shape: presentational JSON tree ŌĆö pure render component with internal `useState`-driven expand/collapse + clipboard-copy affordance per node. No `Modal` / `ModalFooter` / layer-stack registration (this is not a modal). **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/CollapsibleJsonViewer.parity.test.ts`, 12 stories: 6 happy + 6 negative ŌĆö meets brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 8 catalog-shape vitest guards):** render-shape oriented per the ToggleButtonGroup / SessionListItem precedent (`hasElement` + `hasText` only ŌĆö click semantics, hover-to-reveal-copy, and clipboard-write semantics belong to the future feature-consumer's catalog or to the browser-API contract, not the primitive's). Happy-path stories cover object root with `{`/`}` brackets + quoted-key rendering + string/number value rendering, array root with `[`/`]` brackets + numeric entry rendering, primitive value categories (string quoted, number bare, boolean literal, null literal), per-node Copy affordance (`button[title="Copy value"]`), `rootLabel` surfacing as a top-level quoted key, and string truncation with the literal `...` marker when length exceeds `maxStringLength`. Negative-path stories cover primitive root (no brackets, Copy affordance still present), empty object / empty array bracket rendering without children chevron, no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle guard pinning the presentational contract, the no-modal-no-banner structural pin (asserts no story targets `[role="dialog"]` or `[role="alert"]` directly ŌĆö the `:not(:has(...))` exclusion idiom remains permitted), and `undefined` literal rendering. Uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary; passes the IPC-leakage guard, the render-shape-only guard (no interaction verbs), and the no-modal-no-banner structural pin. `AgentErrorModal`'s cross-fork import remains intact in this branch by design ŌĆö the retargeting cleanup is the follow-up wave per the brief. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `CollapsibleJsonViewer` lift").
- [x] ISC-44.layer-2.5.queued_items_list: `QueuedItemsList` (renderer `src/renderer/components/QueuedItemsList.tsx`, 279 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API per pre-flight grep) lifted verbatim into `src/webFull/components/QueuedItemsList.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/QueuedItemsList.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-queued-items-list` (leaf-parade lift wave).
- [x] ISC-44.layer-2.5.tool_call_card: `ToolCallCard` (renderer `src/renderer/components/ToolCallCard.tsx`, 269 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API per pre-flight grep) lifted verbatim into `src/webFull/components/ToolCallCard.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/ToolCallCard.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-tool-call-card` (leaf-parade lift wave). Presentational tool-execution card that surfaces tool call details from OpenCode and Codex session messages. Default-collapsed view: a single horizontal pill with `ChevronRight` + `"Tool: <name>"` label chip + status icon + `"Show more"` button. Click or keyboard activation (Enter/Space) expands to a sticky header with `ChevronDown` + label chip + status icon + `"Collapse"` button, an Input section (collapsible JSON), a status row carrying optional `"Time: <timestamp>"` label + literal `"Status: <status>"` text, and an Output section (collapsible JSON). Both name-keys honoured by exported `getToolName` helper (Claude's `name` key + OpenCode's `tool` key) ŌĆö heterogeneous session entries render correctly. Status icon mapping verbatim: `completed`/`success` ŌåÆ `CheckCircle2` themed-success-green, `running`/`pending` ŌåÆ `Loader2` spinning themed-warning, `error`/`failed` ŌåÆ `AlertCircle` themed-error-red, default ŌåÆ `CheckCircle2`. Pre-flight `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]" src/renderer/components/ToolCallCard.tsx` ŌåÆ empty. The component is pure render ŌĆö all side effects are owned internally by `useState` toggles (expanded/collapsed); no callbacks fire upward. One import-path adapt matching the L2.5 precedent: `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` (standard L2.5 swap ŌĆö webFull has no `types/` aggregator). **Type shapes kept inline:** `ToolState` and `ToolUseEntry` are session-message data shapes the renderer source itself defines inline (the renderer does NOT pull them from a shared aggregator). The lift keeps the same inline definitions ŌĆö they are narrow data shapes the component reads directly off the session message, with the source-of-truth living in the OpenCode / Codex session parsers (`src/main/parsers/`). Matches the `ExecutionQueueIndicator` / `QueuedItemsList` precedent of pulling only the specific data shape each lifted module consumes rather than copying the entire renderer aggregator into `src/shared/` (silent-drift surface audit risk A). **Composition shape:** no Modal / ModalFooter / layer-stack registration ŌĆö the card is an inline transcript artefact rendered between message bubbles. The internal `CollapsibleContent` helper is kept inline verbatim (a tiny JSON.stringify + line-truncate widget); it is NOT replaced by the sibling L2.5 `CollapsibleJsonViewer` lift (which carries a richer tree-render contract ŌĆö the two coexist; the renderer source's own internal helper has different chrome and the brief is a verbatim lift, not a chrome-convergence pass). `lucide-react` icons (`ChevronDown`, `ChevronRight`, `Clock`, `CheckCircle2`, `Loader2`, `AlertCircle`) kept verbatim ŌĆö already a webFull-tree dep. **Theme access pattern:** kept the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention, consistent with every L2.x lift. Parity catalog ships 11 stories total (5 happy + 6 negative ŌĆö exceeds the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor): happy paths cover collapsed pill with `"Tool: <name>"` chip + `"Show more"` button + `[role="button"]` keyboard-activation surface, expanded view with `"Collapse"` button + chip + `"Status: <status>"` + `"Time: <timestamp>"` rows, OpenCode `tool` key honoured (heterogeneous session entry shape), Input/Output section labels with `<pre>` blocks, and timestamp-omission case rendering status row without time row. Negative paths cover empty-`toolUse=[]` null-render via `body:not(:has(button))`, collapsed view excluding the `"Collapse"` affordance (`button:has-text("Collapse")` negation), `getToolName` falling back to `"unknown"` when both name-keys are absent, no-modal-no-banner structural pin (`body:not(:has([role="dialog"]))` + `body:not(:has([role="alert"]))`), no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle pin on mount ŌåÆ expand-click ŌåÆ collapse-click, and errored-status case still rendering the tool chip with `"Status: error"` row. Uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary; 7 catalog-shape vitest guards pass (7/7) including a presentational-contract pin asserting no story targets `[role="dialog"]` or `[role="alert"]` positively (the `:not(:has(...))` exclusion idiom remains permitted). Barrel export added at `src/webFull/components/index.ts` (`export { ToolCallCard, getToolName } from './ToolCallCard';`). Renderer-side consumers are NOT touched ŌĆö feature wiring into the webFull tree (the AI tab transcript view, the group-chat transcript view) is a downstream-layer concern. `src/web/` + `src/renderer/` + `src/main/` + `src/server/` UNTOUCHED (`git diff main..HEAD -- src/web/ src/renderer/ src/main/ src/server/ | wc -l` ŌåÆ 0). `npm run lint` clean. `npx vitest run src/webFull/components/ToolCallCard.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ 7/7 catalog-shape tests passed. Not pushed per brief instruction ŌĆö branch sits locally at `leaf-tool-call-card`, awaiting Trillium's merge. **Unblocks one of two `GroupChatInput` preconditions** per the leaf-parade audit #7 cited in the brief: `src/renderer/components/GroupChatInput.tsx` imports `QueuedItemsList` directly (line 32 of the renderer source) and renders it inline above its textarea (line 456). Landing this leaf reduces the `GroupChatInput` blocking-deps count from 2 ŌåÆ 1; when both blockers land, the `GroupChatInput` lift becomes a verbatim sibling lift with all imports resolving inside the webFull tree, dropping the cross-fork edge the L2.5 `GroupChatPanel` lift currently carries (`'../../renderer/components/GroupChatInput'`). Pre-flight `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]" src/renderer/components/QueuedItemsList.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (`NO_MODULE_LOAD_ELECTRON_REFS` sentinel returned). The component is a pure presentational view ŌĆö all side effects are delivered via prop callbacks (`onRemoveQueuedItem`, `onReorderItems`); internal state is the per-tab dismissal-confirmation id, the `Set<string>` of expanded message ids, and the drag/drop index pair. One import-path adapt matching the L2.5 precedent: `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` (standard L2.5 swap ŌĆö webFull has no `types/` aggregator). **Type-shape adapt:** the renderer source imports `QueuedItem` from `'../types'` which carries 10 fields (`id`, `timestamp`, `tabId`, `type`, `text`, `images`, `command`, `commandArgs`, `commandDescription`, `tabName`, `readOnlyMode`). This view actually touches only 6 of them (`id`, `tabId`, `type`, `text`, `images`, `command`) ŌĆö the others are read by sibling consumers (e.g. `ExecutionQueueIndicator` reads `tabName`; the queue-browser modal reads `commandArgs` / `commandDescription`; the dispatcher reads `timestamp` / `readOnlyMode`). The lift defines a narrow `QueuedItem` shape directly in the lifted module covering the 6 fields it actually reads. This matches the `ExecutionQueueIndicator` precedent of pulling only the specific data shape each lifted module consumes rather than copying the entire renderer aggregator into `src/shared/` (which would create the silent-drift surface audit risk A explicitly warns against). The local shape is strictly narrower than the renderer's full `QueuedItem` so it remains a structural-subtype ŌĆö when/if webFull's `SessionData` grows an `executionQueue` field over the WebSocket protocol carrying the full `QueuedItem`, callers can pass real items directly without changes here. **Composition shape:** no `Modal` / `ModalFooter` / layer-stack registration ŌĆö the panel renders inline above the prompt input and gates its own ephemeral confirmation modal (a bare `fixed inset-0` overlay, not a `Modal` primitive ŌĆö verbatim from the renderer source; future plumbing could re-port the confirmation onto the L2.1 `Modal` if the chrome ever converges, but that's out of scope per the verbatim-lift rule). HTML5 drag-and-drop reordering via the four-handler dance (`onDragStart`, `onDragOver`, `onDragEnd`, `onDragLeave`) preserved verbatim; drag is gated on `onReorderItems` presence AND `filteredQueue.length > 1`. `lucide-react` icons (`X`, `ChevronDown`, `ChevronUp`, `GripVertical`) kept verbatim ŌĆö already a webFull-tree dep. **Theme access pattern:** kept the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention, consistent with every L2.x lift. Parity catalog ships 12 stories total (6 happy + 6 negative ŌĆö exceeds the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor): happy paths cover the QUEUED separator with literal "(<count>)" copy, per-item message and command text rendering, the Remove `[title="Remove from queue"]` affordance, the "Show all (N lines)" expand toggle for messages >200 chars, the plural "<N> images attached" indicator, and the confirmation-modal copy ("Remove Queued Message?" / "Cancel" / "Remove") when Remove is clicked; negative paths cover empty-queue null-render, `activeTabId` filter that excludes all items (null-render), suppression of the "Show all" toggle when message Ōēż200 chars, suppression of the "attached" indicator when no images, the no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle pin, and the singular "1 image attached" boundary case (a future refactor using `>= 1` instead of `> 1` for the plural gate would silently break grammar). Uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards pass (6/6) including a presentational-contract pin asserting the catalog never drifts toward `[role="dialog"]` positive targets on the panel root (the panel is inline chrome, NOT a modal ŌĆö the confirmation it gates is a separate ephemeral surface). Renderer-side consumers (`GroupChatInput`, `TerminalOutput`) are NOT touched ŌĆö feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern. When the second `GroupChatInput` blocking dep (per audit #7) lands, the `GroupChatInput` lift's sibling-import line will resolve to `./QueuedItemsList` inside the webFull tree. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `QueuedItemsList` lift").

Parity catalog declares 5 stories (catalog-shape vitest guards pass 5/5). Renderer-side consumers (the AI tab chrome that mounts the composer + the renderer hooks that wire `setText` / `onSend`) are NOT touched ŌĆö feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern (and depends on the `useTemplateAutocomplete` hook being lifted into `src/webFull/hooks/` per its own brief). 0 IPC namespaces touched. 0 Electron-only APIs touched. 0 `src/main/` / `src/web/` / `src/server/` touches. Export added to `src/webFull/components/index.ts` under the existing "Layer 2.5 leaf-parade primitives" section.
