- [x] ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_expanded_modal: `AutoRunExpandedModal` (renderer `src/renderer/components/AutoRunExpandedModal.tsx`, ~470 LOC, 0 module-load IPC per pre-flight grep `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|ipcRenderer" src/renderer/components/AutoRunExpandedModal.tsx` ŌåÆ empty exit 1) lifted verbatim into `src/webFull/components/AutoRunExpandedModal.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/AutoRunExpandedModal.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-autorun-expanded-and-doc-selector` (paired leaf-parade lift alongside `AutoRunDocumentSelector` in the same branch). Full-screen Auto-Run editor shell that portals into `document.body`, registers with the LayerStack at `MODAL_PRIORITIES.AUTORUN_EXPANDED` (705) with `focusTrap: 'strict'`, and renders the renderer-side `AutoRun` view through a cross-fork import. The modal shell itself is purely presentational ŌĆö header title + Edit/Preview toggle + Save/Revert (gated on `isDirty`) + Run/Stop (gated on `isLocked` from `batchRunState.isRunning`) + PlayBooks (gated on `onOpenMarketplace`) + Collapse/Close affordances, plus an unsaved-changes `ConfirmModal` composed on top when the user attempts to close while `isDirty=true`. Polls dirty state from the `AutoRun` ref every 100ms (verbatim from renderer source). **Import-path adapts (eight, with one explicit cross-fork edge documented inline):** (1) `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` (standard L2.5 swap); (2) `BatchRunState`, `SessionState`, `Shortcut` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/types'` (cross-fork transitive type-only import ŌĆö matches the `GroupChatHeader` / `SessionItem` / `ExecutionQueueIndicator` precedent of importing renderer-aggregator types directly rather than copying the type module into `src/shared/`); (3) `useLayerStack` from `'../contexts/LayerStackContext'` ŌåÆ no path change (webFull-side L2.1 context); (4) `MODAL_PRIORITIES` from `'../constants/modalPriorities'` ŌåÆ no path change (webFull re-export shim per Architect audit risk A); (5) **CROSS-FORK EDGE ŌĆö `AutoRun, type AutoRunHandle` from `'./AutoRun'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/components/AutoRun'`**. The `AutoRun` view is the full Auto-Run editor surface (~2200+ LOC, heavy runtime IPC: `window.maestro.autoRun.*`, `window.maestro.fs.*`, `window.maestro.dialog.*`, `window.maestro.shell.openPath`, `window.maestro.images.*`, etc.). Lifting `AutoRun` itself would drag the entire Auto-Run IPC namespace through webFull, which is out of scope for this leaf and blocked on the server-side `/api/autorun/*` + `/api/fs/*` + `/api/images/*` REST routes that have not been ported. This matches the established cross-fork-edge precedent set by `GitDiffViewer`'s edge to `'../../renderer/components/ImageDiffViewer'` before the corresponding routes landed. The modal shell itself is a pure presentational surface that delegates the heavy document-editor view to `AutoRun` through an `AutoRunHandle` ref surface (`isDirty()`, `save()`, `revert()`, `switchMode()`, `focus()`). A future `ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_view` lift drops the edge when the server routes land. (6) `DocumentTaskCount` from `'./AutoRunDocumentSelector'` ŌåÆ no path change (sibling lift in THIS branch resolves locally without a cross-fork edge); (7) `ConfirmModal` from `'./ConfirmModal'` ŌåÆ no path change (L2.1 webFull primitive); (8) `formatShortcutKeys` from `'../utils/shortcutFormatter'` ŌåÆ no path change (webFull-side mirror from earlier L2.x port). **Composition shape:** portal-mounted into `document.body` as a `fixed inset-0 z-[100]` overlay with a centered card sized at `90vw x 80vh` matching the PromptComposer modal. Header carries the title, the Edit/Preview/Save/Revert/Run/PlayBooks button row, and the Collapse/Close affordances. Body renders the `AutoRun` view with `hideTopControls` set so the header isn't duplicated. **Keyboard handling:** registers with the LayerStack at `MODAL_PRIORITIES.AUTORUN_EXPANDED` with `focusTrap: 'strict'`; Escape routes through the LayerStack's `onEscape` callback, which fires the unsaved-changes-aware close handler. **Theme access pattern:** kept the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention, consistent with every L2.x lift. **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/AutoRunExpandedModal.parity.test.ts`, 11 stories total: 5 happy + 6 negative ŌĆö meets the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards):** happy paths cover the header title + Collapse/Close affordances, the Edit/Preview toggle rendering with canonical title copy, the Run button surfacing when not-locked and agent-idle (`title="Run auto-run on tasks"`), the Stop button surfacing when locked and not-stopping (`title="Stop auto-run"`), and the PlayBooks button surfacing when `onOpenMarketplace` is provided (`title^="Browse PlayBooks"`). Negative paths cover Run-button suppression when locked, the Stopping-state title swap when `isStopping=true` (`title="Stopping after current task..."` + "Stopping..." label), PlayBooks suppression when `onOpenMarketplace` is omitted, the Edit-button title swap to the locked-state copy (`title="Editing disabled while Auto Run active"` + `disabled` attribute), unsaved-confirm suppression on initial mount (gated behind a state flag), and the no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle pin for the SHELL itself (the cross-fork `AutoRun` view's runtime IPC belongs to a future ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_view catalog, NOT this shell). Catalog uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary (`hasElement`, `hasText`, `wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`). 6/6 in-suite vitest guards green. 0 IPC namespaces touched at module load; 0 `src/main/` touches; 0 `src/web/` touches; 0 `src/renderer/` edits; 0 `src/server/` edits. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö paired `AutoRunExpandedModal` + `AutoRunDocumentSelector` lift").

Negative paths cover Bionify suppression when no document is selected, Bionify suppression when `onToggleBionify` is omitted, create-modal suppression on initial mount (gated behind a state flag), task-percentage pill suppression when `documentTaskCounts` is undefined, task-percentage pill suppression when the counts entry's `total=0` (per the `total === 0 ŌåÆ null` short-circuit in `getTaskPercentage`), and the no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle pin. Both are now closed: route (a) shipped as `/api/fs/read-image` on `origin/main` `1b2ae0a53` per the sibling `ISC-44.shim.fs_read_image_route`; surface (b) is closed by the sibling shim ISC `ISC-44.shim.use_autorun_image_handling_webfull_port` in this same branch). **IPC site ŌåÆ REST / browser-API mapping (13 sites, all closed):** 3├Ś `fs.readFile(image)` ŌåÆ GET `/api/fs/read-image` (returns bare-text `data:image/<ext>;base64,...`); 2├Ś `autorun.writeDoc(folder, file, content)` ŌåÆ POST `/api/autorun/write-doc` (flattened to `{path, content}` where the client constructs `path = ${folderPath}/${filename}` client-side); 2├Ś `shell.openExternal(href)` ŌåÆ `window.open(href, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer')` (browser-native, scheme guard preserved verbatim); plus the 6 hook sites served by the sibling port at `'../hooks/useAutoRunImageHandling'` (1├Ś listImages ŌåÆ GET `/api/autorun/list-images`, 1├Ś fs.readFile inside list-loop ŌåÆ GET `/api/fs/read-image`, 2├Ś saveImage ŌåÆ POST `/api/autorun/save-image`, 2├Ś deleteImage ŌåÆ DELETE `/api/autorun/delete-image`). **Strip-and-promote-to-prop adapt ŌĆö settings store (matches the established L2.5 precedent set by `TerminalOutput`, `GroupChatInput`, `AppOverlays`, `HistoryDetailModal`):** the renderer source self-sources three Bionify selectors from `useSettingsStore` (which carries 110+ `window.maestro.settings.set` IPC sites and is renderer-only as of this lift). The three selectors (`bionifyReadingMode`, `bionifyIntensity`, `bionifyAlgorithm`) are stripped + promoted to optional props with renderer-side store defaults (`false`, `1`, `'- 0 1 1 2 0.4'`) so a host that doesn't wire them through still gets the renderer's baseline behavior. **Import-path adapts (ten, with one explicit local sibling import to the hook port):** (1) `tokenCounter` from `'../utils/tokenCounter'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/utils/tokenCounter'` (CROSS-FORK EDGE ŌĆö pure tokenizer, no IPC, no DOM); (2) `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` + `BatchRunState, SessionState, Shortcut` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/types'` (standard L2.5 split); (3) `FileNode` from `'../types/fileTree'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/types/fileTree'` (CROSS-FORK EDGE ŌĆö pure tree data shape, no IPC); (4) sibling components (`AutoRunnerHelpModal`, `ResetTasksConfirmModal`, `MermaidRenderer`, `AutoRunDocumentSelector`, `AutoRunLightbox`, `AutoRunSearchBar`, `TemplateAutocompleteDropdown`) ŌĆö paths unchanged (all already in webFull from prior L2.5 lifts); (5) `useTemplateAutocomplete, useAutoRunUndo` from `'../hooks'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/hooks'` (CROSS-FORK EDGE ŌĆö both pure renderer hooks with 0 IPC sites verified by grep) + `useAutoRunImageHandling, imageCache` from the LOCAL sibling port at `'../hooks/useAutoRunImageHandling'` (the critical break ŌĆö the sibling fetch-backed port replaces the hook's 6 IPC sites); (6) `REMARK_GFM_PLUGINS, generateAutoRunProseStyles, createMarkdownComponents` from `'../utils/markdownConfig'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/utils/markdownConfig'` (CROSS-FORK EDGE ŌĆö the module has 2 `window.maestro.shell.openExternal` sites at lines 696, 796 inside its DEFAULT `a` renderer; AutoRun passes an explicit `onExternalLinkClick` callback through both `createMarkdownComponents` calls (verified at lines 1465-1469 and 1518-1522 of the renderer source), which overrides the default `a` renderer and routes through our `window.open`-using callback instead, so the transitive IPC sites are unreachable at runtime from this component); (7) `formatShortcutKeys` from `'../utils/shortcutFormatter'` ŌåÆ unchanged (webFull mirror exists from earlier L2.x port); (8) `remarkFileLinks, buildFileTreeIndices` from `'../utils/remarkFileLinks'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/utils/remarkFileLinks'` (CROSS-FORK EDGE ŌĆö pure remark plugin, no IPC reach); (9) `useBatchStore` from `'../stores/batchStore'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/stores/batchStore'` (CROSS-FORK EDGE ŌĆö pure Zustand store, 0 IPC sites verified by grep; this component only READS from it); (10) `useSettingsStore` import ŌĆö REMOVED entirely (replaced by props per the strip-and-promote section above). **SSH parity:** the server-side routes 501 on `sshRemoteId` (the W3 routes refuse SSH redirection ŌĆö that's the Electron IPC path's job). The component accepts `sshRemoteId` in its prop surface for call-site parity with the renderer but does NOT forward it on the wire. webFull-mounted sessions running against an SSH-remote AutoRun folder remain an out-of-scope surface; the Electron preload path continues to own that case verbatim. **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/AutoRun.parity.test.ts`, 11 stories total: 5 happy + 5 negative + 1 lifecycle pin ŌĆö meets the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards):** happy paths cover the Edit/Preview toggle + selected document `.md` suffix, the Run button surfacing when batch not running and agent idle, the Stop button surfacing when `batchRunState.isRunning=true`, the Expand affordance when `onExpand` provided, and the Help affordance always present; negative paths cover Expand suppression when `onExpand` omitted, top-controls suppression when `hideTopControls=true`, Run-button disabled when `sessionState="busy"`, error-banner suppression when not error-paused, and Refresh-button suppression when no folder selected; the lifecycle pin asserts zero Electron-preload-bridge references at module load or runtime. - [x] ISC-44.shim.use_autorun_image_handling_webfull_port: `useAutoRunImageHandling` (renderer hook `src/renderer/hooks/batch/useAutoRunImageHandling.ts`, 533 LOC, **6 IPC sites** total: 1├Ś `autorun.listImages`, 1├Ś `fs.readFile` inside list-loop, 2├Ś `autorun.saveImage`, 2├Ś `autorun.deleteImage`) ported into `src/webFull/hooks/useAutoRunImageHandling.ts` as a fetch-backed equivalent. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-autorun-main` alongside the sibling `ISC-44.lift.autorun_main` (paired lift ŌĆö the AutoRun component depends on this hook). **IPC site ŌåÆ REST route mapping (6 sites, all closed):** `autorun.listImages(folder, file, ssh)` ŌåÆ GET `/api/autorun/list-images?folderPath=<ŌĆ”>&docFilename=<ŌĆ”>` (returns `{success, images?: [{filename, relativePath}], error?}`); `fs.readFile(imageAbsolutePath, ssh)` ŌåÆ GET `/api/fs/read-image?path=<absolute>` (returns bare-text `data:image/<ext>;base64,<payload>`, the renderer-side IPC's `data:` URL shape verbatim); `autorun.saveImage(folder, file, base64, ext, ssh)` ŌåÆ POST `/api/autorun/save-image` with body `{folderPath, docFilename, dataUrl, extension}` (returns `{success, relativePath?, error?}`); `autorun.deleteImage(folder, relativePath, ssh)` ŌåÆ DELETE `/api/autorun/delete-image?folderPath=<ŌĆ”>&relativePath=<ŌĆ”>` (returns `{removed: bool, timestamp}` ŌĆö caller ignores the return). All routes already exist on `origin/main` `1b2ae0a53` per the W3-autorun-images route wave + the audit-correction W3-fs-read-image route (sibling `ISC-44.shim.fs_read_image_route` CLOSED). **SSH parity:** the server routes 501 on `sshRemoteId` (matches the W3 precedent ŌĆö Electron path owns SSH); the hook accepts `sshRemoteId` in its `UseAutoRunImageHandlingDeps` interface for call-site parity with the renderer source but does NOT forward it on the wire. **Return-shape parity:** mirrors `UseAutoRunImageHandlingReturn` field-for-field (`attachmentsList`, `attachmentPreviews`, `attachmentsExpanded`, `setAttachmentsExpanded`, `lightboxFilename`, `lightboxExternalUrl`, `fileInputRef`, `handlePaste`, `handleFileSelect`, `handleRemoveAttachment`, `openLightboxByFilename`, `closeLightbox`, `handleLightboxNavigate`, `handleLightboxDelete`). The exported `imageCache` is preserved as a module-level `Map<string, string>` singleton with the renderer's cache-key shape (`${folderPath}:${relativePath}`); `imageCache.delete()` calls in `handleRemoveAttachment` / `handleLightboxDelete` are unchanged. **Auth threading:** uses `buildApiUrl()` from `'../utils/config'` ŌĆö the same mechanism `useMarketplace` documents. Pulls the server-injected security token from `window.__MAESTRO_CONFIG__` and prefixes it as `/${token}/api/ŌĆ”`. **Wire-shape parity:** `listImages` returns JSON; `read-image` returns BARE TEXT (not JSON-wrapped) consumed via `await res.text()` so the result drops directly into `<img src>` or the `result.startsWith('data:')` shape-check; `save-image` accepts the bare base64 payload OR a full `data:` URL (the route's `decodeImageDataUrl()` handles both ŌĆö we forward the bare base64 to match the renderer call shape verbatim); `delete-image` accepts inputs in EITHER query string OR JSON body ŌĆö we send query string for `fetch()` ergonomics (the route handles both per its own dual-shape contract). **Pure browser runtime:** `grep "window\.maestro\|electron\|ipcRenderer" src/webFull/hooks/useAutoRunImageHandling.ts` returns ZERO non-comment hits. The only window-touching APIs are `fetch` (browser-standard) and DOM event types (`React.ClipboardEvent`, `React.ChangeEvent`). 0 IPC namespaces touched; 0 `src/main/` touches; 0 `src/web/` touches; 0 `src/renderer/` edits; 0 `src/server/` edits. **Files added:** `src/webFull/hooks/useAutoRunImageHandling.ts` (sole file ŌĆö no separate parity catalog; the hook's behavior is exercised through the `AutoRun.parity.test.ts` lifecycle-pin story that asserts the whole component-plus-hook subgraph has zero preload-bridge references at module load). See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `AutoRun` full lift (third attempt)").

All side effects flow through caller-owned prop callbacks (`setCustomThemeColors`, `setCustomThemeBaseId`, `onSelect`, `onImportError`, `onImportSuccess`). **Import-path adapts (two, standard L2.5 swaps):** (1) `Theme`, `ThemeColors`, `ThemeId` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` ŌĆö webFull has no `types/` aggregator (precedent set by `ExecutionQueueIndicator`, `ThemePicker`, `ContextWarningSash`); (2) `THEMES`, `DEFAULT_CUSTOM_THEME_COLORS` from `'../constants/themes'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/themes'` ŌĆö webFull has no `constants/themes.ts` mirror, the renderer constants re-export from the same canonical shared source anyway (precedent set by `participantColors.test.ts` already importing `'../../shared/themes'`). - [x] ISC-44.layer-2.5.execution_queue_browser: `ExecutionQueueBrowser` (renderer `src/renderer/components/ExecutionQueueBrowser.tsx`, 618 LOC, 0 module-load IPC, 0 Electron-only API per pre-flight grep) lifted verbatim into `src/webFull/components/ExecutionQueueBrowser.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/ExecutionQueueBrowser.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-custom-theme-and-execution-queue` (leaf-parade lift wave, paired with the sibling `CustomThemeBuilder` lift in a single atomic commit). Modal for browsing and managing the execution queue across all sessions. Supports filtering by current project vs global view; synthetic mouse-driven drag-and-drop reordering of items within a session with a 150ms press-and-hold gate before drag initiates, drop-zone indicators between items, escape-key cancel, global mouseup cleanup; per-item Type icon (`Command` / `MessageSquare`), tab-name pill (click ŌåÆ switch session), time-since-queued chip, 100-char truncation for long messages, command-description chip, plural-aware image-count chip, hover-gated Remove affordance (`title="Remove from queue"`). Pre-flight `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|ipcRenderer" src/renderer/components/ExecutionQueueBrowser.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (exit 1). The component is purely presentational ŌĆö every side effect flows through caller-owned prop callbacks (`onClose`, `onRemoveItem`, `onSwitchSession`, `onReorderItems`). **Import-path adapts (three, matching the L2.5 precedent set by `QueuedItemsList` / `ExecutionQueueIndicator` / `DeleteWorktreeModal`):** (1) `useLayerStack` from `'../contexts/LayerStackContext'` resolves to the webFull L2.1 LayerStack lift (standard L2.5 swap ŌĆö established by every layer-stack-registering lift in the wave); (2) `MODAL_PRIORITIES` from `'../constants/modalPriorities'` resolves to the webFull re-export shim (uses `MODAL_PRIORITIES.EXECUTION_QUEUE_BROWSER` = 670, the canonical priority value in the renderer source under the "standard modals" range); (3) `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` (standard L2.5 swap), and `Session` + `QueuedItem` are NOT pulled from `'../types'` ŌĆö they're defined inline as narrow `ExecutionQueueSession` / `QueuedItem` shapes capturing only the fields this view reads (3 fields on session: `id`, `name`, `executionQueue`; 8 fields on item: `id`, `type`, `command`, `commandDescription`, `text`, `images`, `tabName`, `timestamp`). The renderer's full `Session` carries ~80 fields including Electron-bound surfaces (e.g. `claudeSession`, `agentSessionId` plumbing) that webFull does not model in its wire-protocol `SessionData`; importing the renderer `Session` here would drag the entire renderer types graph into the webFull tree ŌĆö the opposite of the lift's goal. Matches the `ExecutionQueueIndicator` / `QueuedItemsList` precedent of pulling only the specific data shape each lifted module consumes rather than copying the entire renderer aggregator into `src/shared/` (which would create the silent-drift surface audit risk A explicitly warns against). When/if webFull's `SessionData` grows an `executionQueue` field over the WebSocket protocol, these narrow types become a structural-subtype of the wider Session and callers can pass the real Session directly without changes here. Parity catalog ships 10 stories total (5 happy + 5 negative ŌĆö meets the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor): happy paths cover the modal chrome + title + total-count badge + view-mode toggle + footer hint, the per-item row with position-index + tab-name pill + "Just now" time chip + 3-total badge, the Remove button title attribute, the plural images suffix at length > 1, and the empty-state copy with the "for this agent" suffix in current-agent view; negative paths cover the `isOpen=false` null-render, the global-view empty-state copy omitting the "for this agent" suffix, the image-count chip suppression when no images, the singular "1 image" boundary case at length === 1 (a future refactor flipping `> 1` to `>= 1` would silently break grammar), and the "Jump to this session" pill suppression when `item.tabName` is undefined. 7 catalog-shape vitest guards pass (7/7) including the unique-story-names guard and the IPC-leak guard. 0 IPC namespaces touched; 0 `src/main/` touches; 0 `src/web/` touches; 0 `src/renderer/` edits; 0 `src/server/` edits. **Files added (all under `src/webFull/`):** `src/webFull/components/ExecutionQueueBrowser.tsx` (verbatim lift + extended header documenting the three import-path adapts, the narrow-type rationale, and the IN/OUT scope split); `src/webFull/components/ExecutionQueueBrowser.parity.test.ts` (10 stories + 7 catalog-shape guards). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended `export { ExecutionQueueBrowser } from './ExecutionQueueBrowser';` plus `QueuedItem`/`ExecutionQueueSession` type re-exports under the existing Layer 2.5 leaf-parade section, after the `CustomThemeBuilder` block. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö paired `CustomThemeBuilder` + `ExecutionQueueBrowser` lift").
- [x] ISC-44.layer-2.5.transfer_error_modal: `TransferErrorModal` (renderer `src/renderer/components/TransferErrorModal.tsx`, 664 LOC, 0 IPC at module-load AND runtime per pre-flight grep `grep -n "window\.maestro\|window\.electron\|ipcRenderer\|window\.api" src/renderer/components/TransferErrorModal.tsx` ŌåÆ empty) lifted verbatim into `src/webFull/components/TransferErrorModal.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/TransferErrorModal.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-transfer-error-and-history-detail` (leaf-parade lift wave, batched with `HistoryDetailModal`). Cross-agent-transfer error-display modal that surfaces a structured `TransferError` (one of ten `TransferErrorType` buckets ŌĆö `agent_not_installed`, `agent_busy`, `grooming_timeout`, `grooming_failed`, `context_too_large`, `session_creation_failed`, `source_not_found`, `network_error`, `cancelled`, `unknown`) along with up to two recovery action buttons (Retry primary, Skip Grooming secondary) gated by the type-specific `getAvailableActions` table, plus an unconditional Cancel row. The renderer source header explicitly cites `AgentErrorModal` (L2.5 sibling) as its design basis ŌĆö same error-display-modal shape with fixed retry / skip-grooming / cancel callbacks instead of a `recoveryActions` array. **Import-path adapts (five, matching established L2.5 precedent):** (1) `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` (standard L2.5 swap ŌĆö webFull has no `types/` aggregator); (2) `ToolType` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/types'` (the shape already lives in `src/shared/` and the renderer aggregator only re-exports it); (3) `MODAL_PRIORITIES` from `'../constants/modalPriorities'` ŌåÆ webFull's re-export shim at the same relative path (uses `MODAL_PRIORITIES.TRANSFER_ERROR` = 682, per Architect 2026-06-08 audit risk A); (4) `Modal` from `'./ui/Modal'` ŌåÆ L2.1-lifted webFull primitive at `'./ui/Modal'`; (5) `getAgentDisplayName` from `'../services/contextGroomer'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/agentMetadata'` (the renderer service file is a thin one-line passthrough ŌĆö `return getDisplayName(agentType)` ŌĆö to the shared module. CRITICALLY, the service file ALSO carries two `window.maestro.context.*` calls inside its `groomContext` + `cleanupGroomingSession` functions (runtime IPC, NOT module-load), so routing through the shared module bypasses the IPC surface entirely. 0 IPC namespaces touched as a result). **Composition shape:** error-display modal ŌĆö composes the L2.1 `Modal` primitive directly (no `ModalFooter`; the action surface is a column of recovery-action buttons plus a separate Cancel row, neither of which fits the two-button confirm/cancel idiom). Threads `theme` as a prop per the L2.1 / L2.3 / L2.4 / L2.5 convention, uses `initialFocusRef` to land focus on the primary recovery action button. `lucide-react` icons (`AlertCircle`, `RefreshCw`, `Zap`, `XCircle`, `Clock`, `Download`, `Loader2`, `HardDrive`, `ArrowRight`) kept verbatim ŌĆö already a webFull-tree dep. **Also exports the `TransferError` / `TransferErrorType` / `TransferErrorModalProps` types and the pure `classifyTransferError()` helper** which categorizes an error message into one of the ten `TransferErrorType` buckets via case-insensitive substring matching against `'not found'` / `'busy'` / `'timeout'` / `'too large'` / etc. Both the types and the classifier are pure (string-only) and touch no IPC. **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/TransferErrorModal.parity.test.ts`, 13 stories total: 7 happy + 6 negative ŌĆö exceeds the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 6 vitest catalog-shape guards):** happy-path stories cover the grooming-timeout title chrome, the sourceŌåÆtarget agent context row with display-name resolution via `shared/agentMetadata`, the dual-button recovery surface for grooming errors (Skip Grooming secondary + Retry-with-Grooming primary), the unconditional Cancel row, the details-line surfacing of elapsed-time, the install-instructions panel for agent-not-installed errors, and the context-too-large primary action rendering as "Try with Grooming" (the only error type where the skip-grooming branch IS the primary action). Negative-path stories cover the agent-context-row suppression when neither sourceAgent nor targetAgent is supplied, the Skip Grooming button suppression for network errors (canSkipGrooming=false), the Retry button suppression for agent-not-installed errors (canRetry=false), the details-line suppression when `error.details` is undefined, the generic "Transfer Error" title fallback for unknown error types, the `Retrying...` label override + suppression of the retry description when `isRetrying=true`, and Escape-to-close lifecycle via the Modal primitive's layer-stack hook. Catalog uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary (`hasElement`, `hasText`, `wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`) and passes the IPC-leakage guard, the negative-per-happy floor guard, the no-empty-given/when/then guard, and the unique-story-name guard. **Files added (all under `src/webFull/`):** `src/webFull/components/TransferErrorModal.tsx` (verbatim lift of the 664-LOC source plus an extended header documenting the five import-path adapts and the rationale for bypassing the IPC-carrying `contextGroomer` service); `src/webFull/components/TransferErrorModal.parity.test.ts` (13 stories + 6 catalog-shape guards). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended `export { TransferErrorModal, classifyTransferError } from './TransferErrorModal';` + `export type { TransferError, TransferErrorType, TransferErrorModalProps } from './TransferErrorModal';` after the existing `SessionItem` export. **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/main/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/server/` (zero bytes diff vs main). `npm run lint` clean (after `npm run build:prompts` to materialize the pre-existing `src/generated/prompts.ts`). Vitest parity catalog: 6/6 in-suite guards passed.

Sibling precedent: `GroupChatPanel`, `ParticipantCard`, `SessionItem`); (3) `FileNode` from `'../types/fileTree'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/types/fileTree'` (cross-fork transitive type-only import; pure type, six string-typed fields, no IPC ŌĆö sibling precedent: `MarkdownRenderer` lift); (4) `useLayerStack` from `'../contexts/LayerStackContext'` ŌåÆ webFull's L2.1 re-export at the same relative path; (5) `MODAL_PRIORITIES` from `'../constants/modalPriorities'` ŌåÆ webFull's re-export shim at the same relative path (uses `MODAL_PRIORITIES.CONFIRM` = 1000, matching the renderer comment "Use same priority as confirm modal"); (6) `formatElapsedTime` from `'../utils/formatters'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/formatters'` (the renderer file is a thin re-export of the shared module); (7) `stripAnsiCodes` from `'../../shared/stringUtils'` ŌĆö unchanged (already shared); the renderer's `markdownConfig.ts` also carries `window.maestro.shell.openExternal` calls inside `<a onClick>` JSX callbacks of `createMarkdownComponents` ŌĆö runtime IPC NOT module-load IPC, but routing through the surgical extract keeps that surface area out of webFull entirely); (10) `calculateContextDisplay` from `'../utils/contextUsage'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/utils/contextUsage'` (cross-fork transitive import; pure utility consuming `ToolType` and numeric usage stats, returns a `{tokens, percentage}` shape); (11) `getContextColor` from `'../utils/theme'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/utils/theme'` (cross-fork transitive import; pure utility mapping a percentage to a theme colour ŌĆö sibling precedent: `ParticipantCard`, `SessionItem`). `DoubleCheck` from `'./History'` ŌåÆ `'./History/historyConstants'` (the renderer source imports via the `./History/index.ts` barrel which re-exports five files; the webFull side only has the `historyConstants.tsx` re-export shim, so we import directly. The other barrel members are not needed by this component). `safeClipboardWrite` from `'../utils/clipboard'` ŌåÆ L2.5 webFull primitive at the same path (exposes the pure `navigator.clipboard.writeText` surface ŌĆö the Electron-only `safeClipboardWriteImage` companion is NOT carried). **Settings-store adapt (strip-and-promote-to-prop):** The renderer source line 31 reads `const bionifyReadingMode = useSettingsStore((s) => s.bionifyReadingMode)`. The webFull side has no settings-store layer yet, so the hook call is stripped and `bionifyReadingMode` is promoted onto the prop surface as `bionifyReadingMode?: boolean`, defaulting to `false` ŌĆö matching the renderer store's default. Precedent: `AppOverlays` (three hook strips), `GroupChatInput` (one `spellCheck` strip). **Composition shape:** history-entry detail modal with a custom backdrop + card chrome (NOT composed over the L2.1 `Modal` primitive ŌĆö the renderer source registers DIRECTLY with the layer stack via `useLayerStack` because the modal carries its own bespoke header / stats panel / scrollable markdown body / multi-region footer + the INTERNAL Delete confirmation modal layered on top of itself; the parent-modal primitive doesn't model this chrome). Uses `MODAL_PRIORITIES.CONFIRM` to match the layer-stack priority of the renderer source. **Keyboard surface:** ArrowLeft / ArrowRight navigate prev/next entries (gated by `canNavigate = filteredEntries && currentIndex !== undefined && onNavigate`); the handler short-circuits while the Delete confirmation modal is open; Escape closes via the layer-stack `onEscape` handler. `lucide-react` icons (`X`, `Bot`, `User`, `Copy`, `Check`, `CheckCircle`, `XCircle`, `Trash2`, `Clock`, `Cpu`, `Zap`, `Play`, `ChevronLeft`, `ChevronRight`, `AlertTriangle`) kept verbatim ŌĆö already a webFull-tree dep. **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/HistoryDetailModal.parity.test.ts`, 15 stories total: 8 happy + 7 negative ŌĆö exceeds the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 6 vitest catalog-shape guards):** happy-path stories cover the AUTO-entry chrome with success indicator + type pill + body markdown, the USER-entry chrome with type pill, the session-ID octet + Resume button cluster when `agentSessionId` + `onResumeSession` are both supplied, the Prev/Next nav surfacing when `canNavigate` is true at a middle index, the Delete button surfacing when `onDelete` is supplied, the Validated toggle surfacing for AUTO entries with `success && onUpdate`, the internal Delete confirmation modal mounting on Delete click, and the agent-name header surfacing when the unified history shape carries an `agentName` field. Modal that prompts the user for a folder + filename and writes the supplied markdown content to disk. **Strip-and-promote-to-prop adapts (two ŌĆö the two lambda-deferred IPC sites):** (1) `dialog.selectFolder` ŌåÆ `onBrowseFolder` prop (optional): when undefined OR `isRemoteSession === true`, the FolderOpen button is hidden (gating logic preserved, just unioned). When defined, the resolved string populates the folder field. (2) `fs.writeFile` ŌåÆ `onWriteFile` prop (required): same `(path, content, sshRemoteId?) => Promise<{success: boolean; error?: string}>` shape as the renderer IPC contract; making it required reflects reality ŌĆö saving without a write surface is meaningless. Precedent for both: `HistoryDetailModal` (one settings strip), `AppOverlays` (three store strips), `GroupChatInput` (one `spellCheck` strip). **Import-path adapts (three, matching L2.5 precedent):** (1) `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'`; (2) `Modal, ModalFooter` from `'./ui/Modal'` ŌĆö unchanged (L2.1 webFull primitive); (3) `MODAL_PRIORITIES` from `'../constants/modalPriorities'` ŌĆö unchanged (webFull's constants module re-exports from renderer; uses `MODAL_PRIORITIES.SAVE_MARKDOWN` = 160). **Composition shape:** `createPortal`-rendered modal with the L2.1 Modal primitive providing chrome + layer-stack registration via internal `useModalLayer`, ModalFooter for the right-side Cancel/Save button pair, a custom footer slot to host the "Open in Tab" checkbox on the left. Width 480, closeOnBackdropClick true. Two text inputs (folder path, filename) with the filename field auto-focused on mount via requestAnimationFrame. The `.md` extension is auto-appended in `handleSave` if not provided. Enter key triggers `handleSave` when both fields are non-empty AND not currently saving. **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/SaveMarkdownModal.parity.test.ts`, 12 stories total: 6 happy + 6 negative ŌĆö exceeds the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 6 vitest catalog-shape guards):** happy-path stories cover the modal title + Folder/Filename labels chrome, the folder/filename inputs with placeholders, the `.md` extension hint copy, Cancel + Save button row, "Open in Tab" checkbox when `onOpenInTab` is wired, and the folder-browse affordance when `!isRemoteSession && onBrowseFolder`. Negative-path stories cover the folder-browse suppression when `isRemoteSession=true`, the folder-browse suppression when no picker callback is wired (common webFull case), the "Open in Tab" suppression when `onOpenInTab` is undefined, Save button disabled when either field is empty, no-IPC-on-mount lifecycle pin (both former IPC sites are now strip-and-promote callbacks), and the informational-chrome-stability pin on the `.md` hint copy. **Files added (all under `src/webFull/`):** `src/webFull/components/SaveMarkdownModal.tsx` (243 LOC lift + extended header documenting the two strip-and-promote adapts and the three import-path adapts); `src/webFull/components/SaveMarkdownModal.parity.test.ts` (12 stories + 6 catalog-shape guards). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended `export { SaveMarkdownModal } from './SaveMarkdownModal';` + `export type { SaveMarkdownModalProps } from './SaveMarkdownModal';` after the `HistoryDetailModal` export. **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/main/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/server/` (zero bytes diff vs main). `npm run lint` clean. Vitest parity catalog: 6/6 in-suite guards passed.
- [x] ISC-44.terminal_output.lifted: `TerminalOutput` (renderer `src/renderer/components/TerminalOutput.tsx`, 1923 LOC, 0 module-load IPC AND 0 lambda-deferred IPC per pre-flight grep `grep -n "window\.maestro\|window\.electron\|ipcRenderer\|window\.api" src/renderer/components/TerminalOutput.tsx` ŌåÆ empty) lifted verbatim into `src/webFull/components/TerminalOutput.tsx` with parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/TerminalOutput.parity.test.ts`. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `feat/terminal-output-path` (leaf-parade lift wave ŌĆö the conversation-pipeline unlock per audit #10). This is the AI Terminal / Command Terminal **conversation surface** ŌĆö the scrollback panel that renders every log entry (user prompts, AI responses, tool calls, thinking, errors) with per-entry chrome (copy / delete / save-to-file / local filter / error details), markdown rendering with bionify reading mode, ANSI ŌåÆ HTML for terminal mode, debounced search, throttled scroll-position tracking, an inline `QueuedItemsList`, and a child `SaveMarkdownModal` gated on `saveModalContent !== null`. Every side effect (`onDeleteLog`, `onRemoveQueuedItem`, `onScrollPositionChange`, `onAtBottomChange`, `onReplayMessage`, `onFileClick`, `onShowErrorDetails`, `onFileSaved`, `onOpenInTab`, `setLightboxImage`, `setActiveFocus`, `setOutputSearchOpen`, `setOutputSearchQuery`, `setMarkdownEditMode`, `onWriteMarkdownFile`, `onBrowseMarkdownFolder`) flows through caller-owned prop callbacks. **Mount-wiring is deferred** ŌĆö TerminalOutput is not yet rendered inside `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx`. The next wave will integrate this with the rest of the conversation pipeline (history, sessions); this brief lifts + tests only. **Direct precondition lifted in the SAME branch:** `SaveMarkdownModal` (243 LOC), which TerminalOutput imports at module-load time and renders inline whenever `saveModalContent !== null`. All other sibling deps (`MarkdownRenderer`, `QueuedItemsList`, `LogFilterControls`, `Modal`/`ModalFooter`, `MODAL_PRIORITIES`, `LayerStackContext`, `safeClipboardWrite`, `processLogTextHelper`/`filterTextByLinesHelper`/`getCachedAnsiHtml`, `formatShortcutKeys`, `generateTerminalProseStyles`) were already in webFull from earlier leaf-parade waves. **Import-path adapts (L2.5 standard set):** (1) `Session`, `LogEntry`, `FocusArea`, `AgentError` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/types'` (cross-fork transitive type-only imports; sibling precedent: `GroupChatInput`, `SendToAgentModal`, `HistoryDetailModal`, `GroupChatPanel`); (2) `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` (standard L2.5 swap); (3) `FileNode` from `'../types/fileTree'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/types/fileTree'` (cross-fork transitive type-only import; `MarkdownRenderer`, `HistoryDetailModal` precedent); (4) `useLayerStack` from `'../contexts/LayerStackContext'` ŌĆö unchanged; (5) `MODAL_PRIORITIES` from `'../constants/modalPriorities'` ŌĆö unchanged; (6) `processLogTextHelper, filterTextByLinesHelper, getCachedAnsiHtml` from `'../utils/textProcessing'` ŌĆö unchanged (L2.5 lifted webFull primitives); (7) `formatShortcutKeys` from `'../utils/shortcutFormatter'` ŌĆö unchanged (L2.5 lifted webFull primitive); (8) `MarkdownRenderer`, `QueuedItemsList`, `LogFilterControls`, `SaveMarkdownModal` from `'./...'` ŌĆö unchanged (all sibling L2.5 lifts in webFull; SaveMarkdownModal landed in THIS same branch); (9) `generateTerminalProseStyles` from `'../utils/markdownConfig'` ŌåÆ `'../utils/terminalProseStyles'` (the surgical extract route ŌĆö `HistoryDetailModal` precedent. The renderer's `markdownConfig.ts` also carries `window.maestro.shell.openExternal` calls inside `createMarkdownComponents` lambdas ŌĆö routing through the surgical extract keeps that surface out of webFull entirely); (10) `safeClipboardWrite` from `'../utils/clipboard'` ŌĆö unchanged. **Cross-fork sibling imports (renderer-side, transitively pure):** (1) `getActiveTab` from `'../utils/tabHelpers'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/utils/tabHelpers'` ŌĆö the renderer source's `tabHelpers.ts` is 1717 LOC and lifting it whole would explode this leaf into a multi-file wave; `getActiveTab` is the pure 11-LOC `session.aiTabs.find(...)` helper with no IPC. Cross-fork import precedent applies ŌĆö pure helper, no transitive Electron surface. (2) `useDebouncedValue, useThrottledCallback` from `'../hooks'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/hooks/utils/useThrottle'` ŌĆö the renderer hook module is a single 153-LOC file with three pure React hooks (no IPC). Imported directly from the leaf file rather than through the `'../hooks'` aggregator barrel to avoid pulling 17 other hook subdirectory exports (many of which DO touch IPC). **Strip-and-promote-to-prop adapt ŌĆö settings store (three values, mirrors `HistoryDetailModal`/`AppOverlays` precedent):** The renderer source self-sources `globalBionifyReadingMode` / `globalBionifyIntensity` / `globalBionifyAlgorithm` from `useSettingsStore`. `useSettingsStore` is renderer-only as of this lift (it carries 20+ `window.maestro.settings.set` callsites). The three hook calls are stripped and the values are promoted onto the prop surface as `bionifyReadingMode?: boolean` (default `false`), `bionifyIntensity?: number` (default `1`), `bionifyAlgorithm?: string` (default `'- 0 1 1 2 0.4'`) ŌĆö defaults match the renderer settings-store defaults so the lift produces identical observable behavior when the host omits these props. The host wires these from whatever settings surface is available (REST `useSettings()`, WebSocket-broadcast snapshot, etc.). Bionify override (per-component user toggle) stays internal ŌĆö `bionifyOverride` state and `effectiveBionifyReadingMode = bionifyOverride ?? globalBionifyReadingMode` derivation are unchanged from the renderer source. **Bridge props for the inline SaveMarkdownModal child:** `onWriteMarkdownFile` (required, mirrors SaveMarkdownModal's `onWriteFile`) and `onBrowseMarkdownFolder` (optional, mirrors SaveMarkdownModal's `onBrowseFolder`) ŌĆö these pass through the strip-and-promote surface SaveMarkdownModal exposes so the TerminalOutput host owns the write/picker contract. **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/TerminalOutput.parity.test.ts`, 14 stories total: 8 happy + 6 negative ŌĆö exceeds the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 7 vitest catalog-shape guards):** happy-path stories cover user message render, AI response markdown paragraph render, fenced code-block content render, tool-call entry with tool identifier render, error-entry chrome (svg icon presence), search input surfacing when `outputSearchOpen=true`, the inline SaveMarkdownModal mount when `saveModalContent !== null`, and the QueuedItemsList inline render when `executionQueue` is populated. Negative-path stories cover the empty-state render (no logs but `role="region"` chrome present), search input suppression when `outputSearchOpen=false`, raw markdown render when `markdownEditMode=true`, SaveMarkdownModal suppression when `saveModalContent=null`, QueuedItemsList suppression when the queue is empty OR `inputMode=terminal`, no-IPC-on-mount/scroll/type lifecycle pin, and the bionify-default fallback when prop is omitted. Plus a conversation-surface-minimum guard pinning coverage of (user, assistant, code-block, tool, error, empty) and a strip-and-promote-contract guard pinning `onWriteMarkdownFile` as the required write surface. Catalog uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary (`hasElement`, `hasText`, `wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`) and passes the IPC-leakage guard, the negative-per-happy floor guard, and the no-empty-given/when/then guard. **Files added (all under `src/webFull/`):** `src/webFull/components/TerminalOutput.tsx` (1923 LOC verbatim lift + extended header documenting the L2.5 standard import-path adapts, the cross-fork sibling imports for `getActiveTab` / `useDebouncedValue` / `useThrottledCallback`, the three-value settings-store strip-and-promote, and the two SaveMarkdownModal bridge props); `src/webFull/components/TerminalOutput.parity.test.ts` (14 stories + 7 catalog-shape guards); `src/webFull/components/SaveMarkdownModal.tsx` + `.parity.test.ts` (the direct precondition lifted in the same branch ŌĆö see ISC-44.layer-2.5.save_markdown_modal above). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended `export { TerminalOutput, getTerminalScrollSnapshot, addTerminalHighlightMarkers } from './TerminalOutput';` + `export type { TerminalScrollSnapshot } from './TerminalOutput';` after the `SaveMarkdownModal` export. **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/main/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/server/` (zero bytes diff vs main). `npm run lint` clean. `npm run build:webfull` succeeds. Vitest parity catalog: 7/7 in-suite guards passed. **What this unlocks:** TerminalOutput is the AI tab content surface. With it lifted, webFull's first real AI tab content view becomes mountable ŌĆö the next wave can mount TerminalOutput into `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` (or a new desktop AI-pane wiring) alongside `SessionList` / `TabBar` / `GroupChatPanel` etc. The mount-wiring + history + session-state plumbing is its own subsequent wave; this brief lifts + tests only.
- [x] ISC-44.layer-4.2.tab_switch_lifted: AI-tab navigation (TabBar) lifted from `src/webFull/mobile/TabBar.tsx` (719 LOC, originally a verbatim copy of `src/web/mobile/TabBar.tsx`) to `src/webFull/components/TabBar.tsx`, plus renderer-aligned keyboard shortcuts wired through a new `useTabKeyboardShortcuts` hook. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `layer-4.2-tab-switch`. The desktop oracle `src/renderer/components/TabBar.tsx` (2137 LOC) carries file-preview tabs, HTML5 drag-and-drop, merge-session, summarize-and-continue, gist publish, copy-context, export-html, mark-unread filter, close-others/left/right batches, and reopen-closed-tab ŌĆö all out of scope for L4.2 (each enumerated as a DROPPED entry in the TabBar header + the parity catalog so the partial-parity surface stays countable per the ISC-44.<tab>.<deferral> convention). What's IN this lift: tab row rendering with `role="tablist"` / `aria-selected` / `data-tab-id` / `data-tab-state` / `data-tab-starred` parity-friendly attributes; click-to-select firing `onSelectTab(tabId)`; close-button per tab firing `onCloseTab(tabId)`; "+ New tab" button firing `onNewTab()`; "Search tabs" button firing `onOpenTabSearch()` when provided; long-press actions popover for rename / star / move-left / move-right (renderer's "Move Left / Move Right" intent reproduced ergonomically without the HTML5 DnD plumbing); single-tab early-return matching renderer + mobile contract. Keyboard shortcuts (new `src/webFull/hooks/useTabKeyboardShortcuts.ts`): Cmd+Shift+] / Cmd+Shift+[ for next/previous AI tab (wraps), Cmd+1..9 for jump-to-index, Cmd+0 for jump-to-last ŌĆö matching `src/renderer/constants/shortcuts.ts` lines 58-59 + 183+ (`prevTab` / `nextTab` / `goToTab1..9` / `goToLastTab`). Browser-conflict caveat documented in the hook header + parity catalog: Cmd+Shift+[/] and Cmd+1..9 are bound to browser tab navigation in Chrome / Safari / Firefox on macOS in a regular browser tab; they work in PWA / standalone display mode (the intended deployment) and in the headless test harness. The hook calls `e.preventDefault()` defensively and is inert when any `<input>` / `<textarea>` / contenteditable is focused (renderer's `isInputFocused` guard mirrored). The existing `useMobileKeyboardHandler` Cmd+[/] (no Shift) navigation is kept additive ŌĆö mobile muscle memory unchanged. Parity catalog at `src/webFull/components/TabBar.parity.test.ts` ships 14 stories total: 9 happy-path (tab row rendering with state attrs, click-to-select, close-button, new-tab, search, Cmd+Shift+] / Cmd+Shift+[ wrap, Cmd+digit index jump, Cmd+0 last-tab) + 5 negative-path (clicking active tab is idempotent, single-tab case hides bar, Cmd+digit out-of-range is no-op, shortcuts inert while typing, drag-reorder is intentionally absent) ŌĆö exceeds the "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 7 vitest catalog-shape guards (happy floor, negative-per-happy floor, allowed-verbs, non-empty given/when/then, IPC-leak guard, browser-conflict flag guard, droppedFromWebFull-is-negative guard). Wire-up in `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx`: one import-path adjustment (`from './TabBar'` ŌåÆ `from '../components/TabBar'`), one additive import (`useTabKeyboardShortcuts`), one additive hook call adjacent to the existing `useMobileKeyboardHandler` mount. The old `src/webFull/mobile/TabBar.tsx` is now a 13-line re-export shim that points at `'../components/TabBar'` so legacy imports keep resolving. **Scope guards verified:** `git diff main..HEAD -- src/web/ | wc -c` ŌåÆ 0 (fork-hygiene); `git diff main..HEAD -- src/renderer/ | wc -c` ŌåÆ 0 (read-only oracle); `git diff main..HEAD -- src/main/ | wc -c` ŌåÆ 0 (no new routes needed ŌĆö existing `select_tab` / `new_tab` / `close_tab` / `rename_tab` WS frames from the original mobile-remote-control surface cover the wire). 0 IPC namespaces touched. Deferrals from the renderer surface tracked as sub-ISCs below.

- [x] ISC-44.shim.big_3_ipc_strategy: IPC-shim strategy for the three Hard-bucket modals (`NewInstanceModal` + `AutoRun` + `MarketplaceModal`) captured as ONE shared Decision rather than three dedicated briefs. Per Architect audit #10, the three modals collapse onto a single shared route surface: `/api/agents/{detect,getConfig,setConfig,getModels,refresh}` + `/api/ssh-remotes/list` + `/api/fs/{homeDir,stat,readFile}` + `/api/autorun/writeDoc` + `/api/dialog/selectFolder` (with `shell.openExternal` swapped per-site to `window.open(url, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer')` per the StandingOvationOverlay precedent ŌĆö no server route needed). Strategy enumerates per-site disposition (existing route / new route / strip-and-promote / window.open swap) and identifies the SUBSET of new routes that unblocks all three modals simultaneously (the generalising unlock the audit named). Future implementation work spawns child ISCs per route + per modal. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on this Decision entry. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("IPC-shim strategy for the big-3 (NewInstanceModal + AutoRun + MarketplaceModal)").
- [x] ISC-44.wiring.appoverlays*and_modals_mounted: Per audit #10 PIVOT ("stop lifting orphans, start WIRING already-lifted components into webFull's reachable UI"), the AppOverlays dispatcher trio (`AppOverlays` + `FirstRunCelebration` + `KeyboardMasteryCelebration` + `StandingOvationOverlay`), `ContextWarningSash`, `ShortcutsHelpModal`, `AutoRunnerHelpModal`, `HistoryHelpModal`, `QuitConfirmModal`, and `FileSearchModal` are now MOUNTED in `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx`. Pre-wiring, the mobile shell imported only 5 webFull components (Badge, SessionList, TabBar, Terminal, ThemeProvider) while ~70+ components sat lifted but unmounted; this wave closes the trigger gap for ten of those orphans in one atomic commit. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `feat/mount-appoverlays-and-modals`. **Pattern shape ŌĆö render-slot dispatcher + gate-state hook.** `AppOverlays` is the renderer-derived dispatcher whose lift policy preserved the dispatcher's pure visibility-gate contract: three data props (`standingOvationData`, `firstRunCelebrationData`, `pendingKeyboardMasteryLevel`) and three render-prop slots (`standingOvationSlot`, `firstRunCelebrationSlot`, `keyboardMasterySlot`). The host (mobile/App.tsx) decides the data source and binds the slots pre-rendered, keeping the dispatcher decoupled from the overlay surfaces. Data state is local `useState` triplets (nullable ŌĆö `null` Ōåö "do not render this overlay" per the dispatcher contract); slots are inline JSX that constructs the concrete overlay only when its matching data is non-null. **Gate-state primitive ŌĆö `useModalGate()`.** New hook at `src/webFull/hooks/useModalGate.ts` (~50 LOC) collapses the six independent `useState<boolean>` + `useCallback` pairs (one per show/hide modal: ShortcutsHelp, AutoRunnerHelp, HistoryHelp, QuitConfirm, FileSearch) into a single `useModalGate()` call returning a stable `{open, show, hide}` triple. Inlining six copies of the `useState` + two `useCallback` pattern would have exceeded the brief's ~30 LOC threshold for a factor-out. Hook is intentionally narrow: no layer-stack interaction, no priority logic, no Escape handling (the lifted modals that DO use the LayerStack ŌĆö QuitConfirm, ShortcutsHelp, StandingOvation, FileSearch ŌĆö register internally via `useLayerStack`). Pure: zero IPC, zero browser-API touches, zero module-load side effects. **Debug-trigger keyboard chord surface.** A single new `useEffect` mounts a `keydown` listener on `window` that maps debug keybindings to the gate-shows: `Shift+?` / `Cmd+Shift+/` ŌåÆ ShortcutsHelpModal; `Cmd+Shift+H` ŌåÆ HistoryHelpModal; `Cmd+Shift+R` ŌåÆ AutoRunnerHelpModal; `Cmd+Shift+Q` ŌåÆ QuitConfirmModal; `Cmd+P` ŌåÆ FileSearchModal; `Cmd+Alt+F` ŌåÆ synthetic FirstRunCelebration; `Cmd+Alt+K` ŌåÆ synthetic KeyboardMasteryCelebration at level 0. Each binding short-circuits when `isInputFocused()` is true (same guard pattern as `useTabKeyboardShortcuts` and the renderer's `useMainKeyboardHandler`). Mac key-code edge handles `e.key === 'ŲÆ'` (Alt+f), `e.key === '╦Ü'` (Alt+k) ŌĆö macOS sometimes emits the typographically-substituted character rather than the underlying letter when Alt is held. The chord set is intentionally debug-flavored rather than renderer-canonical: the goal here per the audit #10 pivot is "make at least \_triggerable* from a debug menu or keybinding" ŌĆö full host wiring (real first-run-celebration when an AutoRun completes; real quit-confirm on a quit attempt with busy agents; real file-search wired to the project's file tree) lands in subsequent waves. **ContextWarningSash data source.** Reuses the existing `estimateContextUsage()` formula that `MobileHeader` already calls ŌĆö same `usageStats` source (active tab's stats, falling back to session-level), same `toolType` parameter. Render is always-mounted (the sash component internally gates on `enabled` + threshold predicates) but `enabled` is set to `contextUsage >= 60` so the gate is closed when usage is low. `onSummarizeClick` is a logging stub pending the `/summarize` WS frame wiring in a subsequent wave. **Modals that surface but defer host-data wiring.** `ShortcutsHelpModal` mounts with `DEFAULT_SHORTCUTS` + `TAB_SHORTCUTS` from `renderer/constants/shortcuts.ts` as the static parity-shape fallback (webFull doesn't yet thread the full shortcuts settings store). `QuitConfirmModal` mounts with `busyAgentCount=0` and empty `busyAgentNames` (the busy-agent computation requires walking the sessions list ŌĆö host wave). `FileSearchModal` mounts with `fileTree=[]` (the project's file tree comes from a host source not yet wired; the empty tree proves the keybinding/registration/LayerStack interaction works). Each modal is annotated inline with the host-data TODO so the next wave knows what to wire. **Theme threading.** Added `useTheme` destructure alongside the existing `useThemeColors` call; the lifted overlays / modals all take `theme: Theme` as a prop per the L2.1 / L2.3 / L2.4 / L2.5 / L4.1 / L4.2 prop-threading convention. No theme-store changes ŌĆö the existing ThemeProvider context that wraps `WebApp` (App.tsx line 287) already provides the value mobile/App.tsx now reads. **Files added (all under `src/webFull/`):** `src/webFull/hooks/useModalGate.ts` (~55 LOC including header doc). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/hooks/index.ts` (+5 LOC ŌĆö Audit #10 section re-exports `useModalGate` + `ModalGate` type); `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` (+322 LOC ŌĆö additive imports, gate state, keyboard handler, mounted JSX; the 322-LOC delta exceeds the brief's "~200 LOC ceiling" but ~88 of those 322 lines are explanatory comments documenting the wiring pattern + per-modal host-data TODOs; the functional code delta is ~234 LOC). **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö fork-hygiene); `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö read-only oracle); `src/main/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/server/` (zero bytes diff vs main). 0 IPC namespaces touched ŌĆö all the lifted modals already routed side effects through prop callbacks per their L2.5 lift policy. **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean (3 tsconfigs after `npm run build:prompts`); `npm run build:webfull` succeeds; `npx vitest run src/webFull/` ŌåÆ 531/531 tests passed (81 test files, no regressions); grep proof ŌĆö `grep -E 'AppOverlays|FirstRunCelebration|ShortcutsHelpModal|KeyboardMasteryCelebration|StandingOvationOverlay|QuitConfirmModal|AutoRunnerHelpModal|HistoryHelpModal|FileSearchModal|ContextWarningSash' src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` returns 10 matches (one per wired component), where the pre-edit grep returned 0. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Audit #10 pivot ŌĆö orphan-to-mounted wiring of AppOverlays + 5 modals + ContextWarningSash").
- [x] ISC-44.wiring.terminal_output_mounted: Per audit #10 PIVOT continuation ("biggest user-felt parity unlock ŌĆö until now webFull has no view of the actual AI agent conversation"), the lifted `TerminalOutput` component (2079 LOC, the largest L2.5 leaf lift) is now MOUNTED in `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx`. Pre-wiring grep `grep -n '<TerminalOutput' src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` ŌåÆ 0 matches; post-wiring ŌåÆ 1 match. The mobile shell rendered `MessageHistory` (the smaller parsed-log surface) for both AI and shell modes, which meant the rich conversation chrome (per-entry copy / delete / save-to-file / local filter buttons, debounced search affordance, queued-items list, ANSI ŌåÆ HTML for terminal mode, full markdown rendering with bionify reading mode, the inline `SaveMarkdownModal` child) was unreachable in webFull. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `feat/mount-terminal-output`. **Render gate ŌĆö desktop-only, mobile small screens keep `MessageHistory`.** The brief explicitly authorized either an unconditional render or a `isSmallScreen || isMobile ? mobile chrome : TerminalOutput on desktop` gate; chose the latter because (a) zero regression risk on the working mobile chrome, (b) `TerminalOutput`'s per-entry chrome is desktop-shaped (copy/delete/save/filter buttons + search affordance + queued-items list), (c) the existing mobile chrome handles touch-first interactions that the renderer surface doesn't optimize for. Mobile parity for the conversation surface is a separate concern. **Renderer-shape session adapter.** `TerminalOutput`'s `session: RendererSession` prop is the full renderer shape (~50+ fields including `aiLogs`, `shellLogs`, `workLog`, `fullPath`, `projectRoot`, `changedFiles`, `isGitRepo`, `fileTree`, etc.). webFull's `Session` (extends `SessionData` from useWebSocket) is the thin wrapper (~13 fields). A `useMemo`-cached adapter (`rendererShapedSession`) at the host synthesizes the renderer shape from `activeSession + sessionLogs`. The adapter widens the webFull `LogEntry.source: 'user' | 'stdout' | 'stderr'` type to the renderer's `'stdout' | 'stderr' | 'system' | 'user' | 'ai' | 'error' | 'thinking' | 'tool'` (strict subset, safe cast); routes `sessionLogs.aiLogs` into the active tab's `logs` array; fills missing renderer fields (`workLog: []`, `fileTree: []`, `changedFiles: []`, etc.) with safe empty defaults. **Multi-tab log fidelity gap.** webFull's WS protocol does NOT yet bucket session output by tabId ŌĆö `sessionLogs` is a session-level pair (`aiLogs[] + shellLogs[]`) not a per-tab payload. The adapter routes all logs into the active tab's `logs` array, leaving other tabs empty. Single-tab sessions (the common case today) render correctly; multi-tab sessions show only the active tab's accumulated stream. The fidelity gap is documented as a follow-up brief: the `session_output` WS frame needs a `tabId` field that the client uses to bucket logs into per-tab arrays. This wave does NOT add the new WS frame ŌĆö the brief explicitly authorized stopping at "STOP and report" if a new server WS frame was needed. **Write-doc bridge.** `TerminalOutput` requires a non-optional `onWriteMarkdownFile` prop (the `SaveMarkdownModal` strip-and-promote contract). Wired to `POST /api/autorun/write-doc` ŌĆö the W3-fs route that backs `autorun:writeDoc` in the Electron path and accepts the same `{path, content}` body shape that `SaveMarkdownModal` sends. `/api/fs/write-file` does NOT exist on this branch; `write-doc` is the closest analog the brief explicitly authorized. The async bridge returns the contract shape `{success: boolean; error?: string}` ŌĆö server errors propagate through `webLogger.error()` + `error.message` rather than throwing. **Optional / stubbed props.** `onBrowseMarkdownFolder` omitted (folder-browse button stays hidden, matching the renderer's `!isRemoteSession` gating). `setLightboxImage` is an inert stub (webFull doesn't yet surface a lightbox ŌĆö host-data TODO). `setActiveFocus` is an inert stub (webFull doesn't thread a focus-area store). `onInterrupt` wired to the existing `handleInterrupt(sessionId)` REST call (same handler `CommandInputBar` uses). `fontFamily` defaults to a monospace stack (`'ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, monospace'`) ŌĆö webFull doesn't yet thread a typed font-family setting. `maxOutputLines` defaults to `10000` (matches the renderer default range). `markdownEditMode` + `outputSearchOpen` + `outputSearchQuery` held in local `useState` (the renderer pulls these from `useUIStore` / `useSettingsStore`; webFull doesn't yet thread those). `bionifyReadingMode` wired from the existing `useDesktopTheme` source already on webFull; `bionifyIntensity` + `bionifyAlgorithm` use the prop defaults documented in the lift policy (`1` and `'- 0 1 1 2 0.4'`). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` (+~210 LOC including the adapter doc block + the desktop-gated render branch + the write-doc bridge). **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö fork-hygiene); `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö read-only oracle); `src/main/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/server/` (zero bytes diff vs main). 0 IPC namespaces touched ŌĆö the write bridge uses an existing REST route. **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean (3 tsconfigs after `npm run build:prompts`); `npm run build:webfull` succeeds; `npx vitest run src/webFull/` ŌåÆ 544/544 tests passed (83 test files, no regressions); grep proof ŌĆö `grep -n '<TerminalOutput' src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` returns 1 match (where pre-edit was 0). **Follow-up briefs (data-fidelity gaps).** (1) Per-tab log bucketing: add `tabId` to the `session_output` WS frame on the server side; have `useMobileSessionManagement` bucket logs into a `Record<tabId, LogEntry[]>` keyed map; have the adapter route per-tab arrays into each `aiTab.logs` instead of routing all aiLogs into the active tab. (2) Lightbox modal surface: add a webFull `Lightbox` component + `useLightbox()` hook to make `setLightboxImage` a real surface. (3) Focus-area store: add a webFull focus tracker to make `setActiveFocus` real. (4) FileNode tree wiring: thread `fileTree` from a host file-explorer source so `onFileClick` resolves to real navigation. (5) Error-details modal wiring: thread `onShowErrorDetails` to an `AgentErrorModal` host (already lifted, just unmounted). (6) Replay-message wiring: thread `onReplayMessage` to the command-input bar. (7) Settings store for bionify intensity / algorithm + maxOutputLines + autoScrollAiMode + userMessageAlignment + chatRawTextMode ŌĆö these are cosmetic settings that should come from `useSettings()` once typed. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Audit #10 pivot ŌĆö TerminalOutput mount wiring").
- [x] ISC-44.terminal_output.per_tab_log_bucketing: Closes follow-up brief (1) from `ISC-44.wiring.terminal_output_mounted` ŌĆö the largest of the 8 follow-ups that mount wave called out ŌĆö by switching the webFull AI-output ingestion path from a session-flat `aiLogs[]` shape to a per-tab `aiLogsByTab: Record<tabId, LogEntry[]>` shape. Multi-tab sessions now render the correct per-tab conversation in TerminalOutput instead of routing the union of streamed output into the active tab and leaving every other tab empty. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `feat/per-tab-log-bucketing`. **Server-side: no changes required.** Pre-flight inspection of `src/main/process-listeners/data-listener.ts` confirmed the `session_output` WS frame ALREADY carries `tabId` (line 152 ŌĆö extracted from session IDs of the form `{baseSessionId}-ai-{tabId}` via `REGEX_AI_TAB_ID`). The client-side `SessionOutputMessage` type at `src/webFull/hooks/useWebSocket.ts:251` ALREADY declares `tabId?: string`. The REST `/api/session/:id?tabId=<tab>` route ALREADY filters logs to the requested tab server-side (see `setGetSessionDetailCallback` in `src/server/index.ts:567` and the analogous web-server-factory branch). Server-half of the parity restoration was completed by the prior mount wave ŌĆö what remained was wiring the data through the client. The brief explicitly authorized stopping and reporting if a server WS frame change had been needed; none was. **Client-side bucketing.** `src/webFull/hooks/useMobileSessionManagement.ts` extends `SessionLogsState` with `aiLogsByTab: Record<string, LogEntry[]>` alongside the legacy flat `aiLogs: LogEntry[]` view. New exported constant `AI_LOGS_NO_TAB_BUCKET = '__notab__'` defines a sentinel key for entries arriving without a `tabId` ŌĆö the back-compat fallback for (a) in-flight `session_output` frames from a pre-bucketing server build during a rolling deploy, (b) `user_input` frames which carry only `sessionId + inputMode`, and (c) `addUserLogEntry()` calls made before any tab is selected. The flat `aiLogs` view is recomputed on every mutation as `aiLogsByTab[activeTabId] Ōł¬ aiLogsByTab[AI_LOGS_NO_TAB_BUCKET]` so consumers reading `sessionLogs.aiLogs` directly (e.g. the `inputMode === 'ai' ? aiLogs : shellLogs` branch in `App.tsx:1548`) keep seeing the same data they did pre-bucketing. `onSessionOutput` now buckets by `tabId ?? AI_LOGS_NO_TAB_BUCKET` instead of skipping when `tabId !== activeTabId` ŌĆö that previous skip dropped streaming output from non-active tabs entirely, which was the multi-tab fidelity loss this brief closes. Streaming-append heuristic (last entry, `source === 'stdout'`, `< 5s` since `timestamp`) operates per-bucket so each tab's stream coheres on its own. `fetchSessionLogs` writes the fetched logs into `aiLogsByTab[returnedTabId ?? activeTabId ?? AI_LOGS_NO_TAB_BUCKET]` and recomputes the flat view. `addUserLogEntry` and `onUserInput` (AI mode) write to `aiLogsByTab[activeTabIdRef.current ?? AI_LOGS_NO_TAB_BUCKET]`. **Adapter wiring in `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx`.** The `rendererShapedSession` memo's tab synthesis switched from `logs: tab.id === activeTabId ? aiLogs : []` to `logs: aiLogsByTab[tab.id] ?? []`, with the active tab additionally folding in the no-tab fallback bucket so untagged frames remain visible on at least one surface during the back-compat window. The empty-tabs fallback path (sessions with no `aiTabs` at all ŌĆö pure single-tab legacy case) routes the flat `aiLogs` view (which already includes the no-tab fallback) into the synthesized default tab. The 11-line "Multi-tab fidelity gap" comment block above the adapter was rewritten to document the new bucketing contract instead. **Back-compat strategy.** Three layers: (1) the `aiLogs` flat view is preserved on `SessionLogsState` so any caller still reading it sees consistent data; (2) the `AI_LOGS_NO_TAB_BUCKET` sentinel catches frames missing `tabId` and surfaces them on the active tab; (3) server-side WS frame shape is unchanged ŌĆö `tabId` is still optional, not required, so a downgraded server (or a non-tab-aware code path emitting raw output) doesn't break the client. **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/hooks/useMobileSessionManagement.ts` (+~80 LOC net ŌĆö `AI_LOGS_NO_TAB_BUCKET` const + `aiLogsByTab` state field + bucketed `onSessionOutput` + bucketed `addUserLogEntry` + bucketed `onUserInput` + bucketed `fetchSessionLogs`); `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` (+~25 LOC net ŌĆö additive `AI_LOGS_NO_TAB_BUCKET` import, per-tab `logs` routing in the `rendererShapedSession` memo, rewritten comment block). **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö fork-hygiene); `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö read-only oracle); `src/main/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö server frame already carried `tabId`); `src/server/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö REST detail route already filtered by `tabId`). 0 IPC namespaces touched. 0 WS frame shape changes. **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean (3 tsconfigs after `npm run build:prompts`); `npm run build:server` clean; `npm run build:webfull` succeeds (mobile bundle within prior headroom); `npx vitest run src/webFull/` ŌåÆ 562/562 tests passing across 85 test files (no regressions; +18 tests vs pre-edit baseline from intermediate batch-1 adoptions on `main`). See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Per-tab log bucketing ŌĆö closes the biggest TerminalOutput mount-wave follow-up").
- [x] ISC-44.shim.fs_read_image_route: Audit-correction sub-ISC under the umbrella `ISC-44.shim.big_3_ipc_strategy`. The AutoRun lift discovered this gap after the W3-autorun-images cluster shipped: the AutoRun shell's `useAutoRunImageHandling` hook (`src/renderer/hooks/batch/useAutoRunImageHandling.ts:175-186`) calls `window.maestro.fs.readFile()` on image paths and consumes the result with `dataUrl.startsWith('data:')` ŌĆö the renderer-side `fs:readFile` IPC handler at `src/main/ipc/handlers/filesystem.ts:166-175` has a dual contract (text for non-image extensions, `data:image/<ext>;base64,...` URL for image extensions) that the W3-fs `/api/fs/read-file` route deliberately did NOT port (text-only, 400s on binary). Ships `GET /api/fs/read-image?path=<absolute>` ŌĆö an image-aware sibling route that mirrors the renderer's image branch byte-for-byte. **Route shape:** bare text body (Content-Type: `text/plain; charset=utf-8`) carrying the `data:image/<ext>;base64,<payload>` URL string, NOT a JSON envelope. Matches `/api/fs/read-file` wire parity (both bare-string in the renderer-side IPC reply); clients consume `await response.text()` and drop the result directly into `<img src>`. The JSON-wrapper alternative (`{dataUrl}`) was rejected because (1) the only payload is the URL itself ŌĆö wrapping adds parse-and-extract friction with no security or extensibility benefit, and (2) matching the read-file wire shape means a future caller can pick the right route by extension WITHOUT changing how the response is consumed. **Extension allowlist:** png / jpg / jpeg / gif / webp / svg ŌĆö mirrors `READ_IMAGE_ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS` in `src/server/fs-manager.ts` and `ALLOWED_IMAGE_EXTENSIONS` in `src/server/autorun-manager.ts:93` byte-for-byte (deliberately narrower than the renderer's `IMAGE_EXTENSIONS` at `src/main/ipc/handlers/filesystem.ts:41` which also accepts `bmp` + `ico`; gating read-image on the broader set would create a corner where the server returns a data URL for an extension the W3-autorun-images save path cannot round-trip). **Defense-in-depth path validation in BOTH the route layer and the manager** (same rule as the W3-fs `validateFsPath()` Ōåö `isValidFsPath()` pair): `validateFsPath()` (absolute, no NUL, no `..` segments, no encoded `..`) runs FIRST, then `validateReadImageExtension()` (allowlist), then the manager re-validates. **Architecture (additive only):** `src/server/fs-manager.ts` extended with `readImage()` method + `isValidReadImageExtension()` helper + `READ_IMAGE_ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS` constant + `mimeTypeForImageExtension()` mapper (preserves the renderer's `image/svg+xml` / `image/<ext>` mapping byte-for-byte ŌĆö including the renderer's non-canonical `image/jpg` for jpg files, since the webFull `<img src>` consumer already treats it correctly and changing it would break wire parity). `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts` extended with optional `readImage?` method on `FsProvider` + the route handler + `validateReadImageExtension()` + `READ_IMAGE_ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS` constant (the optional `?` lets Electron-host paths that register a custom provider skip the image branch and have the route 501 cleanly rather than crash). `src/server/index.ts` wired `readImage: (p) => fsManager.readImage(p)` in the existing `registerFsProvider` call (1 new line + 7-line comment block). **SSH remote support deliberately out of scope** (matches the W3-fs precedent): `?sshRemoteId=` query param returns 501 so callers don't silently get a local result when a remote was requested. **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö fork-hygiene), `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö read-only oracle), `src/main/ipc/handlers/filesystem.ts` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö renderer-side IPC handler continues to own the image surface inside Electron). **Verification:** `npm run build:server` clean; `dist/server/fs-manager.js` regenerated at 19,762 bytes with zero `from 'electron'` / `require("electron")` matches; manual curl matrix end-to-end (server booted on ephemeral port `:31761` with `MAESTRO_HEADLESS=1` + `MAESTRO_DATA_DIR=/tmp/maestro-w3-readimg-data`): PNG returns 200 `data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoŌĆ”` (Content-Type `text/plain; charset=utf-8`); SVG returns 200 `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyŌĆ”`; text file (`hello.txt`) returns 400 (`extension must be one of png, jpg, jpeg, gif, webp, svg`); `/etc/passwd` returns 400 (no image extension); traversal (`/etc/../etc/foo.png`) returns 400; encoded traversal (`%2e%2e`) returns 400; relative path returns 400 (must be absolute); URL-encoded NUL byte (`%00`) returns 400; missing path (`?path=`) returns 400; missing-file (extension valid) returns 404; `?sshRemoteId=foo` returns 501; unsupported extension (`bmp`) returns 400 with the allowlist in the message. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `w3-fs-read-image`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("W3-fs-read-image ŌĆö audit-correction route for the AutoRun-lift image case").
- [x] ISC-44.shim.fs_routes: First sub-ISC under the umbrella `ISC-44.shim.big_3_ipc_strategy`. Ships the server-side fs-helper REST routes (`GET /api/fs/home-dir`, `GET /api/fs/stat`, `GET /api/fs/read-file`, `POST /api/autorun/write-doc`) backed by a new `src/server/fs-manager.ts` (server-side port of the `fs:*` IPC handlers at `src/main/ipc/handlers/filesystem.ts` plus the `autorun:writeDoc` channel at `src/main/ipc/handlers/autorun.ts:407`, no `electron` import). Provider-registry pattern mirrors W2-wakatime / W2-stats / W2-fonts byte-for-byte (`FsProvider` interface + `registerFsProvider()` + `getFsProvider()` in apiRoutes.ts; headless wiring in `src/server/index.ts` before `server.start()`). Unblocks AutoRun shell sites at AutoRun.tsx:265+285+314+653+748 and NewInstanceModal sites at NewInstanceModal.tsx:129+200+1385 ŌĆö the largest single chunk of the big-3 unlock budget. Path validation (absolute, no NUL, no `..` segments, no encoded `..`) lives in BOTH the route layer and the manager (defense-in-depth); the route 400s before any fs call runs. SSH remote support deliberately out of scope (`?sshRemoteId=` query param returns 501) and ships in a future `W3-ssh-remotes` brief. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `w3-fs-server-port`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("W3-fs ŌĆö server-side fs-helper REST routes").
- [x] ISC-44.testing.playwright_parity_scaffold: Playwright component-test scaffold that closes the behavioral-parity testing gap audit #11 flagged. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `feat/playwright-parity-scaffold`. Existing `.parity.test.ts` files are catalog-shape documents that assert their own SHAPE (story count, allowed verbs, no banned IPC surfaces) but DO NOT render the lifted component or verify the documented `then[]` assertions in a real DOM ŌĆö the catalog can drift arbitrarily from the component's actual behavior and the vitest suite stays green. This scaffold adds a Playwright executor that loads a catalog file, mounts the lifted webFull component into chromium via a Vite-served harness, performs each story's `given/when` setup, and asserts each `then[]` verb against the rendered DOM. **First adoption: `ContextWarningSash`** ŌĆö 8 stories, 8 passing (4 happy + 4 negative), including the dismissal-recurrence stories that require an initial mount + interactive dismiss + re-render cycle. The remaining ~72 parity catalogs follow in subsequent waves ŌĆö one adapter + one spec per component, runner unchanged. **Files added (all under `tests/parity/`, additive only):** `tests/parity/runParityCatalog.ts` (Playwright executor), `tests/parity/playwright.parity.config.ts` (separate from the root `playwright.config.ts` which targets `./e2e`), `tests/parity/vitest-shim.cjs` (Node-side stand-in so Playwright's CJS loader can `require` a catalog file that imports vitest), `tests/parity/harness/index.html` + `bridge.tsx` + `registry.ts` + `vite.harness.config.ts` + `vitest-browser-shim.ts` (in-browser mount harness on port 5180), `tests/parity/harness/adapters/ContextWarningSash.adapter.tsx` (first adoption's proseŌåÆprops translator), `tests/parity/playwright-specs/ContextWarningSash.parity.spec.ts` (~10 LOC ŌĆö imports catalog, calls `runParityCatalog()`), `tests/parity/README.md` (5-step "how to wire your catalog" guide). **Files modified (additive only):** `package.json` (+1 line ŌĆö `"test:parity": "playwright test --config tests/parity/playwright.parity.config.ts"`). **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/`, `src/renderer/`, `src/server/`, `src/main/`, `src/webFull/components/*.parity.test.ts` (catalogs untouched ŌĆö the executor reads them verbatim). **Verb coverage:** `hasElement` + `hasText` wired; the six backend verbs (`wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`) are marked-skipped with a reason rather than silently passing ŌĆö wiring them is a follow-on. **Verification:** `npm run test:parity` ŌåÆ 8/8 passing (2.9s wall); `npx vitest run src/webFull/components/ContextWarningSash.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ 6/6 still passing (catalog-shape layer unbroken); the two layers coexist. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Audit #11 ŌĆö behavioral parity testing scaffold").
- [x] ISC-44.testing.maestro_silhouette_playwright_adopted: `MaestroSilhouette` (and the sibling `AnimatedMaestro` named export) wired into the Playwright parity executor. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `feat/playwright-adopt-batch-1`. 6/6 stories passing (4 happy + 2 negative). Catalog discriminates the two named exports via `alt` attribute (`"Maestro conductor silhouette"` vs `"Animated maestro conductor"`); one happy story mounts BOTH side-by-side via a `<>...</>` fragment so the single `render(story)` contract still returns one ReactElement. Adapter at `tests/parity/harness/adapters/MaestroSilhouette.adapter.tsx`, spec at `tests/parity/playwright-specs/MaestroSilhouette.parity.spec.ts`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Playwright parity adoption batch-1 ŌĆö MaestroSilhouette + WelcomeContent + FontConfigurationPanel + CollapsibleJsonViewer").
- [x] ISC-44.testing.welcome_content_playwright_adopted: `WelcomeContent` wired into the Playwright parity executor. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `feat/playwright-adopt-batch-1`. 7 executed stories (4 happy + 3 negative) all passing; 1 backend-verb story (`welcome-content-touches-no-ipc-or-electron-surface` ŌĆö `notificationFired` + `broadcast`) auto-skipped by the executor per `VERBS_REQUIRING_BACKEND`. Static-mount adapter ŌĆö every story differs only on the `showGetStarted` boolean. Adapter at `tests/parity/harness/adapters/WelcomeContent.adapter.tsx`, spec at `tests/parity/playwright-specs/WelcomeContent.parity.spec.ts`.
