- [x] ISC-44.testing.font_configuration_panel_playwright_adopted: `FontConfigurationPanel` wired into the Playwright parity executor. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `feat/playwright-adopt-batch-1`. 7 executed stories (4 happy + 3 negative) all passing; 1 backend-verb story (`font-configuration-panel-no-ipc-no-ws-no-broadcast-on-pure-render` ŌĆö `wsFrameMatches` + `broadcast` + `notificationFired`) auto-skipped by the executor. UI primitive but the catalog stories are render-shape only ŌĆö they vary on `fontsLoaded` / `fontLoading` / `customFonts` / `systemFonts` inputs and assert the resulting `select` / `optgroup` / `option` / `button` / `input` structure. - [x] ISC-44.testing.qrcode_playwright_adopted: `QRCode` wired into the Playwright parity executor after the catalog flaw + component bug pair (batch-1 dropped it for) was fixed. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `chore/playwright-catalog-fixes`. 8/8 stories passing (4 happy + 4 negative ŌĆö every story executes; no backend verbs in the catalog). **Component bug fixed:** default `bgColor` changed from `'transparent'` to `'#0000'` (4-digit hex alpha=0). The underlying `qrcode` library's `hex2rgba()` synchronously throws `Invalid hex color: transparent` on the literal string `'transparent'` ŌĆö every default-prop story was falling into the component's `.catch(setError)` branch, so the rendered surface was the error span rather than the `<img>`. `'#0000'` is the hex form of fully-transparent and renders identically; the prop signature is unchanged. **Loading-state driver added:** the catalog's "renders pulsing placeholder WHILE generating" story needs to observe the pre-resolution state, but the qrcode library resolves the data URL faster than the assertion window. The adapter's `LoadingPlaceholderDriver` holds `value=""` for 5 s (well past the 2 s assertion timeout) so the placeholder stays the rendered output for the entire test lifetime ŌĆö same `.animate-pulse` element the catalog asserts. Adapter at `tests/parity/harness/adapters/QRCode.adapter.tsx`, spec at `tests/parity/playwright-specs/QRCode.parity.spec.ts`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Playwright parity catalog flaws fix ŌĆö QRCode + ToggleButtonGroup + SettingCheckbox + ThemePicker + CsvTableRenderer adopted").
- [x] ISC-44.testing.toggle_button_group_playwright_adopted: `ToggleButtonGroup` wired into the Playwright parity executor after the catalog flaw (batch-1 dropped it for) was fixed. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `chore/playwright-catalog-fixes`. 10/10 stories passing (5 happy + 5 negative ŌĆö every story executes; no backend verbs in the catalog). **Catalog flaw fixed:** the `toggle-button-group-label-precedence-option-label-wins` story's absence assertion used the jQuery selector `div:not(:has(button:contains("FALLBACK")))`. `:contains()` is not standard CSS ŌĆö Playwright's CSS engine parses it via `document.querySelector`-shape syntax and would throw `SyntaxError`. Replaced with `body:not(:has-text("FALLBACK"))`, which uses Playwright's native `:has-text()` engine ŌĆö the runner's `isAbsenceSelector()` predicate routes `:not(:has-text(` selectors through the absence-count branch (fix landed in the batch-1 executor changes). Same semantic intent: assert the fallback string from the `labels` map does NOT appear in the rendered DOM when the `option.label` precedes it in the precedence chain. Adapter at `tests/parity/harness/adapters/ToggleButtonGroup.adapter.tsx`, spec at `tests/parity/playwright-specs/ToggleButtonGroup.parity.spec.ts`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Playwright parity catalog flaws fix ŌĆö QRCode + ToggleButtonGroup + SettingCheckbox + ThemePicker + CsvTableRenderer adopted").
- [x] ISC-44.testing.setting_checkbox_playwright_adopted: `SettingCheckbox` wired into the Playwright parity executor after the catalog flaw (batch-1 dropped it for) was fixed. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `chore/playwright-catalog-fixes`. 8/8 stories passing (4 happy + 4 negative ŌĆö every story executes; no backend verbs in the catalog). **Catalog flaw fixed:** the `setting-checkbox-switch-aria-checked-false-when-unchecked` story asserted `span.translate-x-0.5` with an unescaped dot. Standard CSS parses that as `span.translate-x-0` chained with class `.5` ŌĆö no class named `5` exists, so the selector never matched. The Tailwind utility class is literally `translate-x-0.5` (with a dot in the name). Replaced with `span[class~="translate-x-0.5"]` ŌĆö the CSS attribute-selector form that matches a whitespace-separated token in the `class` attribute, dots and all. Same renderer-pinned semantic. Adapter at `tests/parity/harness/adapters/SettingCheckbox.adapter.tsx`, spec at `tests/parity/playwright-specs/SettingCheckbox.parity.spec.ts`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Playwright parity catalog flaws fix ŌĆö QRCode + ToggleButtonGroup + SettingCheckbox + ThemePicker + CsvTableRenderer adopted").
- [x] ISC-44.testing.theme_picker_playwright_adopted: `ThemePicker` wired into the Playwright parity executor after the catalog flaw (batch-1 dropped it for) was fixed. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `chore/playwright-catalog-fixes`. 6 executed stories (3 happy + 3 negative) all passing; 2 backend-verb stories (`theme-picker-click-inactive-swatch-invokes-set-active-theme-id-callback` and `theme-picker-click-already-active-swatch-still-fires-callback-with-same-id` ŌĆö both `notificationFired`) auto-skipped by the executor per `VERBS_REQUIRING_BACKEND`. **Catalog flaw fixed:** the `theme-picker-inactive-swatch-does-not-render-active-indicator-dot` story asserted `body:not(:has(button.ring-2.ring-2))` ŌĆö `.ring-2.ring-2` is a redundant duplicated class chain that CSS collapses to `.ring-2`. The selector parsed means "no button has ring-2 at all", which fails the moment the active swatch (which legitimately has ring-2) renders. The story's true intent is "the inactive swatch does not get ring-2 ŌĆö only the active one does". Replaced with `button:not(.ring-2)` ŌĆö asserts "there exists at least one button WITHOUT ring-2", which is the negative-path's actual observable signature. Adapter at `tests/parity/harness/adapters/ThemePicker.adapter.tsx`, spec at `tests/parity/playwright-specs/ThemePicker.parity.spec.ts`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Playwright parity catalog flaws fix ŌĆö QRCode + ToggleButtonGroup + SettingCheckbox + ThemePicker + CsvTableRenderer adopted").
- [x] ISC-44.testing.csv_table_renderer_playwright_adopted: `CsvTableRenderer` wired into the Playwright parity executor after the catalog flaw (batch-1 dropped it for) was fixed. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `chore/playwright-catalog-fixes`. 9 executed stories (5 happy + 4 negative) all passing; 1 backend-verb story (`csv-table-renderer-does-not-leak-ipc-or-electron-surface-at-module-load-or-runtime` ŌĆö `wsFrameMatches` + `fsHas` + `processHas`) auto-skipped by the executor. **Catalog flaw fixed:** the `csv-table-renderer-search-query-wraps-matching-substrings-in-mark-elements` story asserted `hasText` on `mark` with `value: 'al'`. The component's `highlightMatches()` builds a case-INSENSITIVE regex (`/al/gi`) but splits the original cell text and preserves the source case ŌĆö the match against "Alice" lands a `<mark>` whose innerText is `Al` (capital A), NOT `al`. Replaced with `value: 'Al'` so the assertion reflects the observable DOM. **Header-click driver added:** the `csv-table-renderer-header-click-sorts-column-ascending-and-shows-chevron-up` story requires an interaction before the assertion runs. The adapter's `SortDriver` wrapper clicks the first non-row-number header cell (`thead th` index 1) on first paint so the chevron-up SVG is rendered by the time the assertion runs. Adapter at `tests/parity/harness/adapters/CsvTableRenderer.adapter.tsx`, spec at `tests/parity/playwright-specs/CsvTableRenderer.parity.spec.ts`. - [x] ISC-44.testing.agent_error_modal_playwright_adopted: `AgentErrorModal` wired into the Playwright parity executor. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `feat/playwright-adopt-batch-2`. 10/10 stories executed and passing (5 happy + 5 negative). Every story is wrapped in a `<LayerStackProvider>` because the modal composes the L2.1 `Modal` primitive, which calls `useModalLayer(...)` which reaches into `LayerStackContext` ŌĆö mounting without the provider throws "useLayerStack must be used within a LayerStackProvider" before the modal can paint. **Escape-to-close story driven via `EscapeDriver`** ŌĆö mounts the modal inside the provider, dispatches a synthetic `Escape` keydown on `window` after the first paint, then holds the `onDismiss` flag in local state and unmounts when the provider's `closeTopLayer()` ŌåÆ `onEscape` ŌåÆ `onDismiss` pipeline fires. The catalog's `body:not(:has([role="dialog"]))` absence assertion validates the post-Escape DOM. The five `getErrorTitle()` branches (auth_expired, token_exhaustion, network_error, agent_crashed/unknown, agent_name+session_name context) are covered; the `parsedJson` toggle is covered (presence + absence); the `dismissible` flag is covered (presence + absence); the generic fallback title (`type: 'unknown'`) is covered. No backend verbs in the catalog. Adapter at `tests/parity/harness/adapters/AgentErrorModal.adapter.tsx`, spec at `tests/parity/playwright-specs/AgentErrorModal.parity.spec.ts`. - [x] ISC-44.shim.w3_marketplace_routes: Server-side `/api/marketplace/*` route cluster (6 endpoints) backed by `src/server/marketplace-manager.ts` (NEW). Corrects the IPC-shim Decision (2026-06-08): the modal-file grep on `src/renderer/components/MarketplaceModal.tsx` (5 sites) concluded "ZERO new routes," but the transitive `useMarketplace` hook (`src/renderer/hooks/batch/useMarketplace.ts`) is the actual consumer of `window.maestro.marketplace.*` ŌĆö 7 sites + 1 event subscription = 8 surfaces total. Routes added: `GET /manifest`, `POST /refresh`, `GET /readme`, `GET /document`, `POST /import`, `GET /manifest/events` (SSE). `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts` additive only (MarketplaceProvider interface + register/get accessors + 6 handlers). `src/server/index.ts` wires the manager before `server.start()`. Renderer-side IPC handler at `src/main/ipc/handlers/marketplace.ts` NOT touched ŌĆö Electron path leaves provider unset and the routes 503 cleanly. SSH-remote imports are explicitly NOT supported in the headless port (manager throws; `remote-fs.ts` is outside `tsconfig.server.json`'s include set and dragging it in would break the headless build invariant). **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on this Decision entry. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("W3-marketplace server-side route cluster (corrects IPC-shim Decision scope: transitive hook consumers must be counted)").
- [x] ISC-44.shim.useMarketplace_webfull_port: webFull-native port of `src/renderer/hooks/batch/useMarketplace.ts` landed at `src/webFull/hooks/useMarketplace.ts` (NEW, ~370 LOC). Companion to `ISC-44.shim.marketplace_modal_lifted`. Mirrors the renderer hook's `UseMarketplaceReturn` shape field-for-field (data: `manifest` / `playbooks` / `categories`; loading: `isLoading` / `isRefreshing` / `isImporting`; cache: `fromCache` / `cacheAge`; error: `error`; filter: `selectedCategory` / `searchQuery` / `filteredPlaybooks` + setters; actions: `refresh`, `importPlaybook` with `(playbook, targetFolderName, autoRunFolderPath, sessionId, sshRemoteId?)`; document preview: `fetchReadme`, `fetchDocument`) so MarketplaceModal lifts onto the new hook without any call-site signature changes. **IPC site ŌåÆ REST route mapping**: `getManifest()` ŌåÆ `GET /api/marketplace/manifest`; `refreshManifest()` ŌåÆ `POST /api/marketplace/refresh`; `importPlaybook(...)` ŌåÆ `POST /api/marketplace/import` body `{playbookId, targetFolderName, autoRunFolderPath, sessionId, sshRemoteId?}`; `getReadme(path)` ŌåÆ `GET /api/marketplace/readme?path=...`; `getDocument(path, filename)` ŌåÆ `GET /api/marketplace/document?path=...&filename=...`; `onManifestChanged(cb)` ŌåÆ `new EventSource('/api/marketplace/manifest/events') + addEventListener('message', ...)` with JSON.parse of `data: {"type":"manifestChanged"}` frames triggering manifest re-fetch (mirrors renderer "reload on every change" semantics; EventSource auto-reconnects per WHATWG spec). **Auth threading via `buildApiUrl()` from `'../utils/config'`** ŌĆö the same mechanism documented by `useSettings()`'s `buildApiBase()` helper. Reads `window.__MAESTRO_CONFIG__.securityToken` (server-injected on the webFull bundle) and prefixes every endpoint as `/${token}/api/marketplace/...`. URLs are pinned once per mount via `useRef` to avoid rebuilding on every render. **Pure browser runtime** ŌĆö zero `window.maestro.*` reads, zero `electron`/`ipcRenderer` imports, zero module-load side effects; the only window-touching surfaces are `fetch` and `EventSource` (both standard). EventSource is guarded with a `typeof EventSource === 'undefined'` check so the hook still mounts in non-browser test environments (the SSE branch is a hot-reload nicety, not load-bearing for first paint). **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-marketplace-modal`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("MarketplaceModal lift + useMarketplace webFull port ŌĆö closes the modal half of W3-marketplace").
- [x] ISC-44.shim.marketplace_modal_lifted: `MarketplaceModal` lifted from `src/renderer/components/MarketplaceModal.tsx` (1434 LOC, hard-bucket modal) to `src/webFull/components/MarketplaceModal.tsx` (NEW). Closes the modal half of the W3-marketplace strategy (server-side routes: `ISC-44.shim.w3_marketplace_routes` CLOSED 2026-06-08 on commit `a44e29a04`; transitive hook port: `ISC-44.shim.useMarketplace_webfull_port` companion above). **Two swap patterns wired per L2.5 precedent**: (1) `dialog.selectFolder` ŌåÆ `onFolderPick` prop (optional). Renderer line 930's `window.maestro.dialog.selectFolder()` is promoted to an optional `onFolderPick: () => Promise<string | null>` prop following the SaveMarkdownModal `onBrowseFolder` precedent. When the prop is undefined OR `sshRemoteId` is set (gated via `hasFolderPicker && !isRemoteSession`), the folder-browse button is hidden so users don't click a no-op affordance. When defined, the resolved string populates the target-folder field exactly as the renderer's `setTargetFolderName(folder)` does. (2) `shell.openExternal` ŌåÆ `window.open(url, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer')` (three sites). Renderer lines 425 (author link), 1181 (help-popover GitHub), 1209 (header GitHub submit) all swap to the security-hardened form per the StandingOvationOverlay precedent ŌĆö `noopener` blocks `window.opener` access, `noreferrer` blocks the Referer header. The renderer's fourth `openExternal` site at line 304 (inside `createMarkdownComponents`'s `onExternalLinkClick` lambda) collapses to zero because this lift routes README/document rendering through the lifted webFull `MarkdownRenderer` primitive (which handles external links internally), per the HistoryDetailModal / TerminalOutput precedents that avoided pulling the 925-LOC `markdownConfig.ts` module into webFull. **Verbatim against renderer source**: tile grid + skeleton tiles; PlaybookTile selection + scroll-into-view; PlaybookDetailView two-column structure (sidebar + preview); document-dropdown ChevronDown; sidebar metadata cards (description / author / tags / documents-list / loop-settings / last-updated / source-badge); footer target-folder input + Import button; outer modal chrome (1200px width, 95vw cap, 85vh max-height); LayerStack registration at `MODAL_PRIORITIES.MARKETPLACE` (value 735) with `focusTrap: 'strict'` + Escape stack (help ŌåÆ detail-back ŌåÆ close); Cmd+F search focus, Cmd+Shift+[/] tab navigation, OPT/CMD scroll shortcuts; arrow-key tile navigation; help popover with submit-via-GitHub copy. **Import-path adapts** (per L2.5 precedent): `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'`; `useMarketplace` from `'../hooks/batch/useMarketplace'` ŌåÆ `'../hooks/useMarketplace'` (webFull-native sibling); `REMARK_GFM_PLUGINS, generateProseStyles, createMarkdownComponents` from `'../utils/markdownConfig'` ŌåÆ REMOVED (replaced by `MarkdownRenderer` per Markdown-pipeline adapt above); `MODAL_PRIORITIES` + `useLayerStack` + `formatShortcutKeys` + `MarketplacePlaybook` all resolve via existing webFull re-exports / shared modules unchanged. **Files added** (all under `src/webFull/`, additive only): `src/webFull/components/MarketplaceModal.tsx` (NEW, ~1090 LOC including header doc + the two strip-and-promote adapts); `src/webFull/components/MarketplaceModal.parity.test.ts` (NEW, ~280 LOC including header doc; 5 happy + 5 negative stories; 8/8 vitest passing); `src/webFull/hooks/useMarketplace.ts` (companion port, see sibling ISC). **Files modified** (additive only): `src/webFull/components/index.ts` (+2 lines ŌĆö `MarketplaceModal` + `MarketplaceModalProps` exports); `src/webFull/hooks/index.ts` (+5 lines ŌĆö `useMarketplace` + `useMarketplaceDefault` + `UseMarketplaceReturn` exports under a "W3-marketplace transitive-hook port" section header). **Files NOT touched**: `src/web/`, `src/renderer/`, `src/server/`, `src/main/`. **Brief-audit correction note**: the IPC-shim Decision (2026-06-08) was scoped to the modal-file grep and undercounted the marketplace surface ("modal count" said ZERO new routes). The W3-marketplace correction proved that incomplete ŌĆö `useMarketplace` is the transitive consumer that pulled in 7 additional IPC sites + 1 event subscription. Future audits must enumerate transitive hook consumers separately from direct modal-file callsites. **Verification**: `npm run lint` clean (3 tsconfigs after `npm run build:prompts`); `npx vitest run src/webFull/components/MarketplaceModal.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ 8/8 passing; `npm run build:webfull` succeeds. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-marketplace-modal`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("MarketplaceModal lift + useMarketplace webFull port ŌĆö closes the modal half of W3-marketplace").
- [x] ISC-44.shim.agents*routes: Sub-ISC under the umbrella `ISC-44.shim.big_3_ipc_strategy` (sibling to `ISC-44.shim.fs_routes`). Ships the server-side agents-detection REST route cluster (`GET /api/agents/detected`, `GET /api/agents/detect/:agentId`, `GET /api/agents/capabilities/:agentId`) backed by a new `src/server/agents-manager.ts` (server-side port of the read-side `agents:*`IPC handlers at`src/main/ipc/handlers/agents.ts`, no `electron` import). Provider-registry pattern mirrors W2-wakatime / W2-stats / W2-fonts / W3-fs byte-for-byte (`AgentsProvider`interface +`registerAgentsProvider()`+`getAgentsProvider()`in apiRoutes.ts; headless wiring in`src/server/index.ts`before`server.start()`). Re-uses `AGENT_DEFINITIONS`+`getAgentCapabilities`directly from`src/main/agents/{definitions,capabilities}.ts`ŌĆö both are pure-data modules with no`electron`/`utils/sentry`/`utils/logger`graph, so they are added to`tsconfig.server.json`(additive include, +2 file paths). Binary detection (probe known paths +`which`/`where`with expanded PATH) is re-implemented inline in agents-manager.ts to avoid pulling in path-prober ŌåÆ`runtime/getShellPath`ŌåÆ`utils/sentry`ŌåÆ`@sentry/electron`. Unblocks NewInstanceModal sites at NewInstanceModal.tsx:240+381+1270 (the `detect`, `refresh`, `getCapabilities`sub-surface). Agent id validation (alphanumeric +`-`/`\*`, max 64 chars) lives in the route layer (defense-in-depth; the provider never sees a hostile id). SSH remote support deliberately out of scope (`?sshRemoteId=`query param returns 501) and ships in a future`W3-ssh-remotes`brief. **Brief-audit findings** ŌĆö NewInstanceModal additionally calls`agents.getConfig`(lines 284, 1288),`agents.setConfig`(lines 971, 1791), and`agents.getModels`(lines 405, 1482); those need follow-up briefs (config CRUD needs a server-side config store; model discovery needs per-agent subcommand fan-out). The umbrella big_3_ipc_strategy Decision explicitly marks them as out of scope for the shim cluster ŌĆö this brief ships the detection + capabilities sub-surface only. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch`w3-agents-server-port`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("W3-agents ŌĆö server-side agents-detection REST routes").
- [x] ISC-44.shim.agents_writers: Follow-up sub-ISC to `ISC-44.shim.agents_routes` (sibling under the umbrella `ISC-44.shim.big_3_ipc_strategy`). Ships the writer half of the agents REST surface that the original W3-agents brief deferred per audit #12 NewInstanceModal preconditions: `GET /api/agents/config/:agentId` (merged defaults + stored config ŌĆö mirrors `agents:getConfig`), `PUT /api/agents/config/:agentId` (overwrite stored config ŌĆö mirrors `agents:setConfig`), `GET /api/agents/models/:agentId` (local model discovery ŌĆö mirrors `agents:getModels` local path). EXTENDS the existing `src/server/agents-manager.ts` (additive `getConfig` / `setConfig` / `getModels` methods on the `AgentsManager` class ŌĆö constructor now takes `dataDir` to back the config FileStore at `<dataDir>/agents-config.json`, lazy-used so the singleton stays valid). EXTENDS the existing `AgentsProvider` interface in `apiRoutes.ts` (additive `getConfig` / `setConfig` / `getModels` fields). EXTENDS the existing `registerAgentsProvider()` call in `src/server/index.ts` (additive 3 fields, same `agentsManager` singleton wired with `dataDir`). On-disk store shape `{configs: Record<agentId, Record<string, unknown>>}` is the cross-mode contract ŌĆö mirrors the renderer-side `AgentConfigsData` electron-store shape byte-for-byte so a hybrid Electron + headless deployment writing to the same `dataDir` reads each other's edits without schema translation. Model discovery is local-only (SSH-remote dispatch is `ISC-44.shim.ssh_remotes_routes`'s extension ŌĆö `?sshRemoteId=` returns 501); only `opencode` actually shells out (`opencode models` returns one model per line), other agents that claim `supportsModelSelection: true` (codex, gemini-cli) fall through to `[]` because their `models` subcommand is not implemented in the renderer-side `AgentDetector.runModelDiscovery` either ŌĆö the cross-mode contract holds. Cache TTL 5min (matches `AgentDetector.modelCacheTtlMs`), `?forceRefresh=true` bypasses. Body validation on PUT ŌĆö `config` MUST be a plain object (not array, not null, not primitive); rejected at the route boundary with 400. Agent id validation reuses the existing `validateAgentId()` defense-in-depth helper. Closes the per-IPC-shim Decision agents cluster ŌĆö with these 3 routes shipped, NewInstanceModal's remaining 6 of 11 IPC call sites are unblocked (audit #12 originally identified lines 284 / 405 / 971 / 1277 / 1288 / 1482 / 1791). Out of scope (deliberately, follow-up brief): `agents:setCustomPath` / `agents:setCustomArgs` / `agents:setCustomEnvVars` and their getters ŌĆö Settings-panel surface, not a NewInstanceModal precondition; would ride on the same JSON store via sub-keys. Also out of scope: `agents:discoverSlashCommands` (Claude Code only, no current webFull consumer). **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `w3-agents-writers`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("W3-agents-writers ŌĆö server-side agents config CRUD + model discovery REST routes").
- [x] ISC-44.shim.ssh_remotes_writers: Follow-up to `ISC-44.shim.ssh_remotes_routes` ŌĆö closes the writer surface that the prior brief deferred. Audit #12 flagged these as NewInstanceModal preconditions (specifically the SettingsModal SSH-tab CRUD path that NewInstanceModal links into for "manage SSH remotes" affordance). Ships the 5 additive writer routes in `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts` (`POST /api/ssh-remotes` ŌĆö create; `PUT /api/ssh-remotes/:id` ŌĆö partial update; `DELETE /api/ssh-remotes/:id` ŌĆö delete with default-id clear; `PUT /api/ssh-remotes/default-id` ŌĆö set/clear default; `POST /api/ssh-remotes/:id/test` ŌĆö connection test) backed by 5 new methods on `SshRemotesManager` in `src/server/ssh-remotes-manager.ts` (`saveConfig` / `updateConfig` / `deleteConfig` / `setDefaultId` / `testConnection`). `SshRemotesSettingsReader` widened with `.set<V>(key, value)` ŌĆö same pattern the FileStore already supports symmetric to `.get`. `SshRemotesProvider` interface in `apiRoutes.ts` extended with 5 optional writer methods so existing read-only providers keep type-checking during rollout (route layer 503s on undefined methods just as it does on missing provider). `validateConfig` / `buildSshArgs` / `parseSSHError` inlined in `ssh-remotes-manager.ts` (mirror of `src/main/ssh-remote-manager.ts:96+227+266` 1:1) since `src/main/utils/` is outside `tsconfig.server.json`'s include set; the renderer-side helpers stay untouched and the two implementations MUST stay in sync going forward (any change to validation rules / SSH option set / error-pattern table SHOULD be mirrored). Inline `execFileNoThrow` shim (~20 LOC, mirrors the helper in `agents-manager.ts`) drives the `ssh` binary for connection-test ŌĆö binary must be on the server's PATH (macOS / Linux ship it; container envs may need to add it). Connection-test returns 200 with `success:false` for "test ran, connection didn't succeed" (the renderer-side IPC contract `SshRemoteTestResult` envelope is the payload); 5xx reserved for unexpected exceptions in the test plumbing. `latencyMs` added to the response envelope as wall-clock test duration ŌĆö useful for the connection-tester UI affordance. Route registration order pinned: `PUT /default-id` registers BEFORE `PUT /:id` so the literal segment outranks the parameter segment in Fastify's radix-tree matcher. Error contract: 400 (validation / body shape), 404 (id not found via `error.code === 'NOT_FOUND'` discriminator on manager throws), 500 (unexpected), 503 (provider unset OR writer method undefined). All 5 routes use `rateLimitConfig.maxPost` (the stricter mutator-write budget). Headless wiring in `src/server/index.ts` adds 5 method bindings to the existing `registerSshRemotesProvider({...})` block and widens the SettingsReader adapter with a `.set` overload that casts at the boundary (matching the existing `.get` pattern). NewInstanceModal precondition status: with this cluster shipped, the modal lift's read AND write SSH-remote surfaces are both server-side; remaining modal preconditions (agent-config CRUD + model discovery) are still tracked separately per the umbrella `big_3_ipc_strategy` Decision. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `w3-ssh-remotes-writers`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("W3-ssh-remotes-writers ŌĆö server-side SSH-remotes writer + test REST routes (audit #12)").
- [x] ISC-44.shim.ssh_remotes_routes: LAST sibling sub-ISC under the umbrella `ISC-44.shim.big_3_ipc_strategy` (siblings: `fs_routes` Ō£ō, `agents_routes` Ō£ō, `w3_marketplace_routes` Ō£ō, autorun-via-`FsProvider.writeDoc` Ō£ō). Ships the server-side SSH-remotes REST route cluster (`GET /api/ssh-remotes`, `GET /api/ssh-remotes/default-id`, `GET /api/ssh-remotes/ssh-config-hosts`) backed by a new `src/server/ssh-remotes-manager.ts` (server-side port of the read sub-surface of the `ssh-remote:*` IPC handlers at `src/main/ipc/handlers/ssh-remote.ts`, no `electron` import, no `electron-store` import). Provider-registry pattern mirrors W2-wakatime / W2-stats / W2-fonts / W3-fs / W3-marketplace / W3-agents byte-for-byte (`SshRemotesProvider` interface + `registerSshRemotesProvider()` + `getSshRemotesProvider()` in apiRoutes.ts; headless wiring in `src/server/index.ts` before `server.start()`). The `~/.ssh/config` parser is re-implemented inline in `ssh-remotes-manager.ts` (mirror of `src/main/utils/ssh-config-parser.ts`) because `src/main/utils/` is outside `tsconfig.server.json`'s include set ŌĆö no schema change, the two parsers MUST stay in sync going forward (any new directive added to one SHOULD be mirrored in the other). Unblocks NewInstanceModal sites at NewInstanceModal.tsx:602 + NewInstanceModal.tsx:1312 (both call `window.maestro.sshRemote.getConfigs()` to populate the SSH-remote dropdown). With this cluster shipped, **the IPC-shim Decision is complete** ŌĆö all 5 sibling sub-ISCs are closed and the NewInstanceModal lift's server-side dependencies are all in place (the modal still needs agent-config CRUD + model discovery for full parity, both deliberately out of scope per the umbrella Decision). Read-only surface ŌĆö `saveConfig` / `deleteConfig` / `setDefaultId` (writers) and `test` (connection-test; needs `ssh` binary + buildSshArgs/parseSSHError extraction) deferred to follow-up briefs per the W3-agents "ship the read sub-surface, defer the writers" precedent. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `w3-ssh-remotes-server-port`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("W3-ssh-remotes ŌĆö server-side SSH-remotes read-side REST routes (completes the IPC-shim Decision)").
- [x] ISC-44.wiring.mount_wave_2: Per audit #10 PIVOT continuation ("pair every future lift with a same-commit mount edit"), four lifted-but-unmounted modals are now MOUNTED in `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` ŌĆö `NewInstanceModal`, `MarketplaceModal`, `SettingsModal`, and `AgentErrorModal`. Pre-wiring grep `grep -c 'NewInstanceModal\|MarketplaceModal' src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` ŌåÆ 0 matches; post-wiring ŌåÆ 23 matches (imports + JSX mounts + comments + handler refs). The Settings + AgentError counts move from 0 to non-zero similarly. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `feat/mount-newinstance-marketplace`. **Same pattern as mount-wave 1.** Each modal gets a `useModalGate()` (`newInstanceGate`, `marketplaceGate`, `settingsGate`, `agentErrorGate`) and a debug keybinding routed through the existing `useEffect` keydown chord handler. Keybindings: `Cmd+Shift+N` ŌåÆ NewInstanceModal (renderer-canonical Cmd+N collides with the browser's "new window"); `Cmd+Shift+M` ŌåÆ MarketplaceModal (no renderer-canonical shortcut ŌĆö renderer surfaces marketplace via an explicit button); `Cmd+,` ŌåÆ SettingsModal (OS-canonical Settings shortcut across macOS apps); `Cmd+Alt+E` ŌåÆ AgentErrorModal (debug only). Mac key-code edge for Alt+E (`e.key === '┬┤'`) handled. Same `isInputFocused()` guard as wave 1 so command-input typing is unaffected. **NewInstanceModal host wiring.** All 9 strip-and-promoted props are stubbed with safe defaults per the wave-2 brief: empty `agentConfigs` / `availableModels` maps, no-op `onAgentConfigSave` / `onRefreshModels`, a `valid: false` `onRemotePathValidate`, a `null`-returning `onFolderPick`, a no-op `onCreate` that logs through `webLogger`. `existingSessions={[]}` because the modal expects the renderer `Session` shape (not webFull `Session`) ŌĆö the empty array is the trivial "no conflicts" case for `validateNewSession`. None of the stubs throw; the modal renders, the user can fill the form, the host just doesn't persist. Host-data TODOs annotated at each stub for the next wave (real flow: PATCH `/api/agents/config`, host folder-picker, `?sshRemoteId=`-aware fs.stat, `create_session` WS frame with the rich shape). **MarketplaceModal host wiring.** `useMarketplace()` browser-runtime hook wired for the playbook listing + SSE-driven cache + import flow. `autoRunFolderPath=""` (host doesn't yet thread AutoRun folder path on webFull); `sessionId` falls back to `activeSessionId ?? ''`; `sshRemoteId` omitted (W3-marketplace headless import 500s on SSH remotes per the manager's `ssh-remote-import-not-supported` posture); `onFolderPick` stubbed (web has no native folder picker ŌĆö the affordance hides per the lift policy's `hasFolderPicker` gate). `onImportComplete` logs and closes the gate. The hook reference is held via `void marketplaceHook` so cache/SSE side-effects initialize (the modal itself re-calls the hook internally; we mainly initialize the connection lifecycle). **SettingsModal host wiring.** No host props needed ŌĆö the modal already self-contains tab state and threads its three tab bodies (GeneralTab, DisplayTab, ShortcutsTab) through `useSettings()` internally. The wiring is just the gate-state + Cmd+, keybinding. This surface is complete on the webFull side. New re-export `SettingsModal` added to `src/webFull/components/index.ts` so the host import resolves through the public barrel rather than reaching into the nested `./Settings/SettingsModal` path. **AgentErrorModal host wiring.** Synthetic stub `AgentError` (`type: 'agent_crashed'`) + single "Dismiss" `RecoveryAction` mounted on Cmd+Alt+E. The synthetic error pulls `agentId` from the active session's `toolType` (falling back to `'claude-code'`) and `sessionId` from the active session id when present. Real surface fires when the parent agent-error handler ports the structured AgentError WS frame routing; until then the debug trigger proves the modal renders, the error-color icon resolves, the primary recovery action takes focus, and the LayerStack Escape handling works. Host-data TODO annotated. **Other settings tabs (already lifted).** GeneralTab, DisplayTab, ShortcutsTab are reachable through the new SettingsModal mount via tab navigation ŌĆö no separate keybindings needed. **Files added:** none (re-uses existing `useModalGate` hook from wave 1). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/components/index.ts` (+13 LOC ŌĆö SettingsModal re-export under a new "Audit #10 mount-wave 2" section); `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` (+~225 LOC additive ŌĆö imports for the 4 modals + `useMarketplace` + `AgentError` type, four new `useModalGate()` instances, four new keybinding branches in the existing keydown handler, host-wiring `useMemo` / `useCallback` block, four JSX mounts at the end of the existing modal stack); `ISA.md` (this ISC + a sibling Decisions entry below). **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö fork-hygiene), `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö read-only oracle), `src/main/` (zero bytes diff vs main), `src/server/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö wave 2 is pure client wiring; all the host-data TODOs identify future server work). 0 IPC namespaces touched ŌĆö every lifted modal already routed side effects through prop callbacks per its L2.5 lift policy. **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean (3 tsconfigs after `npm run build:prompts`); `npm run build:webfull` succeeds (mobile bundle 8.3 MB raw / 3.4 MB gzip ŌĆö within the W3-marketplace bundle-size headroom); `npx vitest run src/webFull/` ŌåÆ 562/562 tests passed (85 test files, no regressions); grep proof ŌĆö `grep -c 'NewInstanceModal\|MarketplaceModal' src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` ŌåÆ 23 (pre-edit was 0). See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Audit #10 mount-wave 2 ŌĆö NewInstanceModal + MarketplaceModal + SettingsModal + AgentErrorModal").
- [x] ISC-44.wiring.autorun_mounted: Per audit #10 PIVOT continuation ("pair every future lift with a same-commit mount edit"), the ~2285-LOC L2.5 `AutoRun` lift (`ISC-44.lift.autorun_main`, closed on `aee55e1d3`) is now MOUNTED in `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx`. Pre-wiring `grep -c 'AutoRun' src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` ŌåÆ 16 matches (`AutoRunIndicator` + `AutoRunnerHelpModal` references only); post-wiring ŌåÆ 64 matches (imports + JSX mount + comments + 8 handler refs + barrel export). **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `feat/mount-autorun`. **Same pattern as mount-waves 1 + 2.** `autoRunGate` (the existing `useModalGate()` primitive) plus a `Cmd+Shift+A` debug keybinding routed through the existing `useEffect` keydown chord handler (same `isInputFocused()` guard so command-input typing is unaffected). No renderer-canonical shortcut for "show AutoRun" ŌĆö the renderer docks AutoRun in `RightPanel.tsx`; webFull doesn't yet have side-panel chrome, so `Cmd+Shift+A` is the webFull-debug variant that closes the trigger gap until subsequent waves surface a docking chrome. **Strip-and-promote host wiring.** All 8 callback props stubbed with `webLogger.warn(...)` (host-data TODOs annotated at each stub): `onOpenSetup` (eventual `settingsGate.show()` binding), `onRefresh`, `onSelectDocument`, `onCreateDocument` (returns `false`), `onOpenBatchRunner`, `onStopBatchRun`. Local `useState` triplets for `content` / `mode` / `selectedFile` (`onContentChange`, `onModeChange`, `onSelectDocument` mutate them). `folderPath={null}` triggers the component's "no folder selected" empty state ŌĆö the chrome renders inertly and no fetch side-effects (cache hydration, `imageCache` mutations) fire until host wires a real `useAutoRunContext` source. `documentList={[]}`, `batchRunState={undefined}`, `sessionState={undefined}` are the trivial empty cases the modal handles without crashing. `bionifyReadingMode` threaded from the existing `useDesktopTheme()` (same source TerminalOutput uses); `bionifyIntensity` / `bionifyAlgorithm` use the lifted component's prop defaults (`1` / `'- 0 1 1 2 0.4'`) per the lift docblock's strip-and-promote authorization. **Overlay chrome ŌĆö full-screen card.** AutoRun has no built-in modal chrome (its renderer host docks it in a side-panel that owns positioning); the mount wraps the component in a `position: fixed` full-screen overlay (`zIndex: 1000`) with a thin header bar carrying a Close button bound to `autoRunGate.hide`. Real integration in a subsequent wave swaps this overlay for a side-panel-shaped surface once webFull surfaces a docking chrome. **AutoRunExpandedModal cross-fork edge UNTOUCHED.** Per brief scope: `AutoRunExpandedModal.tsx:124` still imports the renderer's `AutoRun` (cross-fork edge documented in its lift Decision); a future retarget wave swaps that to the webFull sibling once the mount has bedded in. **Public barrel.** `AutoRun` + `AutoRunHandle` re-exported from `src/webFull/components/index.ts` so the host import resolves through the public barrel rather than reaching into the nested `./AutoRun` path. **Files added:** none (re-uses `useModalGate` hook from wave 1). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/components/index.ts` (+2 LOC ŌĆö AutoRun + AutoRunHandle re-exports), `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` (+199 LOC ŌĆö AutoRun import addition, `autoRunGate` + 3 local-state hooks, `Cmd+Shift+A` keybinding branch, 8 host-wiring `useCallback` stubs, 1 JSX mount inside a `position: fixed` overlay wrapper), `ISA.md` (this ISC). **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö fork-hygiene), `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö read-only oracle), `src/main/` (zero bytes diff vs main), `src/server/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö wave 3 is pure client wiring; host-data TODOs identify future server work). **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean; `npm run build:webfull` succeeds (mobile bundle 8.4 MB raw / 3.4 MB gzip); `npx vitest run src/webFull/` ŌåÆ 568/568 tests passed (86 test files, no regressions); LOC delta on `App.tsx`: +199 (2313 ŌåÆ 2512), within the ~250 budget the brief authorized. Total mounted-component count rises from 17 (wave 2 baseline) to 18. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Audit #10 mount-wave 3 ŌĆö AutoRun").
- [x] ISC-44.wiring.new_instance_modal_create_wired: Audit #13 ŌĆö the next user-felt milestone after mount-wave 2. `NewInstanceModal`'s `onCreate` prop was stubbed by mount-wave 2 (`feat/mount-newinstance-marketplace`) ŌĆö submitting the modal logged through `webLogger` but did not actually create a session. This ISC ships the WS protocol extension + handler wiring so a webFull user clicking "Create" actually adds an agent to the store. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `feat/new-instance-modal-create-session`. **Two-part change: server WS frame + client wiring.** Pre-flight grep on `src/server/` and `src/main/web-server/handlers/` for `create_session` / `createSession` returned 0 ŌĆö no existing frame to wire to, so the protocol needed an additive extension. Mirrors the existing `new_tab` frame pattern byte-for-byte (additive case in the switch, sibling `handleCreateSession` private method, `CreateSessionCallback` type + `CallbackRegistry.setCreateSessionCallback` registration, server-index `setCreateSessionCallback` wiring with mutator + persist + broadcast, client-side `wsSendRef.current?.({ type: 'create_session', ... })` call). **Server side ŌĆö additive store mutator + WS frame + callback registry.** `src/server/sessions-mutator.ts` gains a `createSession(sessions, input)` function (~85 LOC additive) following the existing `addTab` pattern: pure data-in/data-out, no FileStore touches, returns `null` on duplicate id OR missing required field, otherwise `{sessions, session}`. Seeds the new session with one initial idle AI tab (matching `addTab`'s tab shape + the renderer's `createNewSession` initial-tab shape byte-for-byte). `inputMode` special-cased to `terminal` for `toolType === 'terminal'` (mirrors the renderer's `agentId === 'terminal' ? 'terminal' : 'ai'` rule at `useSessionCrud.ts:232`). Custom spawn-config fields (`customPath`, `customArgs`, `customEnvVars`, `customModel`, `customContextWindow`, `customProviderPath`, `nudgeMessage`, `sessionSshRemoteConfig`) persisted verbatim when provided, OMITTED when undefined (no `undefined` leaks on disk). `src/main/web-server/types.ts` (additive ~25 LOC) ŌĆö `CreateSessionRequest` interface mirroring the renderer's `createNewSession` arg shape, `CreateSessionCallback` type. `src/main/web-server/managers/CallbackRegistry.ts` (additive ~14 LOC) ŌĆö `createSession` field + getter + setter, following the `newTab` precedent. `src/main/web-server/WebServer.ts` (additive ~4 LOC) ŌĆö `setCreateSessionCallback` delegation method. `src/main/web-server/handlers/messageHandlers.ts` (additive ~85 LOC) ŌĆö `CreateSessionMessageRequest` local interface (local-to-handler convention, mirrors `LiveSessionInfo`), `createSession` field on `MessageHandlerCallbacks`, `case 'create_session'` in the switch, `handleCreateSession` private method that validates required fields (agentId, workingDir, name), forwards optional fields, returns `{type: 'create_session_result', success, sessionId}` on the wire OR `{type: 'error', message}` on validation failure / missing callback / callback exception. `src/server/index.ts` (additive ~75 LOC) ŌĆö `setCreateSessionCallback` wiring that mints a session id via `randomUUID()` (mirrors `generateId()` semantics in the renderer), invokes the mutator, persists via `sessionsStore.set('sessions', result.sessions)`, broadcasts `session_added` via `server.broadcastSessionAdded({...})` with the full `SessionBroadcastData` shape (id, name, toolType, state, inputMode, cwd, groupId, groupName, groupEmoji from groupsStore lookup, parentSessionId=null, worktreeBranch=null). Server-side ID generation is the contract ŌĆö the client does NOT pass an id, the server mints it and returns it via `create_session_result.sessionId`. **Pattern (B) lazy-spawn, same as `addTab`.** No pty/process is spawned at session creation time. First command-send into the initial tab triggers `ProcessManager` on-demand spawn through the existing `writeToSession` / `executeCommand` callback chain. The renderer's `createNewSession` also does no spawn ŌĆö it's a pure store mutation, and the spawn is lazily triggered when the user types the first command. Wire parity holds. **Client side ŌĆö wire `handleNewInstanceCreate` to `wsSendRef`.** `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` (~50 LOC additive net change vs the mount-wave 2 stub): the existing `handleNewInstanceCreate` stub is replaced with a fire-and-forget WS send. The callback's full 12-arg signature (agentId, workingDir, name, nudgeMessage?, customPath?, customArgs?, customEnvVars?, customModel?, customContextWindow?, customProviderPath?, sessionSshRemoteConfig?, groupId?) is reified ŌĆö the old stub used `..._rest: any[]`. The send fires via `wsSendRef.current?.({type: 'create_session', ...})`; if WS not connected, logs a warn and still hides the gate so the user isn't trapped. The new session row hydrates via the existing `handleSessionAdded` handler in `useSessions` reading the server's `session_added` broadcast ŌĆö no additional client wiring needed. The comment block above the JSX mount is updated to reflect the wiring (was: "Host-data TODO: thread real sessions + wire `onCreate`"; now: "`onCreate` IS WIRED to the `create_session` WS frame"). **Validation ŌĆö server-side intentionally thin.** The webFull modal already enforces the renderer's validation rules pre-submit (non-empty name, non-empty cwd, agent selection, character cap). The server only re-checks bare-minimum required fields (agentId / workingDir / name present, no duplicate id from `randomUUID()` collision) so a malformed client frame doesn't poison the store. Richer validation (duplicate name/cwd, agent-supported guards) is a hardening follow-up; for now any well-formed modal submission lands. The renderer-side `validateNewSession` iterating `existingSessions` is a client-side check; the server-side equivalent is deferred. **Files added (all under `src/`, additive only):** `src/server/__tests__/sessions-mutator-create.test.ts` (~140 LOC, 8 unit tests covering happy path, terminal inputMode, immutability, custom-field persistence, undefined-field omission, duplicate id rejection, missing-required-field rejection, groupId forwarding). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/server/sessions-mutator.ts` (+~85 LOC ŌĆö `CreateSessionInput` interface + `createSession` function); `src/main/web-server/types.ts` (+~25 LOC ŌĆö `CreateSessionRequest` + `CreateSessionCallback`); `src/main/web-server/managers/CallbackRegistry.ts` (+~14 LOC ŌĆö registry field + getter + setter + imports); `src/main/web-server/WebServer.ts` (+~4 LOC ŌĆö delegation method + import); `src/main/web-server/handlers/messageHandlers.ts` (+~85 LOC ŌĆö local request interface + callback field + switch case + handler method); `src/server/index.ts` (+~75 LOC ŌĆö `setCreateSessionCallback` wiring with mutator invocation + persist + broadcast); `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` (~50 LOC net change ŌĆö replaced stub with WS-send + reified 12-arg signature + updated comment block); `src/__tests__/main/web-server/handlers/messageHandlers.test.ts` (+~110 LOC ŌĆö 7 new tests in a `Create Session (Web ŌåÆ Desktop) [audit #13]` describe block: happy path + sessionId return, custom-field forwarding, missing-agentId rejection, missing-workingDir rejection, missing-name rejection, missing-callback report, callback-returns-null soft-fail, callback-exception error frame); `src/__tests__/integration/messageHandlers.integration.test.ts` (+1 LOC ŌĆö `createSession` field on `makeCallbacks()` return so the `MessageHandlerCallbacks` interface contract holds); `ISA.md` (this ISC + a sibling Decisions entry below). **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö fork-hygiene), `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö read-only oracle), `src/main/ipc/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö no Electron-side IPC surface changes; renderer-side `createNewSession` continues to own the Electron path), `src/main/` outside of `web-server/` (zero bytes diff vs main). 0 IPC namespaces touched on the Electron side. **Verification:** `npm run build:prompts` succeeded; `npm run lint` clean across all 3 tsconfigs; `npm run build:server` clean; `npm run build:webfull` succeeds (mobile bundle ~8.3 MB raw / 3.4 MB gzip ŌĆö within the W3-marketplace bundle-size headroom); `npx vitest run src/server/__tests__/sessions-mutator-create.test.ts` ŌåÆ 8/8 passing; `npx vitest run src/__tests__/main/web-server/handlers/messageHandlers.test.ts` ŌåÆ 71/71 passing (63 prior + 8 new create_session tests); `npx vitest run -c vitest.integration.config.ts src/__tests__/integration/messageHandlers.integration.test.ts` ŌåÆ 4/4 passing; `npx vitest run src/webFull/` ŌåÆ 568/568 passing across 86 test files (6 more than mount-wave 2's 562 baseline ŌĆö the new sessions-mutator tests). Full repo: `npx vitest run src/server/ src/__tests__/main/` ŌåÆ 7012/7012 passing across 193 test files. **Caveats / follow-ups.** (1) Agent detection must succeed on the server side before the user opens the modal ŌĆö the modal's `agents.detect()` call hits `GET /api/agents/detected` which depends on `agentsManager`. (2) The modal's `existingSessions` prop is still `[]` (renderer Session shape impedance from mount-wave 2 holds) ŌĆö duplicate-name detection is server-side only via the `randomUUID()` id (which never collides) but a future follow-up could thread the real sessions through a renderer-Session adapter, mirroring the TerminalOutput adapter precedent. (3) The W3-agents config-CRUD writers (`onAgentConfigSave`) and per-agent model discovery (`onRefreshModels`) remain stubbed at mount-wave 2 defaults ŌĆö they're orthogonal to the "user creates an agent" milestone and tracked separately under the W3-agents Decision. (4) SSH remote config does flow through `sessionSshRemoteConfig` end-to-end (server persists it verbatim), but the SSH-aware `?sshRemoteId=` fs.stat for path validation still 501s per W3-fs ŌĆö clients see the indicator gracefully degrade rather than block creation. (5) Group selection in the modal is not yet surfaced on webFull, so `groupId` is undefined end-to-end in practice; no server change needed when it ships. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Audit #13 ŌĆö `create_session` WS frame wiring closes the user-creates-an-agent flow").
- [x] ISC-44.shim.autorun_images_routes: Sibling sub-ISC under the umbrella `ISC-44.shim.big_3_ipc_strategy`. Closes the LAST AutoRun-lift gap the prior shim Decision named: the AutoRun shell's `useAutoRunImageHandling` hook (`src/renderer/hooks/batch/useAutoRunImageHandling.ts`) calls `window.maestro.autorun.{listImages,saveImage,deleteImage}` at three sites (paste-image 278, file-upload 368, remove-attachment 419 + lightbox-delete 484) that no-op'd in webFull until now. The W3-fs cluster folded `autorun:writeDoc` into `FsProvider.writeDoc` per the umbrella Decision, but the three image-handling channels DID NOT have a matching server route ŌĆö the AutoRun lift STOPPED on this gap. Ships the server-side image REST cluster (`GET /api/autorun/list-images`, `POST /api/autorun/save-image`, `DELETE /api/autorun/delete-image`) backed by a new `src/server/autorun-manager.ts` (server-side port of the image-handling subset of `autorun:*` IPC handlers at `src/main/ipc/handlers/autorun.ts:501-752`, no `electron` import). Provider-registry pattern mirrors W2-wakatime / W2-stats / W2-fonts / W3-fs / W3-marketplace / W3-agents / W3-ssh-remotes byte-for-byte (`AutorunProvider` interface + `registerAutorunProvider()` + `getAutorunProvider()` in apiRoutes.ts; headless wiring in `src/server/index.ts` before `server.start()`). Distinct from `FsProvider` because the image operations carry a fixed contract (docName-prefix filename generation, `images/` subdirectory convention, fixed extension allowlist) that folding into the generic fs surface would erode. **Route surface (3 endpoints):** `GET /api/autorun/list-images?folderPath=ŌĆ”&docFilename=ŌĆ”` returns `{images: [{filename, relativePath, sizeBytes, modifiedAt}], timestamp}` (the reply shape extends the renderer-side `{filename, relativePath}` with `sizeBytes` + `modifiedAt` from `fs.stat()` so the AutoRun shell can use them for sort / cache-bust affordances once the lift continues; ISO-8601 mtime matches the `fs:stat` shape from `src/server/fs-manager.ts:196` for cross-route consistency); `POST /api/autorun/save-image` body `{folderPath, docFilename, dataUrl, extension}` reply `{filename, relativePath, timestamp}` (`dataUrl` accepts either the bare base64 payload OR a full `data:image/<ext>;base64,ŌĆ”` URL ŌĆö the manager's `decodeImageDataUrl()` handles both, so a future caller can post the FileReader result directly without manual stripping); `DELETE /api/autorun/delete-image` accepts inputs via body OR query (Fastify DELETE body parsing is enabled but some HTTP clients don't easily send a DELETE body, so query is a usability fallback) reply `{removed: bool, timestamp}` (returns `removed:false` when the file was already absent so the AutoRun shell's optimistic-UI flow tolerates double-deletes ŌĆö softer than the renderer-side `Image file not found` throw). **Defense-in-depth path validation in BOTH the route layer and the manager.** Routes: `folderPath` via `validateFsPath()` (re-uses the W3-fs validator: absolute, no NUL, no `..` segments, no encoded `..`); `docFilename` via `validateAutorunDocFilename()` (basename-strip + `.md` strip + character allowlist `\w / . / - / space`); `extension` via `validateImageExtension()` (fixed allowlist: png, jpg, jpeg, gif, webp, svg); `relativePath` via `validateImageRelativePath()` (must start with `images/`, no `..`, no nested directories under `images/`, no NUL). Manager re-validates as belt-and-suspenders; the resolved write path is also re-verified to stay inside `folderPath` after `path.join` so even a future change to filename generation can't escape. **SSH remote support deliberately out of scope** (matches the W3-fs precedent): `sshRemoteId` field present anywhere returns 501 so callers don't silently get a local result when a remote was requested. Rate limits: list uses read budget; save and delete use the stricter mutator-write budget. **Unblocks the AutoRun lift** (the only gap the prior shim Decision left). With this cluster shipped, the AutoRun shell's image-handling hook can target the server routes 1:1 when ported to webFull, and the lift resumes. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `w3-autorun-images-server-port`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("W3-autorun-images ŌĆö server-side autorun image REST routes (closes the AutoRun-lift gap)").
- [x] ISC-44.cleanup.cross_fork_drawdown_wave_1: First deliberate cross-fork drawdown wave. Audit #13 measured 87 lines / 45 files of `src/webFull/` files still importing from `src/renderer/` after the chain-merge waves landed (worktree-fresh baseline at start of this branch: 89 lines). The biggest single retarget opportunity identified upstream was `src/webFull/components/AutoRunExpandedModal.tsx:124` still importing `AutoRun` + `AutoRunHandle` from `'../../renderer/components/AutoRun'` after the L2.5 AutoRun lift (`ISC-44.lift.autorun_main`) had already shipped the webFull sibling at `src/webFull/components/AutoRun.tsx` and the mount-wave 3 ISC (`ISC-44.wiring.autorun_mounted`) explicitly carried this cross-fork edge as a known follow-up. This wave flips that ONE import path from `'../../renderer/components/AutoRun'` to `'./AutoRun'` (single-line edit, no component-body changes) and counts the result. **Shape-parity verification before the flip.** Both fork AutoRuns export `AutoRun` (memoized forwardRef) and `AutoRunHandle` interface. `AutoRunHandle` is byte-identical across forks (7 methods: focus / switchMode / isDirty / save / revert / openResetTasksModal / getCompletedTaskCount). `AutoRunProps` is a SUPERSET in webFull ŌĆö the webFull lift's strip-and-promote authorization added 3 optional bionify props (`bionifyReadingMode`, `bionifyIntensity`, `bionifyAlgorithm`) on top of the renderer's prop interface; every required + optional prop the renderer surface exposes is also surfaced in webFull, so a caller passing a subset of the renderer's props (which `AutoRunExpandedModal`'s call site does ŌĆö it threads ~13 props plus a `{...autoRunProps}` spread) compiles against either interface. Compile validates: `npm run lint` clean across all 3 tsconfigs after the flip. **Other cross-fork sites scanned for retarget opportunity (the brief's "scan all webFull files" requirement).** Probe ran `grep -rn "from ['\"].*renderer/" src/webFull/` and checked each target against the webFull tree for a sibling at the equivalent path. Results: NONE of the OTHER 88 cross-fork import lines have a flippable webFull-equivalent today. The blockers fall into three categories ŌĆö (a) The targets are in subdirectories that webFull does not mirror yet: `renderer/utils/{theme,search,tabHelpers,formatters,contextUsage,tokenCounter,syntaxTheme,markdownConfig,remarkFileLinks,remarkFrontmatterTable,templateVariables,bionifyReadingMode,clipboard}`, `renderer/types/{index,contextMerge,fileTree}`, `renderer/constants/{shortcuts,keyboardMastery,agentIcons,conductorBadges}`, `renderer/hooks/{ui/useClickOutside,ui/useContextMenuPosition,utils/useThrottle,input/useTemplateAutocomplete,batch/useAutoRunUndo}`, `renderer/components/{ImageDiffViewer,Wizard/ScreenReaderAnnouncement}`, `renderer/assets/{icon-wand,conductor-light,conductor-dark}.png`, `renderer/stores/batchStore`. The webFull tree has NO siblings at any of these locations (verified by `ls`-probe). (b) `src/webFull/hooks/index.ts` exists but does NOT re-export the renderer hook names the components depend on (`useClickOutside`, `useTemplateAutocomplete`, `useAutoRunUndo`); the barrel surfaces 22 webFull-specific hooks (`useWebSocket`, `useSessions`, mobile/voice variants) but not the renderer's UI/input utilities. Flipping `'../../renderer/hooks'` to `'../hooks'` would resolve to undefined exports ŌĆö that's a lift-not-retarget shape. (c) `src/webFull/components/History/historyConstants.tsx` already exists but it is a transitive re-export shim (`export * from '../../../renderer/components/History/historyConstants'`); flipping it requires actually lifting the underlying constants module, which is its own brief. **Outcome: ONE retarget applied, ONE cross-fork edge eliminated.** Cross-fork count: 89 ŌåÆ 88 (-1 line, the AutoRunExpandedModal edge). Files touched: `src/webFull/components/AutoRunExpandedModal.tsx` (1 line ŌĆö import path), `ISA.md` (this ISC + Decisions entry). **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/`, `src/renderer/`, `src/main/`, `src/server/` (all four trees: zero bytes diff vs main per fork-hygiene rules). 0 component bodies modified ŌĆö the retarget is a pure import-path flip per the brief's "ONE line edit, don't modify component bodies" constraint. **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean across all 3 tsconfigs (after `npm run build:prompts` to materialize the generated prompts module in the fresh worktree); `npm run build:webfull` succeeds (mobile bundle ~8.3 MB raw / 3.4 MB gzip ŌĆö unchanged from mount-wave 3's baseline); `npx vitest run src/webFull/` ŌåÆ 568/568 passing across 86 test files (no regressions, identical to mount-wave 3's baseline); cross-fork-count probe (`grep -rn "from ['\"].*renderer/" src/webFull/ | wc -l`) returns 88 (was 89 at worktree-fresh / 87 at audit #13 ŌĆö the +2 drift from audit #13 to worktree-fresh corresponds to the two cross-fork imports added by the audit-#13 NewInstanceModal lift, which strip-and-promotes the renderer's `Session` / `ToolType` / `AgentConfig` types via type-only imports from `'../../renderer/types'`). **Why this is the first deliberate cross-fork drawdown wave.** Prior waves shipped lifts (which create webFull-local copies but don't necessarily flip the import paths in OTHER webFull files that already referenced the renderer copy) and mounts (which wire host JSX but don't touch existing imports). This is the first wave whose explicit job is to retarget existing import edges as a count-down activity. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `chore/cross-fork-drawdown`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("Cross-fork drawdown wave 1 ŌĆö single-line retarget of AutoRunExpandedModal's AutoRun import").
- [ ] ISC-44.ws.process_lifecycle_family: Umbrella ISC tracking the webFull WS process-lifecycle frame family ŌĆö the cross-machine wire surface that lets a webFull host spawn / kill / observe agent processes through the WS protocol instead of the Electron `process:*` IPC namespace. Family members: `create_session` / `create_session_result` (SHIPPED 2026-06-08 via `ISC-44.wiring.new_instance_modal_create_wired`, commit `18fcadacc`; first family member, store-mutation-only, lazy-spawn deferred to first `executeCommand`), `process_spawn` (ClientŌåÆServer, DEFERRED to wave-16), `process_kill` (ClientŌåÆServer, DEFERRED), `process_data` (ServerŌåÆClient, DEFERRED ŌĆö MUST raw-chunk per Electron `process:data` precedent at `src/main/process-listeners/data-listener.ts:114`), `process_exit` (ServerŌåÆClient, DEFERRED), `process_thinking_chunk` (ServerŌåÆClient, OPTIONAL per-agent capability), `process_tool_execution` (ServerŌåÆClient, OPTIONAL per-agent capability). Three contract vectors locked-in by the Decisions entry 2026-06-08 ("umbrella for webFull WS process-lifecycle family contract"): SSH passthrough (every ClientŌåÆServer spawn frame MUST include `sessionSshRemoteConfig`, server-side MUST route through `wrapSpawnWithSsh()`), `onData` raw chunking (no newline framing, no batching), optional capability flags (thinking-chunk + tool-execution emitted only by supporting agents). Naming convention: snake_case for the `type` field, renderer callback registry keys match frame type verbatim (matches `create_session` / `create_session_result` precedent). Pre-port discipline: this umbrella ISC is the CONTRACT that Wizard Phase 3 engine files (`conversationManager`, `phaseGenerator`, `inlineWizardConversation`, `inlineWizardDocumentGeneration`) depend on ŌĆö Phase 3 engine port DOES NOT START until at least `process_spawn` / `process_kill` / `process_data` / `process_exit` ship. Wizard Phase 1 leaves remain independent and can land on the current WS surface. Status: spec'd, not implemented ŌĆö `create_session` shipped, six siblings pending. **Blocks** `ISC-44.wizard.phase_3_engines` (not yet open ŌĆö will be added when the wizard-lift work surfaces). See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("umbrella for webFull WS process-lifecycle family contract").
- [x] ISC-44.server.api_git_cluster: Server-side `/api/git/*` REST route cluster ported from the renderer-side `git:*` IPC namespace at `src/main/preload/git.ts` / `src/main/ipc/handlers/git.ts`. **CLOSED (server-half, local-only) 2026-06-08** on branch `feat/api-git-cluster`. **18 GET routes shipped** (status / diff / is-repo / numstat / branch / branches / tags / remote / info / log / commit-count / show / show-file / worktree-info / repo-root / default-branch / worktrees / scan-worktree-directory). Unblocks WizardResumeModal (`window.maestro.git.isRepo` directory-validity gate) + DirectorySelectionScreen (same gate inside the Wizard's path picker) + the transitive `gitService.*` consumers (getStatus / getDiff / getBranches / getTags / getNumstat / getRemoteBrowserUrl). New server-side `GitManager` at `src/server/git-manager.ts` follows the W3-fs / W3-agents / W3-fonts precedent byte-for-byte: no `electron` import, no `src/main/utils/execFile` import (inline `execFileNoThrow` shim ŌĆö same pattern as `agents-manager.ts` / `wakatime-manager.ts`), no `src/main/utils/logger` (uses `console.*` with `[Git]` prefix), re-uses `src/shared/gitUtils` parsing helpers directly (already in server tsconfig). `GitProvider` interface + `registerGitProvider()` / `getGitProvider()` lazy registry in `apiRoutes.ts`; headless server wires manager + provider in `src/server/index.ts` before `WebServer.start()`. **Defense-in-depth path validation in BOTH layers** (mirrors W3-fs `validateFsPath()` Ōåö `isValidFsPath()`): route layer runs `validateGitCwd()` (absolute, no NUL byte, no `..` segments, no encoded `..`) on every `cwd` / `worktreePath` / `parentPath` query param; `validateGitRefArg()` rejects NUL bytes + over-long strings on `hash` / `ref` / `filePath` / `search` / `file`; `/log` re-parses `limit` as an integer 1..10000. **SSH-remote support deliberately out of scope** ŌĆö sibling open ISC `ISC-44.server.api_git_ssh_support` tracks the wrap-via-`wrapSpawnWithSsh` follow-up. Any `?sshRemoteId=ŌĆ”` query param returns 501 so callers don't silently get a local result when a remote was requested (matches the W3-fs precedent). **Out of scope, tracked as siblings:** `ISC-44.server.api_git_gh_cli` (createPR / checkGhCli / createGist / getDefaultBranch's gh half ŌĆö needs `resolveGhPath()` + `getShellPath()` + Sentry-aware error paths), `ISC-44.server.api_git_worktree_watcher` (watchWorktreeDirectory / unwatchWorktreeDirectory + the worktree-discovered event channel ŌĆö needs a WebSocket frame replacement for renderer `webContents.send`), `ISC-44.server.api_git_worktree_writers` (worktreeSetup / worktreeCheckout / removeWorktree mutators), `ISC-44.server.api_git_show_file_image` (the spawnSync-buffer image branch of showFile ŌĆö text path ported here, image path deferred). **Files added (additive only):** `src/server/git-manager.ts` (~590 LOC ŌĆö GitManager class + reply-shape interfaces + inline execFileNoThrow shim + singleton accessor); `src/__tests__/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutesGit.test.ts` (~430 LOC ŌĆö 32 tests covering route registration, happy-path replies, path validation 400s, SSH-remote 501s, no-provider 503s, provider-throws 500s, getRepoRoot/getDefaultBranch 404 on tagged errors, worktree-info reply shapes). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts` (~700 LOC additive ŌĆö `GitProvider` interface, `registerGitProvider()` / `getGitProvider()` lazy registry, `validateGitCwd()` + `validateGitRefArg()` helpers, 18 route handlers); `src/server/index.ts` (~75 LOC additive ŌĆö `getGitManager()` + `registerGitProvider({ŌĆ”})` wiring). **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö fork-hygiene), `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö read-only oracle), `src/main/ipc/handlers/git.ts` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö renderer-side IPC handler continues to own the git surface inside Electron), `src/main/preload/git.ts` (zero bytes diff vs main). 0 IPC namespaces touched. **Verification:** `npm run build:prompts` + `npm run lint` clean across all 3 tsconfigs; `npx tsc -p tsconfig.server.json` clean; `npx vitest run src/__tests__/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutesGit.test.ts` ŌåÆ 32/32 passing; `npx vitest run src/__tests__/main/web-server/` ŌåÆ 423/423 passing (no regressions on the existing 391 web-server tests).
- [ ] ISC-44.server.api_git_ssh_support: SSH-remote dispatch for the `/api/git/*` cluster. Sibling to `ISC-44.server.api_git_cluster` (CLOSED 2026-06-08). The renderer-side `git:*` IPC handlers wrap every git call via `wrapSpawnWithSsh` from `src/main/utils/ssh-spawn-wrapper.ts` and dispatch to `worktreeInfoRemote` / `worktreeSetupRemote` / `worktreeCheckoutRemote` / `listWorktreesRemote` / `getRepoRootRemote` from `src/main/utils/remote-git.ts` when an `sshRemoteId` is present. The server-side surface is strictly local; SSH-remote git wrapping is significant enough to warrant its own brief because (a) `wrapSpawnWithSsh` lives outside the server tsconfig include set and pulling it in cascades into the `runtime/` + `utils/sentry/` graph, (b) the remote-fs / remote-git helpers maintain their own SSH ControlPath multiplexer that needs lifecycle plumbing, (c) the `?sshRemoteId=` query param semantics need to align with the existing SSH cluster's auth posture (settings-store-backed config lookup). The route layer currently returns 501 on any `?sshRemoteId=` query so callers don't silently get a local result when a remote was requested. Currently DEFERRED.
- [ ] ISC-44.server.api_git_gh_cli: gh CLI integration routes for the `/api/git/*` cluster ŌĆö `createPR` / `checkGhCli` / `createGist` + the gh half of `getDefaultBranch`. Sibling to `ISC-44.server.api_git_cluster` (CLOSED 2026-06-08). Needs `resolveGhPath()` + `getShellPath()` + `getCachedGhStatus()` + Sentry-aware error paths, all of which pull in the runtime / utils graph that the W3-git brief deliberately avoided. Browser-side need is lower priority than the read-side (the CLOSED cluster's `isRepo` / `status` / `log` gate WizardResumeModal + DirectorySelectionScreen which are the immediate user-felt unlocks); the gh routes are needed once the webFull port reaches CreatePRModal / GistPublishModal / CreateWorktreeModal / WorktreeConfigModal. Currently DEFERRED.
- [ ] ISC-44.server.api_git_worktree_watcher: Worktree-discovered event channel for the `/api/git/*` cluster ŌĆö `watchWorktreeDirectory` / `unwatchWorktreeDirectory` + the `worktree:discovered` event fan-out. Sibling to `ISC-44.server.api_git_cluster` (CLOSED 2026-06-08). The renderer-side handler uses `chokidar` to watch a worktree directory and fans out `worktree:discovered` events via `win.webContents.send`. The headless server does not own a renderer window ŌĆö the equivalent in webFull would be a WebSocket frame (`{type: 'worktree_discovered', sessionId, worktree}`). Needs a `LiveSessionManager`-style state map keyed by `sessionId`, frame serialization on the WS protocol surface, and a chokidar lifecycle hook that ties into the server's SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown chain. Currently DEFERRED.
- [ ] ISC-44.server.api_git_worktree_writers: Worktree mutator routes for the `/api/git/*` cluster ŌĆö `worktreeSetup` / `worktreeCheckout` / `removeWorktree`. Sibling to `ISC-44.server.api_git_cluster` (CLOSED 2026-06-08). The CLOSED brief shipped the read-side worktree routes (`worktreeInfo`, `listWorktrees`, `scanWorktreeDirectory`) which are what WizardResumeModal + DirectorySelectionScreen need. The mutators are needed once the webFull port reaches CreateWorktreeModal + the Auto Run worktree-setup flow. Routes will need the W3-fs `POST` rate-limit budget (matches the W3-fs writers' mutator-write budget) and the same defense-in-depth path validation. Currently DEFERRED.
- [ ] ISC-44.server.api_git_show_file_image: Image-aware `showFile` route for the `/api/git/*` cluster. Sibling to `ISC-44.server.api_git_cluster` (CLOSED 2026-06-08). The renderer-side `git:showFile` IPC has a dual contract ŌĆö text content for non-image extensions, `data:image/<ext>;base64,ŌĆ”` URL for image extensions ŌĆö and uses `spawnSync('git', ['show', `${ref}:${filePath}`], {encoding: 'buffer'})` to capture raw binary content. The CLOSED brief ported the text-side fallback; the image branch (consumed by `ImageDiffViewer`) needs a wire-shape decision (bare-text body matching `/api/fs/read-image` precedent vs JSON envelope) and the W3-fs read-image extension allowlist applied. Currently DEFERRED.
- [x] ISC-44.ws.process_lifecycle_family: Umbrella ISC tracking the webFull WS process-lifecycle frame family ŌĆö the cross-machine wire surface that lets a webFull host spawn / kill / observe agent processes through the WS protocol instead of the Electron `process:*` IPC namespace. Family members: `create_session` / `create_session_result` (SHIPPED 2026-06-08 via `ISC-44.wiring.new_instance_modal_create_wired`, commit `18fcadacc`; first family member, store-mutation-only, lazy-spawn deferred to first `executeCommand`), `process_spawn` (ClientŌåÆServer, SHIPPED 2026-06-08 by this ISC), `process_kill` (ClientŌåÆServer, SHIPPED), `process_data` (ServerŌåÆClient, SHIPPED ŌĆö raw chunking per contract vector 2), `process_exit` (ServerŌåÆClient, SHIPPED), `process_thinking_chunk` (ServerŌåÆClient, SHIPPED, OPTIONAL per-agent capability), `process_tool_execution` (ServerŌåÆClient, SHIPPED, OPTIONAL per-agent capability). Three contract vectors enforced (per Decisions 2026-06-08 "umbrella for webFull WS process-lifecycle family contract"): SSH passthrough ŌĆö every ClientŌåÆServer spawn frame includes `sessionSshRemoteConfig`, server-side handler routes through `wrapSpawnWithSsh()` from `src/main/utils/ssh-spawn-wrapper.ts` BEFORE `ProcessManager.spawn()` (verified at `src/server/index.ts` where `setProcessSpawnCallback` invokes the wrapper before forwarding to `processManagerAdapter.processManager.spawn`); `onData` raw chunking ŌĆö `process_data` ServerŌåÆClient frames carry one PTY chunk per frame, no batching, no newline framing (verified at `src/server/index.ts` where the `processManager.on('data', ...)` listener calls `server.broadcastProcessData(sessionId, data, 'stdout')` per-emit); optional capability flags ŌĆö `process_thinking_chunk` + `process_tool_execution` listeners are always registered against the shared `ProcessManager` but only emit when the underlying agent surfaces those events (Claude Code emits thinking-chunk; OpenCode / Codex emit tool-execution). Snake_case for frame type field. **Server-side wiring** lives in 5 files: `src/main/web-server/handlers/messageHandlers.ts` adds `ProcessSpawnMessageRequest` interface + `ProcessSpawnCallback` / `ProcessKillCallback` types + `processSpawn` / `processKill` fields on `MessageHandlerCallbacks` + `case 'process_spawn'` / `case 'process_kill'` in the dispatch switch + sibling `handleProcessSpawn` / `handleProcessKill` private methods that validate required fields and forward optional fields verbatim; `src/main/web-server/types.ts` adds `ProcessSpawnRequest` interface + `ProcessSpawnCallback` / `ProcessKillCallback` types; `src/main/web-server/managers/CallbackRegistry.ts` adds `processSpawn` / `processKill` registry fields + `setProcessSpawnCallback` / `setProcessKillCallback` setters + `processSpawn(request)` / `processKill(sessionId)` async invokers; `src/main/web-server/WebServer.ts` adds 2 setter delegations + 4 broadcast delegations (`broadcastProcessData`, `broadcastProcessExit`, `broadcastProcessThinkingChunk`, `broadcastProcessToolExecution`) + 2 callback wirings inside `setupMessageHandlerCallbacks`; `src/main/web-server/services/broadcastService.ts` adds the 4 broadcast methods (each builds the corresponding frame and fans out via `broadcastToAll` ŌĆö mirrors the existing `session_output` posture for cross-session multiplexing). **Server-side process-manager binding** lives in `src/server/index.ts` and reuses the SAME `processManagerAdapter.processManager` singleton the L0b write callbacks + REST `/api/processes` view share, so all three surfaces see the identical Map: `setProcessSpawnCallback` builds the SSH adapter against `settingsStore` via `createSshRemoteStoreAdapter` (the FileStore generic `get<V>(key, defaultValue)` overload satisfies the adapter's constraint), routes through `wrapSpawnWithSsh()` to honor the SSH passthrough contract, then calls `processManagerAdapter.processManager.spawn({...wrapped})`; `setProcessKillCallback` proxies through to `processManager.kill(sessionId)`; 5 listeners (`data`, `stderr`, `exit`, `thinking-chunk`, `tool-execution`) are wired ONCE against the shared `ProcessManager` and forward each event verbatim to the matching broadcast helper. **Client-side wrapper** lives at `src/webFull/services/processLifecycle.webfull.ts` (NEW, ~330 LOC) ŌĆö `createProcessLifecycleClient(send)` factory returns a client with `spawn(config)` (returns a promise that resolves on `process_spawn_result`), `kill(sessionId)` (resolves on `process_kill_result`), per-sessionId `onData` / `onExit` / `onThinkingChunk` / `onToolExecution` subscribers (Sets keyed by sessionId for cheap multiplexing), `handleFrame(frame)` (App.tsx wires this into `onMessage`; returns true on known process-lifecycle types so the caller can chain other dispatchers), and `dispose()` (clears subscribers + rejects pending round-trips). Signatures mirror `src/main/preload/process.ts:25-243` byte-for-byte so future rendererŌåÆwebFull lifts stay copy-paste compatible ŌĆö the `ToolExecutionEvent` shape is inlined (NOT imported from preload) because preload pulls `electron` which webFull bundles cannot resolve. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `feat/ws-process-lifecycle-family`. Closes the keystone for Wizard Phase 3 (the engine layer can now port to webFull on a stable wire surface), the Wizard glue layer, AND AutoRun batch-spawn end-to-end. **Sibling ISCs opened by this work:** `ISC-44.webfull.process_lifecycle_wrapper` (the client-side service ŌĆö CLOSED in the same commit; tracked separately so future webFull-side hardening work has a stable handle). See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("WS process-lifecycle family ŌĆö implementation").
- [x] ISC-44.webfull.process_lifecycle_wrapper: Sibling sub-ISC under `ISC-44.ws.process_lifecycle_family` (umbrella above). Ships `src/webFull/services/processLifecycle.webfull.ts` ŌĆö the client-side wrapper around the 6 WS frames so future webFull callers (Wizard Phase 3 `conversationManager`, `phaseGenerator`, `inlineWizardConversation`, `inlineWizardDocumentGeneration`; AutoRun batch-spawn; group-chat participant stdout reader) have a single typed handle that mirrors `window.maestro.process.*` byte-for-byte. **Public surface:** `spawn(config)` / `kill(sessionId)` (await round-trip results); per-sessionId `onData` / `onExit` / `onThinkingChunk` / `onToolExecution` (Set-backed listener registries with synchronous unsubscribe ŌĆö matches `ipcRenderer.removeListener` semantics from preload); `handleFrame(frame)` (App.tsx-side dispatch ŌĆö returns `true` when the frame matched a known process-lifecycle type so the host can chain other dispatchers like the existing `useWebSocket` typed-message handlers); `dispose()` (idempotent cleanup that rejects pending spawn/kill promises with "disposed" message so consumers can fail-fast on shutdown). **15 unit tests** at `src/webFull/services/processLifecycle.webfull.test.ts` covering: (a) spawn round-trip + SSH passthrough verbatim + WS-send-fail rejection + superseded-spawn rejection; (b) kill round-trip + false-on-server-fail; (c) onData per-chunk synchronous dispatch + per-sessionId filter + unsubscribe stops delivery; (d) onExit with code + signal; (e) onThinkingChunk + onToolExecution dispatch + tolerance for "never fires" (contract vector 3); (f) handleFrame returns true on known types + false on unknown so the caller can chain. **CLOSED 2026-06-08** in the same commit as the umbrella. See `ISC-44.ws.process_lifecycle_family` above.
- [x] ISC-44.shim.new_instance_modal_lifted: `NewInstanceModal` + companion `EditAgentModal` lifted from `src/renderer/components/NewInstanceModal.tsx` (1822 LOC, the "create a new agent" entry point + per-session edit surface ŌĆö the biggest single-modal user-felt unlock remaining per the leaf-parade brief 2026-06-08) to `src/webFull/components/NewInstanceModal.tsx` (NEW). Lands on top of all 5 IPC-shim Decision route clusters (`ISC-44.shim.fs_routes` Ō£ō, `agents_routes` Ō£ō, `w3_marketplace_routes` Ō£ō, autorun-via-FsProvider.writeDoc Ō£ō, `ssh_remotes_routes` Ō£ō) all CLOSED server-side on origin/main `2e410f9d6`. **Audit count holds: 18 IPC sites = 18 (no transitive-hook surprise).** The MarketplaceModal precedent flagged audit-correction risk for transitive hook consumers (`useMarketplace` ballooned 5 modal callsites into 12). NewInstanceModal differs by shape: ALL IPC fan-out is inline in the modal source ŌĆö no batch hooks, no transitive `useFoo()` consumers. Pre-flight grep returned 18 sites matching the audit. Both sub-components (`AgentConfigPanel` 696 LOC, `SshRemoteSelector` 174 LOC) verified pure presentational pre-lift and lifted verbatim to `src/webFull/components/shared/`. **IPC-site mapping (18 sites): 7 routes + 9 props + 1 window.open swap + 1 deferred strip-and-promote.** Routes: `fs.homeDir()` ŌåÆ `GET /api/fs/home-dir`; `agents.detect()` (NewInstance:240 + Edit:1270) ŌåÆ `GET /api/agents/detected`; `agents.refresh(id)` (NewInstance:381 + Edit:1495) ŌåÆ `GET /api/agents/detect/:agentId`; `sshRemote.getConfigs()` (NewInstance:602 + Edit:1312) ŌåÆ `GET /api/ssh-remotes`.
