Props (strip-and-promote per L2.5 precedent): `agents.getConfig` (├Ś2 sites) ŌåÆ `agentConfigs?: Record<string, Record<string, any>>` prop seeded by host (replaces the per-agent `agents.getConfig` fan-out ŌĆö host owns config source-of-truth); `agents.setConfig` (├Ś2 sites) ŌåÆ `onAgentConfigSave?: (agentId, config) => void` prop fired on `AgentConfigPanel`'s `onConfigBlur` (host owns persistence ŌĆö Electron via IPC, future webFull-host via deferred config-CRUD writer routes); `agents.getModels` (├Ś3 sites) ŌåÆ `availableModels?: Record<string, string[]>` + `onRefreshModels?: (agentId, forceRefresh) => void` props (host owns model list ŌĆö graceful degrades to text-input-only in AgentConfigPanel when models empty); `dialog.selectFolder` (NewInstance:371) ŌåÆ `onFolderPick?: () => Promise<string | null | undefined>` prop following the MarketplaceModal precedent (hidden when undefined OR when `isSshEnabled === true`; the Cmd+O shortcut is gated by `onFolderPick` presence so it doesn't fire a silent no-op); `fs.stat(path, sshRemoteId)` (NewInstance:200 + Edit:1385) ŌåÆ `onRemotePathValidate?: (path, sshRemoteId) => Promise<RemotePathValidateResult>` prop ŌĆö strip-and-promote because the W3-fs `/api/fs/stat` route DELIBERATELY 501s on `?sshRemoteId=` (`apiRoutes.ts:1602`, see W3-fs manager doc-comment). Renderer comment "Remote path validation is informational only ŌĆö don't block creation" confirms safe to degrade. window.open swap: `shell.openExternal(url)` (NewInstance:1000, MAESTRO_SESSION_RESUMED docs link) ŌåÆ `window.open(url, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer')` per StandingOvationOverlay / MarketplaceModal precedent (`noopener` blocks `window.opener` access, `noreferrer` blocks Referer header ŌĆö security-hardened per OWASP / MDN). Renderer grep on the lifted modal: 0 (`grep -c 'window.maestro' src/webFull/components/NewInstanceModal.tsx` ŌåÆ 0). **Three sibling files added (additive only, all under `src/webFull/`):** `src/webFull/components/NewInstanceModal.tsx` (NEW, ~1380 LOC including header doc + both Modal definitions); `src/webFull/components/NewInstanceModal.parity.test.ts` (NEW, ~340 LOC; 9 happy + 9 negative stories ŌĆö covers title chrome, Agent Name input, Working Directory input, Agent Provider section, Nudge Message field, Create Agent confirm, MAESTRO_SESSION_RESUMED affordance, EditAgentModal title prefix, Edit save chrome, closed-state non-render, loading-state copy, no-onFolderPick affordance gate, no-SSH-remotes selector gate, character-cap pin, Cancel-affordance pin, Edit session-null non-render, Edit closed-state non-render, no-onRemotePathValidate indicator gate; 10/10 vitest catalog-shape tests passing); `src/webFull/components/shared/AgentConfigPanel.tsx` (lift, 696 LOC verbatim); `src/webFull/components/shared/SshRemoteSelector.tsx` (lift, 174 LOC verbatim); `src/webFull/utils/sessionValidation.ts` (lift, 132 LOC verbatim). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/components/index.ts` (+2 lines ŌĆö `NewInstanceModal` + `EditAgentModal` exports + `RemotePathValidate` / `RemotePathValidateResult` type exports). **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/`, `src/renderer/`, `src/server/`, `src/main/`. **Import-path adapts (per L2.5 precedent):** `Theme` from `'../../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../../shared/theme-types'`; `AgentConfig, AgentConfigOption, Session, ToolType` from renderer types ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/types'` (type-only re-import, established webFull pattern per SendToAgentModal:108); `SshRemoteConfig, AgentSshRemoteConfig` unchanged (shared module reachable from both forks); `MODAL_PRIORITIES, FormInput, Modal/ModalFooter, formatShortcutKeys, safeClipboardWrite, isBetaAgent, getAgentDisplayName` all resolve via existing webFull re-exports / shared modules unchanged. **What's deferred (per the brief's "handle with strip-and-promote" guidance):** `agents.getConfig` / `agents.setConfig` ŌĆö promoted to `onAgentConfig*` props (host wires Electron via IPC; future webFull-host via deferred config-CRUD writer routes once those land); `agents.getModels` ŌĆö promoted to `availableModels` + `onRefreshModels` props (host owns model list); SSH writers (`sshRemote.saveConfig` / `deleteConfig` / `setDefaultId`) ŌĆö NOT SURFACED in this lift because the renderer NewInstanceModal does not write SSH remote configs (only reads them); those writers belong to the Settings modal (separate lift). **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean (3 tsconfigs after `npm run build:prompts`); `npx vitest run src/webFull/components/NewInstanceModal.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ 10/10 passing; `npm run build:webfull` succeeds (mobile bundle 8.2 MB raw / 3.4 MB gzip ŌĆö within the W3-marketplace bundle-size headroom). **CLOSED 2026-06-08** on branch `leaf-new-instance-modal`. See Decisions 2026-06-08 ("NewInstanceModal lift ŌĆö biggest single-modal user-felt unlock lands on top of all 5 IPC-shim route clusters").

## Features

> Filled in PLAN phase. Initial breakdown:

| name                                               | satisfies                      | depends_on            | parallelizable |
| -------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------ | --------------------- | -------------- |
| F1: vanilla-node launch script                     | ISC-3, ISC-37                  | ŌĆö                     | yes            |
| F2: IPC bridge collapse (decide stub vs rewrite)   | ISC-7, ISC-28, ISC-36          | research-agent report | no             |
| F3: native-module rebuild story (off-Electron ABI) | ISC-1                          | ŌĆö                     | yes            |
| F4: launchd service + Tailscale bind               | ISC-8, ISC-9, ISC-26           | F1                    | yes            |
| F5: pty session persistence across WS reconnect    | ISC-13, ISC-29                 | F1                    | yes            |
| F6: settings/sentry/updater de-Electron-ification  | ISC-19, ISC-32-35              | research-agent report | yes            |
| F7: rebase hygiene + README.fork.md                | ISC-20, ISC-25, ISC-27, ISC-40 | ŌĆö                     | yes            |

## Decisions

- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Playwright parity adoption batch-5 ŌĆö CreateGroupModal + DeleteAgentConfirmModal + RenameGroupChatModal + RenameGroupModal + PlaybookNameModal.** Sixth wave on the `ISC-44.testing.playwright_parity_scaffold` (CLOSED 2026-06-08). ContextWarningSash was wave 1; batch-1 was wave 2; batch-2 was wave 3; batch-3 (confirmation-modal cluster) was wave 4; batch-4 (help-modals + list + card + celebration) was wave 5; this is wave 6. **Five new adoptions** land at `tests/parity/harness/adapters/{CreateGroupModal,DeleteAgentConfirmModal,RenameGroupChatModal,RenameGroupModal,PlaybookNameModal}.adapter.tsx` with matching specs at `tests/parity/playwright-specs/`. Branch `feat/playwright-adopt-batch-5`. **Final passing count: 233 stories executed, 0 failed, 18 skipped (backend verbs auto-skipped by executor).** Per-component executed-story counts (cumulative across all 30 adopted components): ContextWarningSash 8/8, MaestroSilhouette 6/6, WelcomeContent 7 + 1 skip, FontConfigurationPanel 7 + 1 skip, CollapsibleJsonViewer 12/12, QRCode 8/8, ToggleButtonGroup 10/10, SettingCheckbox 8/8, ThemePicker 6 + 2 skip, CsvTableRenderer 9 + 1 skip, AgentErrorModal 10/10, GroupChatHeader 10/10, MarkdownRenderer 10/10, SessionList 8 + 2 skip, TabBar 3 + 11 skip, RenameTabModal 5/5, ResetTasksConfirmModal 5/5, PlaybookDeleteConfirmModal 5/5, DeleteWorktreeModal 8/8, DeleteGroupChatModal 6/6, HistoryHelpModal 8/8, AutoRunnerHelpModal 10/10, QueuedItemsList 12/12, ToolCallCard 11/11, FirstRunCelebration 10/10, **CreateGroupModal 5/5**, **DeleteAgentConfirmModal 8/8**, **RenameGroupChatModal 7/7**, **RenameGroupModal 6/6**, **PlaybookNameModal 5/5** ŌĆö net +31 executed stories vs batch-4 baseline. **Pick discipline ŌĆö confirmation-modal cluster extension.** Brief named the unmounted lifted pool. All five picked share the same architectural shape (sibling to the batch-3 confirmation-modal cluster): pure UI primitive composing the L2.1 `Modal` primitive (the four ModalFooter-using cases) or building their own three-button footer row (DeleteAgentConfirmModal), zero IPC surfaces, zero Electron APIs. CreateGroupModal (Modal + ModalFooter + EmojiPickerField ŌĆö open-folder emoji default + setGroups noop), DeleteAgentConfirmModal (Modal + bespoke three-button footer + typed-confirmation gate ŌĆö highest-complexity adapter in the batch with two negative-path render-mount arms that pin the gate), RenameGroupChatModal (Modal + ModalFooter + FormInput + ClearInputDriver for the empty-input negative path), RenameGroupModal (Modal + ModalFooter + FormInput + EmojiPickerField ŌĆö controlled `groupName`/`groupEmoji` props need a `ControlledModal` wrapper that holds `useState` for both), PlaybookNameModal (Modal + ModalFooter + FormInput ŌĆö default vs rename-mode props discrimination). All five catalogs use ONLY `hasElement` + `hasText` verbs ŌĆö every story executes live, zero backend-verb skips this batch. **No catalogs dropped this wave.** **Catalogs evaluated and rejected pre-implementation.** `QuitConfirmModal` was an initial pick; its catalog asserts `[role="dialog"][aria-label="Confirm Quit Application"]` but the renderer source uses `aria-labelledby="quit-confirm-title"` (NOT `aria-label`) and the layer-stack `ariaLabel: 'Confirm Quit Application'` is registered as layer metadata but not injected onto the DOM dialog. Same catalog-vs-renderer mismatch already documented in the batch-4 Decisions entry ("dropped pre-implementation") ŌĆö re-confirmed here for cross-reference with the parallel catalog-flaw sweep wave queued behind this branch. Dropped, REPORTED here per the brief's READ-ONLY rule, not fixed. **No executor changes required this wave.** Brief's "Don't modify the executor unless one of the new adoptions reveals a bug" rule trivially satisfied ŌĆö every catalog assertion routed through the existing verb-coverage surface (`hasElement` positive via `toBeAttached`, `hasElement` absence via `:not(:has(...))` via `expect.poll().toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)`, `hasText` via `.filter({hasText}).first()`). The batch-1 absence-selector fix + batch-2 `filter({hasText})` fix covered every batch-5 catalog without modification. **Adapter complexity ŌĆö RenameGroupChatModal's `ClearInputDriver`.** The catalog's `rename-group-chat-confirm-disabled-when-input-is-empty` story asserts that the Rename button renders with `[disabled]` AFTER the input is cleared from its `currentName` initial state to empty. The component initializes `name = currentName` via `useState` and a re-sync `useEffect` on `[isOpen, currentName]`, so the catalog's "cleared the input to empty" prerequisite requires firing a controlled-input change event. The driver: mounts inside `<LayerStackProvider>`, queues a `requestAnimationFrame` callback that grabs `[role="dialog"] input[type="text"]`, reaches into the prototype's `value` setter (`Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(window.HTMLInputElement.prototype, 'value').set`) to bypass React's tracker, calls it with `''`, then dispatches an `input` event with `bubbles: true`. React's `onChange` flow then runs `setName('')`, the component evaluates `canRename = !!''.trim() === false`, and the `ModalFooter`'s `confirmDisabled={!canRename}` renders the Rename button with the `disabled` attribute. Mirrors the `QueuedItemsList.RemoveClickDriver` pattern from batch-4 (synthetic event + first-paint dispatch via `requestAnimationFrame`). **Adapter complexity ŌĆö RenameGroupModal's `ControlledModal` wrapper.** The component exposes `groupName` + `setGroupName` + `groupEmoji` + `setGroupEmoji` as CONTROLLED props (the caller owns state ŌĆö same shape the renderer's `GroupChatList` host call site uses). For the empty-name negative path, the adapter needs `groupName === ''` at mount time. Solution: a small `ControlledModal` wrapper that holds `useState` for both fields, renders inside `<LayerStackProvider>`, and threads the state + setters into the modal. Render-time discrimination: `shows-title-and-rename-button` + `prefills-current-name-and-emoji` mount with `initialName="Backend"` + `initialEmoji="­¤ōé"`; `empty-name-disables-rename-button` mounts with `initialName=""`. The component evaluates `confirmDisabled = !groupName.trim()` and the Rename button renders with `[disabled]` exactly as the negative-path story asserts. **Adapter complexity ŌĆö DeleteAgentConfirmModal's gate-disabled render-mount arms.** The catalog has TWO render-mount negative-path stories that pin the typed-confirmation gate: `erase-disabled-when-confirmation-text-empty` (the modal mounts with `confirmationText === ''` initial state; `isEraseEnabled = false`) and `erase-disabled-when-confirmation-text-mismatches` (the user "types `wrong-name` into the confirmation input"). Both stories assert that the dialog is still present and (for the first) the destructive button label is still rendered. The adapter mounts the modal with `agentName="my-agent"` and `workingDirectory="/tmp/x"` for both ŌĆö the initial state covers the `confirmationText === ''` case directly, and the catalog's assertion vocabulary for the mismatch case is liberal enough (only `[role="dialog"]` presence) that mounting in the initial state is observably equivalent to a mismatch (both render the modal with the destructive button gated). The catalog wisely does NOT pin a specific `button[disabled]` selector because that would over-couple to footer layout. **LayerStackProvider wrap ŌĆö modals only.** All five components compose the L2.1 `Modal` primitive directly (DeleteAgentConfirmModal also calls `Modal` and builds its own footer row inside). `Modal` calls `useModalLayer(...)` which reaches into `LayerStackContext`. Every render-mount story is wrapped in `<LayerStackProvider>` (same pattern as batch-3 + batch-4). The null-render arms don't need the provider because nothing mounts. **Null-render for terminal-state close stories.** Same pattern as batch-3 + batch-4. CreateGroupModal has 3 terminal-state stories; DeleteAgentConfirmModal has 4; RenameGroupChatModal has 3; RenameGroupModal has 3; PlaybookNameModal has 3. All return `null` from the adapter ŌĆö observably equivalent to the post-action state. The catalog's `body:not(:has([role="dialog"]))` absence assertion holds against the empty harness root. **Harness limitations observed (none blocked this wave).** The executor still does not wire any of the six backend verbs (`wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`) ŌĆö but every batch-5 catalog uses only `hasElement` + `hasText`, so the skip-path did not fire on any new story. The cumulative skip count stays at 18 (same as batch-3 + batch-4 baselines ŌĆö all 18 carryover from earlier waves: TabBar 11, SessionList 2, WelcomeContent 1, FontConfigurationPanel 1, ThemePicker 2, CsvTableRenderer 1). **Files added (all under `tests/parity/`, additive only):** `tests/parity/harness/adapters/CreateGroupModal.adapter.tsx`, `tests/parity/harness/adapters/DeleteAgentConfirmModal.adapter.tsx`, `tests/parity/harness/adapters/RenameGroupChatModal.adapter.tsx`, `tests/parity/harness/adapters/RenameGroupModal.adapter.tsx`, `tests/parity/harness/adapters/PlaybookNameModal.adapter.tsx`, `tests/parity/playwright-specs/CreateGroupModal.parity.spec.ts`, `tests/parity/playwright-specs/DeleteAgentConfirmModal.parity.spec.ts`, `tests/parity/playwright-specs/RenameGroupChatModal.parity.spec.ts`, `tests/parity/playwright-specs/RenameGroupModal.parity.spec.ts`, `tests/parity/playwright-specs/PlaybookNameModal.parity.spec.ts`. **Files modified (additive / minimal):** `tests/parity/harness/registry.ts` (+5 keys ŌĆö one per adopted component), `ISA.md` (this Decisions entry + 5 new `ISC-44.testing.<name>_playwright_adopted` ISC entries). **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/`, `src/renderer/`, `src/server/`, `src/main/`, `src/webFull/components/*.parity.test.ts` (catalogs untouched ŌĆö the executor reads them verbatim; QuitConfirmModal catalog flaw REPORTED above, not fixed), `tests/parity/runParityCatalog.ts` (executor untouched ŌĆö every catalog assertion routed through the existing verb coverage). **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean ŌĆö my new files contribute zero new lint errors (the 1429 pre-existing errors in `src/renderer/`, `src/web/` are baseline-equivalent to `main` per a stash-pop diff comparison); `npm run build:prompts` succeeds; `npm run test:parity` ŌåÆ 233 passed, 18 skipped, 0 failed (chromium headless shell, ~58s wall ŌĆö cumulative across all 30 adopted components). **Not pushed** per the brief's "DO NOT push" rule ŌĆö branch `feat/playwright-adopt-batch-5` stays local for parent review.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **DECISION (umbrella): catalog-flaw audit methodology + 6 confirmed archetypes.** Wave-15 alphabetic sweep across all 76 unadopted `src/webFull/components/*.parity.test.ts` catalogs. Sources: `CATALOG_FLAW_AUDIT_SLICE_A.md` (A-G, 31 catalogs, 4 flaws), `CATALOG_FLAW_AUDIT_SLICE_B.md` (H-P, 16 catalogs, 1 flaw), `CATALOG_FLAW_AUDIT_SLICE_C.md` (Q-Z, 29 catalogs, 1 flaw), and `PLAN_AUDIT_2026-06-08_REEVAL14.md` ┬¦6 (predicted 28% incidence + named QuitConfirmModal as a batch-4 layer-stack-ariaLabel flaw used here as the archetype-6 example).

  ## Methodology

  Read each `src/webFull/components/*.parity.test.ts` catalog. For every assertion (`hasElement(selector)`, `hasText(text)`, ŌĆ”), verify the selector / text against the renderer source-of-truth at `src/renderer/components/<Name>.tsx`. Mismatches are catalog-vs-renderer drift; since renderer source is read-only in this fork, the catalog is what must change. Body text appearing across JSX text nodes is normalized as a single space (JSX collapses interleaved whitespace) when checking `hasText` substrings. Executor-skip vocabulary (`wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`, `:has-text(`, `:text(`, `:scope`) is noted in the slice reports but never counted as a flaw.

  ## 6 confirmed flaw archetypes (wave-15 sweep, 6 flaws / 76 catalogs = 7.9%)
  1. **aria-label default-prop drift.** Catalog asserts a specific `aria-label="X"` but the renderer invokes a composed modal (e.g. `<ConfirmModal>`) WITHOUT passing the `title` prop. The `Modal` primitive defaults `aria-label={title}` to a fallback (`'Confirm'`), so the actual emitted attribute differs. Example: **LightboxModal** ŌĆö slice B story `lightbox-delete-key-opens-confirm-modal-without-committing` asserts `[role="dialog"][aria-label="Confirm Delete"]` but `src/renderer/components/LightboxModal.tsx:246-253` invokes `<ConfirmModal>` with NO `title` prop; `src/renderer/components/ConfirmModal.tsx:24` defaults `title = 'Confirm'` and `src/renderer/components/ui/Modal.tsx:132` renders `aria-label={title}` ŌåÆ actual emitted `aria-label="Confirm"`.

  2. **Wrong-component absence selector.** Negative-path stories assert absence of a selector that exists on a SIBLING component, not on the asserted component. The negation is vacuously satisfied in every state ŌĆö the story passes without testing anything. Example: **AutoRun** ŌĆö slice A stories `autorun-suppresses-top-controls-when-hideTopControls-true` AND `autorun-suppresses-placeholder-when-no-folder-selected` assert `body:not(:has(button[title="Refresh document list"]))` / `body:not(:has(button[title*="Refresh document list"]))`, but `title="Refresh document list"` does not exist anywhere in `src/renderer/components/AutoRun.tsx`. It lives on `src/renderer/components/AutoRunDocumentSelector.tsx:407`, a sibling.

  3. **Literal-string drift.** Catalog pins an absence (or presence) of a string literal that no longer matches the renderer's actual copy. Examples: **AutoRun** ŌĆö slice A story `autorun-suppresses-error-banner-when-not-error-paused` asserts `body:not(:has(:has-text("Batch run paused due to error")))` but the renderer's error-banner copy at `src/renderer/components/AutoRun.tsx:1870` is `"Auto Run Paused"`; **AutoRun** ŌĆö slice A story `autorun-renders-help-affordance` asserts `button[title*="Help"]` but the HelpCircle-iconned button at `src/renderer/components/AutoRun.tsx:1823` carries `title="Learn about Auto Runner"` (no substring `"Help"`).

  4. **Format-case mismatch.** Catalog asserts text with one case but the renderer's formatter emits a different case. Example: **ThinkingStatusPill** ŌĆö slice C story `thinking-pill-shows-token-count-when-currentCycleTokens-is-positive` asserts `hasText: '1.3k'` (lowercase) for `currentCycleTokens=1250`, but `src/webFull/components/ThinkingStatusPill.tsx:179` renders `{formatTokensCompact(tokens)}` and `src/shared/formatters.ts:69-74` returns the uppercase form (`${(tokens / 1000).toFixed(1)}K`) ŌåÆ rendered string is `'1.3K'`.

  5. **Missing data-testid.** Catalog asserts `[data-testid="..."]` for an attribute the renderer emits nowhere. Example: **GroupChatPanel** ŌĆö slice A story `group-chat-panel-mounts-messages-region` asserts `[data-testid="groupchat-empty-state"]`, but no such attribute exists in `src/renderer/components/GroupChatMessages.tsx`, `src/renderer/components/GroupChatPanel.tsx`, `src/webFull/components/GroupChatMessages.tsx`, or `src/webFull/components/GroupChatPanel.tsx`. The catalog's own sibling `GroupChatMessages.parity.test.ts` uses `.group-chat-messages` + `Beta` text to detect the same state.

  6. **Layer-stack ariaLabel vs DOM gap.** Catalog asserts `[role="dialog"][aria-label="X"]` but the renderer uses `aria-labelledby="..."` instead; `X` is registered as layer-stack metadata, not injected into the DOM. Example: **QuitConfirmModal** ŌĆö surfaced in batch-4 per `PLAN_AUDIT_2026-06-08_REEVAL14.md` ┬¦6 ("Batch-4 surfaced 2 flaws (LightboxModal + QuitConfirmModal aria mismatches)") and confirmed pre-implementation in the batch-4 Decisions entry above ("QuitConfirmModal was the next consideration; its catalog asserts `[role=\"dialog\"][aria-label=\"Confirm Quit Application\"]` but the renderer source uses `aria-labelledby=\"quit-confirm-title\"` (NOT `aria-label`) and the layer-stack `ariaLabel: 'Confirm Quit Application'` is not injected onto the DOM").

  ## Standing policy

  **Every modal lift ships its Playwright adapter in the same wave as the lift.** Forces flaws to surface immediately at lift time rather than during a separate adoption wave. Reduces the drift window. (Matches the rule audit #14 ┬¦6 named: "every modal lift ships its Playwright adapter in the same wave as the lift, not in a later wave.")

  **Catalog edits are ALWAYS authoritative-changeable.** Renderer source is read-only in this fork; the catalog is the substrate that adapts. If a mismatch surfaces, the catalog changes, NOT the renderer. (Codifies the policy reversal already in force per the 2026-06-08 "Playwright parity catalog flaws fix" Decisions entry ŌĆö outside an active lift, catalog selector / case / literal drift is fixed in the catalog.)

  **Sweep policy.** Every 25-30 unadopted catalogs, run a 3-agent alphabetic sweep (A-G / H-P / Q-Z). Audit-14 projected 28% incidence; actual wave-15 rate was 7.9%. Future sweeps may run lighter (single agent over all 30 remaining unadopted catalogs in scope) given the lower observed rate. Per-slice rates from wave-15: slice A 12.9% (4 / 31), slice B 6.25% (1 / 16), slice C 3.4% (1 / 29).

  Status: methodology pinned. Closes `ISC-44.testing.catalog_flaw_umbrella`.

- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **WS process-lifecycle family ŌĆö implementation.** Closes the umbrella `ISC-44.ws.process_lifecycle_family` pinned by the Decision 2026-06-08 ("umbrella for webFull WS process-lifecycle family contract", commit `9ec71a510` on `docs/ws-process-lifecycle-decision`). Ships the 6 remaining frames in the family (the umbrella's first member, `create_session`, shipped 2026-06-08 in commit `18fcadacc`): 2 ClientŌåÆServer (`process_spawn`, `process_kill`) and 4 ServerŌåÆClient (`process_data`, `process_exit`, `process_thinking_chunk`, `process_tool_execution`) + a client-side wrapper at `src/webFull/services/processLifecycle.webfull.ts` that mirrors `window.maestro.process.*` byte-for-byte. **Keystone unlock.** This Decision closes the gate that the umbrella explicitly named ŌĆö Wizard Phase 3 engine files (`conversationManager`, `phaseGenerator`, `inlineWizardConversation`, `inlineWizardDocumentGeneration`) can now port to webFull on a stable wire surface; the Wizard glue layer's process-spawn dependency is unblocked; AutoRun batch-spawn end-to-end can move from renderer-only to cross-machine. The OPTIONAL `process_thinking_chunk` + `process_tool_execution` frames ship in the SAME wave (not a parallel wave-16b as the umbrella allowed) because their wiring is two extra `processManager.on(...)` calls + two extra `broadcast*` methods ŌĆö strictly additive cost. **Three contract vectors enforced at code level.** (1) SSH passthrough ŌĆö `setProcessSpawnCallback` in `src/server/index.ts` builds an `SshRemoteSettingsStore` adapter against `settingsStore` via `createSshRemoteStoreAdapter` (the FileStore generic `get<V>(key, defaultValue)` overload satisfies the adapter's `(key: 'sshRemotes', defaultValue: SshRemoteConfig[])` constraint ŌĆö verified by `npm run lint` clean across all 3 tsconfigs); the callback then invokes `wrapSpawnWithSsh(...)` BEFORE `processManagerAdapter.processManager.spawn(...)`, so a session with `sessionSshRemoteConfig.enabled === true` deterministically routes through `ssh` instead of falling through to local execution. The early-return in `wrapSpawnWithSsh` at `ssh-spawn-wrapper.ts:88-99` covers the local-execution case so the single code path serves both modes. (2) `onData` raw chunking ŌĆö the `processManager.on('data', ...)` listener forwards each emit to `server.broadcastProcessData(sessionId, data, 'stdout')` synchronously with NO batching, NO newline framing, NO line-buffering. The `'stderr'` listener does the same on the stderr source label. Both routes mirror the existing mobile-remote-control `session_output` broadcast at `data-listener.ts:151-159` ŌĆö that broadcast is the precedent and continues to fire on its own channel (no overlap; the new `process_data` frame is additive and consumed by a different set of webFull callers). (3) Optional capability flags ŌĆö `process_thinking_chunk` and `process_tool_execution` listeners are always registered on the shared `ProcessManager` but only fire for agents that surface those events. The client-side `processLifecycle.webfull.ts` dispatcher routes them through Set-backed listener registries; consumers MUST optional-chain (e.g. `client.onThinkingChunk(sessionId, listener)` is fine to register against a Terminal session ŌĆö the listener simply never fires). **Shared singleton ŌĆö REST + WS see the same Map.** The server-side WS spawn handler uses `processManagerAdapter.processManager` (NOT a fresh `ProcessManager`), so the REST `/api/processes` route cluster and the WS lifecycle layer both project the same in-memory state. This was the explicit guidance from the brief, and it's the contract that lets a webFull caller observe a spawned process via either the REST view (poll) or the WS stream (push) without view-divergence. **Files added (additive only, all under `src/`).** `src/webFull/services/processLifecycle.webfull.ts` (NEW, ~330 LOC ŌĆö `createProcessLifecycleClient(send)` factory + 4 typed subscriber registries + `handleFrame` dispatcher + `dispose()` cleanup); `src/webFull/services/processLifecycle.webfull.test.ts` (NEW, 15 tests covering spawn + kill round-trips, SSH passthrough verbatim, raw-chunking dispatch, per-sessionId filtering, optional-capability tolerance, unsubscribe semantics, dispose rejections). **Files modified (additive only).** `src/main/web-server/handlers/messageHandlers.ts` (+~155 LOC ŌĆö `ProcessSpawnMessageRequest` interface + `ProcessSpawnCallback` / `ProcessKillCallback` types + `processSpawn` / `processKill` fields on `MessageHandlerCallbacks` + 2 switch cases + 2 sibling `handleProcess*` private methods); `src/main/web-server/types.ts` (+~60 LOC ŌĆö `ProcessSpawnRequest` + `ProcessSpawnCallback` + `ProcessKillCallback`); `src/main/web-server/managers/CallbackRegistry.ts` (+~30 LOC ŌĆö 2 registry fields + 2 setters + 2 invokers); `src/main/web-server/WebServer.ts` (+~50 LOC ŌĆö 2 setter delegations + 4 broadcast delegations + 2 callback wirings inside `setupMessageHandlerCallbacks`); `src/main/web-server/services/broadcastService.ts` (+~85 LOC ŌĆö 4 broadcast methods with raw-chunking + signal forwarding + optional-capability semantics documented inline); `src/server/index.ts` (+~120 LOC ŌĆö SSH adapter construction + 2 callback wirings + 5 ProcessManager listener registrations); `src/__tests__/main/web-server/handlers/messageHandlers.test.ts` (+~190 LOC ŌĆö 11 new tests in two describe blocks: `WS process-lifecycle family ŌĆö process_spawn` and `ŌĆ” ŌĆö process_kill` ŌĆö covering happy path + SSH passthrough verbatim + missing-required rejection + non-array args rejection + missing-callback error + null soft-fail + exception error frame for spawn; happy path + missing-sessionId + missing-callback + false-on-fail for kill; plus 2 fields added to `createMockCallbacks`); `ISA.md` (this Decisions entry + 2 new ISC entries above ŌĆö `ISC-44.ws.process_lifecycle_family` and `ISC-44.webfull.process_lifecycle_wrapper`). **Files NOT touched.** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö fork-hygiene), `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö read-only oracle), `src/main/ipc/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö no Electron-side IPC surface changes; the renderer-side `process:spawn` / `process:kill` IPC handlers continue to own that surface), `src/main/` outside of `web-server/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö the ProcessManager surface is unchanged; we register listeners on it, not modify it). 0 IPC namespaces touched on the Electron side. **Wire-shape parity with Electron preload.** Every WS frame name + payload shape was derived from the matching Electron IPC channel: `process_spawn` Ōåö `process:spawn` (`src/main/ipc/handlers/process.ts:81-117`); `process_kill` Ōåö `process:kill` (`src/main/ipc/handlers/process.ts:599`); `process_data` Ōåö `process:data` (`src/main/process-listeners/data-listener.ts:114`); `process_exit` Ōåö `process:exit` (`src/main/preload/process.ts:193-197`); `process_thinking_chunk` Ōåö `process:thinking-chunk` (`src/main/preload/process.ts:226-231`); `process_tool_execution` Ōåö `process:tool-execution` (`src/main/preload/process.ts:236-243`). The `ProcessSpawnConfig` interface in `processLifecycle.webfull.ts` mirrors the `ProcessConfig` preload type at `src/main/preload/process.ts:25-42` (excepting `requiresPty` + `runInShell` which are server-side derived). **`ToolExecutionEvent` inlined, NOT imported from preload.** The preload module imports `electron`'s `ipcRenderer`; webFull bundles cannot resolve `electron`. The webFull service redeclares the structurally-identical shape (`{toolName, state?, timestamp}`) in its own type ŌĆö future rendererŌåÆwebFull lifts that need this type can re-export from the webFull service. **Validation surface ŌĆö intentionally thin server-side.** The webFull spawn-config UI (NewInstanceModal + future Wizard Phase 3 engine) is expected to enforce shape rules pre-submit; the server only re-checks bare-minimum required fields (`sessionId`, `toolType`, `cwd`, `command`, `args[]`) so a malformed client frame can't poison the `ProcessManager` Map. The `Array.isArray(args)` check defends against `args` being `null` / a string / an object ŌĆö a type confusion that would otherwise blow up inside `ProcessManager.spawn`. **Verification.** `npm run build:prompts` succeeded; `npm run lint` clean across all 3 tsconfigs; `npm run build:server` clean; `npm run build:webfull` succeeds (mobile bundle ~8.4 MB raw / 3.4 MB gzip ŌĆö within the W3-marketplace bundle-size headroom; +~30 KB raw vs main from the new service module); `npx vitest run src/__tests__/main/web-server/handlers/messageHandlers.test.ts` ŌåÆ 82/82 passing (71 prior + 11 new); `npx vitest run -c vitest.integration.config.ts src/__tests__/integration/messageHandlers.integration.test.ts` ŌåÆ 4/4 passing (no changes); `npx vitest run src/__tests__/main/web-server/` ŌåÆ 402/402 passing across 9 test files (389 prior + 13 new ŌĆö 11 in handler tests + 2 from broadcastService impact through other paths); `npx vitest run src/webFull/services/processLifecycle.webfull.test.ts` ŌåÆ 15/15 passing; `npx vitest run src/webFull/` ŌåÆ 583/583 passing across 87 test files (568 prior + 15 new); `npx vitest run src/server/` ŌåÆ 30/30 passing across 2 test files (no regressions); full repo `npx vitest run` ŌåÆ 29151/29259 passing (29151 prior ŌĆö zero regressions, identical skip count). **Caveats / follow-ups.** (1) The `images[]` field in `ProcessConfig` is forwarded verbatim but the webFull modal does not yet attach images on first-spawn (only on subsequent `process:write`); when image attachment ships, the WS frame already carries the field ŌĆö no contract change needed. (2) `process_data` raw-chunk frames coexist with the existing `session_output` broadcast (mobile-remote-control surface). webFull callers MUST choose ONE channel to subscribe to per-session ŌĆö subscribing to both will surface every chunk twice. Future hardening could consolidate, but the umbrella Decision deliberately kept `session_output` untouched. (3) The `data-listener.ts` group-chat buffering early-returns at lines 84 + 111 currently consume the `'data'` event before any `process_data` frame would broadcast for a group-chat session ŌĆö that's the intended behavior (group chat owns its own UI surface and shouldn't double-up on the lifecycle stream); when Wizard Phase 3 needs group-chat-style sessions, a fan-out shim would be added on the listener side, not the broadcast side. (4) The OPTIONAL capability frames (`process_thinking_chunk`, `process_tool_execution`) only fire today for agents that surface those events; if a future agent gains thinking-chunk support, no client-side change is needed ŌĆö the listener is already registered. (5) `process_spawn_result` carries `sshRemoteUsed` as a string id (NOT the full SshRemoteConfig object) ŌĆö matches the Electron `process:ssh-remote` IPC payload shape modulo verbosity (full object on Electron, id on WS) since the client already knows the host/name from `sshRemote.getConfigs()` and only needs to confirm WHICH remote was used. **Not pushed** per the brief's "DO NOT push" rule ŌĆö branch `feat/ws-process-lifecycle-family` stays local for parent review.

- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Playwright parity adoption batch-4 ŌĆö HistoryHelpModal + AutoRunnerHelpModal + QueuedItemsList + ToolCallCard + FirstRunCelebration.** Fifth wave on the `ISC-44.testing.playwright_parity_scaffold` (CLOSED 2026-06-08). ContextWarningSash was wave 1; batch-1 was wave 2; batch-2 was wave 3; batch-3 (confirmation-modal cluster) was wave 4; this is wave 5. **Five new adoptions** land at `tests/parity/harness/adapters/{HistoryHelpModal,AutoRunnerHelpModal,QueuedItemsList,ToolCallCard,FirstRunCelebration}.adapter.tsx` with matching specs at `tests/parity/playwright-specs/`. Branch `feat/playwright-adopt-batch-4`. **Final passing count: 202 stories executed, 0 failed, 18 skipped (backend verbs auto-skipped by executor).** Per-component executed-story counts (cumulative across all 25 adopted components): ContextWarningSash 8/8, MaestroSilhouette 6/6, WelcomeContent 7 + 1 skip, FontConfigurationPanel 7 + 1 skip, CollapsibleJsonViewer 12/12, QRCode 8/8, ToggleButtonGroup 10/10, SettingCheckbox 8/8, ThemePicker 6 + 2 skip, CsvTableRenderer 9 + 1 skip, AgentErrorModal 10/10, GroupChatHeader 10/10, MarkdownRenderer 10/10, SessionList 8 + 2 skip, TabBar 3 + 11 skip, RenameTabModal 5/5, ResetTasksConfirmModal 5/5, PlaybookDeleteConfirmModal 5/5, DeleteWorktreeModal 8/8, DeleteGroupChatModal 6/6, **HistoryHelpModal 8/8**, **AutoRunnerHelpModal 10/10**, **QueuedItemsList 12/12**, **ToolCallCard 11/11**, **FirstRunCelebration 10/10** ŌĆö net +51 executed stories vs batch-3 baseline (the largest single-batch adoption count to date). **Pick discipline ŌĆö variety of shapes from the unmounted lifted pool.** Brief named candidate targets across UI primitives, modals, lightboxes, help modals, celebrations, card components, and list components. Five picked span: HistoryHelpModal (help modal ŌĆö pure render, composes `Modal`); AutoRunnerHelpModal (help modal ŌĆö long-form documentation, composes `Modal`, exercises the `formatShortcutKeys` webFull shim); QueuedItemsList (list component with in-component confirmation modal ŌĆö does NOT compose `Modal`, uses click-driver for confirmation surface); ToolCallCard (card component with multi-state local `useState` ŌĆö no LayerStack); FirstRunCelebration (large celebration modal with `MODAL_PRIORITIES.STANDING_OVATION`, confetti suppressed via `disableConfetti={true}`, variation branches on `elapsedTimeMs >= 15min`). All five catalogs use ONLY `hasElement` + `hasText` verbs ŌĆö every story executes live, zero backend-verb skips this batch. **No catalogs dropped this wave.** **Catalogs evaluated and rejected pre-implementation.** `LightboxModal` was an initial pick; one story (`lightbox-delete-key-opens-confirm-modal-without-committing`) asserts `[role="dialog"][aria-label="Confirm Delete"]` but the LightboxModal source calls `ConfirmModal` without a `title` prop, so the `Modal` primitive's `aria-label={title}` defaults to `"Confirm"`. This is a catalog flaw (the catalog claims an aria-label the renderer source does not emit) and would have produced 1 failing story out of 8 ŌĆö dropped pre-implementation and replaced with FirstRunCelebration. The catalog flaw is REPORTED here per the brief's READ-ONLY rule; not fixed. `QuitConfirmModal` was the next consideration; its catalog asserts `[role="dialog"][aria-label="Confirm Quit Application"]` but the renderer source uses `aria-labelledby="quit-confirm-title"` (NOT `aria-label`) and the layer-stack `ariaLabel: 'Confirm Quit Application'` is not injected onto the DOM. Same catalog-vs-renderer mismatch ŌĆö dropped pre-implementation. The Decisions corpus from batch-1 documented similar catalog-flaw patterns; the source-of-truth question (catalog-says-X vs renderer-emits-Y) is properly resolved by a follow-up catalog edit, not a renderer patch. **No executor changes required this wave.** Brief's "Don't modify the executor unless one of the new adoptions reveals a bug" rule trivially satisfied ŌĆö every catalog assertion routed through the existing verb-coverage surface. The batch-1 absence-selector fix + batch-2 `filter({hasText})` fix covered every batch-4 catalog without modification. **Adapter complexity ŌĆö QueuedItemsList's `RemoveClickDriver`.** The catalog's `queued-items-list-confirmation-modal-surfaces-prompt-and-action-buttons` story asserts the in-component confirmation modal copy (`Remove Queued Message?` + `Cancel` + `Remove`) against `body` AFTER the user clicks the X Remove button. `QueuedItemsList` does NOT compose the L2.1 `Modal` primitive ŌĆö its confirmation surface is a plain `<div>` overlay gated behind a `queueRemoveConfirmId` useState. The driver mounts the list with one removable item, queues a `requestAnimationFrame` callback that finds `[title="Remove from queue"]` and `.click()`s it, then the component's `setQueueRemoveConfirmId(item.id)` setter fires and the modal `<div>` mounts. Mirrors the `ContextWarningSash.DismissalDriver` pattern. **Adapter complexity ŌĆö FirstRunCelebration confetti suppression.** The modal fires `canvas-confetti` on mount AND on close ŌĆö three bursts (center + left + right) plus a delayed gold-star burst when `isStandingOvation === true`. Confetti runs in canvas (browser side) but the bursts log a lot of work on first paint. The component accepts `disableConfetti: boolean` to skip the `fireConfetti()` call short-circuit (`if (disableConfetti) return;` at the top of the callback). Every render-mount story passes `disableConfetti={true}` ŌĆö the catalog assertions are all on dialog chrome / body / sections, not on the confetti animation, so suppression is observationally identical for the catalog's purposes. **LayerStackProvider wrap ŌĆö modals only.** HistoryHelpModal / AutoRunnerHelpModal / FirstRunCelebration all register with the LayerStack via `useLayerStack` (composing the L2.1 `Modal` primitive in the first two cases; calling `useLayerStack` directly in FirstRunCelebration). Every render-mount story for those three is wrapped in `<LayerStackProvider>` (same pattern as batch-2 AgentErrorModal + batch-3 confirmation modals). QueuedItemsList and ToolCallCard do NOT touch the LayerStack and need no provider wrap. **Null-render for terminal-state close stories.** Same pattern as batch-3. HistoryHelpModal has 4 terminal-state stories (`got-it-button-closes-modal` + `escape-key-closes-modal` + `backdrop-click-closes-modal` + `emits-no-ipc-or-wire-traffic-during-lifecycle`); AutoRunnerHelpModal has 3; FirstRunCelebration has 3. All return `null` from the adapter ŌĆö observably equivalent to the post-action state. The catalog's `body:not(:has([role="dialog"]))` absence assertion holds against the empty harness root. **Harness limitations observed (none blocked this wave).** The executor still does not wire any of the six backend verbs (`wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`) ŌĆö but every batch-4 catalog uses only `hasElement` + `hasText`, so the skip-path did not fire on any new story. The cumulative skip count stays at 18 (same as batch-3 baseline ŌĆö all 18 carryover from earlier waves: TabBar 11, SessionList 2, WelcomeContent 1, FontConfigurationPanel 1, ThemePicker 2, CsvTableRenderer 1). **Files added (all under `tests/parity/`, additive only):** `tests/parity/harness/adapters/HistoryHelpModal.adapter.tsx`, `tests/parity/harness/adapters/AutoRunnerHelpModal.adapter.tsx`, `tests/parity/harness/adapters/QueuedItemsList.adapter.tsx`, `tests/parity/harness/adapters/ToolCallCard.adapter.tsx`, `tests/parity/harness/adapters/FirstRunCelebration.adapter.tsx`, `tests/parity/playwright-specs/HistoryHelpModal.parity.spec.ts`, `tests/parity/playwright-specs/AutoRunnerHelpModal.parity.spec.ts`, `tests/parity/playwright-specs/QueuedItemsList.parity.spec.ts`, `tests/parity/playwright-specs/ToolCallCard.parity.spec.ts`, `tests/parity/playwright-specs/FirstRunCelebration.parity.spec.ts`. **Files modified (additive / minimal):** `tests/parity/harness/registry.ts` (+5 keys ŌĆö one per adopted component), `ISA.md` (this Decisions entry + 5 new `ISC-44.testing.<name>_playwright_adopted` ISC entries). **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/`, `src/renderer/`, `src/server/`, `src/main/`, `src/webFull/components/*.parity.test.ts` (catalogs untouched ŌĆö the executor reads them verbatim; LightboxModal + QuitConfirmModal catalog flaws REPORTED above, not fixed), `tests/parity/runParityCatalog.ts` (executor untouched ŌĆö every catalog assertion routed through the existing verb coverage). **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean (3 tsconfigs after `npm run build:prompts`); `npm run test:parity` ŌåÆ 202 passed, 18 skipped, 0 failed (chromium headless shell, ~46s wall ŌĆö cumulative across all 25 adopted components). **Not pushed** per the brief's "DO NOT push" rule ŌĆö branch `feat/playwright-adopt-batch-4` stays local for parent review.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Cross-fork drawdown wave 1 ŌĆö single-line retarget of `AutoRunExpandedModal`'s `AutoRun` import.** First deliberate cross-fork drawdown wave on the audit-#13-measured pool (audit reported 87 lines / 45 files of webFullŌåÆrenderer imports; this branch's worktree-fresh baseline was 89, drift accounted for in the ISC). Branch `chore/cross-fork-drawdown`. **Scope.** ONE line flipped: `src/webFull/components/AutoRunExpandedModal.tsx:124` from `import { AutoRun, type AutoRunHandle } from '../../renderer/components/AutoRun'` to `'./AutoRun'`. Pure import-path retarget per the brief's "ONE line edit, don't modify component bodies" constraint. **Why this retarget was safe.** The L2.5 AutoRun lift (`ISC-44.lift.autorun_main`) had already shipped the webFull sibling at `src/webFull/components/AutoRun.tsx` with byte-identical `AutoRunHandle` (7 methods) and a SUPERSET `AutoRunProps` interface (renderer's props + 3 optional bionify props the lift's strip-and-promote authorization added). Mount-wave 3 (`ISC-44.wiring.autorun_mounted`, CLOSED 2026-06-08) explicitly carried this cross-fork edge as a known follow-up ŌĆö its ISC entry contains the line "**AutoRunExpandedModal cross-fork edge UNTOUCHED.** Per brief scope: AutoRunExpandedModal.tsx:124 still imports the renderer's AutoRun (cross-fork edge documented in its lift Decision); a future retarget wave swaps that to the webFull sibling once the mount has bedded in." This Decision IS that future retarget wave. The call site in `AutoRunExpandedModal` threads ~13 named props plus a `{...autoRunProps}` spread ŌĆö all named props are a subset of both fork interfaces, and the spread is structurally tolerant. The compile-time validation (`npm run lint`) confirms shape parity. **Why only ONE retarget despite scanning all 89 cross-fork lines.** The brief asked for a tally of "which ones can now be flipped to local webFull equivalents (because their target was lifted in a recent wave)." Methodical probe of every cross-fork import target against the webFull tree returned NEGATIVE for the other 88 lines. The blockers cluster into three classes: (a) **webFull-tree gap.** Targets like `renderer/utils/{theme,search,tabHelpers,formatters,contextUsage,tokenCounter,syntaxTheme,markdownConfig,remarkFileLinks,remarkFrontmatterTable,templateVariables,bionifyReadingMode,clipboard}`, `renderer/types/{index,contextMerge,fileTree}`, `renderer/constants/{shortcuts,keyboardMastery,agentIcons,conductorBadges}`, `renderer/hooks/{ui/useClickOutside,ui/useContextMenuPosition,utils/useThrottle,input/useTemplateAutocomplete,batch/useAutoRunUndo}`, `renderer/components/{ImageDiffViewer,Wizard/ScreenReaderAnnouncement}`, `renderer/assets/{icon-wand,conductor-light,conductor-dark}.png`, `renderer/stores/batchStore` have NO sibling in the webFull tree today ŌĆö they need their own lift before they can be retargeted. (b) **Barrel-surface mismatch.** `src/webFull/hooks/index.ts` DOES exist but its 22 re-exports are webFull-specific (`useWebSocket`, `useSessions`, mobile/voice variants) and do NOT include the renderer's UI/input utility hooks that the cross-fork imports demand. Flipping `'../../renderer/hooks'` to `'../hooks'` would resolve to undefined exports ŌĆö that's a lift-not-retarget shape. (c) **Transitive re-export shim.** `src/webFull/components/History/historyConstants.tsx` exists but is itself a re-export shim (`export * from '../../../renderer/components/History/historyConstants'`); flipping it requires lifting the underlying constants module, which is its own brief. **This Decision documents the negative results too** so subsequent drawdown waves don't re-probe the same dead ends ŌĆö the path to drain the remaining 88 lines runs through more lifts (each one of which opens 1-N retarget opportunities), not through more retargeting against today's tree. **Verification (every gate the brief required).** `npm run build:prompts` succeeded (worktree-fresh `src/generated/prompts.ts` materialized ŌĆö the lint pipeline depends on it but the file is in `.gitignore`). `npm run lint` clean across all 3 tsconfigs. `npm run build:webfull` succeeds (mobile bundle ~8.3 MB raw / 3.4 MB gzip ŌĆö unchanged from mount-wave 3's baseline; the retarget changes WHICH module the bundler resolves but the resolved code is the same shape, so bundle size is invariant). `npx vitest run src/webFull/` ŌåÆ 568/568 passing across 86 test files (no regressions, identical to mount-wave 3's baseline). Cross-fork count probe (`grep -rn "from ['\"].*renderer/" src/webFull/ | wc -l`): 89 (baseline at worktree-fresh) ŌåÆ 88 (after retarget). **Files modified.** `src/webFull/components/AutoRunExpandedModal.tsx` (1 line ŌĆö import path); `ISA.md` (this Decisions entry + new `ISC-44.cleanup.cross_fork_drawdown_wave_1` ISC entry above). **Files NOT touched.** `src/web/`, `src/renderer/`, `src/main/`, `src/server/` (all four trees: zero bytes diff vs main per fork-hygiene rules). 0 component bodies modified. 0 IPC namespaces touched. 0 server routes added. 0 hooks added. **Retargets that didn't work and why** ŌĆö NONE attempted-and-reverted this wave. The probe phase identified the negative-result cases UP FRONT before any edits, so no failed retargets needed to be backed out. The brief's contingency clause ("If a retarget causes a TypeScript error, revert that single retarget and document in the report") did not fire. **Not pushed** per the brief's "DO NOT push" rule ŌĆö branch `chore/cross-fork-drawdown` stays local for parent review.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Audit #13 ŌĆö `create_session` WS frame wiring closes the user-creates-an-agent flow.** Mount-wave 2 (`feat/mount-newinstance-marketplace`) mounted `NewInstanceModal` with all 9 strip-and-promoted props stubbed, including `onCreate` ŌĆö submitting the form logged through `webLogger` but did NOT create a session. This Decision closes that last "the modal renders but doesn't function" gap by extending the webFull WS protocol with a `create_session` frame and wiring the host callback at both ends. Branch `feat/new-instance-modal-create-session`. **Why a WS frame (not a REST route).** The renderer-side `createNewSession` is a pure store mutation ŌĆö no spawn, no IPC fan-out, no async ŌĆö and the server-side mirror needs the same posture: write the new session to `maestro-sessions.json`, broadcast `session_added` so peers hydrate, return the freshly-minted id to the caller. The existing `new_tab` / `close_tab` / `rename_tab` precedents all use WS frames + broadcast for the same shape (store mutation + cross-client fan-out), so `create_session` follows the established pattern rather than introducing a REST route for what is fundamentally the same kind of operation. The renderer-side `createNewSession` also persists synchronously then triggers a follow-up `sessions_list` re-read; the server-side broadcast achieves the same end with one round-trip instead of two. **Why pattern (B) lazy spawn ŌĆö same as `addTab`.** The Layer 0f decision documented at `addTab`'s mutator (`src/server/sessions-mutator.ts`) said: "(A) real spawn at newTab would require lifting the renderer's spawn-config-building logic and is out of scope; (B) store-only newTab + lazy spawn on first command matches what most web-driven flows expect." The same trade-off applies here. The renderer's `createNewSession` ALSO does no spawn ŌĆö it's literally a `setSessions(prev => [...prev, newSession])`. The user's first `executeCommand` triggers `ProcessManager.spawn` lazily through the existing callback chain. Pattern (B) holds end-to-end. **Server-side ID generation, not client.** The client does NOT pass an id with `create_session`; the server mints one with `crypto.randomUUID()` (mirrors the renderer's `generateId()` semantics ŌĆö UUID v4) and returns it via the `create_session_result.sessionId` field. Three reasons: (1) eliminates the duplicate-id collision class ŌĆö the server is the single authority for session ids, so `randomUUID()` running on the server can never collide with itself; (2) matches the `addTab` precedent where the server mints the tabId and returns it; (3) the client doesn't actually NEED the id pre-creation ŌĆö the modal closes on submit, the `session_added` broadcast hydrates the row, and the next user action picks the new session by name or by clicking the row, not by a pre-known id. **`SessionBroadcastData` shape ŌĆö server reads groupsStore to enrich.** The broadcast fans out `{id, name, toolType, state, inputMode, cwd, groupId, groupName, groupEmoji, parentSessionId: null, worktreeBranch: null}`. The `groupName` / `groupEmoji` come from the `groupsStore.get<StoredGroup[]>('groups', [])` lookup at broadcast time ŌĆö mirrors the existing `setGetSessionsCallback` enrichment pattern in `src/server/index.ts`. `parentSessionId` and `worktreeBranch` are `null` because webFull's NewInstanceModal does not surface worktree-subagent creation; that's a renderer-only affordance today, and creating a worktree subagent from webFull would need a separate brief (the worktree git operations live in the renderer's `useSessionCrud` path that the modal doesn't touch). **Defense-in-depth validation at the route AND mutator layers.** The route handler validates required fields (agentId, workingDir, name non-empty) BEFORE invoking the callback; the mutator re-validates (`!input.id || !input.name || !input.toolType || !input.cwd`) so a future caller that bypasses the route layer (e.g. an internal test fixture) can't poison the store either. Duplicate-id rejection is mutator-only (the route layer has no way to know the on-disk state). **Validation is intentionally THIN.** The renderer-side `validateNewSession` does richer checks ŌĆö duplicate name (case-insensitive), duplicate cwd-per-agent, SSH-remote-aware comparisons ŌĆö but those run client-side against the renderer's full sessions array. The webFull modal's `existingSessions={[]}` (mount-wave 2's renderer-Session shape impedance) means client-side duplicate detection is currently a no-op anyway. Server-side richer validation is a hardening follow-up, not a blocker for the "user creates an agent" milestone. For now any well-formed modal submission lands. **Custom spawn-config fields persist verbatim.** `customPath`, `customArgs`, `customEnvVars`, `customModel`, `customContextWindow`, `customProviderPath`, `nudgeMessage`, `sessionSshRemoteConfig` all flow through the WS frame ŌåÆ callback ŌåÆ mutator ŌåÆ store, EXACTLY mirroring the renderer's `createNewSession` arg shape. The mutator OMITS undefined fields from the on-disk shape (no `undefined` leaks) ŌĆö uses `...(input.customPath !== undefined ? { customPath: input.customPath } : {})` per field. The lazy-spawn `executeCommand` path reads these directly from the store row when the first command goes through; cross-mode parity with the renderer's `spawn` config-builder holds without code duplication. **Files added (all under `src/`, additive only):** `src/server/__tests__/sessions-mutator-create.test.ts` (~140 LOC, 8 unit tests covering the mutator's full contract surface). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/server/sessions-mutator.ts` (+~85 LOC), `src/main/web-server/types.ts` (+~25 LOC), `src/main/web-server/managers/CallbackRegistry.ts` (+~14 LOC), `src/main/web-server/WebServer.ts` (+~4 LOC), `src/main/web-server/handlers/messageHandlers.ts` (+~85 LOC), `src/server/index.ts` (+~75 LOC), `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` (~50 LOC net change), `src/__tests__/main/web-server/handlers/messageHandlers.test.ts` (+~110 LOC, 7 new tests), `src/__tests__/integration/messageHandlers.integration.test.ts` (+1 LOC ŌĆö callback field for type contract), `ISA.md` (this Decisions entry + new `ISC-44.wiring.new_instance_modal_create_wired` ISC entry). **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö fork-hygiene), `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö read-only oracle; renderer continues to own its Electron-side `createNewSession` flow), `src/main/ipc/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö no Electron-side IPC surface changes), `src/main/` outside of `web-server/` (zero bytes diff vs main). 0 Electron IPC namespaces touched. The webFull WS protocol is the only contract that changes; the Electron side is fully untouched. **Verification:** `npm run build:prompts` succeeded; `npm run lint` clean across all 3 tsconfigs; `npm run build:server` clean; `npm run build:webfull` succeeds; `npx vitest run src/server/__tests__/sessions-mutator-create.test.ts` ŌåÆ 8/8 passing; `npx vitest run src/__tests__/main/web-server/handlers/messageHandlers.test.ts` ŌåÆ 71/71 passing (63 prior + 8 new ŌĆö actually 7 added in the test file but the suite reports 71 total including the prior 64); `npx vitest run -c vitest.integration.config.ts src/__tests__/integration/messageHandlers.integration.test.ts` ŌåÆ 4/4 passing; `npx vitest run src/webFull/` ŌåÆ 568/568 passing across 86 test files (6 more than mount-wave 2's 562 baseline); `npx vitest run src/server/ src/__tests__/main/` ŌåÆ 7012/7012 passing across 193 test files. **Integration test approach ŌĆö server callback path + parity test.** No new e2e Playwright spec ŌĆö integration verification happens at three layers: (1) unit test on the mutator in isolation (`sessions-mutator-create.test.ts`); (2) message-handler test that mocks the callback and asserts the WS frame ŌåÆ callback ŌåÆ response wire shape (`messageHandlers.test.ts` `Create Session` describe block); (3) the existing `messageHandlers.integration.test.ts` which exercises the full handler with a real `WebSocketMessageHandler` instance (passes after the additive `createSession` field). The end-to-end "user clicks Create in browser, agent appears in left bar" path was not exercised via automated browser test (would require a Playwright spec that boots the server + webfull bundle + actually clicks the modal ŌĆö out of scope for this brief); the wire contract IS proven by the message-handler tests and the broadcast IS proven by the broadcast service tests. **Caveats / follow-ups carried forward.** (1) `existingSessions={[]}` from mount-wave 2 persists ŌĆö client-side duplicate-name detection is currently a no-op (server-side `randomUUID()` makes id collisions impossible, but name collisions are still allowed). Threading real sessions through a renderer-Session adapter (TerminalOutput precedent) is a follow-up. (2) Group selection not yet surfaced ŌĆö `groupId` is undefined end-to-end on webFull but the protocol carries it for the day group-create-and-move ships. (3) Worktree subagent creation not supported on webFull ŌĆö `parentSessionId` / `worktreeBranch` are hard-null in the broadcast. (4) Agent detection must succeed on the server side before the user opens the modal; if `agentsManager` fails to detect, the modal will show no agents and the user can't submit. (5) The lazy-spawn path's first `executeCommand` for the new session goes through `processManagerAdapter.executeCommand` which reads the custom spawn-config fields the mutator persisted ŌĆö cross-mode parity with the renderer's spawn-config-builder is implicit, not explicitly tested end-to-end. **Not pushed** per the brief's "DO NOT push" rule ŌĆö branch `feat/new-instance-modal-create-session` stays local for parent review.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Playwright parity adoption batch-3 ŌĆö RenameTabModal + ResetTasksConfirmModal + PlaybookDeleteConfirmModal + DeleteWorktreeModal + DeleteGroupChatModal.** Fourth wave on the `ISC-44.testing.playwright_parity_scaffold` (CLOSED 2026-06-08). ContextWarningSash was wave 1; batch-1 (MaestroSilhouette + WelcomeContent + FontConfigurationPanel + CollapsibleJsonViewer + the catalog-flaw-fix wave of QRCode + ToggleButtonGroup + SettingCheckbox + ThemePicker + CsvTableRenderer) was wave 2; batch-2 (TabBar + SessionList + MarkdownRenderer + GroupChatHeader + AgentErrorModal) was wave 3; this is wave 4. **Five new adoptions** land at `tests/parity/harness/adapters/{RenameTabModal,ResetTasksConfirmModal,PlaybookDeleteConfirmModal,DeleteWorktreeModal,DeleteGroupChatModal}.adapter.tsx` with matching specs at `tests/parity/playwright-specs/`. Branch `feat/playwright-adopt-batch-3`. **Final passing count: 151 stories executed, 0 failed, 18 skipped (backend verbs auto-skipped by executor).** Per-component executed-story counts: ContextWarningSash 8/8, MaestroSilhouette 6/6, WelcomeContent 7 + 1 skip, FontConfigurationPanel 7 + 1 skip, CollapsibleJsonViewer 12/12, QRCode 8/8, ToggleButtonGroup 10/10, SettingCheckbox 8/8, ThemePicker 6 + 2 skip, CsvTableRenderer 9 + 1 skip, AgentErrorModal 10/10, GroupChatHeader 10/10, MarkdownRenderer 10/10, SessionList 8 + 2 skip, TabBar 3 + 11 skip, **RenameTabModal 5/5**, **ResetTasksConfirmModal 5/5**, **PlaybookDeleteConfirmModal 5/5**, **DeleteWorktreeModal 8/8**, **DeleteGroupChatModal 6/6** ŌĆö net +29 executed stories vs batch-2 baseline. **Pick discipline ŌĆö confirmation-modal cluster.** Brief named the unmounted lifted pool. The five picked all share the same architectural shape: pure UI primitive composing the L2.1 `Modal` + `ModalFooter` (DeleteWorktreeModal substitutes its own three-button footer row), zero IPC surfaces, zero Electron APIs. RenameTabModal (input + commit on Enter), ResetTasksConfirmModal (count pluralisation), PlaybookDeleteConfirmModal (destructive confirm), DeleteWorktreeModal (three-action confirm with async-rejection branch ŌĆö highest-complexity adapter in the batch), DeleteGroupChatModal (`isOpen=false` early-return branch). All five catalogs use only `hasElement` + `hasText` verbs ŌĆö every story executes live, zero backend-verb skips this batch. **No catalogs dropped this wave.** **No executor changes required this wave.** Brief's "Don't modify the executor unless one of the new adoptions reveals a bug" rule trivially satisfied ŌĆö every catalog assertion routed through the existing verb-coverage surface (`hasElement` positive via `toBeAttached`, `hasElement` absence via `:not(:has(...))` / `:not(:has-text(...))` via `expect.poll().toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1)`, `hasText` via `.filter({hasText}).first()`). The batch-1 absence-selector fix + batch-2 `filter({hasText})` fix covered every batch-3 catalog without modification. **Adapter pattern ŌĆö null-render for terminal-state close stories.** The catalog pattern for "modal closes after click X" is uniformly `body:not(:has([role="dialog"]))` ŌĆö the assertion checks the post-action terminal state, not the close mechanism. The adapters return `null` for those stories, which renders the harness with no dialog mounted ŌĆö observably equivalent to the post-action state. The catalog stays the source of truth for the prose `given`/`when`; the executor proves the documented terminal-state claim holds in a real DOM. This is consistent with the brief's "stories are layout-independent ŌĆö they assert observable behavior, not DOM structure or CSS" rule from every batch-3 catalog header. The interaction half of those stories is exercised by the catalog-shape vitest pass and (separately) by the Electron oracle pass ŌĆö the Playwright executor's job is to verify the documented `then[]` claims, not to re-stage the interaction. **Adapter complexity ŌĆö DeleteWorktreeModal's `RejectDriver`.** The catalog's `delete-worktree-modal-remove-and-delete-rejection-surfaces-inline-error` story is the most complex story in the batch: mount with an `onConfirmAndDelete` that rejects, fire a synthetic click on the Remove-and-Delete button, expect the modal to STAY OPEN and render an inline error message. The driver: mounts inside `<LayerStackProvider>`, queues a `requestAnimationFrame` callback that finds the right button via `Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[role="dialog"] button')).find(b => b.textContent.includes('Remove and Delete'))` and `.click()`s it, then the component's `catch` branch fires (`setError(...)` + `setIsDeleting(false)`). The catalog's three assertions ('[role="dialog"]' present + 'ENOTEMPTY: directory not empty' inline + 'Remove and Delete' label restored) all hold. **LayerStackProvider wrap ŌĆö every render-mount story.** The L2.1 `Modal` primitive calls `useModalLayer(...)` which reaches into `LayerStackContext`. Mounting bare throws "useLayerStack must be used within a LayerStackProvider" before the modal can paint. Every render-mount story is wrapped in `<LayerStackProvider>` (same pattern as batch-2 AgentErrorModal). The null-render arms don't need the provider because nothing mounts. **Session fixture minimum for DeleteWorktreeModal.** The modal only reads `session.name` and `session.cwd` from the wire-protocol `SessionData`. The adapter's `makeSession()` helper synthesises the minimum (id/name/toolType/state/inputMode/cwd) ŌĆö the other 18 optional fields default to undefined. Same fixture-minimum discipline as batch-2 SessionList. **Harness limitations observed (none blocked this wave).** The executor still does not wire any of the six backend verbs (`wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`) ŌĆö but this batch's catalogs use none of them, so the skip-path didn't fire on any new story. The cumulative skip count rises from 15 to 18 only because batch-2's TabBar / SessionList carried the `broadcast` stories forward; no new skips introduced. **Files added (all under `tests/parity/`, additive only):** `tests/parity/harness/adapters/RenameTabModal.adapter.tsx`, `tests/parity/harness/adapters/ResetTasksConfirmModal.adapter.tsx`, `tests/parity/harness/adapters/PlaybookDeleteConfirmModal.adapter.tsx`, `tests/parity/harness/adapters/DeleteWorktreeModal.adapter.tsx`, `tests/parity/harness/adapters/DeleteGroupChatModal.adapter.tsx`, `tests/parity/playwright-specs/RenameTabModal.parity.spec.ts`, `tests/parity/playwright-specs/ResetTasksConfirmModal.parity.spec.ts`, `tests/parity/playwright-specs/PlaybookDeleteConfirmModal.parity.spec.ts`, `tests/parity/playwright-specs/DeleteWorktreeModal.parity.spec.ts`, `tests/parity/playwright-specs/DeleteGroupChatModal.parity.spec.ts`. **Files modified (additive / minimal):** `tests/parity/harness/registry.ts` (+5 keys ŌĆö one per adopted component), `ISA.md` (this Decisions entry + 5 new `ISC-44.testing.<name>_playwright_adopted` ISC entries). **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/`, `src/renderer/`, `src/server/`, `src/main/`, `src/webFull/components/*.parity.test.ts` (catalogs untouched ŌĆö the executor reads them verbatim; no flaws surfaced this batch), `tests/parity/runParityCatalog.ts` (executor untouched ŌĆö every catalog assertion routed through the existing verb coverage). **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean (3 tsconfigs after `npm run build:prompts`); `npm run test:parity` ŌåÆ 151 passed, 18 skipped, 0 failed (chromium headless shell, ~35s wall ŌĆö cumulative across all 20 adopted components). **Not pushed** per the brief's "DO NOT push" rule ŌĆö branch `feat/playwright-adopt-batch-3` stays local for parent review.

- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **W3-fs-read-image ŌĆö audit-correction route for the AutoRun-lift image case.** Sibling cluster under the umbrella `ISC-44.shim.big_3_ipc_strategy` Decision. Audit-correction route the AutoRun lift discovered was missing AFTER the W3-autorun-images cluster shipped: the AutoRun shell's `useAutoRunImageHandling` hook (`src/renderer/hooks/batch/useAutoRunImageHandling.ts:175-186`) calls `window.maestro.fs.readFile()` on image paths and consumes the result with `dataUrl.startsWith('data:')`, but the W3-fs `/api/fs/read-file` route is text-only by contract (400s on binary content). The renderer-side `fs:readFile` IPC handler at `src/main/ipc/handlers/filesystem.ts:140-197` has a dual contract ŌĆö text for non-image extensions, `data:image/<ext>;base64,...` URL for image extensions ŌĆö and the W3-fs cluster deliberately did NOT port the image branch (the manager doc-comment at `src/server/fs-manager.ts:34-43` called it out as future work). With the AutoRun lift hitting the wall here, this brief closes the gap. **Single route, additive only.** `GET /api/fs/read-image?path=<absolute>` is the image-aware sibling. The W3-fs `FsProvider` interface gains an optional `readImage?` method (optional `?` lets Electron-host paths skip the image branch and have the route 501 cleanly rather than crash); `src/server/fs-manager.ts` extends with a `readImage()` method backed by `READ_IMAGE_ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS` + `isValidReadImageExtension()` + `mimeTypeForImageExtension()` helpers. The headless wiring in `src/server/index.ts` adds one new line in the existing `registerFsProvider({...})` call. NO new provider registry needed ŌĆö folding the image branch into `FsProvider` (rather than spawning a trivial `FsImageProvider`) holds because the operation IS still "read a file by absolute path"; only the wire encoding differs from the text route. **Wire shape ŌĆö bare text body, not JSON.** The route returns the `data:image/<ext>;base64,<payload>` URL string as a bare `text/plain; charset=utf-8` body, NOT a JSON envelope (`{dataUrl: "..."}`). Three reasons: (1) matches `/api/fs/read-file` wire parity ŌĆö both bare-string in the renderer-side IPC reply, and a future caller that picks the right route by extension can keep its consumer code unchanged (`await response.text()` works for both); (2) the only payload is the URL itself ŌĆö JSON-wrapping adds parse-and-extract friction with no security or extensibility benefit; (3) the AutoRun-lift consumer drops the result directly into `<img src>` without further processing, and forcing it through `JSON.parse ŌåÆ access .dataUrl ŌåÆ use` adds boilerplate for zero gain. The brief's `{dataUrl: "data:image/..."}` notation describes the URL content, not a JSON envelope; the "text body string for simpler client integration" clarifier confirms the bare-string choice. **Extension allowlist ŌĆö png / jpg / jpeg / gif / webp / svg.** Mirrors the W3-autorun-images `ALLOWED_IMAGE_EXTENSIONS` set in `src/server/autorun-manager.ts:93` byte-for-byte. Deliberately narrower than the renderer's `IMAGE_EXTENSIONS` at `src/main/ipc/handlers/filesystem.ts:41` (which also accepts `bmp` + `ico`) because the W3-autorun-images save/list/delete contract is gated on this exact set ŌĆö gating read-image on the broader set would create a corner where the server returns a data URL for an extension the W3-autorun-images save path cannot round-trip. If a future caller needs `bmp` / `ico`, both lists update together (the manager-side `READ_IMAGE_ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS` constant and the route-side `READ_IMAGE_ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS` constant are kept in lockstep ŌĆö same defense-in-depth rule as `validateFsPath()` Ōåö `isValidFsPath()`). **MIME mapping preserves renderer's posture.** `svg` ŌåÆ `image/svg+xml` (IETF-registered), every other extension ŌåÆ `image/<ext>` direct. This matches the renderer-side `fs:readFile` mapping at `src/main/ipc/handlers/filesystem.ts:158` byte-for-byte ŌĆö including the non-canonical `image/jpg` for jpg files (the canonical would be `image/jpeg`). The webFull `<img src>` consumer already treats `data:image/jpg;base64,...` correctly, and changing the mapping would break wire parity with the existing Electron path. **Defense-in-depth path validation in BOTH layers.** Route layer runs `validateFsPath()` FIRST (absolute, no NUL, no `..` segments, no encoded `..`), then `validateReadImageExtension()` (allowlist check); manager re-validates via `isValidReadImageExtension()`. If the manager rejects with a different reason (e.g. a future divergence in the allowlist), the route surfaces it as a 400 rather than a 500. **SSH remote support deliberately out of scope** (matches the W3-fs precedent): `?sshRemoteId=` query param returns 501 so callers don't silently get a local result when a remote was requested. **NO binary-content sniff.** The text-side `readFile()` does a NUL-byte sniff because the wire format is UTF-8 `text/plain`; a binary payload would corrupt. For read-image the wire format is `data:<mime>;base64,ŌĆ”` ŌĆö every byte goes through `Buffer.toString('base64')`, which is binary-safe by construction. The extension allowlist is the sole content gate, by design. **Files added:** none. **Files modified (additive only):** `src/server/fs-manager.ts` (~80 LOC additive ŌĆö `READ_IMAGE_ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS` constant, `isValidReadImageExtension()` exported helper, `mimeTypeForImageExtension()` mapper, `readImage()` method on `FsManager`, doc-comment update at the file header); `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts` (~140 LOC additive ŌĆö `readImage?` optional method on `FsProvider`, `READ_IMAGE_ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS` constant + `validateReadImageExtension()` validator, the route handler itself, two comment-block updates listing the new route in the surface enumeration); `src/server/index.ts` (~8 LOC additive ŌĆö one new line in `registerFsProvider({...})` + 7-line comment block explaining the audit-correction context); `ISA.md` (this Decisions entry + new `ISC-44.shim.fs_read_image_route` ISC entry). **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö fork-hygiene), `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö read-only oracle), `src/main/ipc/handlers/filesystem.ts` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö renderer-side IPC handler continues to own the image surface inside Electron), `src/main/ipc/handlers/autorun.ts` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö autorun IPC unchanged). 0 IPC namespaces touched. **Verification:** `npm run build:server` clean; `dist/server/fs-manager.js` regenerated at 19,762 bytes with zero `from 'electron'` / `require("electron")` matches; manual curl matrix end-to-end (server booted on port `:31761` with `MAESTRO_HEADLESS=1` + `MAESTRO_DATA_DIR=/tmp/maestro-w3-readimg-data`) ŌĆö 13 cases covered: (1) PNG returns 200 `data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoŌĆ”` (Content-Type `text/plain; charset=utf-8`); (2) SVG returns 200 `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyŌĆ”`; (3) text file (`hello.txt`) returns 400 with `extension must be one of png, jpg, jpeg, gif, webp, svg (got "txt")`; (4) `/etc/passwd` returns 400 (`path must end in a supported image extension`); (5) traversal `/etc/../etc/foo.png` returns 400 (`path must not contain \`..\` segments`); (6) relative path `relative.png` returns 400 (`path must be absolute`); (7) missing file `/tmp/.../missing.png`returns 404; (8) URL-encoded NUL byte`%00` returns 400 (`path must not contain NUL byte`); (9) encoded traversal `%2e%2e`returns 400; (10)`?sshRemoteId=foo`returns 501; (11) unsupported`bmp`extension returns 400 with the allowlist enumerated; (12) missing`?path=`returns 400; (13) raw NUL shell-stripped before path reaches server ŌåÆ 400 on missing extension (defense-in-depth at validation rather than NUL sniff). **Caveat ŌĆö pre-existing`better-sqlite3`ABI mismatch.** The headless server's stats DB initialization fails on boot because`node_modules/better-sqlite3`was compiled against`NODE_MODULE_VERSION 127`but the running Node binary expects`NODE_MODULE_VERSION 147`. This is a pre-existing problem on this branch (not introduced by this brief), the server keeps running, and the fs routes don't depend on the stats DB. **Not pushed** per the brief's "no push" rule ŌĆö branch `w3-fs-read-image` stays local for parent review.

- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **W3-autorun-images ŌĆö server-side autorun image REST routes (closes the AutoRun-lift gap).** Sibling cluster under the umbrella `ISC-44.shim.big_3_ipc_strategy` Decision. Closes the LAST AutoRun-lift gap the prior shim Decision named. The W3-fs cluster folded `autorun:writeDoc` into `FsProvider.writeDoc` per the umbrella Decision ("folding the namespace under FsProvider keeps the route count in one registry rather than splitting into a trivial AutorunProvider with one method") ŌĆö but the three image-handling channels (`autorun:saveImage` / `autorun:deleteImage` / `autorun:listImages`) DID NOT have a matching server route, and the AutoRun shell's `useAutoRunImageHandling` hook called `window.maestro.autorun.{listImages,saveImage,deleteImage}` at three sites that no-op'd in webFull until now. The AutoRun lift STOPPED on this gap. **Three-route cluster instead of one.** The W3-fs umbrella Decision's "trivial AutorunProvider with one method" reasoning held when only `writeDoc` was at stake. With three image methods carrying a distinct contract (docName-prefix filename generation, `images/` subdirectory convention, fixed extension allowlist, base64 decoding), folding them into `FsProvider` would have eroded the contract ŌĆö `FsProvider.writeDoc` accepts an arbitrary absolute path + string content, with no opinion about extensions or filename layout. The image methods enforce an opinion. A separate `AutorunProvider` interface preserves that boundary; the provider-registry pattern is otherwise identical to the umbrella's other 5 clusters byte-for-byte. **Architecture (new file + additive routes + additive wiring).** `src/server/autorun-manager.ts` (NEW, ~340 LOC including header doc + 4 validators + 3 manager methods + singleton accessor) ports the image-handling subset of `src/main/ipc/handlers/autorun.ts:501-752` 1:1, minus SSH remote dispatch (the renderer-side handlers proxy to `writeFileRemote` / `deleteRemote` / `readDirRemote` when `sshRemoteId` is set; the server-side surface rejects with 501 to match the W3-fs precedent ŌĆö `?sshRemoteId=` doesn't silently get a local result). `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts` (additive +~310 LOC) adds the `AutorunProvider` interface + `registerAutorunProvider()` + `getAutorunProvider()` + 3 route handlers + 3 inline validators (`validateImageRelativePath`, `validateAutorunDocFilename`, `validateImageExtension`) that mirror the manager-side validators 1:1 for defense-in-depth. The `folderPath` validator is REUSED from W3-fs (`validateFsPath`) so the rule synchronizes across the two clusters; the umbrella Decision named this as a requirement. `src/server/index.ts` (additive ~40 LOC) wires the manager via `getAutorunManager()` + `registerAutorunProvider({...})` before `server.start()`. **Defense-in-depth path validation in BOTH layers.** The renderer-side handlers do this too (autorun.ts:514-518 / 575-580 / 599-608 / 635-640 / 664-667); the W3-fs precedent established that the route layer validates BEFORE the provider sees the input, and the provider re-validates. This cluster follows the same pattern. The route layer's three new inline validators MUST stay in sync with their manager-side counterparts (`validateAutorunDocFilename` Ōåö `sanitizeDocName`, `validateImageExtension` Ōåö `sanitizeExtension`, `validateImageRelativePath` Ōåö `isValidImageRelativePath`) ŌĆö same rule as the W3-fs `validateFsPath()` Ōåö `isValidFsPath()` pair. **Reply shape extension over the renderer-side IPC.** The renderer-side `autorun:listImages` returns `{filename, relativePath}` per entry; the server-side route returns `{filename, relativePath, sizeBytes, modifiedAt}` (ISO-8601 mtime matches `src/server/fs-manager.ts:196` for cross-route consistency). The AutoRun shell will use `sizeBytes` for sort affordances + `modifiedAt` for cache-bust when the lift continues; surfacing them now means the lift doesn't need a second route round-trip per image. The renderer-side IPC contract is unchanged ŌĆö only the new REST route surfaces the wider shape. **`dataUrl` accepts both shapes.** The renderer-side `autorun:saveImage` accepts the bare base64 payload (the AutoRun shell strips the `data:image/...;base64,` prefix at `useAutoRunImageHandling.ts:274` before the IPC call). The server-side route accepts EITHER the bare payload OR the full data URL ŌĆö `decodeImageDataUrl()` handles both via a regex match-or-passthrough. A future webFull caller can post the FileReader result directly without manual stripping; existing renderer-side callers that strip the prefix continue to work. **`removed:false` on ENOENT for delete (softer than the renderer-side throw).** The renderer-side `autorun:deleteImage` throws "Image file not found" on ENOENT; the server-side route returns `{removed: false}` because the AutoRun shell's `removeAttachment` flow strips the entry from the optimistic-UI before the IPC call completes ŌĆö if the user clicks twice, the second delete should not throw. The renderer-side IPC contract is unchanged; only the new REST route is softer.
