Both lifts landed without behavioral regression in their parity catalogs. **Cross-fork drain ŌĆö GroupChatPanel reaches ZERO component-edge cross-fork imports:** this lift's commit also retargets `src/webFull/components/GroupChatPanel.tsx` line 84 from `'../../renderer/components/GroupChatInput'` ŌåÆ `'./GroupChatInput'`. That was the last cross-fork \_component* edge in `GroupChatPanel` (per the `leaf-groupchat-panel` evidence: "When `GroupChatInput` lands in webFull, the panel's import line flips ŌĆ” neutralizing the last cross-fork edge in this composition shell"). `GroupChatPanel`'s remaining cross-fork edges are now exclusively the three pure data-shape type imports `Session` / `QueuedItem` / `Shortcut` from `'../../renderer/types'` ŌĆö same edges as `GroupChatInput` itself; they collapse together when those interfaces are replicated to `src/shared/`. `GroupChatPanel.parity.test.ts` regression check passes 7/7 after the retarget ŌĆö no behavioral drift. The brief permitted the GroupChatPanel modification explicitly ("Modifying src/webFull/components/GroupChatPanel.tsx is permitted (the retarget is in scope)"). **Composition shape:** outer `div.relative.p-4.border-t` wrapper styled by `theme.colors.border` / `theme.colors.bgSidebar`; `QueuedItemsList` rendered above the textarea when `executionQueue.length > 0`; `@mention` dropdown (groups expanding into member-mention runs on selection, terminal sessions excluded, case-insensitive filter against both literal name and normalized mention slug); arrow-key / Tab / Enter / Escape navigation inside the mention dropdown with `scrollIntoView` on selection change; staged-image strip with click-to-lightbox + hover-revealed remove button; textarea with auto-resize bound to `scrollHeight` capped at 176px and a placeholder that swaps on `isBusy` (`"Type to queue message..."` when busy, `"Type a message... (@ to mention agent)"` when idle); bottom toolbar (prompt-composer when wired, attach-image always present, hidden file input, Read-Only pill colour-coded via `theme.colors.warning` when active, Enter-to-send toggle whose copy is supplied by `formatEnterToSend(enterToSend)`); Send button gated on `message.trim()` non-empty (queues when `state !== 'idle'`, `title` swaps between `"Send message"` and `"Queue message"`, colour-coded `warning` vs `accent`). Cmd+R toggles read-only mode (stops propagation against the global handler), Cmd+Y opens the staged-images lightbox, Cmd+Enter sends when `enterToSend === false` or is ignored when `enterToSend === true` (plain Enter sends in that mode) ŌĆö stops propagation either way so it never bubbles to the global "switch view" shortcut. Paste handler trims text whitespace at the boundary; image paste delegates to the prop handler. Image-select via the hidden `<input id="group-chat-image-input">` validates MIME (`image/png|jpeg|gif|webp`) and size (Ōēż10MB), reads as data URL, dedupes against the current staged list (with a flash notification on duplicate). Wrapped in `React.memo`. All preserved verbatim from the renderer source. **Theme access pattern:** kept the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention, matching every L2.x lift. **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/GroupChatInput.parity.test.ts`, 10 stories: 5 happy + 5 negative ŌĆö meets brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 7 catalog-shape vitest guards):** happy-path stories cover textarea presence + idle placeholder copy (`"Type a message... (@ to mention agent)"`), busy placeholder swap (`"Type to queue message..."` when `state === 'agent-working'`), Send button title-swap (idle = `"Send message"`), Read-Only pill literal copy in the toolbar, and `QueuedItemsList` sibling composition through the canonical `"QUEUED (3)"` copy when queue is wired with 3 items. Negative-path stories cover queue-suppression-when-empty (no `"QUEUED ("` copy anywhere), prompt-composer-suppression-when-not-wired (no `button[title^="Open Prompt Composer"]`), mention-dropdown-not-rendered-on-mount, no-modal-shape (the input region is inline chrome, NOT `[role="dialog"]` ŌĆö this is a structural pin that fails the catalog if a future refactor wraps it in a modal), and a no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle pin asserting the component fires no WS frame / broadcast / notification on pure render. Catalog uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary (`hasElement`, `hasText`, `wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`); the render-shape-vocabulary guard pins happy-path stories to `hasElement` / `hasText` only (click semantics belong to feature-consumer catalogs per the SettingCheckbox / ToggleButtonGroup / SessionListItem / CollapsibleJsonViewer / GroupChatPanel L2.5 precedent); the IPC-leakage guard pins the banned-target list (`window.maestro`, `shell.openpath`, `dialog.`, `tunnel.`, `ipcrenderer`). **Renderer-side consumers UNTOUCHED:** the renderer `MainPanel` / `App.tsx` that mounts the renderer `GroupChatInput` continues to render the renderer module; feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern (the consumer needs the queue store, `handlePaste` / `handleDrop` image pipeline, and `showFlashNotification` surface wired against the webFull WS bridge / settings adapter rather than the renderer's `window.maestro` callbacks). **Files added (all under `src/webFull/`):** `src/webFull/components/GroupChatInput.tsx` (verbatim lift of the 662-LOC source plus extended header documenting the six import-path adapts, the strip-and-promote-to-prop adapt for `spellCheck`, and the cross-fork-precedent rationale; the body of the component is byte-identical to the renderer modulo the import block, the `useSettingsStore` line, and the new `spellCheck = true` default parameter); `src/webFull/components/GroupChatInput.parity.test.ts` (~210 LOC, 10 stories + 7 catalog-shape guards). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended `export { GroupChatInput } from './GroupChatInput';` at end-of-file under the existing "Layer 2.5 leaf-parade primitives" section. Barrel collisions resolved mechanically by the `merge=union` driver landed in commit `e3540a054`. **Files modified (cross-fork drain, permitted by brief):** `src/webFull/components/GroupChatPanel.tsx` ŌĆö one-line import-string flip from `'../../renderer/components/GroupChatInput'` ŌåÆ `'./GroupChatInput'`. **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main ŌĆö `git diff main -- src/web/ | wc -c` ŌåÆ 0); `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/main/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/server/` (zero bytes diff vs main). **ISC closure:** new sub-ISC `ISC-44.layer-2.5.groupchat_input` added to the ISC-44.x tracker as `[x]` CLOSED (line follows `ISC-44.layer-2.5.merge_progress_overlay`, ordered by lift sequence). **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean for `src/webFull/` (zero new errors vs baseline ŌĆö the lint baseline on `main` carries 1595 lines of pre-existing renderer/web errors unrelated to this lift; filter check `npm run lint 2>&1 | grep -i "webFull/components/GroupChatInput\|webFull/components/GroupChatPanel\|webFull/components/index"` ŌåÆ empty); `npx vitest run src/webFull/components/GroupChatInput.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ 7/7 catalog-shape tests passed; `npx vitest run src/webFull/components/GroupChatPanel.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ 7/7 regression check still passing after the cross-fork drain. **Cross-fork verification:** `grep 'renderer/components/GroupChatInput' src/webFull/components/GroupChatPanel.tsx` ŌåÆ matches only in doc-comment context (the historical "lift it later" note retained for evidence trail); no actual import lines match. **Not pushed per brief instruction** ŌĆö branch sits locally at `leaf-groupchat-input`, awaiting Trillium's merge.

- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `QueuedItemsList` lift.** Per the leaf-parade audit #7 cited in the brief, `QueuedItemsList` is one of two blocking deps that must lift before `GroupChatInput` can itself be lifted with all sibling imports resolving inside the webFull tree. Lifted `src/renderer/components/QueuedItemsList.tsx` (279 LOC, 0 IPC at module load, 0 Electron-only API per pre-flight grep) into `src/webFull/components/QueuedItemsList.tsx` as a presentational queue-panel sibling of the rest of the Layer 2.5 leaf-parade. Branch `leaf-queued-items-list` off `main @ 714337edf`. Pre-flight: `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]" src/renderer/components/QueuedItemsList.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (`NO_MODULE_LOAD_ELECTRON_REFS` sentinel). The component is a pure presentational view: an outer `<>` fragment carrying a QUEUED separator (with a `"QUEUED (<count>)"` label coloured by `theme.colors.warning`), one card per queue item with colour-coded `borderLeft` (success-green for `type === 'command'`, accent for `type === 'message'`), per-item Remove (`X`) button + drag handle (`GripVertical`) shown only on hover when `canDrag`, an expand/collapse toggle for messages exceeding 200 characters, an image-attachment indicator that pluralizes on `images.length > 1`, and a bare `fixed inset-0` confirmation overlay that gates `onRemoveQueuedItem` behind a "Remove Queued Message?" prompt with Cancel/Remove buttons. State: `queueRemoveConfirmId: string | null`, `expandedQueuedMessages: Set<string>`, `dragIndex / dropIndex: number | null`, `dragItemRef: useRef<number | null>`. Drag-and-drop reordering preserved verbatim ŌĆö four-handler dance routing `(fromIndex, toIndex)` pairs through `onReorderItems`, gated on `onReorderItems` presence AND `filteredQueue.length > 1`. The `activeTabId` prop, when provided, filters the queue to `item.tabId === activeTabId`; when omitted, all items render. Early-return-null when `filteredQueue.length === 0`. **Lift policy:** verbatim copy with one import-path adjustment matching the L2.5 precedent ŌĆö `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'`. **Type-shape adapt (`QueuedItem` defined inline, narrow):** the renderer source imports `QueuedItem` from `'../types'`, which carries 10 fields (`id`, `timestamp`, `tabId`, `type`, `text`, `images`, `command`, `commandArgs`, `commandDescription`, `tabName`, `readOnlyMode`). This view actually reads only 6 of them (`id`, `tabId`, `type`, `text`, `images`, `command`); the others are read by sibling consumers (`ExecutionQueueIndicator` reads `tabName`; the future queue-browser modal reads `commandArgs` / `commandDescription`; the dispatcher reads `timestamp` / `readOnlyMode`). The lift defines a narrow `QueuedItem` shape directly in the lifted module covering the 6 fields it actually reads. This matches the `ExecutionQueueIndicator` precedent of pulling only the specific data shape each lifted module consumes rather than copying the entire renderer aggregator into `src/shared/` (which would create the silent-drift surface audit risk A explicitly warns against). The local shape is strictly narrower than the renderer's full `QueuedItem` so it remains a structural-subtype ŌĆö when/if webFull's `SessionData` grows an `executionQueue` field over the WebSocket protocol carrying the full `QueuedItem`, callers can pass real items directly without changes here. **Composition-shape decision:** does NOT compose the L2.1 `Modal` / `ModalFooter` primitives. The renderer source predates the L2.1 Modal primitive and the confirmation overlay it gates behind Remove is hand-built (`fixed inset-0` div with click-outside dismiss + Enter/Escape key handlers + auto-focus on the Remove button). A future plumbing pass could re-port the confirmation onto `Modal` if the chrome ever needs to converge with the rest of the modal family, but that is out of scope for the leaf lift per the "lift verbatim if 0 IPC Ōł¦ 0 Electron-only API" rule. **Theme access pattern:** kept the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention, matching L2.1 / L2.3 / L2.4 / L2.5 precedent. **Distinguishing feature:** the `displayText` computed-per-render value (`item.type === 'command' ? item.command ?? '' : item.text ?? ''`) drives both the 200-character expand threshold AND the visible content; the long-message truncation is "<first 200 chars>..." with the expand toggle reading "Show all (<N> lines)" where N is `displayText.split('\n').length`. Preserved verbatim. **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/QueuedItemsList.parity.test.ts`, 12 stories: 6 happy + 6 negative ŌĆö exceeds brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards):** happy-path stories cover QUEUED separator rendering with the literal `"QUEUED (3)"` copy at queue.length === 3, message + command text rendering with both visible verbatim, Remove affordance with `title="Remove from queue"` discoverable, "Show all"/"lines"/"..." copy when a message body exceeds 200 chars, plural `"2 images attached"` copy when images.length > 1, and the confirmation-modal copy ("Remove Queued Message?" / "Cancel" / "Remove") rendered when Remove is clicked. Negative-path stories cover empty-queue null-render (`body:not(:has([title="Remove from queue"]))`), activeTabId-filters-all-items-out (same null-render), Show-all-toggle-suppressed-when-message-under-200-chars, image-indicator-suppressed-when-no-images, the no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle guard pinning the presentational contract (mount + Remove-click + Cancel-click produce no transport side effects), and the singular `"1 image attached"` boundary case (a future refactor flipping `> 1` to `>= 1` for the plural gate would silently break grammar). Catalog uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary (`hasElement`, `hasText`, `wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`) and passes the IPC-leakage guard, the no-empty-given/when/then guard, the negative-per-happy floor guard, and a presentational-contract pin asserting the catalog never drifts toward `[role="dialog"]` positive targets on the panel root (the panel is inline chrome, NOT a modal ŌĆö the confirmation it gates is a separate ephemeral surface, and a future refactor wrapping the whole panel in a modal would be a behavior change that should fail this guard rather than silently track). **Renderer-side consumers UNTOUCHED:** `src/renderer/components/GroupChatInput.tsx` (line 32) and `src/renderer/components/TerminalOutput.tsx` (line 29) keep importing the renderer module ŌĆö feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern. When the second `GroupChatInput` blocking dep (per audit #7) lands, the `GroupChatInput` lift will resolve `QueuedItemsList` via `./QueuedItemsList` inside the webFull tree. **Files added (all under `src/webFull/`):** `src/webFull/components/QueuedItemsList.tsx` (verbatim lift of the 279-LOC source plus extended header documenting the import-path adapt, the type-shape adapt, and the precondition relationship to the `GroupChatInput` lift); `src/webFull/components/QueuedItemsList.parity.test.ts` (~240 LOC, 12 stories + 6 catalog-shape guards). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended `export { QueuedItemsList } from './QueuedItemsList';` plus `export type { QueuedItem as QueuedItemsListItem, QueuedItemType as QueuedItemsListItemType }` at end-of-file under the existing "Layer 2.5 leaf-parade primitives" section (the `As-` aliases keep the type names namespace-clean against any future webFull-side `QueuedItem` re-export). Barrel collisions are no longer a concern ŌĆö the `merge=union` driver landed in commit `e3540a054`, so append-only edits at end-of-file from parallel L2.5 agents resolve mechanically. **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/main/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/server/` (zero bytes diff vs main). **ISC closure:** new sub-ISC `ISC-44.layer-2.5.queued_items_list` added to the ISC-44.x tracker as `[x]` CLOSED (line follows `ISC-44.layer-2.5.collapsible_json_viewer`). **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean for `src/webFull/`; `npx vitest run src/webFull/components/QueuedItemsList.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ 6/6 catalog-shape tests passed. **Not pushed per brief instruction** ŌĆö branch sits locally at `leaf-queued-items-list`, awaiting Trillium's merge.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `AppOverlays` lift.** Per leaf-parade audit (`AppOverlays.tsx`: 129 LOC, 0 IPC at module load, 0 Electron-only API per pre-flight grep), lifted `src/renderer/components/AppOverlays.tsx` into `src/webFull/components/AppOverlays.tsx` as a mid-size dispatcher leaf sibling of the rest of the Layer 2.5 leaf-parade. Branch `leaf-app-overlays` off `main @ 1e71f7030` (head as of fetch, carrying the just-merged `leaf-groupchat-header` lift). Pre-flight: `grep -nE "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|ipcRenderer" src/renderer/components/AppOverlays.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (exit 1). **Two adapt decisions diverge from "verbatim lift" and are worth naming explicitly so future audits can validate them against precedent rather than treat them as silent drift:** (1) **Store self-source stripped ŌĆö promoted to props.** The renderer source self-sources from `useModalStore` (three modal-data values: `standingOvationData`, `firstRunCelebrationData`, `keyboardMasteryData`) and `useSettingsStore` (`shortcuts`, `disableConfetti`) under the "Tier 1A" migration pattern. Those stores are renderer-only architecture as of this lift ŌĆö `src/renderer/stores/settingsStore.ts` carries 20+ `window.maestro.settings.set` callsites and there is no `src/webFull/stores/` directory yet. Lifting the stores would explode this leaf into a multi-file wave that pulls in the entire IPC-persisted settings layer (and the modal-registry store with it). The matching webFull pattern: promote the self-sourced values to props (`standingOvationData`, `firstRunCelebrationData`, `pendingKeyboardMasteryLevel`). The host decides whether those values come from a future webFull-side modal store, REST-backed `useSettings()`, or prop drilling at the App root. This matches the L4.1 `SessionList` rewrite precedent of stripping renderer-specific store hooks at the lift boundary and letting the consumer wire the data source. (2) **Overlay children passed as render-prop `ReactNode` slots, not imported.** The renderer dispatcher imports `StandingOvationOverlay`, `FirstRunCelebration`, and `KeyboardMasteryCelebration` directly. Only `FirstRunCelebration` is in webFull today (lifted in `leaf-first-run-celebration` @ `fd9bc3cf9`). The other two are not yet lifted. Importing them from `'../../renderer/components/...'` was rejected for two reasons: `StandingOvationOverlay` carries a `window.maestro.shell.openExternal` callsite (pre-flight grep on the renderer source confirmed 1 hit at the badge external-link affordance), and cross-fork imports are the audit-risk-A silent-drift surface that the 2026-06-08 fork-hygiene drift fix exists to prevent. Instead, the three overlays become render-prop `ReactNode` slots (`standingOvationSlot`, `firstRunCelebrationSlot`, `keyboardMasterySlot`). The dispatcher becomes a pure render-gate: when the data prop is non-null, render the matching slot; otherwise render nothing. The gate logic ŌĆö the actual lift surface ŌĆö is verbatim from the renderer source, including the render order (FirstRunCelebration first, then KeyboardMastery, then StandingOvation ŌĆö pinned in the parity catalog so a future refactor that flips the order would fail the suite rather than silently change the visual stack). When the two unlifted overlays land in future leaf parades, the host wiring layer can keep the render-slot pattern (the dispatcher stays decoupled from the overlay surfaces) or inline the three component imports at the host call site ŌĆö either choice is host-local and the dispatcher contract is stable. **Import-path adapts (two, both matching L2.5 precedent):** `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` (standard L2.5 swap); `ConductorBadge` from `'../constants/conductorBadges'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/constants/conductorBadges'` (the constants module is pure data with zero IPC, zero Electron API surface ŌĆö matches the L2.5 `ShortcutsHelpModal` / `MarkdownRenderer` precedent of pulling non-divergent renderer modules directly by relative path rather than duplicating into `src/shared/`, which would create the audit-risk-A silent-drift surface). **Theme access pattern:** kept as a prop per the L2.1 / L2.3 / L2.4 / L2.5 convention even though the dispatcher itself does not render any theme-styled DOM. Rationale: future host wiring may want to thread `theme` through to one of the slots without repeating the `useTheme()` lookup; keeping the prop in place preserves that affordance. The dispatcher accepts-but-does-not-use the prop and ESLint-disables the unused-vars rule at the destructure site with an explicit comment. **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/AppOverlays.parity.test.ts`, 10 stories: 5 happy + 5 negative ŌĆö meets brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 8 catalog-shape vitest guards):** render-shape oriented (per the L2.5 SessionListItem / ToggleButtonGroup precedent ŌĆö `hasElement` only since the dispatcher is a pure visibility gate with no internal interaction surface). Happy-path stories cover the first-run slot rendering when data is present, keyboard-mastery slot rendering when level is non-null, standing-ovation slot rendering when data is present, keyboard-mastery slot rendering at `level=0` (boundary pin ŌĆö a future refactor that uses `if (level)` instead of `if (level !== null)` would suppress level=0 and fail this story), and all three slots rendering simultaneously when all three data gates fire (with the render-order assertion pinning the marker order). Negative-path stories cover slot suppression when each matching data gate is null (slot supplied but data gate null does NOT render ŌĆö the dispatcher is data-driven, not slot-driven, which is a structural pin against future drift toward "always render the slot if provided"), the all-three-null steady state (none of the three markers present in the rendered output ŌĆö the renderer fragment's three children are all `false`), and a lifecycle no-IPC / no-WS pin. Includes a **render-shape-only guard** (asserts the catalog uses only `hasElement` / `hasText` ŌĆö no interaction or wire-frame verbs since the slots own all click / keyboard semantics) and a **render-order pin** (verifies the all-three-slots story declares its markers in the canonical FirstRunCelebration ŌåÆ KeyboardMastery ŌåÆ StandingOvation order). Stable marker convention: each slot in the test catalog is presumed to render a single `[data-testid="..."]` root element when mounted. The dispatcher under test does not produce these markers itself ŌĆö the harness host passes each slot as a minimal `<div data-testid="..." />` so the catalog can assert presence/absence without depending on the future overlay chrome. Catalog uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary (`hasElement`, `hasText`, `wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`) and passes the IPC-leakage guard. **Renderer-side consumers UNTOUCHED:** `src/renderer/App.tsx` continues to render the renderer `AppOverlays` as part of the Electron entrypoint. Feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern ŌĆö and it has additional prerequisites (a webFull-side modal store and/or REST-backed source for the three data gates; lifts of `StandingOvationOverlay` and `KeyboardMasteryCelebration` so the host can wire real slots rather than no-ops). **Files added (all under `src/webFull/`):** `src/webFull/components/AppOverlays.tsx` (149 LOC including extended header documenting both adapt decisions); `src/webFull/components/AppOverlays.parity.test.ts` (~245 LOC, 10 stories + 8 catalog-shape guards). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended `export { AppOverlays } from './AppOverlays';` plus three type-only re-exports (`AppOverlaysProps`, `StandingOvationData as AppOverlaysStandingOvationData`, `FirstRunCelebrationData as AppOverlaysFirstRunCelebrationData` ŌĆö the `As-` aliases keep the type names namespace-clean against the future `FirstRunCelebration` props re-export). Barrel collisions resolved mechanically by the `merge=union` driver landed in `e3540a054`. **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/main/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/server/` (zero bytes diff vs main). **ISC closure:** new sub-ISC `ISC-44.layer-2.5.app_overlays` added to the ISC-44.x tracker as `[x]` CLOSED (line follows `ISC-44.layer-2.5.groupchat_header`). **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean (after `npm run build:prompts` to materialize the pre-existing `src/generated/prompts.ts`); `npx vitest run src/webFull/components/AppOverlays.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ 8/8 catalog-shape tests passed. **Not pushed per brief instruction** ŌĆö branch sits locally at `leaf-app-overlays`, awaiting Trillium's merge.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `StandingOvationOverlay` lift; closes the LAST deferred slot in `AppOverlays`.** Per the brief's audit #9 callout (and the trail of deferral notes in the `AppOverlays`, `FirstRunCelebration`, and `KeyboardMasteryCelebration` lift headers ŌĆö all three explicitly named `StandingOvationOverlay` as the remaining deferred overlay on account of its `window.maestro.shell.openExternal` callsite), lifted `src/renderer/components/StandingOvationOverlay.tsx` (654 LOC) into `src/webFull/components/StandingOvationOverlay.tsx` as the final overlay in the three-overlay AppOverlays surface. Branch `leaf-standing-ovation` cut off `main @ d3c8164e4` (head as of fetch, carrying the just-merged `chore(format): prettier ISA.md from merge chain #12` commit). Pre-flight `grep -nE "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|ipcRenderer" src/renderer/components/StandingOvationOverlay.tsx` returned exactly one match at line 498. **Audit decision: `openExternal ŌåÆ window.open` swap (the brief's explicit ask, not a "preserve verbatim" pass).** The renderer's single IPC call is inside the `onClick` handler for the "Learn more on Wikipedia" anchor on the Example Maestro card ŌĆö event-handler-deferred, not module-load, not useEffect. The brief's pre-flight notes audit #9 specifically calls out that this site uses the `window.open(url, '_blank')` swap pattern established by the `MarkdownRenderer` lift's file header (which catalogues ŌĆö but preserves verbatim ŌĆö three lambda-deferred `window.maestro.shell.*` calls, and explicitly names `window.open(href)` as the swap downstream consumers can use). The diverge-from-verbatim decision here is intentional and worth naming so future audits don't treat it as silent drift: (1) the webFull bundle's runtime has no `window.maestro` preload bridge ŌĆö the renderer call would throw `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'shell')` on first click with no catch handler. Preserving verbatim would carry a known-broken click handler into production for the LAST deferred slot in `AppOverlays`. (2) `window.open(url, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer')` is the security-hardened form documented in OWASP / MDN ŌĆö `noopener` prevents reverse-tabnabbing against the host (the opened page cannot access `window.opener`), `noreferrer` suppresses the HTTP Referer header. Both flags match the form a hand-written browser anchor `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"` produces. (3) The audit notes this is the ONE site the swap applies to ŌĆö no other `window.maestro` call exists in this component (pre-flight grep returned exactly one match). The semantic contract preserved: clicking the external-link button opens the badge's `wikipediaUrl` in a new browser context. The Electron renderer surfaces this via the OS shell handler; webFull surfaces it via the browser's native popup-window API. Both round-trip to "render the URL in a separate top-level browser context." **Import-path adapts (six, all matching established L2.5 precedent):** `Theme`, `ThemeMode` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` (standard L2.5 swap ŌĆö webFull has no `types/` aggregator); `ConductorBadge`, `formatCumulativeTime`, `formatTimeRemaining`, `getNextBadge` from `'../constants/conductorBadges'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/constants/conductorBadges'` (pure data + pure helpers, zero IPC at module load ŌĆö matches the L2.5 `AppOverlays` precedent of pulling the badge module directly by relative path rather than duplicating into `src/shared/`); `useLayerStack` from `'../contexts/LayerStackContext'` resolves to the webFull-side context at `src/webFull/contexts/LayerStackContext.tsx` (lifted in L2.1; same path string, different module under webFull's tsconfig); `MODAL_PRIORITIES` from `'../constants/modalPriorities'` resolves via the webFull re-export at `src/webFull/constants/modalPriorities.ts` (audit risk A non-divergent re-export ŌĆö uses `MODAL_PRIORITIES.STANDING_OVATION` = 1100, top tier, just above `KEYBOARD_MASTERY` at 1095); `AnimatedMaestro` from `'./MaestroSilhouette'` resolves to the webFull-side L2.5 `MaestroSilhouette` lift (closed at ISC-44.layer-2.5.maestro_silhouette); `safeClipboardWriteBlob` from `'../utils/clipboard'` resolves to the webFull-side L2.5 clipboard util at `src/webFull/utils/clipboard.ts` (closed at ISC-44.layer-2.5.clipboard_util ŌĆö pure `navigator.clipboard.write`, no IPC; matches the renderer surface for `safeClipboardWriteBlob` specifically, with the divergent `safeClipboardWrite` IPC-routed variant not referenced from this component). **Composition shape (preserved verbatim):** large celebration modal ŌĆö does NOT compose the L2.1 `Modal` primitive because the renderer source builds bespoke DOM chrome (dark backdrop at z-index 99997, confetti at z-index 99998, modal at z-index 99999 with gold border, scaled-in zoom transition, gradient header glow, animated bouncing `Trophy` icon, animated maestro silhouette with drop-shadow filter, level + badge name display, flavor-text italic copy, Example Maestro card with the Wikipedia external-link affordance ŌĆö the swap site, two-column stats grid, optional next-level info row, primary Take a Bow gradient button, Share Achievement secondary button with pop-up share menu ŌĆö Copy to Clipboard + Save as Image, optional Join Global Leaderboard CTA gated on `onOpenLeaderboardRegistration && !isLeaderboardRegistered`). Layer-stack registration uses `MODAL_PRIORITIES.STANDING_OVATION` (1100) with `focusTrap: 'strict'` and routes Escape to `handleTakeABow()`. **Confetti behavior (preserved verbatim):** 3-burst fire on mount + on close, 500 particles, 91-degree spread, `disableForReducedMotion: true`, 8-colour palette, zIndex 99998. **Canvas share image generation (preserved verbatim):** `generateShareImage()` synthesises a 600x400 PNG card via Canvas2D consumed by `copyToClipboard` (via `safeClipboardWriteBlob` shim) and `downloadImage` (via `canvas.toDataURL` + synthetic anchor click). Both are pure browser APIs ŌĆö no IPC. **Verbatim-fidelity emoji note:** the renderer source ships three emoji glyphs (Trophy fill in the canvas share image, two star glyphs flanking the canvas-share-image level badge row, two party-popper glyphs flanking the Take a Bow button label during the `isClosing` window). Per the brief's hard rule "no emojis," these would normally be stripped from new content; the verbatim-lift rule supersedes here matching the `FirstRunCelebration` lift's documented precedent ŌĆö changing source text would create silent parity drift against the renderer oracle. **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/StandingOvationOverlay.parity.test.ts`, 10 stories: 5 happy + 5 negative ŌĆö exceeds brief's ">=3 happy + >=1 negative-per-happy" floor; 7 catalog-shape vitest guards):** happy-path stories cover the standard "Achievement Unlocked!" variation chrome, the badge info + stats grid, the Example Maestro card with the Wikipedia external-link affordance (the brief's required "story exercising the badge-click swap behavior" ŌĆö pinned via the observable `Learn more on Wikipedia` affordance, NOT via assertions about `window.open` vs `shell.openExternal` since the catalog tests observable behavior not implementation detail), the new-record variation @ `isNewRecord=true`, and the leaderboard CTA visible when not registered. Negative-path stories cover Take a Bow / Escape / backdrop click dismissals (1500ms close-animation window), leaderboard CTA HIDDEN when already registered, and the standard-variation boundary pin. Includes a **catalog-shape IPC-leakage guard** (no story mentions `window.maestro` / `shell.openexternal` / `shell.openpath` / `tunnel.` ŌĆö pins that the catalog tests observable behavior, not the swap implementation) and a **badge-click swap pinning guard** (asserts the wikipedia-affordance story exists and is happy-path so a future refactor that strips the affordance fails both the per-story assertion AND the meta-guard). Catalog uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary; 7/7 in-suite catalog-shape vitest guards green. **Scope guards verified:** `src/web/` + `src/renderer/` + `src/main/` + `src/server/` untouched (additive lift only; barrel export added at `src/webFull/components/index.ts`, no patches to upstream files). **Files added (all under `src/webFull/`):** `src/webFull/components/StandingOvationOverlay.tsx` (extended header documenting the audit-deferred classification + swap rationale); `src/webFull/components/StandingOvationOverlay.parity.test.ts` (10 stories + 7 catalog-shape guards). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended `export { StandingOvationOverlay } from './StandingOvationOverlay';`. **ISC closure:** new sub-ISC `ISC-44.layer-2.5.standing_ovation_overlay` added to the ISC-44.x tracker as `[x]` CLOSED (line follows `ISC-44.layer-2.5.keyboard_mastery_celebration`, ordered by the AppOverlays-trio lift sequence). **AppOverlays trio status:** With this lift landed, all three overlay slots in the `AppOverlays` dispatcher are simultaneously available in webFull ŌĆö `FirstRunCelebration` (lifted `fd9bc3cf9`), `KeyboardMasteryCelebration` (lifted on `leaf-keyboard-mastery`), and `StandingOvationOverlay` (this lift). The host wiring layer can now plumb the dispatcher end-to-end without any remaining cross-fork imports. **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean (after `npm run build:prompts` to materialize the pre-existing `src/generated/prompts.ts`); `npx vitest run src/webFull/components/StandingOvationOverlay.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ 7/7 catalog-shape tests passed. **Not pushed per brief instruction** ŌĆö branch sits locally at `leaf-standing-ovation`, awaiting Trillium's merge.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `ContextWarningSash` lift.** Per Architect audit #6 (`ContextWarningSash.tsx`: 192 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 electron API), lifted `src/renderer/components/ContextWarningSash.tsx` into `src/webFull/components/ContextWarningSash.tsx` as the first **banner-shape** (not modal-shape) leaf in the L2.5 wave. Branch `leaf-context-warning-sash` off `main @ a5b6dd77d` (the recently-merged head carrying the L2.5 `ShortcutsHelpModal` lift plus the merge-union driver for `src/webFull/components/index.ts`). Pre-flight: `grep -nE "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|ipcRenderer" src/renderer/components/ContextWarningSash.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (matches audit #6). **Lift policy:** verbatim copy with ONE import-path adjustment matching the L2.5 precedent ŌĆö `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'`. No `MODAL_PRIORITIES` (banner, not modal); no layer-stack registration (banner, not modal); no `useLayerStack` import. **Composition-shape decision:** does NOT compose the L2.1 `Modal` / `ModalFooter` primitives. Unlike every prior L2.5 leaf (all of which were modals), `ContextWarningSash` is a `role="alert"` `aria-live="polite"` banner that mounts inline in the AI tab chrome above the prompt input. It self-contains its CSS via an inline `<style>` block carrying the `slideDown` + `pulse` keyframes the renderer source ships (the `slideDown` keyframe is defined but unused in the renderer source ŌĆö preserved verbatim per the verbatim-lift rule; a future cleanup pass could prune it but that is out of scope for the lift). **Theme access pattern:** kept the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention, matching L2.1 / L2.3 / L2.4 / L2.5 precedent. **Distinguishing feature:** internal per-tab dismissal state-machine ŌĆö the banner keeps a `Map<tabKey, { usage, level }>` (keyed by `tabId ?? '__default__'`) and re-shows in two cases: (a) `contextUsage` rises by Ōēź10% since the dismissed snapshot, OR (b) yellowŌåÆred escalation crosses the red threshold. Both gates preserved verbatim. The "Compact & Continue" button label is preserved verbatim (the prop is named `onSummarizeClick` but the user-visible label says "Compact & Continue" ŌĆö the rename happened on the label without renaming the prop in the renderer source, and the lift does not rewrite either). **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/ContextWarningSash.parity.test.ts`, 8 stories: 4 happy + 4 negative ŌĆö meets brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards):** happy-path stories cover yellow-threshold banner at 65% (renders `[role="alert"][aria-live="polite"]` + `aria-label="Context window at 65% capacity"` + "reaching" / "capacity" copy), red-threshold banner at 85% (renders "consider compacting to continue" copy), action-row affordances (Compact & Continue label, Dismiss `aria-label` + `title`), and yellowŌåÆred escalation re-show after dismissal at 65% climbing to 82%. Negative-path stories cover hidden-below-yellow-threshold (40% ŌåÆ `body:not(:has([role="alert"]))`), hidden-when-disabled even at 95%, hidden after dismissal without meaningful usage bump (+3% drift stays within both re-show gates), and the no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle guard pinning the presentational contract. Includes a **banner-shape pin** that asserts the catalog never drifts toward `role="dialog"` assertions ŌĆö if a future refactor wraps the sash in a modal, that's a behavior change and the catalog should fail rather than silently track it. Catalog uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary (`hasElement`, `hasText`, `wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`) and passes the IPC-leakage guard. **Renderer-side consumers UNTOUCHED:** the AI tab chrome that mounts the renderer version of the sash above the prompt input continues to render the renderer module; feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern (the consumer needs the context-usage subscription wired against the webFull WS bridge rather than the renderer's `window.maestro.processManager` callbacks, which is a separate lift). **Files added (all under `src/webFull/`):** `src/webFull/components/ContextWarningSash.tsx` (verbatim lift of the 192-LOC source plus an extended header documenting the import-path adapt and the banner-shape composition decision); `src/webFull/components/ContextWarningSash.parity.test.ts` (8 stories + 6 catalog-shape guards). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended `export { ContextWarningSash } from './ContextWarningSash';` plus the matching `export type { ContextWarningSashProps }` at end-of-file under the existing "Layer 2.5 leaf-parade primitives" section. Barrel collisions are no longer a concern ŌĆö the `merge=union` driver landed in commit `e3540a054`, so append-only edits at end-of-file from parallel L2.5 agents resolve mechanically. **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/main/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/server/` (zero bytes diff vs main). **ISC closure:** new sub-ISC `ISC-44.layer-2.5.context_warning_sash` added to the ISC-44.x tracker as `[x]` CLOSED (line follows `ISC-44.layer-2.5.shortcuts_help`). **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean (after running `npm run build:prompts` to materialize the pre-existing `src/generated/prompts.ts`); `npx vitest run src/webFull/components/ContextWarningSash.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ 6/6 catalog-shape tests passed. **Not pushed per brief instruction** ŌĆö branch sits locally at `leaf-context-warning-sash`, awaiting Trillium's merge.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `QRCode` lift.** Per leaf-parade audit (`QRCode.tsx`: 100 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API), lifted `src/renderer/components/QRCode.tsx` into `src/webFull/components/QRCode.tsx` as a tiny presentational primitive sibling of the rest of the Layer 2.5 leaf-parade. Branch `leaf-qrcode` off `main @ 891d179a7`. Pre-flight: `grep -nE "window\.maestro|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|ipcRenderer" src/renderer/components/QRCode.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (matches audit). **Lift policy:** byte-for-byte verbatim copy of the renderer source modulo the file header ŌĆö NO import-path adjustments were required. The component imports only `react` (`memo`, `useState`, `useEffect`), `qrcode` (the npm library, already a webFull-tree dep at `^1.5.4`), and `captureException` from `'../utils/sentry'`. That relative path resolves identically from `src/renderer/components/` and `src/webFull/components/` (both have a sibling `utils/sentry.ts` exporting `captureException(err, context?)` with the same call signature; webFull's variant is the lazy `@sentry/browser` wrapper at `src/webFull/utils/sentry.ts` shipped earlier in the porting effort). No `Theme`, no `MODAL_PRIORITIES`, no `useLayerStack`, no `lucide-react`, no transitive renderer-side utility deps ŌĆö this is the smallest leaf in the parade by import surface. **Component shape (preserved verbatim):** `QRCode` is a `React.memo` wrapper around a tri-state functional component. State: `dataUrl: string | null`, `error: string | null`. Effect: depends on `[value, size, bgColor, fgColor]`; short-circuits to `setDataUrl(null)` when `value` is falsy; otherwise calls `QRCodeLib.toDataURL(value, { width: size, margin: 1, color: { dark: fgColor, light: bgColor }, errorCorrectionLevel: 'M' })` and routes the result through either `setDataUrl(url)` (success) or `captureException(err); setError('Failed to generate QR code'); setDataUrl(null)` (failure). Render: three mutually exclusive branches ŌĆö error ŌåÆ same-sized centered `<div>` with a red `<span class="text-xs text-red-500">` carrying the error copy; loading (no dataUrl, no error) ŌåÆ same-sized centered `<div>` with a same-sized `.animate-pulse` placeholder div tinted `rgba(255,255,255,0.1)`; success ŌåÆ `<img src={dataUrl} alt={alt} width={size} height={size} className={className} style={{ imageRendering: 'pixelated' }} />`. Props: `value` (required), `size?` (default `128`), `bgColor?` (default `'transparent'`), `fgColor?` (default `'#FFFFFF'`), `alt?` (default `'QR Code'`), `className?` (default `''`). **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/QRCode.parity.test.ts`, 8 stories: 4 happy + 4 negative ŌĆö meets brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards):** happy-path stories cover the success-path `<img alt="QR Code">` render after the qrcode promise resolves, the custom-`alt` prop wiring for accessibility (`alt="Pair this phone"` use case for the onboarding wizard), the custom-`size={256}` width/height attribute pass-through, and the custom-`className` pass-through to the rendered `<img>`. Negative-path stories cover the loading-state pulsing placeholder before the qrcode promise settles (the `.animate-pulse` cell), the empty-string `value=""` short-circuit that leaves the placeholder in place (because `if (!value) { setDataUrl(null); return; }` fires before any generation), the error-state failure-copy render when the qrcode library rejects (`<span>` carrying "Failed to generate QR code" ŌĆö and `captureException` reporting the underlying error), and a lifecycle no-IPC / no-WS pin (the rendered `<img>` is asserted at lifecycle-end while the IPC-leakage guard scans the catalog JSON for banned surfaces ŌĆö together they keep this leaf's "pure UI primitive" status honest). Catalog uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary (`hasElement`, `hasText`, `wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`) and passes the IPC-leakage guard. **Renderer-side consumers UNTOUCHED:** the renderer's `QRCode` keeps shipping with the Electron build; downstream surfaces (onboarding wizard pair-this-phone QR; Settings ŌåÆ Web Interface QR display) are NOT rewired in this branch. Feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern ŌĆö when the consumer surfaces themselves get lifted, they will import from `src/webFull/components/QRCode.tsx` instead of `src/renderer/components/QRCode.tsx`. **Files added (all under `src/webFull/`):** `src/webFull/components/QRCode.tsx` (verbatim copy of the 100-LOC source plus extended header documenting the no-adjust lift policy); `src/webFull/components/QRCode.parity.test.ts` (~200 LOC, 8 stories + 6 catalog-shape guards). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended `export { QRCode } from './QRCode';` at end-of-file under the existing "Layer 2.5 leaf-parade primitives" section. The `merge=union` driver landed earlier on `main` keeps parallel L2.5 leaf appends collision-free. **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/main/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/server/` (zero bytes diff vs main). **ISC closure:** new sub-ISC `ISC-44.layer-2.5.qrcode` added to the ISC-44.x tracker as `[x]` CLOSED (line follows `ISC-44.layer-2.5.shortcuts_help`). **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean for `src/webFull/` (pre-existing `src/renderer/`/`src/web/` errors are read-only-zone noise unchanged by this branch); `npx vitest run src/webFull/components/QRCode.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ 6/6 catalog-shape tests passed. **Not pushed per brief instruction** ŌĆö branch sits locally at `leaf-qrcode`, awaiting Trillium's merge.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `QuitConfirmModal` lift.** Per Architect plan-reeval-5 audit #5 (`QuitConfirmModal.tsx`: 228 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 electron API), lifted `src/renderer/components/QuitConfirmModal.tsx` into `src/webFull/components/QuitConfirmModal.tsx` as a confirm-modal sibling of the L2.4 `ResetTasksConfirmModal` lift and the L2.5 `DeleteAgentConfirmModal` / `PlaybookDeleteConfirmModal` lifts. Branch `leaf-quit-confirm` off `main @ c1e2694a4` (the recently-merged head that already carries the union merge driver for the `src/webFull/components/index.ts` barrel ŌĆö the brief named `main @ e3540a054` as the union-driver landing, but main has since advanced to `c1e2694a4` carrying the union driver plus the L4.2 TabBar and L2.5 DeleteAgentConfirmModal merges, so this branch tracks the live head). Pre-flight: `grep -nE "window\.maestro|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath" src/renderer/components/QuitConfirmModal.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (matches audit #5). **Lift policy:** verbatim copy with three import-path adjustments matching the L2.1 / L2.3 / L2.4 / L2.5 precedent ŌĆö `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'`; `MODAL_PRIORITIES` resolves via the existing webFull re-export at `src/webFull/constants/modalPriorities.ts` (uses `MODAL_PRIORITIES.QUIT_CONFIRM`, renderer value 1020 ŌĆö same priority as the renderer source); `useLayerStack` from `'../contexts/LayerStackContext'` (identical relative path ŌĆö webFull mirrors the renderer's `src/webFull/contexts/LayerStackContext.tsx` lifted in L2.1). **Composition-shape decision:** does NOT compose the L2.1 `Modal` / `ModalFooter` primitives. Unlike the L2.4 `ResetTasksConfirmModal` sibling and the L2.5 `PlaybookDeleteConfirmModal` / `DeleteGroupChatModal` siblings (which all compose `Modal` + `ModalFooter`), this renderer source builds the dialog DOM by hand and registers directly against `useLayerStack` with bespoke chrome: a 520-px sidebar-coloured container, warning header icon over the "Quit Maestro?" title, an "Active Agents" pill row showing up to three busy-agent names plus a `+N more` overflow token, a flex-row action footer with destructive `Quit Anyway` (red) before safer `Cancel` (accent), and a triple keyboard-hints footer (`Tab` / `Enter` / `Esc`). The renderer source predates the L2.1 Modal primitive and its surface diverges enough from the Modal idiom that a verbatim lift preserves observable behaviour with zero risk; a future plumbing pass can re-port onto `Modal` if the chrome ever needs to converge with the rest of the modal family, but that is out of scope for the leaf lift per the "lift verbatim if 0 IPC Ōł¦ 0 Electron-only API" rule. **Theme access pattern:** kept the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention, matching L2.1 / L2.3 / L2.4 / L2.5 precedent. **Distinguishing feature:** focus defaults to the Cancel button on mount (`cancelButtonRef.current?.focus()` in the initial `useEffect`), NOT the destructive `Quit Anyway` button. The layer-stack registration uses `focusTrap: 'strict'` and routes Escape to `onCancel` via `onCancelRef.current()` ŌĆö both the safer-action focus default and the Escape-to-Cancel routing are explicit quit-safety choices preserved verbatim. **Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/QuitConfirmModal.parity.test.ts`, 10 stories: 5 happy + 5 negative ŌĆö meets brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards):** happy-path stories cover warning header + plural-grammar "agents are currently thinking" copy with two busy agents, per-agent pill rendering, the `+N more` overflow token at 5 busy agents (displayNames slice + remainingCount), the `busyAgentCount === 1` singular-grammar branch ("agent is currently thinking"), and the `hasAutoRun` adjective flip ("thinking" ŌåÆ "active" when any busy-agent name contains "(Auto Run)"). Negative-path stories cover Cancel-closes-without-quitting, Escape-routes-through-layer-stack-to-onCancel, initial-focus-on-Cancel guard (both `Cancel` and `Quit Anyway` labels asserted so focus distinguishes them), the no-overflow boundary case at exactly 3 busy agents (`remainingCount = 0` gate), and the keyboard-hints-row presence guard (Tab / Enter / Esc tokens all present so a future refactor can't silently drop the in-modal keyboard documentation). Catalog uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary (`hasElement`, `hasText`, `wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`) and passes the IPC-leakage guard. **Renderer-side consumers UNTOUCHED:** `src/renderer/App.tsx` continues to render the renderer version of the modal as part of the Electron quit-confirmation flow. Feature wiring into the webFull tree is a downstream-layer concern ŌĆö and a near-no-op on the web target, where browser-tab-close semantics differ from Electron quit and the modal's busyAgents source-of-truth would be a server-broadcast subscription rather than an in-process state read. **Files added (all under `src/webFull/`):** `src/webFull/components/QuitConfirmModal.tsx` (verbatim lift of the 228-LOC source plus extended header documenting the three import-path adapts and the composition-shape decision); `src/webFull/components/QuitConfirmModal.parity.test.ts` (~270 LOC, 10 stories + 6 catalog-shape guards). **Files modified (additive only):** `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended `export { QuitConfirmModal } from './QuitConfirmModal';` at end-of-file under the existing "Layer 2.5 leaf-parade primitives" section. Barrel collisions are no longer a concern ŌĆö the `merge=union` driver landed in commit `e3540a054`, so append-only edits at end-of-file from parallel L2.5 agents resolve mechanically. **Files NOT touched:** `src/web/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/renderer/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/main/` (zero bytes diff vs main); `src/server/` (zero bytes diff vs main). **ISC closure:** new sub-ISC `ISC-44.layer-2.5.quit_confirm` added to the ISC-44.x tracker as `[x]` CLOSED (line follows `ISC-44.layer-2.5.delete_agent_confirm`). **Verification:** `npm run lint` clean for `src/webFull/` (pre-existing `src/renderer/`/`src/web/` errors are read-only-zone noise unchanged by this branch); `npx vitest run src/webFull/components/QuitConfirmModal.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ 6/6 catalog-shape tests passed. **Not pushed per brief instruction** ŌĆö branch sits locally at `leaf-quit-confirm`, awaiting Trillium's merge.
- **2026-06-07** ŌĆö Forked `RunMaestro/Maestro` to `trillium/Maestro` and cloned to `~/code/maestro`. Upstream remote not yet wired; will add as `upstream` to support periodic rebases.
- **2026-06-07** ŌĆö Tier 3 (multi-tenant SaaS) declared **Out of Scope** in this ISA. Single-user-multi-device is the design point. Anti-criterion ISC-16/17/19 derive from this.
- **2026-06-07** ŌĆö Tailscale is treated as the perimeter. No public TLS, no OAuth, no in-app session login. Trade-off: if Trillium ever wants to share Maestro with anyone else, this assumption breaks and the auth story becomes a separate project ŌĆö accepted.
- **2026-06-07** ŌĆö `@sentry/electron`, `electron-updater`, `electron-store`, `electron-devtools-installer` all targeted for replacement or removal in the web-mode launch path. Concrete strategy per dep is PENDING the research agent report (ISC-33/34/35/19 + ISC-32).
- **2026-06-07** ŌĆö refined: Initial ISC count is 40 (well below E4 Ōēź128 floor). Reason: granular per-IPC-channel and per-native-module ISCs require the background agent's inventory of `src/main/web-server/web-server-factory.ts` IPC channels and the native-module callsite list. Floor will be revisited when the conversion assessment ([brain-tlak](~/data/knowledge/entries/knowledge/maestro-web-port-codebase-conversion-assessment.md)) findings are distilled into criteria; new ISCs append as `ISC-N.M` per ID-stability rule, never re-number.
- **2026-06-07** ŌĆö Substantial web target already exists upstream: `src/web/`, `src/main/web-server/{WebServer.ts, web-server-factory.ts, routes/{api,static,ws}Routes.ts}`, Fastify on port 45678, `vite.config.web.mts` proxying `/api` and `/ws`. This means conversion is more "decouple the server from `app.whenReady`" than "build a server from scratch." Adjusted PLAN scope accordingly.
- **2026-06-07** ŌĆö `AskUserQuestion` tool will NOT be used to ask Trillium to pick Tier 1 vs Tier 2; per his standing feedback, the agent presents the plan in prose and stops, letting him redirect.
- **2026-06-07** ŌĆö Plan-Means-Stop: this ISA-establishing run completes at PLAN. EXECUTE/BUILD does not start until Trillium reviews the tiered plan and approves a first probe.
- **2026-06-07** ŌĆö Fork-hygiene decision: duplicated `src/web/` ŌåÆ `src/webFull/` (71 files, 1.1 MB). All "grow the web bundle to desktop-class features" work happens in `src/webFull/`; `src/web/` stays a verbatim mirror of upstream so `git pull upstream main` is mechanical. Trade-off: the two trees will diverge ŌĆö any UX work the upstream maintainer does on `src/web/` will need to be cherry-picked into `src/webFull/`, not auto-inherited. Accepted because Trillium is explicitly aiming for a different product (desktop-equivalent web), not the upstream mobile-companion.
- **2026-06-07** ŌĆö Background agent's web-port codebase conversion assessment ([brain-tlak](~/data/knowledge/entries/knowledge/maestro-web-port-codebase-conversion-assessment.md), ~14 KB) materially changed the picture. Key new findings: (1) shipped web bundle is mobile-remote-control, not full Maestro; (2) `src/cli/` is electron-free, proving Node-only execution path is structurally already there; (3) 303 `ipcMain.handle` channels across 30 files but web client has ZERO direct IPC (talks only HTTP/WS via token-in-URL); (4) `tunnel-manager.ts` (Cloudflare tunnel) already exists upstream and proves the "browser-from-anywhere" mental model is precedented; (5) no xterm.js anywhere in renderer ŌĆö PTY-in-browser is a separate sub-project. **New Tier 0 ("use what's there") added to plan** ŌĆö run upstream Electron headless on mini2, expose existing dashboard URL over Tailscale. May be the right first answer.
- **2026-06-07** ŌĆö All time estimates stripped from this ISA and the support docs per principal's standing instruction. Plan is ordered by dependency, not by duration. Logical port order lives in `WEB_PORT_ORDER.md`. See feedback memory `feedback_no_time_estimates`.
- **2026-06-07** ŌĆö **Delegation-first execution model adopted.** Trillium's framing: this whole port project is delegate-able because we have a working Electron app as a golden reference. Per-feature agents read `src/renderer/<surface>`, drive both Electron (via CDP at `localhost:9222`) and `src/webFull/` (via Vite dev server) side-by-side, port the UI to talk to the existing WS protocol, and verify by comparing observed behavior. Each agent runs in a worktree-isolated branch. Layer 0 from `WEB_PORT_ORDER.md` must complete BEFORE the delegation pipeline starts ŌĆö agents need a vanilla-Node server entrypoint to run their builds against. After Layer 0, Layers 1-9 fan out cleanly: most features within a layer have no inter-feature dependencies and can run in parallel agents. The delegation harness itself (per-feature agent contract, comparison loop, merge protocol) is the next concrete artifact to design once Layer 0 work begins.
- **2026-06-07** ŌĆö **Function parity is the verification bar, not structural/protocol parity.** Methodology lives in [brain-aq5m](~/data/knowledge/entries/knowledge/maestro-web-port-function-parity-verification-methodology.md): every feature port ships with a user-story catalog of (`Given`, `When`, `Then`) triples; assertions use a fixed vocabulary (`hasElement`, `hasText`, `wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`) that's deliberately layout-independent; the catalog is recorded against the running Electron app (the test oracle) and replayed against webFull; pass criteria = every catalog story passes on both targets. Pixel-perfect, DOM-identical, and CSS-identical parity are explicitly rejected as the wrong bar. The catalog IS the spec ŌĆö not the renderer source ŌĆö to protect against agent hallucination and renderer-bug canonization.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Layer 0a shipped: bootable headless server with read-only callbacks.** The Fastify+WebSocket server in `src/main/web-server/WebServer.ts` now boots from a vanilla Node entrypoint (`src/server/index.ts`) with no `electron` import in the runtime path. What landed: (1) `src/shared/data-dir.ts` ŌĆö dual-mode userData resolver that returns `app.getPath('userData')` under Electron else `MAESTRO_DATA_DIR ?? ~/.config/maestro` (covers ISC-29); (2) `src/shared/file-store.ts` ŌĆö `electron-store`-shape JSON file store that preserves on-disk schema so a desktop data dir is portable into headless mode (covers ISC-36); (3) `src/server/index.ts` ŌĆö entrypoint that constructs `WebServer`, wires READ callbacks (`getSessions`, `getSessionDetail`, `getTheme`, `getBionifyReadingMode`, `getCustomCommands`, `getHistory` ŌĆö the last stubbed to `[]` pending HistoryManager port) to file-backed stores; WRITE callbacks (`writeToSession`, `executeCommand`, `interruptSession`, `switchMode`, `selectSession`, `selectTab`, `newTab`, `closeTab`, `renameTab`, `starTab`, `reorderTab`, `toggleBookmark`) log a warning and return `false`; (4) `tsconfig.server.json` ŌĆö server-only TS build (rootDir `src`, outDir `dist`) so `dist/server/index.js` is the runnable artifact; (5) `package.json` scripts `build:server` + `start:web`. **Upstream files touched:** zero ŌĆö every change is in NEW files (`src/server/`, `src/shared/`, `tsconfig.server.json`) plus a two-line `package.json` scripts addition.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Layer 0b shipped: write/interrupt/execute callbacks wired via ProcessManager.** `src/server/process-manager-adapter.ts` (108 LOC) instantiates a single `ProcessManager` at server startup; `src/server/index.ts` routes `setWriteToSessionCallback` / `setExecuteCommandCallback` / `setInterruptSessionCallback` through it. Suffix logic (`-ai` / `-terminal`) mirrors `src/main/web-server/web-server-factory.ts` lines 248-272 verbatim ŌĆö the adapter reads the live sessions store on every call (lookup closure), matches by `s.id`, and resolves `inputMode === 'ai' ? '${id}-ai' : '${id}-terminal'`. ProcessManager confirmed electron-free (grep across `src/main/process-manager/`, `src/main/parsers/`, `src/shared/` returned zero `from 'electron'` hits). `tsconfig.server.json` `include` widened to add `src/main/process-manager/**/*.ts` and `src/main/parsers/**/*.ts`; build still emits cleanly and `grep -r "from 'electron'" dist/server/` stays empty. **Out of scope (still stubbed, deferred to Layer 0c):** `switchMode`, `selectSession`, `selectTab`, `newTab`, `closeTab`, `renameTab`, `starTab`, `reorderTab`, `toggleBookmark` ŌĆö these need write-back to the sessions store and WebSocket broadcast plumbing. `executeCommand`'s "spawn new session if none exists" semantics also deferred ŌĆö full session-creation flow lives in the renderer today and needs a server-side port plus a UI surface in Layer 3.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Layer 1.1 shipped: `vite.config.webfull.mts` sibling config + dev/build scripts.** Pattern: a parallel Vite config file (sibling to `vite.config.web.mts`) drives the `src/webFull/` divergent tree without touching `vite.config.web.mts`, `tsconfig.json`, or any file under `src/web/`. Surgical changes from the original: `root` and `publicDir` repointed to `src/webFull/` (+ `src/webFull/public/`); `outDir` ŌåÆ `dist/webfull/`; dev port 5174 ŌåÆ 5176, preview 5175 ŌåÆ 5177 (both `strictPort: true`); `@web` alias re-pointed at `src/webFull` so any import that resolves through the alias stays inside the webfull tree (no new `@webfull` alias added ŌĆö keeping import sites stable across the two trees). All other settings (`define`, `esbuild`, build target, manualChunks logic, css, optimizeDeps, proxy) carried over verbatim. The `mobile/` and `desktop/` path-based chunk naming continues to work because `src/webFull/` was forked verbatim from `src/web/`. Files touched: NEW `vite.config.webfull.mts`; 2-line scripts addition in `package.json` (`dev:webfull`, `build:webfull`). NOT added to the aggregate `build` script ŌĆö that stays upstream-compatible. No edits to `src/web/`, `vite.config.web.mts`, or `tsconfig.json`. Plumbing only ŌĆö subsequent web-UI port agents now have a `src/webFull/` build target to land against.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **mini2 deploy spike pre-staged in `infra/` directory.** The Architect's 2026-06-08 audit flagged the mini2 deploy story as the most underspecified part of the plan and pointed out it is the actual Vision target (a Tailscale-reachable Maestro server running on `mini2`). This commit pre-stages the artifacts so the first real deploy is one command, not a debugging expedition. Three files land under a new `infra/` directory at the repo root: (1) `infra/DEPLOY_SPIKE.md` ŌĆö Trillium-readable runbook covering prerequisites on mini2 (Tailscale, Node 22.x via fnm, Python 3.11 for `node-gyp`, Xcode CLT), one-time setup (clone path `~/code/maestro`, `npm install`, `npm run build:server`, verify-and-edit plist, bootstrap launchd), the steady-state deploy sequence (`./infra/deploy.sh`), four post-deploy verification checks (service state, loopback HTTP, Tailscale HTTP, browser load), data-dir backup/restore commands, log location, rollback procedure, and six explicit open questions for the first real run; (2) `infra/com.maestro.server.plist` ŌĆö launchd LaunchAgent (not LaunchDaemon ŌĆö runs as Trillium's user, no privileged listener) with `KeepAlive=true`, `RunAtLoad=true`, `ProcessType=Background`, `ThrottleInterval=10`, env vars wired (`MAESTRO_DATA_DIR=~/.config/maestro`, `MAESTRO_WEB_PORT=45678`, `NODE_ENV=production`, `PATH` including the fnm Node prefix), and stdout/stderr piped to `~/Library/Logs/maestro/`; (3) `infra/deploy.sh` ŌĆö executable git pull + `npm ci` (fallback `npm install`) + `npm run build:server` + `launchctl bootout` + `launchctl bootstrap` + 3-second sleep + curl health probe + error-log tail on failure. The plist hard-codes the absolute node path (`/Users/trillium/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v22.22.1/installation/bin/node`) with a TODO comment block at the top of the file documenting that the path needs verification on mini2 because launchd does not expand `$HOME` or PATH lookups in `ProgramArguments`. **NOT executed in this commit** ŌĆö the agent authoring this does not have access to mini2. Trillium runs the first real deploy manually and reports findings back via a follow-on Decisions entry. **Six open questions surfaced for the first run:** Q1 data-dir migration from existing Electron `maestro-dev/` (untested per the audit); Q2 `customSyncPath` not consulted by `src/shared/data-dir.ts` so the server silently reads the wrong directory if Trillium has set it in Electron; Q3 exact fnm Node binary path on mini2; Q4 deploy-tag convention for rollback (`git tag deploy-...` in `deploy.sh` or `git reflog`?); Q5 `postinstall` electron-rebuild behavior on a host that never runs Electron; Q6 Tailscale port-exposure model (`0.0.0.0:45678` bind verified mini2-safe because no public IPv4, but should it be locked to the Tailscale interface?). **Files touched in this turn:** three NEW files under `infra/` plus this Decisions entry + the Verification entry below in `ISA.md`. **No source touched** ŌĆö `git diff main..HEAD -- src/web/ | wc -c` and `git diff main..HEAD -- src/main/ | wc -c` both 0.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **`WEB_PORT_ORDER.md` restructured for primitives-first lift strategy.** The doc was rewritten on branch `docs-port-order-lift-first` to reflect findings from `/tmp/web-ui-lift-scope.md` (445-line lift audit). Three structural changes: (1) **inserted a new Layer 2 ŌĆö Lift UI primitives** between the existing Layer 1 (webfull build target) and the existing Layer 2 (Identity + Settings). Layer 2 lifts `ui/Modal.tsx` (+ `LayerStackContext` + `useModalLayer` + `useLayerStack` + `MODAL_PRIORITIES`), `ui/FormInput.tsx`, `ui/EmojiPickerField.tsx`, `ConfirmModal.tsx`, and `GitStatusWidget.tsx` ŌĆö all with 0 IPC and 0 Electron-only APIs per audit ┬¦B4. The Tailwind glob fix (`src/webFull/**` added to `tailwind.config.mjs:3`) is L1.2, a hard prerequisite for L2; (2) **renumbered subsequent layers** ŌĆö old Layer 2 (Identity + Settings) ŌåÆ Layer 3; old Layer 3 (Create + Navigate) ŌåÆ Layer 4; old Layer 4 (xterm.js / raw-byte PTY) ŌåÆ Layer 6 and made explicitly scope-gated by ISC-42; History/AutoRun/Agents ŌåÆ Layer 7; Markdown/Files/Diffs ŌåÆ Layer 8; polish (LogViewer/About/ProcessMonitor/Sentry swap) ŌåÆ Layer 9; (3) **added a Lift-vs-Rewrite decision rule** as a top-level subsection: lift verbatim if 0 IPC Ōł¦ 0 Electron-only API; rewrite-with-lifted-primitives if Ōēź1 IPC Ōł© Ōēź1 Electron-only API; hybrid (lift JSX, rewrite hook) if 1ŌĆō3 IPCs are isolated in one hook. Cites the audit's NOT-easy examples (`NewInstanceModal.tsx` 1822 LOC / 18 IPC, `GeneralTab.tsx` 1522 LOC / 17 IPC, `App.tsx` 3357 LOC) as canonical "rewrite, don't lift" cases. Also updated the IPC-substitution reference with the audit's one-line rule (`window.maestro.X.Y(args)` ŌåÆ `fetch('/${token}/api/X/Y', ...)` / POST / `useWebSocket()` subscription) and the 32-vs-886 server/renderer-surface imbalance the lift work compounds against. The "Working rule" at the top now reads "every layer item is built in `src/webFull/` and most items COMPOSE the primitives lifted in Layer 2 rather than re-lifting renderer components." **Cross-reference impact:** ISA.md line 129 (ISC-42) still reads "Layer 4 in `WEB_PORT_ORDER.md`" ŌĆö the doc's Layer 6 self-notes the rename, but the ISA wording is intentionally left as-is per the brief's scope guard (the only ISA edit permitted in this turn was this Decisions append). When the ISC-42 scope decision is made, that line gets updated then; for now the layer-rename is one-line-documented at the bottom of `WEB_PORT_ORDER.md` Layer 6. **Files touched in this turn:** `WEB_PORT_ORDER.md` (new file at repo root reflecting the restructured plan) + this Decisions entry in `ISA.md`. No source touched.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Drift fix: `MODAL_PRIORITIES` and `Layer` types in webFull converted from verbatim copies to re-exports from `src/renderer/`.** Architect's 2026-06-08 audit (risk A) flagged that L2.1 lifted `src/renderer/constants/modalPriorities.ts` and `src/renderer/types/layer.ts` into `src/webFull/constants/modalPriorities.ts` and `src/webFull/types/layer.ts` as byte-for-byte copies. Verbatim copies create silent-drift risk: any new modal priority or layer-kind added to the renderer source will fail to propagate, and TypeScript can't catch the mismatch because the two files are independent type sources. The drift-prevention pattern adopted: **for types/constants that are explicitly shared between renderer and webFull and have no semantic reason to diverge, the webFull file becomes a single-line `export * from '../../renderer/...';` re-export.** Rationale: (1) the rendering target is irrelevant ŌĆö modal stacking semantics are determined by application logic, not by Electron-vs-browser; (2) the layer-stack discriminated union is a domain model, not a target-specific concern; (3) vite's resolver already handles cross-directory imports cleanly (webFull/vite config aliases `@renderer` to `src/renderer/`, and existing webFull files like `mobile/App.tsx`, `mobile/WebReadingContent.tsx`, `mobile/MobileMarkdownRenderer.tsx`, `mobile/SessionStatusBanner.tsx` already use `'../../renderer/...'` relative imports against `utils/contextUsage` and `utils/bionifyReadingMode` in production). tsconfig.json `include` covers `src/renderer` (the source) but NOT `src/webFull` ŌĆö type-checking for webFull rides on vite/esbuild, not tsc, so the re-export resolves at bundle time without a tsc-level include change. **When to use this pattern:** the type/constant is named identically on both sides, the semantics are identical, divergence has no use case, and the audit/diff workflow would treat divergence as a bug. **When NOT to use:** anything that genuinely diverges (e.g. components with different DOM trees, hooks with different store dependencies, types with different optional fields). For divergent surfaces, the lift-and-adapt pattern from L2.1 remains correct. **Files touched:** `src/webFull/constants/modalPriorities.ts` (242 LOC of body replaced with 1 re-export line + 2 comment lines citing risk A), `src/webFull/types/layer.ts` (107 LOC of body replaced with 1 re-export line + 2 comment lines). No edits to `src/renderer/`, `src/web/`, `src/main/`, vite config, or tsconfig ŌĆö the existing vite alias and resolver are sufficient. The renderer-side files remain the single source of truth; the webFull files are now derived artifacts that cannot drift by construction.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Layer 3.1 shipped: Settings General tab rewritten in `src/webFull/` using lifted Modal primitive; first feature port to wire `<LayerStackProvider>` and the first additive REST routes on the server.** Per the lift-vs-rewrite rule, the renderer's `src/renderer/components/Settings/tabs/GeneralTab.tsx` (1522 LOC across 5 IPC namespaces ŌĆö `settings`, `wakatime`, `sync`, `stats`, `shells`/`shell`) is firmly in the "rewrite, don't lift" zone; this wave delivers a webfull-native rewrite that preserves the OBSERVABLE FUNCTION (open settings ŌåÆ see general options ŌåÆ change them ŌåÆ save) without verbatim-copying renderer markup. **Files added (new files only, all under `src/webFull/`):** `src/webFull/components/Settings/SettingsModal.tsx` (96 LOC ŌĆö webfull SettingsModal shell using lifted `Modal` primitive at `MODAL_PRIORITIES.SETTINGS` (450), tab strip ready for subsequent tab agents but only General wired today), `src/webFull/components/Settings/tabs/GeneralTab.tsx` (~320 LOC ŌĆö covers the `settings.get/set` namespace: `conductorProfile`, `logLevel`, `enterToSendAI`, `enterToSendTerminal`, `defaultSaveToHistory`, `defaultShowThinking` (three-state), `autoScrollAiMode`, `spellCheck`, `automaticTabNamingEnabled`, `checkForUpdatesOnStartup`, `enableBetaUpdates`, `crashReportingEnabled`; explicit "deferred to subsequent layers" panel surfaces the partial-parity gaps so they are NOT silently dropped), `src/webFull/hooks/useSettings.ts` (130 LOC ŌĆö REST-backed `useState`+`fetch` hook with optimistic update + rollback on PATCH failure; intentionally NOT Zustand per audit ┬¦C4), `src/webFull/components/Settings/parity.test.ts` (180 LOC ŌĆö 5 stories: 3 happy-path + 2 negative-path; assertion vocabulary restricted to `hasElement`/`hasText`/`fsHas` per the function-parity verification methodology rules ([brain-aq5m](~/data/knowledge/entries/knowledge/maestro-web-port-function-parity-verification-methodology.md)); vitest smoke pass guards the catalog shape until the record-and-replay harness lands). **Files edited (purely additive):** `src/webFull/App.tsx` ŌĆö added `LayerStackProvider` import + wrap between `<ThemeProvider>` and `<Suspense>` (lines 20 + 285-294 approx, mounting around the line range documented as 285 in Layer 2.1's Decisions); `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts` ŌĆö added two new routes (`GET /:token/api/settings` and `PATCH /:token/api/settings`) at the end of `registerRoutes()` plus a module-level `SettingsProvider` registry with `registerSettingsProvider`/`getSettingsProvider`/`_resetDefaultSettingsStore` exports; existing routes and `ApiRouteCallbacks` interface unchanged. **Server design decision ŌĆö lazy default FileStore provider:** the brief authorizes editing `apiRoutes.ts` but explicitly forbids touching `WebServer.ts` (which is where existing callbacks get wired). To make the routes useful end-to-end without touching `WebServer.ts`, the route module exports a `registerSettingsProvider(p)` setter AND falls back to a lazily-instantiated `FileStore<Record<string, unknown>>` rooted at `getDataDir()` if no provider is registered. This means: (a) headless Node startup at `src/server/index.ts` can opt to register a provider later without further `apiRoutes.ts` edits; (b) until then, the routes work directly against the same `maestro-settings.json` file `src/server/index.ts` already reads via its own `FileStore`. Trade-off accepted: a second FileStore instance exists transiently against the same JSON file. FileStore's write path uses temp-file-rename (`src/shared/file-store.ts:54-57`) so the on-disk file stays valid under concurrent reads; the renderer/Electron path never calls these routes, so the only real client is the webFull bundle. **LayerStackProvider mount point:** `src/webFull/App.tsx` ŌĆö the L2.1 agent reported it would land around line 285; in this branch it landed at the corresponding `<ThemeProvider>` body (which moved by 7 lines after the comment + provider insertion). The provider wraps `<Suspense fallback={<LoadingFallback />}>` so every modal lifted into the lazy mobile bundle has a layer stack to register against. **New REST endpoints (additive in `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts`):** `GET /:token/api/settings` returns `{ settings: Record<string, unknown>, timestamp: number }`; `PATCH /:token/api/settings` accepts body `{ patch: Record<string, unknown> }` and returns the same shape (with the patch applied). 503 when no provider is registered AND the default store can't be instantiated (e.g. permissions); 400 on invalid PATCH body shape; 500 on read/write errors. **Partial-parity gaps surfaced (per the brief's "reject patterns that bail out of full parity" rule):** the General tab body shows a "Coming in subsequent layers" panel listing exactly what's deferred ŌĆö WakaTime CLI status & API-key validation (`wakatime:*` namespace), sync / storage location picker (`sync:*` namespace ŌĆö needs a server-side `/api/sync/*` plus a typed folder-path input since the browser can't dialog-pick a folder), stats DB size/clear/earliest-timestamp (`stats:*` namespace ŌĆö port adds `/api/stats/*`), shell detection & custom shell path / args / env vars (`shells:detect` is a local-machine concept; the renderer's machine ŌēĀ the server's machine in headless mode), sleep prevention (`power.setEnabled` ŌĆö Electron-only; no browser equivalent), GPU acceleration toggle (Electron renderer setting), "Open in Finder" affordances (`shell.openPath` ŌĆö no browser equivalent). Each gap is named explicitly so subsequent Settings-tab agents can pick them up rather than wonder what was silently dropped. **Lift-vs-rewrite call made:** REWRITE-with-lifted-primitives. The renderer's GeneralTab has 17 IPC callsites across 5 namespaces; the audit threshold for "lift" is 0 IPC Ōł¦ 0 Electron-only API; for "rewrite" is Ōēź1 IPC Ōł© Ōēź1 Electron-only API. GeneralTab is well past the threshold. The lifted Modal primitive (Layer 2.1) carries 100% of the modal shell behavior; only the body content is webfull-native. **Scope guards (verified post-write, pre-commit):** `git diff main..HEAD -- src/web/ | wc -c` ŌåÆ 0; `git diff main..HEAD -- src/renderer/ | wc -c` ŌåÆ 0. Only edits outside `src/webFull/` are the additive REST routes in `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts`. No new env vars, no new dependencies, no new package.json scripts.
- **2026-06-08** ŌĆö **Layer 2.1 shipped: visual primitives (Modal + FormInput + ConfirmModal) lifted from `src/renderer/` into `src/webFull/`, plus webFull added to Tailwind content glob.** This wave proves the lift-and-adapt pattern from the `/tmp/web-ui-lift-scope.md` audit by lifting the leaf-most visual atoms that have zero IPC and zero Electron API ŌĆö subsequent feature ports now have UI building blocks already in `src/webFull/`. **Tailwind config:** one-line edit to `tailwind.config.mjs:3` adds `'./src/webFull/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}'` to the content array (the audit-flagged blocker ŌĆö without it any Tailwind class in webFull files gets purged at build). This is the ONLY upstream-file edit in this wave; rationale for selecting it as the single allowed upstream edit: the content glob is the most rebase-stable part of a Tailwind config (Tailwind itself authoring guidance frames it as "add your source paths here"), so the rebase-conflict cost is minimal vs the alternative of leaving the bug in place and shipping broken CSS in production. **Lifted primitives (3 files):** `src/webFull/components/ui/Modal.tsx` (200 LOC), `src/webFull/components/ui/FormInput.tsx` (189 LOC), `src/webFull/components/ConfirmModal.tsx` (74 LOC). **Lifted supporting infra (the audit's "pre-flight" set ŌĆö required because all three primitives transitively depend on the layer stack system, and webFull had no layer stack of its own yet):** `src/webFull/types/layer.ts` (108 LOC), `src/webFull/constants/modalPriorities.ts` (243 LOC), `src/webFull/hooks/useLayerStack.ts` (300 LOC), `src/webFull/contexts/LayerStackContext.tsx` (90 LOC), `src/webFull/hooks/useModalLayer.ts` (124 LOC). **Theme access pattern decision:** primitives keep the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention rather than calling `useTheme()` internally. Rationale per `/tmp/web-ui-lift-scope.md` A3: the renderer threads `theme` as a prop from `App.tsx` while webFull provides theme via `useTheme()` context, but the primitives themselves are tree-shape-agnostic ŌĆö by accepting `theme` as a prop they work identically under either pattern. Consumers in webFull call `const { theme } = useTheme()` at the feature-component level and pass `theme` into the primitive; that keeps the primitive's diff vs the renderer source minimal (smaller rebase surface) and avoids coupling the primitive's signature to webFull's specific ThemeProvider.
