#### Deferred / out-of-scope for this brief

- **Lifting `GitStatusContext` / `GitStatusProvider` / the `useGitStatusPolling` hook.** This is the IPC-bound substrate the widget self-sources from in the renderer; lifting it would require a server-side `/api/git/status` route (with polling cadence + per-session keying), a webFull-side hook to consume the route, and a WS frame to invalidate cached file details when files change. That is a multi-file architecture lift, explicitly out of scope per the brief's guard.
- **Wiring the lifted `GitStatusWidget` into a webFull header host.** The widget contract now reads `fileCount` and `fileDetails` from props; a future feature-wiring pass that mounts this widget in the webFull header will need the `/api/git/status` substrate above to source those props. Until then, the widget ships as a primitive available in the L2.5 export surface without a live consumer.
- **Lifting `GitFileChange` into a shared types module.** The interface is currently defined twice: once at `src/renderer/hooks/git/useGitStatusPolling.ts` line 36, and once at `src/webFull/components/GitStatusWidget.tsx` (the local mirror this lift adds). When the git-status substrate lifts, the canonical home for `GitFileChange` should be `src/shared/git-types.ts` (with both the renderer hook and the webFull widget importing from there). For now, the local mirror is the lowest-risk pattern and matches the `ExecutionQueueIndicator` precedent.
- Wiring the lifted `MaestroSilhouette` / `AnimatedMaestro` into a webFull Standing Ovation overlay or brand-header host ŌĆö those consumers are downstream-layer scope and live in larger feature lifts (`AppOverlays` itself already landed at L2.5 and currently still resolves the renderer-source silhouette via cross-fork import; that import line will flip in a follow-up sweep after this lift lands).
- Wiring the lifted `TemplateAutocompleteDropdown` into a webFull `AgentPromptComposerModal` or the Auto Run document editor ŌĆö both consumers need the template-autocomplete hook (`useTemplateAutocomplete`) wired through with browser-safe transitive deps (`useClickOutside`, `TEMPLATE_VARIABLES`), which is its own brief.
- Lifting `useTemplateAutocomplete` itself into `src/webFull/hooks/` ŌĆö the hook is composable at module-load time but its full lift is a downstream-layer concern (it owns the autocomplete reducer and the click-outside wiring that this presentational dropdown delegates to).
- Wiring `loadColorPreferences` / `saveColorPreferences` into webFull ŌĆö when a downstream consumer needs persisted color preferences, the right move is a webFull settings adapter (likely WS-backed against the server-side settings store) at that point in the port. Not part of this lift.
- Lifting the remaining cross-fork imports inside `GroupChatMessages` (`textProcessing`, `markdownConfig`, `clipboard`) ŌĆö each is its own leaf-parade brief.
- Lifting `GroupChatInput` ŌĆö the consumer this lift is precondition #2 for, per Architect audit #7. With this lift landed, the input lift's pre-flight on the messages-view dependency chain shrinks to the remaining four cross-fork imports.

### 2026-06-08 ŌĆö Layer 2.5 evidence (leaf-parade ŌĆö three pure util lifts in one branch: `textProcessing` + `clipboard` + `terminalProseStyles`; drains the GCM cross-fork debt to zero)

**Closes:** ISC-44.layer-2.5.text_processing_util, ISC-44.layer-2.5.clipboard_util, ISC-44.layer-2.5.terminal_prose_styles_util.

**Branch:** `leaf-gcm-utils` (worktree at `../maestro-leaf-utils`).

#### Context

Three of the five remaining cross-fork `renderer/utils/` imports inside `src/webFull/components/GroupChatMessages.tsx` are pure leaves (or have a pure subset that can be cleanly extracted). The L2.5 parade has been chipping at them one at a time; this branch bundles the three pure-util lifts into a single landing because the lifts are independent and the test surfaces don't interact.

The post-lift state ŌĆö and the headline outcome of this branch ŌĆö is that `GroupChatMessages.tsx` now has ZERO cross-fork `renderer/` imports remaining. `grep -E "^import.*renderer/|from ['\"].*renderer/" src/webFull/components/GroupChatMessages.tsx` returns empty. The 5 occurrences of `renderer/` still in the file are all inside the header comment block documenting the original lift's context ŌĆö the import surface is fully drained.

#### Lift 1 ŌĆö `textProcessing` util (verbatim full lift)

**Pre-flight grep on `src/renderer/utils/textProcessing.ts`** for `window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|ipcRenderer` ŌåÆ empty (exit 1). The renderer file is a fully pure module ŌĆö no IPC, no Electron deps, no `window.maestro` references at any depth. Verbatim full lift, no signature changes.

**Lifted surface:** `processCarriageReturns`, `processLogTextHelper`, `filterTextByLinesHelper`, `getCachedAnsiHtml`, `clearAnsiCache`, `stripMarkdown`, and the `ANSI_CACHE_MAX_SIZE` constant. Only `stripMarkdown` is consumed by GroupChatMessages today; the rest port cleanly so they're pre-positioned for future webFull-side consumers (e.g. terminal-output rendering).

**Utility test** (`src/webFull/utils/textProcessing.test.ts`, 15 vitest cases):

- `stripMarkdown` (7 cases ŌĆö exceeds the brief's Ōēź5 floor): bold/italic/strikethrough removal, inline-code backtick stripping, fenced code-block content extraction without fences, ATX-header + blockquote stripping, link + image markdown to visible text (documents the quirk that link regex runs before image regex, so `![alt](url)` collapses to `!alt` ŌĆö verbatim renderer behavior preserved with an inline comment in the test), mixed-bullet normalization to leading dashes, numbered-list ordering preservation after normalization.
- `processCarriageReturns` (2 cases): last-non-empty-segment-after-\r semantics; lines without \r left untouched.
- `processLogTextHelper` (2 cases): bare bash/zsh prompt + empty-line filtering in terminal mode; pass-through in AI mode.
- `filterTextByLinesHelper` (4 cases): empty-query passthrough, plain-text include, regex exclude, invalid-regex fallback to plain text.

All 15 pass. One test was initially asserting the "wrong" stripMarkdown behavior on image markdown; the test runner caught it and the test was updated to match the renderer-verbatim behavior with an explanatory comment. That's a feature of the parity-via-tests approach ŌĆö the test catches drift even on the first write.

#### Lift 2 ŌĆö `clipboard` util (pure surface only; `safeClipboardWriteImage` deliberately NOT lifted)

**Pre-flight grep on `src/renderer/utils/clipboard.ts`** returned 1 hit: `await window.maestro.shell.copyImageToClipboard(dataUrl)` at line 48, inside the `safeClipboardWriteImage` function body, lambda-deferred via optional chaining (`if (window.maestro?.shell?.copyImageToClipboard)`).

The hit is at runtime inside a function body, NOT at module-load time. Per the same precedent that `leaf-groupchat-messages` used to accept `markdownConfig.ts`'s lambda-deferred `window.maestro.shell.openExternal` calls, this lift COULD have shipped the whole module ŌĆö but the right move here is different: GroupChatMessages only uses `safeClipboardWrite` (text path). The image variant has no current webFull consumer, and the renderer's image-path implementation reaches for an Electron-only IPC bridge that webFull genuinely cannot fulfill. So the lift carries only the pure subset.

**Lifted surface:** `safeClipboardWrite` (pure `navigator.clipboard.writeText` wrapper that swallows `NotAllowedError`), `safeClipboardWriteBlob` (pure `navigator.clipboard.write` wrapper for binary clipboard items).

**NOT lifted:** `safeClipboardWriteImage`. The header comment-block documents the omission and notes the right moves when a webFull consumer needs image-to-clipboard support: (1) an explicit webFull adapter that calls the browser Clipboard API directly (the renderer's fallback path is already pure), or (2) a host-aware capability injection from the webFull entrypoint. Neither smuggles `window.maestro` into webFull.

**Utility test** (`src/webFull/utils/clipboard.test.ts`, 5 vitest cases ŌĆö exceeds the brief's Ōēź3 floor):

- `safeClipboardWrite` (3 cases): success returns true and forwards text; `NotAllowedError` from `navigator.clipboard.writeText` returns false; `navigator.clipboard === undefined` returns false (vitest's jsdom env doesn't provide a writable clipboard, so the suite installs a mock via `Object.defineProperty` with `configurable: true` and restores via `afterEach`).
- `safeClipboardWriteBlob` (2 cases): success returns true and forwards `ClipboardItem[]`; rejection returns false.

All 5 pass.

#### Lift 3 ŌĆö `terminalProseStyles` (surgical extract from `markdownConfig.ts`, NOT a full lift)

**Why surgical, not full.** `src/renderer/utils/markdownConfig.ts` is 925 LOC. It exports `generateProseStyles`, the `createMarkdownComponents` family (wizard bubble + release notes flavors), `generateAutoRunProseStyles`, `generateDiffViewStyles`, and the symbol we need ŌĆö `generateTerminalProseStyles`. The file pulls in `react-markdown` `Components`, `react-syntax-highlighter`, the renderer-side `BionifyText` React component, and `getSyntaxStyle` from the theme registry. The wizard-bubble and release-notes factories contain `window.maestro.shell.openExternal` callsites inside their anchor `onClick` lambda bodies ŌĆö lambda-deferred, but still a surface webFull would have to either tolerate or strip.

GroupChatMessages only consumes ONE symbol: `generateTerminalProseStyles(theme, scopeSelector) -> string`. That function is a pure CSS-string generator that reads `theme.colors` and concatenates a template literal. Its only external collaborator is `getBionifyReadingModeStyles` from `bionifyReadingMode.tsx`, which is ALSO a pure CSS-string generator (the rest of `bionifyReadingMode.tsx` is React components that this CSS function doesn't touch).

**Extract carries** (into the new `src/webFull/utils/terminalProseStyles.ts`):

- Constants matching the renderer defaults: `DEFAULT_BIONIFY_REST_OPACITY = 0.65`, `DEFAULT_BIONIFY_INTENSITY = 1`.
- `clamp` helper (3-line pure function, no need to import from anywhere).
- `resolveBionifyRestOpacity(intensity, theme?)` ŌĆö pure helper, identical signature and body to the renderer source.
- `getBionifyReadingModeStylesCss(scopeSelector, theme?)` ŌĆö pure CSS-string generator, byte-equivalent to the renderer's `getBionifyReadingModeStyles` for the `(scopeSelector, theme)` call shape used by `generateTerminalProseStyles`. Named with `Css` suffix locally to avoid shadowing the renderer-side React-component-injecting variant for readers comparing the two side-by-side.
- `generateTerminalProseStyles(theme, scopeSelector)` ŌĆö verbatim CSS template from the renderer source line 833+.

**Extract OMITS:** the React-side `BionifyText` / `BionifyTextBlock` components, `transformBionifyNode` and friends, the style-injection side-effect (`hasInjectedBionifyStyles`), algorithm-parsing for runtime config, all other `generate*ProseStyles` functions, the `createMarkdownComponents` family, `generateDiffViewStyles`.

**Parity guarantee documented in the file header:** the CSS string the extract emits MUST match the renderer's output byte-for-byte for the same `(theme, scopeSelector)` inputs. If a future webFull port needs more of the markdown-config surface, the right move is a SECOND surgical extract ŌĆö NOT a full module lift ŌĆö so the Electron-only deps don't leak.

**Utility test** (`src/webFull/utils/terminalProseStyles.test.ts`, 5 vitest cases ŌĆö exceeds the brief's Ōēź3 floor):

- Returns a non-empty CSS string scoped to the given selector + `.prose`.
- Emits theme colors for headings (h1=accent, h2=success, h3=warning), code backgrounds (`bgSidebar`), and accent-with-underline links.
- Includes the inlined Bionify reading-mode rules (`.bionify-word`, `.bionify-word-emphasis`, `.bionify-word-rest`) with the dark-theme default `--bionify-rest-opacity` of 0.65.
- Shifts the Bionify rest-opacity fallback for light-mode themes ŌĆö `mode: 'light'` produces `var(--bionify-rest-opacity, 0.73)` (verifies the `resolveBionifyRestOpacity` branch on `theme?.mode === 'light'`).
- Scoping pin: every emitted rule selector begins with the provided scope prefix.

All 5 pass.

#### GroupChatMessages retarget (cross-fork neutralization payoff)

Three import-line flips in `src/webFull/components/GroupChatMessages.tsx`:

```
- import { stripMarkdown } from '../../renderer/utils/textProcessing';
+ import { stripMarkdown } from '../utils/textProcessing';
- import { generateTerminalProseStyles } from '../../renderer/utils/markdownConfig';
+ import { generateTerminalProseStyles } from '../utils/terminalProseStyles';
- import { safeClipboardWrite } from '../../renderer/utils/clipboard';
+ import { safeClipboardWrite } from '../utils/clipboard';
```

**Cross-fork debt drawdown:** before this branch, `grep -c 'renderer/utils/\|renderer/types\|renderer/' src/webFull/components/GroupChatMessages.tsx` returned 8 (three import lines + five comment-block references). After: returns 5 ŌĆö every `renderer/` mention in the file is now inside the explanatory header comment. The import surface is fully drained. `grep -E "^import.*renderer/|from ['\"].*renderer/" src/webFull/components/GroupChatMessages.tsx` returns empty (exit 1).

`npx vitest run src/webFull/components/GroupChatMessages.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ 6/6 still pass (no behavioral regression ŌĆö the three retargets are byte-equivalent at the call site since all three lifts preserve identical function signatures).

`src/webFull/utils/index.ts` gains three new export blocks under the Layer 2.5 leaf-parade primitives section:

- "Layer 2.5 leaf-parade: text processing (verbatim lift from renderer)" ŌĆö the seven function/constant exports from `textProcessing`.
- "Layer 2.5 leaf-parade: clipboard (pure surface only ŌĆö image helper not lifted)" ŌĆö `safeClipboardWrite`, `safeClipboardWriteBlob`.
- "Layer 2.5 leaf-parade: terminal prose styles (surgical extract from markdownConfig)" ŌĆö `generateTerminalProseStyles`.

Additive only ŌĆö no prior exports changed.

### 2026-06-08 ŌĆö Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `KeyboardMasteryCelebration` lift

#### Why this lift exists

Per the leaf-parade audit, `src/renderer/components/KeyboardMasteryCelebration.tsx` (350 LOC) is a leaf component ŌĆö 0 IPC namespaces and 0 Electron-only API at module load (pre-flight `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]"` ŌåÆ empty, exit 1). It is the second of the three overlays referenced in `src/webFull/components/AppOverlays.tsx`'s file header to land in webFull, after `FirstRunCelebration` (`leaf-first-run-celebration` @ `fd9bc3cf9`). The L2.5 `AppOverlays` dispatcher accepts `keyboardMasterySlot: ReactNode | null` and gates rendering on `pendingKeyboardMasteryLevel !== null`; the slot was a deferral because the underlying component was renderer-only at the time. With this lift landed, the host wiring layer can pass `<KeyboardMasteryCelebration ... />` directly into `AppOverlays`'s `keyboardMasterySlot`, or keep the render-prop indirection (host's call ŌĆö either choice is host-local; the dispatcher contract is stable).

`StandingOvationOverlay` remains the only deferred overlay of the three, because it carries a `window.maestro.shell.openExternal` callsite that would drag an IPC site through the webFull bundle entry point even lambda-deferred. That lift will need a webFull-side external-link surface (likely a `window.open(url, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer')` adapter under a feature flag) before it can land.

#### Import-path adapts (seven, all matching L2.5 precedent)

- `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` (standard L2.5 swap ŌĆö webFull has no `types/` aggregator that re-exports `Theme`; the renderer barrel itself routes through `src/renderer/types/index.ts` which re-exports from `src/shared/theme-types`).
- `Shortcut` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/types'` directly (the interface lives in the renderer types barrel only ŌĆö `src/renderer/types/index.ts` ŌĆö and is not yet replicated to `src/shared/`. Matches the L2.5 `ShortcutsHelpModal` / `GroupChatHeader` precedent of pulling specific types from the canonical renderer aggregator rather than copying into `src/shared/`. `Shortcut` is a pure data shape with no transitive `window.maestro` references).
- `useLayerStack` from `'../contexts/LayerStackContext'` resolves to the webFull-side context at `src/webFull/contexts/LayerStackContext.tsx` (lifted in L2.1).
- `MODAL_PRIORITIES` from `'../constants/modalPriorities'` resolves via the webFull re-export at `src/webFull/constants/modalPriorities.ts` (per Architect 2026-06-08 audit risk A ŌĆö non-divergent constants stay re-exported from renderer to prevent silent drift). Uses `MODAL_PRIORITIES.KEYBOARD_MASTERY` (1095, just below `STANDING_OVATION` at 1100).
- `KEYBOARD_MASTERY_LEVELS` from `'../constants/keyboardMastery'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/constants/keyboardMastery'`. The constants module is pure data (level definitions `{ id, name, threshold, description }`, zero IPC at module load ŌĆö matches the L2.5 `ShortcutsHelpModal` precedent of pulling non-divergent renderer constants directly by relative path rather than duplicating into `src/shared/`).
- `DEFAULT_SHORTCUTS` from `'../constants/shortcuts'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/constants/shortcuts'`. Same rationale ŌĆö pure data module (shortcut key arrays + metadata), zero IPC at module load. Used as the fallback when the optional `shortcuts` prop is not supplied.
- `isMacOSPlatform` from `'../utils/platformUtils'` resolves to the webFull-side shim at `src/webFull/utils/platformUtils.ts` which uses `navigator.userAgent` instead of the renderer's `window.maestro.platform` preload bridge. This is the same platform-detection divergence the L2.5 `shortcutFormatter` shim handles for other lifts.

The component's local `formatShortcutKeys(keys: string[], isMac: boolean)` helper is preserved verbatim from the renderer source ŌĆö it takes a `(keys, isMac)` signature and joins without a separator (e.g. `['Meta', '/']` ŌåÆ `'Ōīś/'`), which is a different signature from the webFull `shortcutFormatter` shim's `formatShortcutKeys(keys, separator?)`. Keeping the helper local preserves source fidelity ŌĆö the renderer uses this no-separator form intentionally for the shortcut hint copy ("Press Ōīś/ to see all shortcuts").

#### Composition shape

Large celebration modal ŌĆö does NOT compose the L2.1 `Modal` primitive directly because the renderer source builds bespoke DOM chrome (dark backdrop at z-index 99997, gradient header glow, animated bouncing icon, level-progress dot row, music-themed confetti palette with per-level intensity scaling, gradient dismiss button, level-specific message swap between `Keyboard` + "Level Up!" for levels 0-3 and `Trophy` + "Keyboard Maestro!" for level 4).

Confetti behavior preserved verbatim: per-level intensity table (50/100/200/300/500 particles for levels 0-4); `zIndex` stays at 99998 (between backdrop and modal). Maestro variant (`level === 4`) fires an extra delayed 100-particle gold-star burst at 300ms after mount. All confetti calls honour `prefers-reduced-motion` AND the `disableConfetti` prop. All timeouts are tracked in `timeoutsRef` and cleared on unmount to avoid leaking timers.

Layer-stack registration uses `MODAL_PRIORITIES.KEYBOARD_MASTERY` (1095) with `focusTrap: 'strict'`, `blocksLowerLayers: true`, `capturesFocus: true`, `ariaLabel: 'Keyboard Mastery Level Up Celebration'`, and routes Escape to `handleClose()`. Celebration-on-close pattern: clicking the dismiss button (or pressing Enter/Escape) sets `isClosing`, fires a second confetti burst, then calls `onClose()` after an 800ms animation delay. The Continue button label flips to "Onwards!" during the `isClosing` window ŌĆö preserved verbatim from the renderer source.

#### Files added (all under `src/webFull/`)

- `src/webFull/components/KeyboardMasteryCelebration.tsx` (~470 LOC including extended file-header documenting the seven import-path adapts and the composition-shape rationale; ~350 LOC of source code verbatim from the renderer).
- `src/webFull/components/KeyboardMasteryCelebration.parity.test.ts` (~290 LOC, 10 stories + 6 catalog-shape vitest guards).

#### Files modified (additive only)

- `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended `export { KeyboardMasteryCelebration } from './KeyboardMasteryCelebration';` immediately after the `FirstRunCelebration` export to keep the L2.5 leaf exports grouped by lift wave.

#### Files NOT touched

- `src/web/` ŌĆö zero bytes diff vs main.
- `src/renderer/` ŌĆö zero bytes diff vs main (read-only oracle).
- `src/main/` ŌĆö zero bytes diff vs main.
- `src/server/` ŌĆö zero bytes diff vs main.

#### Parity catalog

`src/webFull/components/KeyboardMasteryCelebration.parity.test.ts` declares 10 stories (5 happy + 5 negative ŌĆö meets brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor). Happy-path stories cover:

1. The standard "Level Up!" variation chrome (dialog with the canonical `aria-label="Keyboard Mastery Level Up"`, level name + description from `KEYBOARD_MASTERY_LEVELS`, Continue button).
2. The next-level encouragement copy ("Keep using shortcuts to reach <next>") with dismiss hint.
3. The level-progression dot row rendering.
4. The Maestro variation at `level === 4` swapping title to "Keyboard Maestro!" + "the highest level" subhead + "You've mastered all keyboard shortcuts!" encouragement copy.
5. The level=0 boundary case (Beginner tier ŌĆö pin against future refactors that use `if (level)` instead of explicit `level === 4`).

Negative-path stories cover:

1. Continue button ŌåÆ unmounts the dialog after the 800ms close-animation.
2. Escape key ŌåÆ same close path via layer-stack `onEscape`.
3. Backdrop click ŌåÆ close (with the card's `onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()}` preserved so card-clicks DO NOT close).
4. No "Keep using shortcuts to reach the next level" copy at Maestro (the next-level branch must NOT render at level 4).
5. No "Level Up!" title at Maestro (the standard branch must NOT render at level 4).

Uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary (`hasElement`, `hasText`, `wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`). Catalog-shape vitest guards: Ōēź3-story floor, Ōēź3-happy-path floor, Ōēź1-negative-per-happy floor, allowed-verbs guard, non-empty given/when/then, no-IPC-leakage guard. 6/6 in-suite vitest guards green.

#### Verification

- **Pre-flight on the renderer source:** `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]" src/renderer/components/KeyboardMasteryCelebration.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (exit 1).
- **Lint:** `MAESTRO_HEADLESS=1 npm run lint` ŌåÆ clean (after `MAESTRO_HEADLESS=1 npm run build:prompts` to materialize the pre-existing `src/generated/prompts.ts`).
- **Parity test:** `MAESTRO_HEADLESS=1 npx vitest run src/webFull/components/KeyboardMasteryCelebration.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ PASS, 6/6 in-suite vitest guards green.
- **Scope-guard `git diff`s:** `git diff main..HEAD -- src/web/ src/renderer/ src/main/ src/server/ | wc -c` ŌåÆ 0 (read-only oracles + main + server untouched).
- **Symlink hygiene:** `node_modules` symlinked to `/Users/trilliumsmith/code/maestro/node_modules` for the lint/test run; removed before commit so the worktree stays clean.
- **Push status:** NOT pushed per brief instruction. Branch sits locally at `leaf-keyboard-mastery`, awaiting Trillium's merge.

#### Deferred / out-of-scope for this brief

- Wiring the lifted `KeyboardMasteryCelebration` into `src/webFull/App.tsx` (directly or through `AppOverlays`'s `keyboardMasterySlot`). That host-wiring pass needs the level-transition source wired (either a webFull-side modal-data store with a `pendingKeyboardMasteryLevel: number | null` field, or REST-backed `useSettings()` for the mastery-percentage threshold-crossing detector, plus the `useSettingsStore` `shortcuts` + `disableConfetti` reads ŌĆö all renderer-only architecture today). Each of those is its own downstream-layer brief.
- Lifting `StandingOvationOverlay` ŌĆö the third and final overlay referenced in `AppOverlays.tsx`'s file header. Pre-flight grep returns 1 hit (`window.maestro.shell.openExternal` inside the badge external-link affordance). Its lift will need a webFull-side external-link surface before it can land verbatim.
- Lifting `useModalStore` / `useSettingsStore` into webFull ŌĆö needed for the host-wiring pass to source `keyboardMasteryData` and `shortcuts` / `disableConfetti` without prop-drilling from the App root. Multi-file architectural lift, explicitly out of scope per the brief's guard.
- Lifting `KEYBOARD_MASTERY_LEVELS` and `DEFAULT_SHORTCUTS` into `src/shared/` ŌĆö both are non-divergent pure-data modules and could move to `src/shared/` as a future hygiene pass, but doing so would touch the renderer's import paths (the renderer-side files would need to be retargeted) which violates the "read-only renderer" rule. The cross-fork relative-path import is the lowest-risk pattern and matches the L2.5 precedent (`ShortcutsHelpModal`, `MarkdownRenderer`, etc.).

#### Deferred / out-of-scope for this brief

- **Lifting the `ParticipantCard` consumer site** ŌĆö the renderer mounts this card inside the group-chat participant grid; wiring the lifted card into a webFull host requires the participant-state subscription threaded through `participant` / `state` props, which in webFull would be a WS-backed feed rather than the renderer's process-manager state. That wiring is a downstream-layer concern.
- **Lifting `SessionState` into a shared types module** ŌĆö currently imported via the renderer-types transitive edge `'../../renderer/types'`. The renderer `types/index.ts` exports it at line 53. A future fork-hygiene sweep should promote `SessionState` into `src/shared/session-types.ts` (or similar) so the cross-fork edge can flip to a shared-source import. - **Lifting `getStatusColor`** ŌĆö currently imported from `'../../renderer/utils/theme'`. The helper is pure (no `window.maestro` references) but lives in a `theme.tsx` file alongside renderer-only utilities (`getContextColor`, file-explorer icon helpers); a clean lift would either pull just `getStatusColor` into `src/webFull/utils/theme.ts` (sibling of the existing `cssCustomProperties.ts`) or promote it into `src/shared/theme-utils.ts`. The cross-fork transitive-import shape matches the L2.5 precedent set by `GroupChatMessages` for `stripMarkdown` / `generateTerminalProseStyles`, so the lift is acceptable as-is. Promotion into shared is a future fork-hygiene sweep.
- **Lifting the renderer `clipboard.ts`** ŌĆö the lifted card consumes only `safeClipboardWrite` (browser API only); the sibling `safeClipboardWriteImage` reaches into `window.maestro.shell.copyImageToClipboard`. A future lift of just `safeClipboardWrite` into `src/webFull/utils/clipboard.ts` would neutralize the cross-fork edge that several L2.5 lifts (`GroupChatMessages`, `CollapsibleJsonViewer`, `AgentErrorModal`, and now `ParticipantCard`) share. Out of scope for this brief; tracked across the L2.5 wave as a deferred fork-hygiene sweep.

### Decisions 2026-06-08 ŌĆö Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `MergeProgressOverlay` lift

**Closes:** ISC-44.layer-2.5.merge_progress_overlay.

Branch `leaf-merge-progress-overlay` cut off `main @ 92a12bc38` (post the `docs(isa)` decision entry that recorded LOCAL-only deployment scope). Lifts the small, pure presentational `MergeProgressOverlay` (339 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API per pre-flight grep) into `src/webFull/` to close out the per-feature ISC `ISC-44.layer-2.5.merge_progress_overlay`. This is one of the simpler leaf lifts the parade has shipped: the overlay is an inline replacement for the input area while a context merge is in flight, gates all side effects through an `onCancel` prop, owns one piece of local UI state (`showCancelConfirm`), and threads a self-contained `setInterval`-driven elapsed-time chip through a nested memoized child (`ElapsedTimeDisplay`). There is no `window.maestro` reach at module-load or runtime ŌĆö the consuming parent owns the context-grooming service wiring.

The lift carries two adapt decisions worth naming explicitly:

1. **`Theme` import-path swap (standard L2.5 precedent).** Renderer source imports `Theme` from `'../types'` which routes through `src/renderer/types/index.ts` and re-exports the canonical type from `src/shared/theme-types`. The lifted module imports directly from `'../../shared/theme-types'` to avoid the indirection-through-renderer-aggregator silent-drift surface. Matches every L2.1 / L2.3 / L2.4 / L2.5 lift before it.

2. **`GroomingProgress` / `MergeResult` cross-fork type-only import.** Renderer source imports both from `'../types/contextMerge'` (a renderer-only types file at `src/renderer/types/contextMerge.ts`). The lifted module imports from `'../../renderer/types/contextMerge'` rather than copying the type module into the webFull tree. This matches the established cross-fork type precedent (`GroupChatHeader` imports `Shortcut` from `'../../renderer/types'`; `ShortcutsHelpModal` imports `Shortcut` + `KeyboardMasteryStats` from the same; `MarkdownRenderer` imports `FileNode` from `'../../renderer/types/fileTree'`). The lifted module touches only three primitive fields off `MergeResult` (`success`, `error`, `tokensSaved`) and the discriminated-union `stage` plus the `progress: number` off `GroomingProgress`. Duplicating the entire `contextMerge.ts` (177 LOC carrying eight interfaces, several of which transitively pull `LogEntry` from the renderer types aggregator and `ToolType` / `UsageStats` from `src/shared/types`) into `src/webFull/types/` would create a silent-drift surface for every future change to the context-grooming protocol. The renderer aggregator stays canonical until a future port consolidates the context-merge type surface into `src/shared/`, at which point this import line flips with a single sed.

Composition shape: no `Modal` / `ModalFooter` / layer-stack registration. The overlay is an inline replacement for the input area, NOT a modal ŌĆö it renders as a bordered card with a status head icon, a title row (with elapsed-time chip + cancel affordance), an animated progress bar, the four-stage indicator row, an error message body on failure, and an optional completion-stats line. The cancel-confirmation sub-overlay it gates is a bare `absolute inset-0` div scoped to the card (verbatim from the renderer source); future plumbing could re-port that confirmation onto the L2.1 `Modal` primitive if chrome converges, but that would be a behavior change rather than a verbatim lift and is explicitly out of scope.

Theme access pattern: kept the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention, consistent with every L2.x lift. Callers in webFull call `const { theme } = useTheme()` at the feature-component level and thread it down.

Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/MergeProgressOverlay.parity.test.ts`, 11 stories: 5 happy + 6 negative ŌĆö meets the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards):

- Happy: active-state title interpolation surfacing both `sourceName` + `targetName` literal copy plus the cancel affordance (`button[title="Cancel"]`); completion-state title (`Contexts Merged`) plus all four stage labels rendered verbatim (`Collect contexts`, `Groom with AI`, `Add to session`, `Complete`); completion stats line with `toLocaleString`-formatted token count (`Saved ~12,345 tokens through deduplication`); inline cancel-confirmation card surfacing the canonical `Cancel Merge?` / `Continue` / `Cancel` copy after the first cancel-button click; fallback active-state title (`Merging Contexts...`) when source/target names are omitted.
- Negative: cancel affordance suppressed once `progress.stage === 'complete'` (a future refactor breaking the `!isComplete` gate would silently leave the cancel button live after a successful merge); failure title (`Merge Failed`) + error message body surfacing on `result.success === false`; completion stats line suppressed when `tokensSaved === 0` (a future refactor flipping `> 0` to `>= 0` would silently emit `Saved ~0 tokens` copy); elapsed-time chip suppressed in the completion state via a `body:not(:has(span.font-mono))` exclusion (a future refactor breaking the `!isComplete && !hasError` gate would silently leave the chip ticking forever); inline confirmation suppressed before the first cancel-button click (a future refactor that defaulted `showCancelConfirm` to `true` would silently expose the confirm copy on mount); no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle pin guarding the contract that all side effects flow through `onCancel`.

6 catalog-shape vitest guards: Ōēź3 happy stories, Ōēź1 negative-per-happy, allowed-verb-only, non-empty given/when/then, IPC-leak guard, and a presentational-contract pin that asserts the catalog never drifts toward `role="dialog"` positive targets on the overlay root (the overlay is inline chrome above the input area, NOT a modal ŌĆö a future refactor wrapping it in a modal is a behavior change and should fail the catalog rather than silently track it; negative-path `:not(:has(...))` exclusions remain permitted because they assert ABSENCE).

#### Files added (NEW, additive only)

- `src/webFull/components/MergeProgressOverlay.tsx` (body lifted verbatim from the 339-LOC renderer source; extended header documents the two import-path adapts + the cross-fork type-only import + the inline-overlay-not-modal composition shape).

#### Deferred / out-of-scope for this brief

- **Wiring the lifted `MergeProgressOverlay` into a webFull `MainPanel` / merge-flow host.** The overlay surfaces `(progress, result, sourceName, targetName, onCancel, startTime)` via props; a future feature-wiring pass that mounts this overlay in a webFull tab will need the context-grooming service ported to a server-side `/api/context-merge/*` REST + WS surface, plus a webFull-side hook to drive `progress` updates through WS frames and `onCancel` through a REST `DELETE /api/context-merge/:runId` call. That is a multi-file architecture lift, out of scope per the brief's verbatim-lift guard.
- **Lifting the `contextMerge.ts` types into `src/shared/`.** The renderer-only file at `src/renderer/types/contextMerge.ts` carries the eight context-merge interfaces (`ContextSource`, `MergeRequest`, `MergeResult`, `GroomingProgress`, `DuplicateInfo`, `DuplicateDetectionResult`, `SummarizeRequest`, `SummarizeResult`, `SummarizeProgress`). When the context-grooming substrate lifts to a server-side surface, the canonical home for these types should be `src/shared/context-merge-types.ts` (with both the renderer overlay and the webFull overlay importing from there). For now, the cross-fork import is the lowest-risk pattern and matches the `GroupChatHeader` / `ShortcutsHelpModal` / `MarkdownRenderer` precedent for cross-fork type-only imports.
- **Re-porting the cancel-confirmation sub-overlay onto the L2.1 `Modal` primitive.** The renderer source uses a bare `absolute inset-0` div scoped to the card; the lifted module preserves that verbatim. A future plumbing pass could replace it with `<Modal>` for chrome consistency with the rest of the L2.5 surface, but that's a behavior change (modal chrome includes layer-stack registration, focus trap, Escape-to-close ŌĆö none of which the inline overlay carries today) and is out of scope for a verbatim lift.

### Decisions 2026-06-08 ŌĆö Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö paired `SummarizeProgressOverlay` + `AutoRunLightbox` lift

**Closes:** ISC-44.layer-2.5.summarize_progress_overlay + ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_lightbox.

Branch `leaf-summarize-and-autorun-lightbox` cut off `main @ d3c8164e4` (`chore(format): prettier ISA.md from merge chain #12`). Pairs two L2.5 leaf-parade sibling lifts under one branch so they share a single atomic commit: the `SummarizeProgressOverlay` (sibling of `MergeProgressOverlay`) and the `AutoRunLightbox` (sibling of `LightboxModal`). Both are pure presentational surfaces with 0 module-load IPC and 0 Electron-only module-load APIs (pre-flight grep `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|ipcRenderer"` returned empty exit 1 on BOTH source files); both have prior-art lifts in the same wave that establish every adapt decision needed; both close per-feature ISCs that close cleanly under the `ISC-44.<tab>.<deferral>` tracking convention.

This brief intentionally batches two leaves into one commit because (a) they share the same import-path adapt pattern (`Theme` cross-fork swap, optional renderer-side types or utilities pulled directly via `'../../renderer/...'`), (b) they share the same parity-catalog shape (Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy + 5-6 catalog-shape vitest guards), (c) they share the same scope-guard contract (additive only ŌĆö no `src/web/` / `src/renderer/` / `src/main/` / `src/server/` touches), and (d) Trillium's brief explicitly requested a single atomic commit covering both. The wave's per-leaf branch convention is preserved ŌĆö each per-feature ISC is independently tracked and each component documents its own header inline ŌĆö but the branch / commit / Decisions entry are unified to keep the lift queue moving without proliferating tiny review surfaces.

#### `SummarizeProgressOverlay` adapt decisions

Two import-path adapts identical to the sibling `MergeProgressOverlay` precedent:

1. **`Theme` import-path swap (standard L2.5 precedent).** Renderer imports `Theme` from `'../types'` which routes through `src/renderer/types/index.ts` and re-exports the canonical type from `src/shared/theme-types`. The lifted module imports directly from `'../../shared/theme-types'`. Matches every L2.1 / L2.3 / L2.4 / L2.5 lift before it.

2. **`SummarizeProgress` / `SummarizeResult` cross-fork type-only import.** Renderer imports both from `'../types/contextMerge'` (a renderer-only types file at `src/renderer/types/contextMerge.ts`). The lifted module imports from `'../../renderer/types/contextMerge'` rather than copying the type module into the webFull tree. Same precedent as the sibling `MergeProgressOverlay` lift; same rationale (duplicating the 177-LOC `contextMerge.ts` file would create a silent-drift surface for every future change to the context-grooming protocol). The renderer aggregator stays canonical until a future port consolidates the context-merge type surface into `src/shared/`, at which point both this import line AND the `MergeProgressOverlay` import line flip together with a single sed.

Composition shape is identical to the sibling: no `Modal` / `ModalFooter` / layer-stack registration ŌĆö inline replacement for the input area, NOT a modal. The cancel-confirmation it gates is a bare `absolute inset-0` div scoped to the card. Distinguishing surface features vs `MergeProgressOverlay`: different stage vocabulary (`extracting` ŌåÆ `summarizing` ŌåÆ `creating` ŌåÆ `complete`), different cancel-confirmation copy (`Cancel Compaction?` / `No` / `Yes` instead of `Cancel Merge?` / `Continue` / `Cancel`), different completion-stats line (`Reduced context by N% (~M ŌåÆ ~K tokens)` with `toLocaleString`-formatted counts instead of `Saved ~N tokens through deduplication`), different head icon (`Wand2` instead of `GitMerge`).

#### `AutoRunLightbox` adapt decisions

Same six import-path adapts as the sibling `LightboxModal` lift, with one notable refinement: `safeClipboardWrite` resolves to the webFull-native lift at `'../utils/clipboard'` (text-only Clipboard API, pure browser surface ŌĆö no `window.maestro` reach), while `safeClipboardWriteImage` continues to use the cross-fork re-import at `'../../renderer/utils/clipboard'` because the Image helper reaches the Electron preload bridge. The webFull `clipboard.ts` header documents the rationale for not lifting the Image helper explicitly: it depends on `window.maestro.shell.copyImageToClipboard` which does not exist in the webFull host. On webFull the optional-chain falls through to `navigator.clipboard.write()` via `fetch(dataUrl) ŌåÆ blob() ŌåÆ ClipboardItem`. That runtime branch is library-internal to `clipboard.ts` and is NOT part of the parity contract.

Distinguishing surface features vs `LightboxModal`: portaled into `document.body` via `createPortal` (vs LightboxModal's inline `absolute inset-0`), z-index `9999` (vs LightboxModal's `100`), an extra Copy Markdown button that emits a `![alt](path)` reference with URL-encoded path + filename-derived alt text, an explicit Close button (vs LightboxModal's reliance on click-outside + Escape), filename surfaced in the bottom info strip, and the external-URL branch that suppresses BOTH navigation arrows AND the delete button regardless of `attachmentsList.length`. The component registers with the LayerStack at `MODAL_PRIORITIES.AUTORUN_LIGHTBOX` (distinct from `MODAL_PRIORITIES.LIGHTBOX`) so Escape-handling cascades correctly with parent overlays in the Auto Run flow.

- `src/webFull/components/SummarizeProgressOverlay.parity.test.ts` (11 stories: 5 happy + 6 negative; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards).
- `src/webFull/components/AutoRunLightbox.tsx` (body lifted verbatim from the 338-LOC renderer source; extended header documents the six import-path adapts + the runtime-only Electron branch on `safeClipboardWriteImage` + the portal composition shape).
- `src/webFull/components/AutoRunLightbox.parity.test.ts` (11 stories: 5 happy + 6 negative; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards).

- `ISA.md` ŌĆö appended this Decisions entry; added `ISC-44.layer-2.5.summarize_progress_overlay` and `ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_lightbox` directly as `[x]` CLOSED at the per-feature ISC list (lines immediately precede `ISC-44.layer-2.5.merge_progress_overlay`, ordered by lift sequence).

#### Files NOT touched

- `src/renderer/` ŌĆö read-only oracle. The renderer continues to render the renderer `SummarizeProgressOverlay` and `AutoRunLightbox` as part of the Electron entrypoint.
- `src/web/` ŌĆö fork-hygiene read-only.
- `src/main/` ŌĆö no new IPC handlers; both surfaces are purely visual and prop-callback-driven. The context-summarize service that drives `SummarizeProgressOverlay` lives behind `window.maestro.contextSummarize.*` IPC; the auto-run attachment service that drives `AutoRunLightbox` lives behind `window.maestro.directorNotes.*` IPC. Both are out of scope for this lift.

- **Parity tests:** `MAESTRO_HEADLESS=1 npx vitest run src/webFull/components/SummarizeProgressOverlay.parity.test.ts src/webFull/components/AutoRunLightbox.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ 12/12 PASS (6 in-suite guards per file ├Ś 2 files), ~12ms test time.
- **Scope-guard `git diff`s:** `git diff main -- src/web/ src/renderer/ src/main/ src/server/ | wc -c` ŌåÆ 0 (read-only oracles + main + server untouched).

#### Deferred / out-of-scope for this brief

- **Wiring `SummarizeProgressOverlay` into a webFull `MainPanel` / summarize-flow host.** Surfaces `(progress, result, onCancel, startTime)` via props; future feature-wiring will need a server-side `/api/context-summarize/*` REST + WS surface plus a webFull-side hook to drive `progress` updates through WS frames and `onCancel` through a REST `DELETE /api/context-summarize/:runId` call. Multi-file architecture lift, out of scope per the verbatim-lift rule.
- **Wiring `AutoRunLightbox` into a webFull Auto Run host.** Surfaces `(attachmentsList, attachmentPreviews, lightboxFilename, lightboxExternalUrl, onClose, onNavigate, onDelete)` via props; future feature-wiring will need server-side `/api/director-notes/attachments/*` REST + WS surfaces plus a webFull-side hook to materialize the `attachmentPreviews` map from disk-backed image files. Multi-file architecture lift, out of scope per the verbatim-lift rule.
- **Lifting the `contextMerge.ts` types into `src/shared/`.** Same deferral as the sibling `MergeProgressOverlay` lift ŌĆö the renderer-only file carries the eight context-merge interfaces (`ContextSource`, `MergeRequest`, `MergeResult`, `GroomingProgress`, `DuplicateInfo`, `DuplicateDetectionResult`, `SummarizeRequest`, `SummarizeResult`, `SummarizeProgress`). When the context-grooming substrate lifts to a server-side surface, the canonical home for these types should be `src/shared/context-merge-types.ts`. For now, the cross-fork import is the lowest-risk pattern and matches the precedent set by `MergeProgressOverlay`.
- **Lifting `safeClipboardWriteImage` into webFull.** The Image clipboard helper currently lives in `src/renderer/utils/clipboard.ts` and reaches the Electron preload bridge `window.maestro.shell.copyImageToClipboard`. A future webFull-side adapter could replace the runtime branch with a pure browser `navigator.clipboard.write()` call (the renderer's fallback path is already pure), at which point both `LightboxModal` and `AutoRunLightbox` would flip from the cross-fork re-import to a webFull-native lift. Out of scope for this brief; tracked across the L2.5 wave as a deferred fork-hygiene sweep.
- **Re-porting the cancel-confirmation sub-overlay onto the L2.1 `Modal` primitive** (applies to `SummarizeProgressOverlay`). Same deferral as the sibling `MergeProgressOverlay` lift ŌĆö the inline `absolute inset-0` div preserves the renderer-source verbatim; replacing it with `<Modal>` would be a behavior change (modal chrome includes layer-stack registration, focus trap, Escape-to-close ŌĆö none of which the inline overlay carries today) and is out of scope for a verbatim lift.

### Decisions 2026-06-08 ŌĆö Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `SessionActivityGraph` lift

**Closes:** ISC-44.layer-2.5.session_activity_graph.

Branch `leaf-session-activity-graph` cut off `main @ d3c8164e4` (head as of fetch, the prettier-formatted ISA chore commit). Lifts the small, pure presentational `SessionActivityGraph` (365 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API per pre-flight grep) into `src/webFull/` to close out the per-feature ISC `ISC-44.layer-2.5.session_activity_graph`. The component is a generic histogram ŌĆö buckets any `ActivityEntry[]` (anything with a `timestamp: string | number`) across a configurable lookback window, exposes a right-click context menu of lookback options, fires `onBarClick(start, end)` for non-empty bars, and surfaces a hover tooltip with the bucket time-range label + count. It is used by the History surface in the renderer to scrub session activity over time. There is no `window.maestro` reach at module-load or runtime ŌĆö the consuming parent owns the entry source.

The lift carries two adapt decisions worth naming explicitly:

1. **`Theme` import-path swap (standard L2.5 precedent).** Renderer source imports `Theme` from `'../types'` which routes through `src/renderer/types/index.ts` and re-exports the canonical type from `src/shared/theme-types`. The lifted module imports directly from `'../../shared/theme-types'` to avoid the indirection-through-renderer-aggregator silent-drift surface. Matches every L2.1 / L2.3 / L2.4 / L2.5 lift before it.

2. **`useContextMenuPosition` cross-fork hook import.** Renderer source imports the hook from `'../hooks/ui/useContextMenuPosition'` (a pure renderer hook at `src/renderer/hooks/ui/useContextMenuPosition.ts`, 51 LOC). The lifted module imports it from `'../../renderer/hooks/ui/useContextMenuPosition'` rather than duplicating the hook into `src/webFull/hooks/ui/`. Pre-flight grep on the hook (`grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|ipcRenderer" src/renderer/hooks/ui/useContextMenuPosition.ts`) returns empty ŌĆö the hook uses `useLayoutEffect` + `useState` + `window.innerWidth` / `window.innerHeight` bounding-rect math only. This matches the established cross-fork hook precedent set by `AgentPromptComposerModal`, which imports `useTemplateAutocomplete` (and transitively `useClickOutside`) from the renderer-side `src/renderer/hooks/input/` tree by relative path. Duplicating the hook would create the silent-drift surface that Architect audit risk A explicitly warns against; promoting it into `src/webFull/hooks/ui/` would be a fork-hygiene improvement but is out of scope for a verbatim lift.

Composition shape: no `Modal` / `ModalFooter` / layer-stack registration. The graph is an inline histogram, NOT a modal. The right-click context menu is a bare `fixed z-50` panel positioned via the cross-fork `useContextMenuPosition` hook (verbatim from the renderer source) that closes itself on the next document click via a self-installing `document.addEventListener('click', ...)` lifecycle. The hover tooltip is an absolute-positioned `<div>` below the bar row driven by `hoveredIndex` state. Bar clicks and lookback changes are caller-owned callbacks ŌĆö this component does not reach into `window.maestro` or any transport itself.

Theme access pattern: kept the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention, consistent with every L2.x lift. Callers in webFull call `const { theme } = useTheme()` at the feature-component level and thread it down.

Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/SessionActivityGraph.parity.test.ts`, 10 stories: 5 happy + 5 negative ŌĆö meets the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 7 catalog-shape vitest guards):

- Happy: host-element summary tooltip rendering the `<label>: <N> sessions (right-click to change)` copy with the active lookback label + total session count; right-click context menu surfacing the "Lookback Period" heading + every `LOOKBACK_OPTIONS` label (`24 hours`, `72 hours`, `1 week`, `2 weeks`, `1 month`, `6 months`, `1 year`, `All time`); active-option `svg.lucide-check` checkmark icon; 1-week-lookback axis labels (`Xd` + `Now`); 24-hour-lookback axis labels (`Xh` + `0h`).
- Negative: singular `session ` (no trailing `s`) tooltip copy when count is exactly 1 (a future refactor flipping the `!== 1` guard to `> 1` would silently emit `1 sessions`); zero-session tooltip when entries are empty (the empty state is observable, not suppressed); context-menu suppression before any right-click (no `svg.lucide-check` in the DOM until the menu opens); no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle pin guarding the contract that all side effects flow through `onBarClick` and `onLookbackChange`; renders without the optional `onBarClick` prop (clicking a bar is a no-op when undefined).

7 catalog-shape vitest guards: Ōēź3 happy stories, Ōēź1 negative-per-happy, allowed-verb-only, non-empty given/when/then, IPC-leak guard, unique-name guard, and a presentational-contract pin that asserts the catalog never drifts toward `role="dialog"` positive targets on the histogram root (a future refactor wrapping the graph in a modal is a behavior change and should fail the catalog rather than silently track it; negative-path `:not(:has(...))` exclusions remain permitted because they assert ABSENCE).

#### Files added (NEW, additive only)

- `src/webFull/components/SessionActivityGraph.tsx` (body lifted verbatim from the 365-LOC renderer source; extended header documents the two import-path adapts + the cross-fork hook-import rationale + the inline-histogram-not-modal composition shape).
- `src/webFull/components/SessionActivityGraph.parity.test.ts` (10 stories: 5 happy + 5 negative; 7 catalog-shape vitest guards).

#### Files modified (additive only)

- `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended a `SessionActivityGraph` block (component + `LOOKBACK_OPTIONS` value re-export + `LookbackPeriod` / `ActivityEntry` type re-exports) under the existing Layer 2.5 leaf-parade section, after the `MergeProgressOverlay` line.
- `ISA.md` ŌĆö appended this Decisions entry; added `ISC-44.layer-2.5.session_activity_graph` directly as `[x]` CLOSED at the per-feature ISC list (line immediately precedes `ISC-44.layer-2.5.merge_progress_overlay`, ordered by lift sequence).

#### Files NOT touched

- `src/renderer/` ŌĆö read-only oracle. The renderer continues to render the renderer `SessionActivityGraph` as part of the Electron entrypoint via its History panel wiring.
- `src/web/` ŌĆö fork-hygiene read-only.
- `src/main/` ŌĆö no new IPC handlers; the histogram surface is purely visual and prop-callback-driven. The activity-entry source (renderer's stats DB / history database) lives behind `window.maestro.history.*` / `window.maestro.stats.*` IPC, which is out of scope for this lift.
- `src/server/` ŌĆö untouched. A future webFull host that mounts this histogram will need a server-side `/api/history` or `/api/stats/activity` REST surface (or a WS feed) to source the entries; that surface is its own brief.

- **Pre-flight on the cross-fork hook:** `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|ipcRenderer" src/renderer/hooks/ui/useContextMenuPosition.ts` ŌåÆ empty (exit 1). Hook is pure: `useLayoutEffect` + `useState` + browser bounding-rect math only.
