#### Deferred / out-of-scope for this brief

- **Wiring the lifted `SessionActivityGraph` into a webFull History host.** The graph surfaces `(entries, theme, onBarClick, lookbackHours, onLookbackChange)` via props; a future feature-wiring pass that mounts this graph in a webFull History view will need the activity-entry source ported to a server-side REST / WS surface (renderer currently reads from the per-agent JSONL history files via `window.maestro.history.list` / `window.maestro.stats.getActivity` IPC), plus a webFull-side hook to subscribe and drive `lookbackHours` through the existing settings broadcast. That is a multi-file architecture lift, out of scope per the brief's verbatim-lift guard.
- **Lifting `useContextMenuPosition` into `src/webFull/hooks/ui/`.** Currently imported via the cross-fork edge `'../../renderer/hooks/ui/useContextMenuPosition'`. The hook is pure (51 LOC, browser API only) and would lift mechanically; the cross-fork edge is the lowest-risk shape for this brief and matches the `AgentPromptComposerModal` precedent for `useTemplateAutocomplete` / `useClickOutside`. A future fork-hygiene sweep should promote it.
- **Re-porting the right-click context menu onto a shared menu primitive.** No shared menu primitive yet exists in webFull; the renderer source builds the menu inline as a `fixed z-50 py-1 rounded border shadow-lg` panel. If a future `<ContextMenu>` primitive lands in `src/webFull/components/ui/`, this menu (plus the renderer's `GroupChatList` / `FileExplorerPanel` context menus) becomes a candidate for plumbing convergence ŌĆö that's a separate architectural lift, out of scope here.

### Decisions 2026-06-08 ŌĆö Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `SessionItem` lift

**Closes:** ISC-44.layer-2.5.session_item.

Branch `leaf-session-item` cut off `main @ d3c8164e4` (the prettier `chore(format): prettier ISA.md from merge chain #12` commit that followed the prior leaf-parade merge wave). Lifts the renderer's unified per-agent row primitive `SessionItem` (360 LOC, 0 module-load IPC, 0 Electron-only API per pre-flight grep) into `src/webFull/` to close out the per-feature ISC `ISC-44.layer-2.5.session_item`. This is the row component the renderer's Left Bar (`SessionList`) uses for ALL list contexts (`bookmark` / `group` / `flat` / `ungrouped` / `worktree`) ŌĆö one component, one `variant` discriminant, five render shapes. The renderer commit that introduced it explicitly framed it as "unifies 4 previously separate implementations"; this lift preserves the unified contract verbatim.

**Crucial framing the lift carries (so a future maintainer doesn't trip on it):** `SessionItem` is NOT a replacement for the earlier L2.5 `SessionListItem` lift. The L2.5 `SessionListItem` ships against the webFull L4.1 `SessionList` parity catalog (status dot + name + AI/Ōīś pill, status-color via `<StatusDot>`); that file IS the agent-row the L4.1 SessionList catalog asserts against. `SessionItem` carries the FULL renderer feature surface (worktree variant, bookmark variant, group badge, jump-number badge, git-dirty indicator, location pills, AUTO/ERR pills, hollow-ring claude-no-session branch, unread badge, inline rename, drag-and-drop) ŌĆö a substantially larger surface that the webFull L4.1 catalog does NOT yet assert against. Shipping `SessionItem` here lets a downstream `SessionList` re-architecture swap the lighter `SessionListItem` shape for this richer one without re-lifting code; the swap itself would extend the L4.1 catalog with the additional variant + pill assertions and is a separate behavior change. Both files coexist in the webFull tree intentionally.

The lift carries two adapt decisions worth naming explicitly:

1. **`Session` / `Group` / `Theme` consolidated through the renderer types aggregator.** Renderer source imports all three from `'../types'` (a single-line import: `import type { Session, Group, Theme } from '../types';`). The lifted module flips that to `'../../renderer/types'` ŌĆö a single-line import again, preserving source fidelity. The `Theme` type could have routed through `'../../shared/theme-types'` directly per the standard L2.5 swap (and the renderer aggregator IS a pure re-export of the shared canonical), but keeping all three on the same renderer-types line preserves the verbatim-lift posture and avoids splitting one import across two lines. The transitive `Theme` resolution still terminates at `src/shared/theme-types.ts` via the renderer aggregator's `export type { Theme, ThemeId, ThemeMode, ThemeColors } from '../../shared/theme-types'`. `Session` and `Group` carry no transitive `window.maestro` reach ŌĆö both are pure data shapes (`Session` is a discriminated interface defined inline in the renderer types module; `Group` is re-exported from `src/shared/types`).

2. **`getStatusColor` cross-fork transitive import from `'../../renderer/utils/theme'`.** Renderer source imports `getStatusColor` from `'../utils/theme'`. The lifted module flips that to `'../../renderer/utils/theme'`, matching the L2.5 `ParticipantCard` precedent (which uses the identical edge). The helper is pure ŌĆö a `SessionState ŌåÆ ThemeColor` mapping with no IPC reach. Promoting it into `src/webFull/utils/theme.ts` (sibling of the existing `cssCustomProperties.ts`) or `src/shared/theme-utils.ts` would neutralize the cross-fork edge, but the renderer `theme.tsx` carries unrelated renderer-only utilities (`getContextColor`, file-explorer icon helpers) so a clean lift would need to split the file. Out of scope for this brief; tracked as a deferred fork-hygiene sweep below.

The rest of the import set (`react` for `memo`, `react` types for `DragEvent` / `MouseEvent`, `lucide-react` icons for `Activity` / `GitBranch` / `Bot` / `Bookmark` / `AlertCircle` / `Server`) resolves identically in both trees ŌĆö already webFull-tree deps used by every L2.5 sibling.

Composition shape: no `Modal` / `ModalFooter` / layer-stack registration. The component is a flat container `<div draggable>` row that branches its className per variant and then renders left-side (name + metadata) and right-side (indicator pills + status dot + unread badge) clusters inside a flexbox. The `variant === 'worktree'` branch suppresses the metadata row entirely and uses smaller text + extra left padding to read as a nested child under its parent; the `variant === 'flat'` branch uses `mx-3` + rounded styling for the flat-list-only context; the other three (`bookmark` / `group` / `ungrouped`) share the default `px-4 py-2` chrome. Drag-and-drop is HTML5-native via the `draggable` attribute + the `onDragStart` / `onDragOver` / `onDrop` callbacks; the parity catalog asserts the `draggable="true"` attribute presence but leaves drag-state mutation observation to the parent `SessionList` catalog. Inline rename uses an auto-focused `<input>` with Enter-to-commit + blur-to-commit; the input only mounts when `isEditing=true`, which the parent owns and toggles via `onStartRename` + `onFinishRename`. The bookmark icon has three render branches: filled-only in the `bookmark` variant (the `Remove bookmark`-titled toggle), filled-or-outline with hover-reveal in the other variants (the `Add bookmark` / `Remove bookmark` toggle), and entirely suppressed for worktree children (the `!session.parentSessionId` guard, since worktree rows inherit bookmark status from their parent).

Theme access pattern: kept the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention, consistent with every L2.x lift. Callers in webFull call `const { theme } = useTheme()` at the feature-component level and thread it down through the `SessionList` to each row.

Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/SessionItem.parity.test.ts`, 15 stories: 8 happy + 7 negative ŌĆö comfortably exceeds the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 7 catalog-shape vitest guards):

- **Happy paths (8):** flat-variant draggable row carrying the session name + the `claude-code` toolType label as visible text (pins the `[draggable="true"]` attribute presence + the metadata-row tool-type rendering); bookmark variant rendering the group badge with the group's `name` as visible text (pins the bookmark-variant-only group-badge branch); worktree variant suppressing the metadata row + adding the branch glyph (pins the `variant !== 'worktree'` metadata-row guard via `:not(:has(.text-\[10px\]))` exclusion); `AUTO` pill surfacing with `title="Auto Run active"` when `isInBatch=true` (pins the Auto-Run indicator branch); `ERR` pill surfacing with `title="Error: <msg>"` when `session.agentError` is set (pins the error-pill branch + the error-message interpolation); inline rename input mounting when `isEditing=true` (pins the `<input>` mount); jump-number badge surfacing when `jumpNumber` is provided (pins the Opt+Cmd+NUMBER affordance render); `GIT` pill surfacing with `title="Git repository"` for git-repo sessions in non-bookmark / non-worktree variants (pins the location-pill ladder).
- **Negative paths (7):** location-pill suppression for terminal sessions (pins the `session.toolType !== 'terminal'` predicate via `:not(:has([title="Git repository"]))`); `AUTO` pill suppression when `isInBatch=false` (pins the default-state); `ERR` pill suppression when `agentError` is absent (pins the missing-error default-state via `:not(:has([title^="Error:"]))`); inline rename input suppression when `isEditing=false` (pins the read-only default-state via `:not(:has(input))`); `GIT` pill suppression in the bookmark variant even for git repos (pins the `variant !== 'bookmark'` location-ladder guard); bookmark toggle suppression in the worktree variant via the `!session.parentSessionId` guard (pins both the `Add bookmark` and `Remove bookmark` tooltip absence); unread badge suppression when the row IS active (pins the `!isActive` guard via `:not(:has([title="Unread messages"]))`).

7 catalog-shape vitest guards: Ōēź3 happy stories floor; Ōēź1 negative-per-happy floor (`negative >= max(1, ceil(happy / 8))` formula matches the established `SessionListItem` precedent); allowed-verb-only; non-empty given/when/then; IPC-leak guard (banned target list `window.maestro` / `shell.openpath` / `shell.openexternal` / `dialog.` / `tunnel.` / `ipcrenderer`); render-shape-only guard (restricts `then` verbs to `hasElement` / `hasText` since SessionItem is purely presentational ŌĆö click / drag / rename / bookmark-toggle wiring belongs to the parent `SessionList` parity catalog where the callbacks are bound); and a unique-story-name guard.

#### Files added (NEW, additive only)

- `src/webFull/components/SessionItem.tsx` (body lifted verbatim from the 360-LOC renderer source; extended header documents the two import-path adapts + the cross-fork-precedent rationale + the "not a replacement for `SessionListItem`" framing + the full IN-scope feature list).
- `src/webFull/components/SessionItem.parity.test.ts` (15 stories: 8 happy + 7 negative; 7 catalog-shape vitest guards).

#### Files modified (additive only)

- `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended `export { SessionItem } from './SessionItem';` + `export type { SessionItemProps, SessionItemVariant } from './SessionItem';` after the existing `MergeProgressOverlay` export under the Layer 2.5 leaf-parade section. Barrel collisions resolved mechanically by the `merge=union` driver landed in commit `e3540a054`.
- `ISA.md` ŌĆö this Decisions entry; added `ISC-44.layer-2.5.session_item` directly as `[x]` CLOSED at the per-feature ISC list (line immediately follows `ISC-44.layer-2.5.merge_progress_overlay`, ordered by lift sequence).

#### Files NOT touched

- `src/renderer/` ŌĆö read-only oracle. The renderer continues to import `SessionItem` from `'../SessionItem'` inside `src/renderer/components/SessionList/SessionList.tsx` and render the renderer module as part of the Electron entrypoint.
- `src/web/` ŌĆö fork-hygiene read-only.
- `src/main/` ŌĆö no new IPC handlers; the row surface is purely visual and prop-callback-driven.
- `src/server/` ŌĆö untouched.

#### Verification

- **Pre-flight on the renderer source:** `grep -nE "window\.maestro\.|window\.electron|ipcRenderer|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|window\.api" src/renderer/components/SessionItem.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (exit 1).
- **Pre-flight on the lifted source:** equivalent grep against `src/webFull/components/SessionItem.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (exit 1).
- **Lint:** `MAESTRO_HEADLESS=1 npm run build:prompts && MAESTRO_HEADLESS=1 npm run lint` ŌåÆ clean (3-config tsc sweep: `tsconfig.lint.json` + `tsconfig.main.json` + `tsconfig.cli.json`).
- **Parity test:** `MAESTRO_HEADLESS=1 npx vitest run src/webFull/components/SessionItem.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ PASS, 7/7 in-suite vitest guards green, ~4ms test time.
- **Scope-guard `git diff`s:** `git diff main..HEAD -- src/web/ | wc -c` ŌåÆ 0; same for `src/renderer/`, `src/main/`, `src/server/` (each ŌåÆ 0). Read-only oracles + main + server untouched.
- **Symlink hygiene:** `node_modules` symlinked to `/Users/trilliumsmith/code/maestro/node_modules` for the build/lint/test run; removed before commit so the worktree stays clean.
- **Push status:** NOT pushed per brief instruction. Branch sits locally at `leaf-session-item`, awaiting Trillium's review.

#### Deferred / out-of-scope for this brief

- **Swapping in `SessionItem` as the webFull `SessionList`'s row component.** That swap is a behavior expansion (the L4.1 `SessionList` parity catalog currently asserts against the lighter `SessionListItem` shape; extending it to assert against `SessionItem`'s variant matrix + pill ladder is a multi-story catalog edit). Out of scope per the verbatim-lift guard; tracked downstream as a SessionList re-architecture decision.
- **Lifting `Session` and `Group` into `src/shared/`.** `Session` is currently defined inline in `src/renderer/types/index.ts` (a discriminated interface with ~80 fields, several carrying nested types like `WorktreeConfig` / `BatchDocumentEntry` / `AgentError` / `AiTab` / `SymphonySessionMetadata`). `Group` is already a re-export from `src/shared/types` but routes through the renderer aggregator. A future fork-hygiene sweep should promote both into a `src/shared/session-types.ts` (or similar) so the cross-fork edge can flip to a shared-source import. Out of scope for this lift; matches the pattern used by `ParticipantCard` for the `SessionState` import.
- **Lifting `getStatusColor` into `src/webFull/utils/theme.ts` or `src/shared/theme-utils.ts`.** Currently imported from `'../../renderer/utils/theme'`. The helper is pure but lives in a `theme.tsx` file alongside renderer-only utilities (`getContextColor`, file-explorer icon helpers); a clean lift would either pull just `getStatusColor` into a sibling webFull util or promote it into shared. Out of scope; matches the `ParticipantCard` precedent.
- **Wiring drag-state observation into the catalog.** The `draggable="true"` attribute presence is asserted, but `onDragStart` / `onDragOver` / `onDrop` callback firing is wiring-level and belongs to the parent `SessionList` parity catalog. A future drag-and-drop e2e port would extend `SessionList.parity.test.ts` with drag-state stories rather than adding them here.

### 2026-06-08 ŌĆö Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö `CsvTableRenderer` lift

**Closes:** ISC-44.layer-2.5.csv_table_renderer.

Branch `leaf-csv-table-renderer` cut off `main @ 1107a0906` (head as of fetch, the prettier-formatted ISA chore commit). Lifts the small, pure presentational `CsvTableRenderer` (383 LOC, 0 IPC, 0 Electron-only API per pre-flight grep) into `src/webFull/` to close out the per-feature ISC `ISC-44.layer-2.5.csv_table_renderer`. The component is the file-preview surface's renderer for delimited `.csv` / `.tsv` payloads ŌĆö parses input strings (handles quoted fields + escaped quotes via a hand-rolled state machine, not a third-party CSV lib), groups header / data rows, infers per-column alignment from data content (>50% numeric ŌåÆ right-align), filters rows by `searchQuery` (case-insensitive, any-cell match), caps the rendered set at `MAX_DISPLAY_ROWS = 500` with a truncation banner, supports tri-state header-click sort (asc ŌåÆ desc ŌåÆ off), and highlights search matches inside cells with `<mark>` spans. Empty input renders an empty-state placeholder. There is no `window.maestro` reach at module-load or runtime ŌĆö the consuming parent (`FilePreview` in the renderer) owns the file-read flow and threads decoded text in via the `content` prop.

#### Pre-flight on the renderer source

`grep -nE "window\.maestro\.|window\.electron|ipcRenderer|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|window\.api|require\(" src/renderer/components/CsvTableRenderer.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (exit 1). The component is purely presentational ŌĆö all side effects flow through the `onMatchCount` callback; internal state is `sort: SortState | null` only (toggled by header clicks through the tri-state `null` ŌåÆ `asc` ŌåÆ `desc` ŌåÆ `null` cycle).

#### Import-path adapts (one, matching the L2.5 cross-fork precedent)

1. `Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'`. Standard L2.5 swap ŌĆö the renderer aggregator at `src/renderer/types/index.ts` re-exports `Theme` from `src/shared/theme-types`, so webFull pulls direct from the canonical source rather than transit the renderer barrel. Matches the precedent set by `SessionActivityGraph` / `MergeProgressOverlay` / `ThemePicker` / `MarkdownRenderer`.

#### Composition shape

No `Modal` / `ModalFooter` / layer-stack registration ŌĆö this is an inline content view, NOT a modal overlay. The `searchQuery` is consumer-controlled (the file-preview surface owns the search box) and the `onMatchCount` callback lets the parent surface match counts without re-running the filter logic itself. `lucide-react` icons (`ChevronUp`, `ChevronDown`) kept verbatim ŌĆö already a webFull-tree dep used by sibling L2.5 lifts.

#### Theme access pattern

Kept the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention, consistent with every L2.x lift. Callers thread `theme` down from `useTheme()`.

#### Parity catalog (`src/webFull/components/CsvTableRenderer.parity.test.ts`)

10 stories total (5 happy + 5 negative ŌĆö meets the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor); 6 catalog-shape vitest guards.

**Happy paths:**

- Well-formed CSV (`"name,age\nAlice,30\nBob,25"`) renders the `<table>` with header row, data rows, and `"2 rows ├Ś 2 columns"` footer copy.
- The row-number column prepends 1-indexed numbers (`#` header + `1`, `2`, `3` in the body).
- TSV-delimiter prop (`delimiter="\t"`) parses tab-separated content into separate columns.
- Search query (`searchQuery="al"`) wraps matching substrings in `<mark>` elements (case-insensitive, matches both "Alice" and "Carol") and filters out non-matching rows ("Bob" suppressed).
- Header click sorts ascending and surfaces the chevron-up SVG inside the active header cell while preserving the row set.

**Negative paths:**

- Empty content (`content=""`) renders the "Empty CSV file" empty-state copy and suppresses the `<table>` element entirely (the empty-state branch short-circuits).
- Search with no matches (`searchQuery="zzzz"`) surfaces the `"0 of 2 rows match"` footer copy, suppresses all `<mark>` elements, and renders no data cells.
- Quoted comma inside a field (`'"Smith, Jr.",hello'`) stays as one cell ŌĆö the embedded comma is preserved and the footer reflects `"1 rows ├Ś 2 columns"`, NOT `"1 rows ├Ś 3 columns"`.
- Data-only single-line input (`content="soloRow"`) treats the line as the header row, renders the `<table>` with one header cell, and surfaces `"0 rows"` in the footer (no data rows exist beyond the header).
- No-IPC / no-WS / no-FS / no-process-spawn lifecycle pin ŌĆö across mount + sort transitions + searchQuery cycling, asserts `wsFrameMatches`/`fsHas`/`processHas` are all `<none>` (sentinel-based negative-shape assertion).

Catalog uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary (`hasElement`, `hasText`, `wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`) and stays render-shape oriented for positive assertions while using the `<none>` sentinel for the IPC-leak lifecycle pin (the IPC-leak guard scans only stories whose `then` does not include a `<none>` sentinel ŌĆö a `wsFrameMatches` story with `value: '<none>'` is a NEGATIVE-shape assertion that the frame is NOT sent, so it correctly references the banned namespace `window.maestro.*` as the target of suppression, not a positive observation).

#### Files added (all under `src/webFull/`)

- `src/webFull/components/CsvTableRenderer.tsx` ŌĆö verbatim lift of the 383-LOC source plus an extended header documenting the single import-path adapt, the cross-fork-precedent rationale, the zero-IPC contract, and the inline-content (non-modal) composition shape.
- `src/webFull/components/CsvTableRenderer.parity.test.ts` ŌĆö ~265 LOC, 10 stories + 6 catalog-shape guards.

#### Files modified (additive only)

- `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended `export { CsvTableRenderer } from './CsvTableRenderer';` + `export type { CsvTableRendererProps } from './CsvTableRenderer';` after the existing `SessionItem` export under the Layer 2.5 leaf-parade section. Barrel collisions resolved mechanically by the `merge=union` driver landed in commit `e3540a054`.
- `ISA.md` ŌĆö this Decisions entry; added `ISC-44.layer-2.5.csv_table_renderer` directly as `[x]` CLOSED at the per-feature ISC list (line immediately precedes `ISC-44.layer-2.5.session_item`, ordered by lift sequence).

#### Files NOT touched

- `src/renderer/` ŌĆö read-only oracle. The renderer continues to import `CsvTableRenderer` from `'./CsvTableRenderer'` inside `src/renderer/components/FilePreview.tsx` and render the renderer module as part of the Electron entrypoint.
- `src/web/` ŌĆö fork-hygiene read-only.
- `src/main/` ŌĆö no new IPC handlers; the renderer is purely visual and prop-callback-driven.

### Decisions 2026-06-08 ŌĆö Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö paired `AutoRunExpandedModal` + `AutoRunDocumentSelector` lift

**Closes:** ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_expanded_modal + ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_document_selector.

Branch `leaf-autorun-expanded-and-doc-selector` cut off `main @ 1107a0906` (the prettier `chore(format): prettier ISA.md from merge chain #14` commit that followed the prior leaf-parade merge wave). Lifts TWO presentational Auto-Run siblings into `src/webFull/` in one branch per the parent brief's "two siblings in one branch" pre-flight directive (both files passed the `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|ipcRenderer" <path>` pre-flight with empty output, exit 1):

1. **`AutoRunExpandedModal`** (~470 LOC) ŌĆö the full-screen Auto-Run editor shell that wraps the renderer-side `AutoRun` view (Edit/Preview toggle, Save/Revert when dirty, Run/Stop, PlayBooks, Collapse/Close, unsaved-changes ConfirmModal).
2. **`AutoRunDocumentSelector`** (~570 LOC) ŌĆö the document-picker dropdown that renders the `.md` file list (flat OR tree), per-doc task-completion percentages, the optional Bionify toggle, and the inline Create-New-Document modal.

The two are paired in one branch because `AutoRunExpandedModal` consumes the type-only export `DocumentTaskCount` from `AutoRunDocumentSelector` ŌĆö shipping them in the same branch lets the modal's type-only import resolve to a local sibling (`'./AutoRunDocumentSelector'`) rather than reaching cross-fork.

#### Cross-fork edges (acknowledged, scoped)

**For `AutoRunExpandedModal`:**

1. **`AutoRun, type AutoRunHandle` from `'../../renderer/components/AutoRun'`.** The `AutoRun` view is the full Auto-Run editor surface (~2200+ LOC, heavy runtime IPC: `window.maestro.autoRun.*`, `window.maestro.fs.*`, `window.maestro.dialog.*`, `window.maestro.shell.openPath`, `window.maestro.images.*`). Lifting it would drag the entire Auto-Run IPC namespace through webFull, which is out of scope for this leaf and blocked on the server-side `/api/autorun/*` + `/api/fs/*` + `/api/images/*` REST routes that have not been ported. Matches the established cross-fork-edge precedent set by `GitDiffViewer`'s edge to `'../../renderer/components/ImageDiffViewer'` before the corresponding routes landed. A future `ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_view` lift drops the edge when the server routes land. The modal SHELL itself touches 0 IPC namespaces at module load ŌĆö the cross-fork edge defers any IPC reach to runtime within the subcomponent, NOT at this shell's module load. The shell's parity contract is observable-behavior-only on the shell surface (header title, mode toggle, Run/Stop, PlayBooks, Collapse/Close); the `AutoRun` view's parity contract belongs to a future ISC, NOT this one.

2. **`BatchRunState`, `SessionState`, `Shortcut` from `'../../renderer/types'`.** Cross-fork transitive type-only import. These types are aggregator-defined in the renderer types module and not yet promoted into `src/shared/`; matches the `GroupChatHeader` / `SessionItem` / `ExecutionQueueIndicator` precedent of importing renderer-aggregator types directly. No transitive `window.maestro` reach ŌĆö all three are pure data shapes. A future fork-hygiene sweep promoting batch + session + shortcut types into `src/shared/` would neutralize the edge; out of scope here.

**For `AutoRunDocumentSelector`:**

3. **`useClickOutside` from `'../../renderer/hooks'`.** Cross-fork edge ŌĆö pure renderer hook with `useEffect` + `addEventListener` only; no `window.maestro`, no Electron-only API, no `from 'electron'` reach. Matches the established cross-fork-edge precedent set by `SessionActivityGraph` for `useContextMenuPosition` and `AutoRunLightbox` for `safeClipboardWriteImage`. Pulling the hook directly from the renderer barrel rather than duplicating it into webFull avoids the silent-drift surface (Architect 2026-06-08 audit risk A). A future fork-hygiene sweep can promote it to `src/webFull/hooks/` (or `src/shared/hooks/`) when other webFull consumers materialize.

The rest of the import sets (`react` for hooks, `lucide-react` for icons, `react-dom` for `createPortal`) resolves identically in both trees ŌĆö already webFull-tree deps used by every L2.5 sibling.

#### Composition shapes

**`AutoRunExpandedModal`** is portal-mounted into `document.body` as a `fixed inset-0 z-[100]` overlay with a centered card sized at `90vw x 80vh` matching the PromptComposer modal. Header carries the title, the Edit/Preview/Save/Revert/Run/PlayBooks button row, and the Collapse/Close affordances. Body renders the `AutoRun` view with `hideTopControls` set so the header isn't duplicated. Registers with the LayerStack at `MODAL_PRIORITIES.AUTORUN_EXPANDED` (705) with `focusTrap: 'strict'`. Polls dirty state from the `AutoRun` ref every 100ms; unsaved-changes `ConfirmModal` composes on top when the user attempts to close while `isDirty=true`.

**`AutoRunDocumentSelector`** is a flex row containing a relative-positioned dropdown (button + absolute menu) + optional Bionify toggle + Create / Refresh / Change-Folder action buttons. The create-document modal is a separate `fixed inset-0 z-[10000]` sibling overlay rendered verbatim from the renderer source (NOT registered with the LayerStack ŌĆö kept verbatim since the renderer doesn't register it either; the modal owns its own Escape handling via its `onKeyDown` handler). Tree-mode renders folders + files recursively with expand/collapse state; flat-mode falls back to a vertical list. Per-document task-completion percentage pills surface both in the collapsed-button summary (for the selected doc) and inline in the dropdown rows (for every doc with a counts entry).

#### Parity catalogs

**`AutoRunExpandedModal.parity.test.ts`** ŌĆö 11 stories total (5 happy + 6 negative ŌĆö meets the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards):

- **Happy paths (5):** header title + Collapse/Close affordances; Edit/Preview toggle with canonical title copy; Run button surfacing when not-locked and agent-idle; Stop button surfacing when locked and not-stopping; PlayBooks button surfacing when `onOpenMarketplace` provided.
- **Negative paths (6):** Run suppression when locked; Stopping-state title swap when `isStopping=true`; PlayBooks suppression when `onOpenMarketplace` omitted; Edit-button locked-state title swap + `disabled` attribute; unsaved-confirm suppression on initial mount; no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle pin for the SHELL.

**`AutoRunDocumentSelector.parity.test.ts`** ŌĆö 12 stories total (6 happy + 6 negative ŌĆö meets the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards):

- **Happy paths (6):** collapsed-button rendering the selected document name with `.md` suffix; placeholder copy when `selectedDocument=null`; create-document modal mounting via the `+` button with canonical `role="dialog"[aria-label="Create New Document"]` + `placeholder="my-tasks"` input; Bionify toggle surfacing with `aria-pressed="false"` when document + callback both provided; task-percentage pill (30%) surfacing on the collapsed button; Refresh button `disabled` attribute when `isLoading=true`.
- **Negative paths (6):** Bionify suppression when no document selected; Bionify suppression when `onToggleBionify` omitted; create-modal suppression on initial mount; task-percentage suppression when `documentTaskCounts` undefined; task-percentage suppression when counts entry's `total=0`; no-IPC / no-WS lifecycle pin.

Both catalogs use only the allowed assertion vocabulary (`hasElement`, `hasText`, `wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`) and pass the IPC-leakage guard, the negative-per-happy floor guard, the no-empty-given/when/then guard, the unique-story-name guard, and the allowed-verbs-only guard.

#### Files added (NEW, additive only)

- `src/webFull/components/AutoRunExpandedModal.tsx` (verbatim lift of the ~470-LOC source plus an extended header documenting the eight import-path adapts and the cross-fork-edge to the renderer-side `AutoRun` view).
- `src/webFull/components/AutoRunExpandedModal.parity.test.ts` (11 stories: 5 happy + 6 negative; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards).
- `src/webFull/components/AutoRunDocumentSelector.tsx` (verbatim lift of the ~570-LOC source plus an extended header documenting the two import-path adapts and the cross-fork-edge to `useClickOutside`).
- `src/webFull/components/AutoRunDocumentSelector.parity.test.ts` (12 stories: 6 happy + 6 negative; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards).

#### Files modified (additive only)

- `ISA.md` ŌĆö this Decisions entry; added `ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_expanded_modal` and `ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_document_selector` directly as `[x]` CLOSED at the per-feature ISC list (lines immediately follow `ISC-44.layer-2.5.session_item`, ordered by lift sequence).

- `src/renderer/` ŌĆö read-only oracle (zero bytes diff vs main). The renderer continues to import `AutoRunExpandedModal` and `AutoRunDocumentSelector` from `'../components/AutoRunExpandedModal'` / `'../components/AutoRunDocumentSelector'` and render the renderer modules as part of the Electron entrypoint.
- `src/web/` ŌĆö fork-hygiene read-only (zero bytes diff vs main).
- `src/main/` ŌĆö no new IPC handlers; the modal SHELL is purely visual and prop-callback-driven, and the document-selector touches 0 IPC namespaces at module load or runtime (`onCreateDocument` is a parent-owned callback that may eventually reach the server via WS frames, but that wiring belongs to the parent that mounts the selector, not this leaf).

### Decisions 2026-06-08 ŌĆö Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö three sibling progress modals lifted in one branch (`MergeProgressModal` + `SummarizeProgressModal` + `TransferProgressModal`)

**Closes:** ISC-44.layer-2.5.merge_progress_modal, ISC-44.layer-2.5.summarize_progress_modal, ISC-44.layer-2.5.transfer_progress_modal.

Branch `leaf-progress-modals` cut off `main @ 1107a0906` (head as of fetch, the prettier `chore(format): prettier ISA.md from merge chain #14` commit that followed the prior leaf-parade merge wave). Lifts three modal-shape sibling progress surfaces into `src/webFull/` in one batch: `MergeProgressModal` (455 LOC), `SummarizeProgressModal` (489 LOC), and `TransferProgressModal` (541 LOC). All three are direct modal-shape siblings of progress surfaces already lifted in the L2.5 wave ŌĆö the merge modal is the blocking-dialog counterpart to the already-lifted inline `MergeProgressOverlay`, the summarize modal is the blocking-dialog counterpart to the already-lifted inline `SummarizeProgressOverlay`, and the transfer modal is the cross-agent variant that ships without an inline-overlay sibling (the transfer flow uses a blocking modal by design ŌĆö `TRANSFER_PROGRESS` priority 683 sits in the modal layer-stack ladder rather than the per-tab inline ladder).

**Three-in-one batching rationale:** the three modals share the same shape pattern (`fixed inset-0 modal-overlay flex items-center justify-center z-[9999]`, 450px-wide centered surface, `role="dialog"` + `aria-modal="true"` + `aria-label="<X> Progress"`, header with conditional close-X button, centered Wand2 spinner / Check head, status message line with elapsed-time chip, percentage progress bar, four-stage vertical indicator list, cancel-confirmation sub-overlay gated by `showCancelConfirm`, footer Cancel/Done button) ŌĆö they differ only in (a) the stage vocabulary, (b) the cancel-confirmation copy, (c) the optional sub-surfaces (token-reduction stats on summarize, agent-transfer indicator on transfer), and (d) the layer-stack priority constant (`MERGE_PROGRESS` 684 / `TRANSFER_PROGRESS` 683 / `SUMMARIZE_PROGRESS` 681). Batching three near-identical lifts in one branch lets the parade close three ISCs with one verification sweep and one set of node_modules-symlink + lint + test + commit overhead rather than three.

**Pre-flight grep on all three renderer sources** (`grep -nE "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|ipcRenderer" src/renderer/components/<X>.tsx` for X Ōłł {MergeProgressModal, SummarizeProgressModal, TransferProgressModal}): empty (exit 1) on all three. None of the three modals touch IPC at module load or runtime; all side effects flow through `onCancel` (and `onComplete` where the prop is declared). The L2.5 verbatim-lift posture applies cleanly to all three.

The lift carries the following import-path adapts, identical across the merge + summarize modals and extended by one extra route on the transfer modal:

1. **`Theme`** from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'` (all three modals). Matches the L2.5 / L2.1 sibling precedent; renderer aggregator is a pure re-export of the shared canonical.

2. **`GroomingProgress` (Merge + Transfer) / `SummarizeProgress` + `SummarizeResult` (Summarize)** from `'../types/contextMerge'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/types/contextMerge'`. The renderer-side aggregator is dependency-clean for the discriminated unions + primitives these modals consume. Pulling via cross-fork import matches the established sibling-overlay precedent (`MergeProgressOverlay` / `SummarizeProgressOverlay` both import from the same cross-fork path).

3. **`useLayerStack`** from `'../contexts/LayerStackContext'` ŌåÆ `'../contexts/LayerStackContext'` (resolves to the L2.1 webFull re-export at `src/webFull/contexts/LayerStackContext.tsx`). All three modals consume the same `registerLayer` / `unregisterLayer` / `updateLayerHandler` surface; the webFull hook is a pure re-export of the renderer's `useLayerStack` and the API surface is identical.

4. **`MODAL_PRIORITIES`** from `'../constants/modalPriorities'` ŌåÆ `'../constants/modalPriorities'` (resolves to the webFull re-export at `src/webFull/constants/modalPriorities.ts`, per Architect 2026-06-08 audit risk A ŌĆö non-divergent constants stay re-exported from the renderer to prevent silent drift). The three modals each consume one priority constant: `MERGE_PROGRESS` (684), `SUMMARIZE_PROGRESS` (681), `TRANSFER_PROGRESS` (683).

5. **`ToolType`** (Transfer-only) from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/types'`. `ToolType` is declared in `src/shared/types.ts` as `type ToolType = AgentId` (a pure type-only re-export); the renderer barrel re-exports it without transform. Pulling directly from `src/shared/` matches the `AgentErrorModal` precedent.

6. **`getAgentDisplayName`** (Transfer-only) from `'../services/contextGroomer'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/agentMetadata'`. This is the one adapt worth naming explicitly. The renderer-side `contextGroomer.ts` is IPC-bearing: `grep -nE "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron" src/renderer/services/contextGroomer.ts` returns `281: const groomedText = await window.maestro.context.groomContext(` and `397: await window.maestro.context.cleanupGroomingSession(sessionId);`. Routing webFull through it would drag a `window.maestro` module-load surface into the lifted modal. The renderer's `getAgentDisplayName` is itself a re-export of the pure helper that lives in `src/shared/agentMetadata.ts` (line 16: `import { getAgentDisplayName as getDisplayName } from '../../shared/agentMetadata';`). Pulling the helper directly from `src/shared/` matches the `AgentErrorModal` / `AgentPromptComposerModal` precedent of routing pure shared helpers around the renderer barrel and avoids the IPC surface entirely.

The rest of the import set (`react` hooks for `useState` / `useEffect` / `useRef` / `useMemo` / `useCallback` / `memo`, `lucide-react` icons for `X` / `Check` / `Loader2` / `AlertTriangle` / `Wand2` plus modal-specific glyphs `GitMerge`-not-used / `TrendingDown` / `ArrowRight`) resolves identically in both trees ŌĆö already webFull-tree deps used by every L2.5 sibling.

**Composition shape (verbatim across all three):** full-viewport blocking modal with layer-stack focus trap. NOT a composition of the L2.1 `Modal` primitive ŌĆö the renderer sources pre-date the L2.1 shared-modal extraction and render their own 450px-wide centered dialog with custom header/footer chrome and an absolute-positioned cancel-confirmation sub-overlay. Lifts are verbatim to preserve behavioral parity (Escape ŌåÆ cancel-confirmation when in flight, Escape ŌåÆ close/complete when done, layer-stack registration with `focusTrap: 'strict'`, priority constant per modal). The summarize modal is the one that ships an additional sub-surface (`TokenReductionStats` card on `result.success === true`, error message card on `result.success === false`); the transfer modal ships an `AgentTransferIndicator` pill row above the spinner. Both extra sub-surfaces are local nested functions inside their respective lifted modules ŌĆö not separate components ŌĆö and are preserved verbatim.

**Theme access pattern:** kept the renderer's `theme: Theme` prop convention, consistent with every L2.x lift. Callers in webFull call `const { theme } = useTheme()` at the feature-component level and thread it down.

**Parity catalogs (one per modal, all at `src/webFull/components/<X>.parity.test.ts`):**

- **`MergeProgressModal.parity.test.ts`** ŌĆö 10 stories: 5 happy + 5 negative. Happy: active-state title interpolating `sourceName`/`targetName`; completion title + stage labels; cancel-confirmation surface after click; fallback title without `sourceName`; Done-button + close-X on completion. Negative: close-X suppression while in flight; elapsed-time chip suppression on completion; confirmation suppression before click; component-not-mounted when `isOpen=false`; no-IPC lifecycle pin (asserts `[role="dialog"]` chrome stays the only side-effect surface). 6 catalog-shape vitest guards (happy floor, negative-per-happy floor, allowed-verbs, non-empty given/when/then, IPC-leak guard, modal-shape contract guard asserting `role="dialog"` + `aria-modal="true"`).

- **`SummarizeProgressModal.parity.test.ts`** ŌĆö 10 stories: 5 happy + 5 negative. Happy: active-state title; completion title + stage labels (`extracting` ŌåÆ `summarizing` ŌåÆ `creating` ŌåÆ `complete`); TokenReductionStats surface on success with "Context Reduced by N%" + Before/After columns; cancel-confirmation surface ("Cancel Compaction?" / "No" / "Yes"); Done-button + close-X on completion. Negative: error message surface on `result.success === false`; close-X suppression while in flight; TokenReductionStats suppression on failure; component-not-mounted when `isOpen=false`; no-IPC lifecycle pin. 6 catalog-shape vitest guards.

- **`TransferProgressModal.parity.test.ts`** ŌĆö 10 stories: 5 happy + 5 negative. Happy: active-state title with cross-agent indicator; completion title + transfer stage labels (`Extract context` ŌåÆ `Groom for target` ŌåÆ `Create session` ŌåÆ `Complete`); cancel-confirmation surface ("Cancel Transfer?" / "Continue Transfer" / "Cancel Transfer"); Done-button + close-X on completion; `aria-modal="true"` chrome pin. Negative: close-X suppression while in flight; elapsed-time chip suppression on completion; confirmation suppression before click; component-not-mounted when `isOpen=false`; no-IPC lifecycle pin. 6 catalog-shape vitest guards.

Each catalog uses only the allowed assertion vocabulary (`hasElement`, `hasText`, `wsFrameMatches`, `dbHasRow`, `fsHas`, `processHas`, `notificationFired`, `broadcast`) and stays render-shape-oriented for `then` verbs since the three modals are presentational. Click ŌåÆ cancel-confirmation surface and Escape ŌåÆ cancel-confirmation surface are asserted observationally (the surface appears after the `when` step) rather than by callback firing ŌĆö the callback wiring is the caller's responsibility and the test fixture binds them.

#### Files added (NEW, additive only ŌĆö six files)

- `src/webFull/components/MergeProgressModal.tsx` (body lifted verbatim from the 455-LOC renderer source; extended header documents the four import-path adapts + the cross-fork-precedent rationale).
- `src/webFull/components/MergeProgressModal.parity.test.ts` (10 stories: 5 happy + 5 negative; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards).
- `src/webFull/components/SummarizeProgressModal.tsx` (body lifted verbatim from the 489-LOC renderer source; extended header documents the four import-path adapts).
- `src/webFull/components/SummarizeProgressModal.parity.test.ts` (10 stories: 5 happy + 5 negative; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards).
- `src/webFull/components/TransferProgressModal.tsx` (body lifted verbatim from the 541-LOC renderer source; extended header documents the six import-path adapts + the IPC-avoidance rationale for routing `getAgentDisplayName` through `src/shared/agentMetadata` rather than `../services/contextGroomer`).
- `src/webFull/components/TransferProgressModal.parity.test.ts` (10 stories: 5 happy + 5 negative; 6 catalog-shape vitest guards).

#### Files modified (additive only)

- `src/webFull/components/index.ts` ŌĆö appended `export { MergeProgressModal } from './MergeProgressModal';` + `export type { MergeProgressModalProps } from './MergeProgressModal';` + `export { SummarizeProgressModal } from './SummarizeProgressModal';` + `export type { SummarizeProgressModalProps } from './SummarizeProgressModal';` + `export { TransferProgressModal } from './TransferProgressModal';` + `export type { TransferProgressModalProps } from './TransferProgressModal';` after the existing `SessionItem` export under the Layer 2.5 leaf-parade section. Barrel collisions resolved mechanically by the `merge=union` driver landed in commit `e3540a054`.
- `ISA.md` ŌĆö this Decisions entry; added three `[x]` CLOSED ISC lines (`ISC-44.layer-2.5.merge_progress_modal`, `ISC-44.layer-2.5.summarize_progress_modal`, `ISC-44.layer-2.5.transfer_progress_modal`) at the per-feature ISC list, placed immediately before `ISC-44.layer-2.5.session_item` so the lift dependency reads top-down (the three modals follow the already-merged `MergeProgressOverlay` + `SummarizeProgressOverlay` overlay pair).

#### Files NOT touched

- `src/renderer/` ŌĆö read-only oracle. The renderer continues to import all three modals from their original paths inside the renderer tree.
- `src/web/` ŌĆö fork-hygiene read-only.
- `src/main/` ŌĆö no new IPC handlers; the progress surfaces are purely visual and prop-callback-driven.
- `src/server/` ŌĆö untouched.

#### Verification

- **Pre-flight on the renderer source:** `grep -nE "window\.maestro\.|window\.electron|ipcRenderer|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|window\.api|require\(" src/renderer/components/CsvTableRenderer.tsx` ŌåÆ empty (exit 1).

- **Parity test:** `MAESTRO_HEADLESS=1 npx vitest run src/webFull/components/CsvTableRenderer.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ PASS, 6/6 in-suite vitest guards green, 509ms total.

#### Deferred / out-of-scope for this brief

- **Wiring `CsvTableRenderer` into the webFull `FilePreview`.** The webFull file-preview surface (the consumer that would feed `content` / `searchQuery` / `onMatchCount` into the renderer) has not been ported. The renderer's `FilePreview` continues to consume the renderer module via `'./CsvTableRenderer'`. A future file-preview port would swap in `'../../webFull/components/CsvTableRenderer'` (or, more likely, route through a higher-level FilePreview lift).
- **Sticky-header observation in the catalog.** The header cells use `position: sticky; top: 0` so they pin to the table's scroll-container ŌĆö this is layout-level CSS not asserted by the catalog (catalog principle: observable behavior, not DOM structure or CSS). A future visual-regression sweep would catch sticky-header drift.
- **Truncation-banner assertions.** The catalog covers the `MAX_DISPLAY_ROWS = 500` cap conceptually (any input over 500 filtered rows surfaces the banner) but does not ship a positive story exercising the cap ŌĆö generating a 501-row fixture inline would balloon the test file without adding observable-behavior coverage the existing stories don't cover. Tracked downstream as a follow-on if the truncation copy ever changes.

### 2026-06-08 ŌĆö Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö paired `MermaidRenderer` lift + `GroupChatModal` STOP

**Closes:** ISC-44.layer-2.5.mermaid_renderer. **STOP-recorded:** ISC-44.layer-2.5.groupchat_modal (stays `[ ]` open until the IPC-adapter precondition lands).

Branch `leaf-mermaid-and-groupchat-modal` cut off `main @ 1107a0906` (head as of fetch, the prettier `chore(format): prettier ISA.md from merge chain #14` commit that followed the prior leaf-parade merge wave). Brief instructed: lift TWO components in one branch ŌĆö `MermaidRenderer` (~475 LOC) and `GroupChatModal` (~498 LOC) ŌĆö and pre-flight each, stopping any that carries module-load IPC. Per the gate, only one of the two landed; the other STOPPED on a hard IPC-module-load finding and is documented inline here so the next agent picking up `GroupChatModal` knows exactly what preconditions to land first.

**Pre-flight: `MermaidRenderer` ŌĆö PASSED.**

```
$ grep -nE "window\.maestro\.|window\.electron|ipcRenderer|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath" \
    src/renderer/components/MermaidRenderer.tsx
(empty ŌĆö exit 1)
```

Zero IPC namespaces. Zero Electron-only APIs at module-load OR runtime. Module imports are pure: `react` hooks, `mermaid` (browser-safe with `securityLevel: 'strict'` + `suppressErrorRendering: true`), `dompurify` (browser-safe SVG sanitiser), and `Theme` from the renderer types aggregator. Both library imports are already webFull-tree deps used by sibling L2.5 lifts (e.g. `MarkdownRenderer` consumes both). One import-path swap was needed (`Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'`) ŌĆö the standard L2.5 swap that every leaf-parade lift carries. Verbatim lift with a single-import-path-adjust posture; the ~260-LOC theme-variable block was preserved byte-for-byte so every diagram surface mermaid exposes (flowchart, sequence, gantt, pie, git-graph, mindmap, quadrant, xy-chart, timeline, sankey, state, ER, requirement, class) tints with the app palette without drift from the renderer source.

**Pre-flight: `GroupChatModal` ŌĆö STOPPED.**

```
$ grep -n "useAgentConfiguration" src/renderer/components/GroupChatModal.tsx
23:import { useAgentConfiguration } from '../hooks/agent';
63:	const ac = useAgentConfiguration({
$ grep -c "window.maestro" src/renderer/hooks/agent/useAgentConfiguration.ts
7
```

Module-load IPC ŌĆö the gate fired. The modal imports `useAgentConfiguration` at the top of the file (line 23), and the hook itself contains seven `window.maestro.*` calls (agent detection, ssh-remote enumeration, agent-config get / set, etc.). The hook is invoked unconditionally at component-top inside the function body (line 63), so the IPC surface lands at first render regardless of whether the modal is open. Per the brief ŌĆö "Pre-flight each; STOP that one if module-load IPC" ŌĆö this is the exact case the gate is meant to catch.

Beyond the IPC-bound hook, the modal would also need the following cross-fork preconditions resolved before a lift could even compile against the webFull tree (documented here so the next agent doesn't have to re-discover them):

1. **`AGENT_TILES`** ŌĆö currently imported from `'./Wizard/screens/AgentSelectionScreen'`. The webFull tree has no `Wizard/` directory at all. Either lift `AGENT_TILES` into a sibling webFull module (and its transitive `WizardScreenProps` / `AgentTile` type surface), or accept a cross-fork edge to `'../../renderer/components/Wizard/screens/AgentSelectionScreen'` (which carries other onboarding screens behind it ŌĆö risk of pulling more surface than intended).
2. **`AgentConfigPanel`** ŌĆö `src/renderer/components/shared/AgentConfigPanel.tsx`, no webFull lift exists. Would need a separate leaf lift (likely IPC-bound too, since agent-config rendering surfaces env-vars / paths / args that the renderer wires through `window.maestro.settings`).
3. **`SshRemoteSelector`** ŌĆö `src/renderer/components/shared/SshRemoteSelector.tsx`, no webFull lift exists. Would need a separate leaf lift (likely WS-backed in webFull since ssh-remote enumeration is an Electron-only surface today).
4. **Cross-fork type imports** ŌĆö `AgentConfig`, `ModeratorConfig`, `GroupChat` from `'../types'`. `GroupChat` lives in the renderer aggregator and is not yet in `src/shared/`. A webFull lift would need either a cross-fork transitive type import (`'../../renderer/types'`) or a fork-hygiene sweep that promotes the three types into `src/shared/group-chat-types.ts` (sibling of the existing shared group-chat-types module).
5. **`useAgentConfiguration` webFull adapter** ŌĆö the central blocker. A webFull-side adapter would need server routes `/api/agents/detect` (replace `window.maestro.agents.detectAgents`), `/api/ssh-remotes/list` (replace `window.maestro.sshRemotes.list`), `/api/agents/config/{get,set}` (replace `window.maestro.settings.{get,set}` for agent-config keys), and a parallel WebSocket frame topology if any of those need push semantics. None of those routes exist today.

Until those land, attempting a webFull lift of `GroupChatModal` would either:

- Drag the full `window.maestro` agent + ssh + settings IPC surface into the webFull tree (the explicit anti-goal of the leaf-parade ŌĆö webFull is meant to be browser-host-safe), or
- Silently emit broken-at-runtime code by stubbing out the hook return shape, which would fail the parity catalog the moment any state assertion ran (no detected agents, no ssh remotes, no config persistence).

Neither outcome is acceptable. The STOP is the correct decision here; the brief's "STOP that one if module-load IPC" gate did its job. The ISC stays `[ ]` open with the precondition list documented inline so the next agent picking this up can land the preconditions first and then re-attempt the lift cleanly.

**Files touched on this branch (additive only):**

- `src/webFull/components/MermaidRenderer.tsx` (NEW ŌĆö verbatim 475-LOC lift + extended header)
- `src/webFull/components/MermaidRenderer.parity.test.ts` (NEW ŌĆö 8 stories + 7 catalog-shape guards)
- `src/webFull/components/index.ts` (additive ŌĆö one new line `export { MermaidRenderer } from './MermaidRenderer';` after the existing `SessionItem` type export under the Layer 2.5 leaf-parade section)
- `ISA.md` (this Decisions block + two new ISC lines: `ISC-44.layer-2.5.mermaid_renderer` `[x]` CLOSED and `ISC-44.layer-2.5.groupchat_modal` `[ ]` STOPPED, both placed immediately after `ISC-44.layer-2.5.image_diff_viewer` under the per-feature ISC list, ordered by lift sequence)

**Files NOT touched (verified):**

```
$ git diff main..HEAD -- src/web/ src/renderer/ src/main/ src/server/ | wc -c
0
```

**Verification:**

- `npm run build:prompts` ŌĆö materialised the pre-existing `src/generated/prompts.ts` (21 prompts) so the lint pass would not trip on a missing generated file.
- `MAESTRO_HEADLESS=1 npm run lint` ŌĆö clean (`tsc -p tsconfig.lint.json && tsc -p tsconfig.main.json --noEmit && tsc -p tsconfig.cli.json --noEmit`).
- `MAESTRO_HEADLESS=1 npx vitest run src/webFull/components/MermaidRenderer.parity.test.ts` ŌĆö 7/7 passing (catalog-shape guards: Ōēź3 happy floor, Ōēź1 negative-per-happy floor, allowed-verbs-only, non-empty given/when/then, IPC-leak guard, no-modal-shape pin, unique-story-name guard).

**Branch hygiene:** `node_modules` was symlinked from the main checkout (`ln -s /Users/trilliumsmith/code/maestro/node_modules node_modules`) so the lint + vitest passes could resolve TypeScript types and React + mermaid + dompurify modules. The symlink will be removed before the final commit so the worktree stays clean ŌĆö `git status` will report only the additive files above.

**Deferred / out-of-scope (explicit):**

- **Lifting `GroupChatModal`.** STOPPED at pre-flight per the brief. The precondition list above is the next-agent-handoff surface. Right move: land the five preconditions (or the IPC-adapter-only subset for a minimal lift) on separate leaf branches before re-attempting.
- **Lifting `useAgentConfiguration` into webFull.** Requires the three server-side routes enumerated above (`/api/agents/detect`, `/api/ssh-remotes/list`, `/api/agents/config/{get,set}`) plus a webFull HTTP / WebSocket adapter that implements the same hook return shape. Separate ISC-44.x sub-ticket; not opened here because the parent decision (whether webFull gets a full agent-detection surface at all) is a product decision the leaf-parade is not the right venue for.
- **Wiring `MermaidRenderer` into the webFull `MarkdownRenderer` chain.** The webFull `MarkdownRenderer` (lifted earlier in the leaf-parade) currently renders fenced `mermaid` code blocks as plain `<pre><code>` ŌĆö a downstream wire-up would swap that to `<MermaidRenderer>` for `language === 'mermaid'`. Out of scope here; this lift makes the wire-up possible by surfacing the component in the webFull tree, but does not perform the wire-up itself.
- **Multi-instance / multi-theme mermaid singleton.** The module-scoped `lastThemeId` cache is preserved verbatim from the renderer source ŌĆö same single-instance-per-document constraint applies. If a future webFull surface needs to render two mermaid charts with different themes side-by-side, the singleton needs to grow per-instance theme tracking; that's a behaviour expansion, not a parity bug.

### Decisions 2026-06-08 ŌĆö Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö paired `FileSearchModal` + `AICommandsPanel` lift

**Closes:** ISC-44.layer-2.5.file_search_modal + ISC-44.layer-2.5.ai_commands_panel.

Branch `leaf-file-search-and-ai-commands` cut off `main @ 1107a0906` (`chore(format): prettier ISA.md from merge chain #14`). Pairs two L2.5 leaf-parade sibling lifts under one branch so they share a single atomic commit: `FileSearchModal` (modal-shape fuzzy file picker, 551 LOC) and `AICommandsPanel` (inline settings-pane editor for custom slash-commands, 552 LOC). Both have 0 module-load IPC and 0 Electron-only module-load APIs per pre-flight `grep -E "window\.maestro\.|from ['\"]electron['\"]|shell\.openExternal|shell\.openPath|ipcRenderer"` returning empty exit 1 on each source file; both close per-feature ISCs under the `ISC-44.<tab>.<deferral>` tracking convention; both have ample prior-art in the wave to establish every adapt decision needed.

This brief intentionally batches two leaves into one commit because (a) the brief explicitly requested it ("Lift TWO components into `src/webFull/` in one branch"), (b) the adapt patterns overlap substantially (`Theme` cross-fork swap, `Shortcut` / `CustomAICommand` renderer-types import, pure renderer-utility imports for `fuzzyMatchWithScore` / `TEMPLATE_VARIABLES_GENERAL`, pure renderer-hook import for `useTemplateAutocomplete`), and (c) it keeps the lift queue moving without proliferating tiny review surfaces. The per-feature ISC tracking is preserved ŌĆö each component documents its own header inline and closes its own ISC line.

#### `FileSearchModal` adapt decisions

Seven import-path adapts (six unique paths plus the `'./TemplateAutocompleteDropdown'` no-op listed for completeness in the inline header):

1. **`Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'`** (standard L2.5 swap; renderer aggregator `src/renderer/types/index.ts` re-exports the canonical type from `src/shared/theme-types`).
2. **`Shortcut` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/types'`** (cross-fork transitive type-only import; matches `GroupChatInput` / `KeyboardMasteryCelebration` precedent ŌĆö `Shortcut` is not yet replicated to `src/shared/`).
3. **`FileNode` from `'../types/fileTree'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/types/fileTree'`** (same cross-fork shape; `fileTree.ts` is six lines of pure interface declarations with zero transitive IPC).
4. **`fuzzyMatchWithScore` from `'../utils/search'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/utils/search'`** (pure renderer helper ŌĆö string-only fuzzy scorer; zero `window.maestro` / Electron / IPC references; pulled direct from renderer per the `useTemplateAutocomplete` / `getStatusColor` precedent).
5. **`useLayerStack` from `'../contexts/LayerStackContext'` ŌåÆ `'../contexts/LayerStackContext'`** (webFull already carries its own L2.1 lift of `LayerStackContext`).
6. **`MODAL_PRIORITIES` from `'../constants/modalPriorities'` ŌåÆ `'../constants/modalPriorities'`** (webFull re-export per Architect 2026-06-08 audit risk A ŌĆö modal priorities don't diverge).
7. **`formatShortcutKeys` from `'../utils/shortcutFormatter'` ŌåÆ `'../utils/shortcutFormatter'`** (webFull already carries a pure-mirror `shortcutFormatter.ts` per `KeyboardMasteryCelebration` precedent).

Composition shape: modal-shape registered at `MODAL_PRIORITIES.FUZZY_FILE_SEARCH` with `focusTrap: 'strict'`, `blocksLowerLayers: true`, `capturesFocus: true`. Dialog root carries `role="dialog"` + `aria-modal="true"` + `aria-label="Fuzzy File Search"`. The search input auto-focuses on mount via `setTimeout(50)`. Tab key toggles between "Visible Files" (only files in expanded folders) and "All Files" (entire tree) modes. Cmd+1..9/0 hotkeys quick-select the visible row badge `[1..9, 0]`. Arrow keys navigate; Enter selects. `flattenPreviewableFiles` is exported alongside the component for any downstream consumer that wants to count previewable files outside the modal mount.

#### `AICommandsPanel` adapt decisions

Four import-path adapts:

1. **`Theme` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'`** (standard L2.5 swap).
2. **`CustomAICommand` from `'../types'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/types'`** (cross-fork transitive type-only import; matches `GroupChatInput` / `SessionItem` precedent ŌĆö `CustomAICommand` is not yet replicated to `src/shared/`).
3. **`TEMPLATE_VARIABLES_GENERAL` from `'../utils/templateVariables'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/utils/templateVariables'`** (pure renderer constant ŌĆö array of `{ variable, description }` rows; zero IPC).
4. **`useTemplateAutocomplete` from `'../hooks'` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/hooks/input/useTemplateAutocomplete'`** (pure renderer hook ŌĆö `useState` + `useEffect` + `useRef` + DOM bounding-rect math only; zero IPC; pulled direct from renderer source path rather than the renderer barrel to keep the import surface narrow).

`TemplateAutocompleteDropdown` from `'./TemplateAutocompleteDropdown'` resolves cleanly inside the webFull tree (the L2.5 sibling lift already carries the webFull version).

Composition shape: NOT a modal ŌĆö inline settings-pane editor. The disclosure for each command is a `<button>` that toggles a `Set<string>` of expanded IDs. The Template Variables reference accordion is a separate collapsible. `TemplateAutocompleteDropdown` is rendered twice (new-command form + inline edit mode), both gated on the `autocompleteState.isOpen` state owned by `useTemplateAutocomplete`. Built-in commands surface a `Lock` badge and suppress the `title="Delete command"` affordance per the `!cmd.isBuiltIn` gate (preserved verbatim).

#### Deferred / out-of-scope for this brief

- **Wiring the three lifted modals into webFull feature surfaces.** The lifts ship the modal components themselves; the webFull-side glue that decides when to mount each modal (the equivalents of the renderer's `useContextMerge` / `useSummarize` / `useCrossAgentTransfer` orchestrator hooks) lives in feature wiring layers that this brief does not touch. A downstream lift would port those orchestrator hooks and wire `<MergeProgressModal>` / `<SummarizeProgressModal>` / `<TransferProgressModal>` into the webFull surface; that wiring would be tracked under an L3.x / L4.x ISC.
- **Promoting `useLayerStack` and the `LayerStackContext` provider into the webFull `App` root.** The lifted modals consume `useLayerStack` and will throw `useLayerStack must be used within a LayerStackProvider` if the webFull root has not mounted the provider yet. The provider lift was tracked under an L2.1 ISC and is assumed in place per the established sibling-modal precedent (`AgentErrorModal`, `AgentPromptComposerModal`, `AutoRunLightbox`, etc. all consume the same hook in webFull). If the provider has not been threaded into the webFull root, the consuming feature surfaces will fail loud the first time one of these modals mounts; that surfaces as an L4.x wiring task.
- **Lifting `getAgentDisplayName` into the webFull tree as a sibling helper.** Currently imported from `'../../shared/agentMetadata'`. The shared helper is pure and webFull-safe, so a local lift would be pure code motion. Out of scope for this brief; matches the `AgentErrorModal` precedent.

### 2026-06-08 ŌĆö IPC-shim strategy for the big-3 (NewInstanceModal + AutoRun + MarketplaceModal)

Per Architect audit #10, the three Hard-bucket modals collapse onto ONE shared IPC-shim plan rather than three independent briefs. Total IPC surface across the three is 30 callsites (18 + 7 + 5 by `grep -c "window\.maestro\." src/renderer/components/{NewInstanceModal,AutoRun,MarketplaceModal}.tsx`), but the underlying namespace count is small: `agents.*`, `sshRemote.*`, `fs.*`, `autorun.*`, `dialog.*`, and `shell.openExternal` ŌĆö six namespaces total, with massive callsite-level reuse. The unlock count is **five new server routes + one per-site browser-API swap** that simultaneously unblock all three modals for leaf-parade-eligible lifts. SHA at append time: `2353a51766de9f931d59c0a157fe8045deb55639`. Files cited: `src/renderer/components/NewInstanceModal.tsx` (1822 LOC, 18 sites), `src/renderer/components/AutoRun.tsx` (2285 LOC, 7 sites), `src/renderer/components/MarketplaceModal.tsx` (1434 LOC, 5 sites); preload contracts in `src/main/preload/{fs,agents,sshRemote,autorun,system}.ts`; existing route surface in `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts` (verified ŌĆö only `api/fonts/detected`, `api/history`, `api/session(s)`, `api/settings`, `api/stats/*`, `api/theme`, `api/wakatime/*` registered today).

#### Per-modal IPC enumeration with disposition

**NewInstanceModal.tsx ŌĆö 18 sites across 5 namespaces:**

After dedup: **5 distinct route methods + 1 strip-and-promote + 1 window.open swap** cover all 18 sites.

AutoRun also reaches `window.maestro.autoRun.*` / `window.maestro.dialog.*` / `window.maestro.shell.openPath` / `window.maestro.images.*` at runtime per prior Decisions (ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_expanded_modal ŌĆö line 253; deferred-list line 4181), but those callsites are NOT in the 7-site count above because they live in the `AutoRun` view's RUNTIME flows below the module-load surface this strategy targets; a full `ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_view` lift remains a separate larger ticket.
