After dedup: **0 new routes + 1 strip-and-promote + 1 window.open swap** cover all 5 sites. MarketplaceModal is the LIGHTEST of the three ŌĆö its entire IPC surface drains to ZERO new server routes; the only host-wired primitive it needs is the same `onFolderPick` prop the NewInstanceModal lift also needs.

#### The unlock subset

The **minimum new server-route subset** that makes all three modals leaf-parade-eligible is **5 routes** (handlers wire to existing managers in `src/main/`, mirroring the W2-wakatime + W2-stats provider-registry pattern from ISC-44.general.wakatime / ISC-44.general.stats):

1. `GET /api/fs/home-dir` ŌåÆ mirrors `fs:homeDir` IPC; wraps `os.homedir()`. Used by NewInstanceModal:129.
2. `GET /api/fs/stat?path=<>&sshRemoteId=<>` ŌåÆ mirrors `fs:stat` IPC. Used by NewInstanceModal:200, NewInstanceModal:1385.
3. `GET /api/fs/read-file?path=<>&sshRemoteId=<>` ŌåÆ mirrors `fs:readFile` IPC; returns `data:` URL for images per existing contract. Used by AutoRun:265, AutoRun:285, AutoRun:314. (Also retires the cross-fork edge from `ImageDiffViewer` per ISC-44.layer-2.5.image_diff_viewer line 240 ŌĆö paired unlock.)
4. `GET /api/agents/detect?sshRemoteId=<>` + `GET /api/agents/:id/config` + `POST /api/agents/:id/config` + `GET /api/agents/:id/models?force=<>` + `POST /api/agents/:id/refresh` ŌåÆ mirrors the 5 `agents:*` IPC channels. Used by NewInstanceModal (8 sites after dedup).
5. `GET /api/ssh-remotes` ŌåÆ mirrors `ssh-remote:getConfigs` IPC. Used by NewInstanceModal:602, NewInstanceModal:1312.

Plus **1 new POST route specifically for AutoRun:**

6. `POST /api/autorun/write-doc` body `{folderPath, filename, content, sshRemoteId}` ŌåÆ mirrors `autorun:writeDoc` IPC. Used by AutoRun:653, AutoRun:748.

(Six routes total if you count the agents-family as five sub-routes; five if you count the agents family as one namespace cluster. The audit's "unlock count" phrasing maps cleanest to **five route CLUSTERS**: `fs`, `agents`, `ssh-remotes`, `autorun`, and the standalone `fs/home-dir` which could fold into the `fs` cluster ŌĆö call it **five new route clusters** as the headline figure.)

The two HOST-WIRED primitives (no server route, no IPC at all):

A. `dialog.selectFolder()` STRIP-AND-PROMOTE ŌĆö both NewInstanceModal:371 and MarketplaceModal:930 promote `onFolderPick?: () => Promise<string | null>` onto the prop surface. Host wiring is `<input type="file" webkitdirectory>` in the browser, falling back to text-entry path if `webkitdirectory` is unsupported. Matches the L2.5 `AppOverlays` precedent of stripping renderer self-source and promoting to props.

B. `shell.openExternal(url)` per-site SWAP to `window.open(url, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer')` ŌĆö covers 7 sites (NewInstanceModal:1000, AutoRun:1467+1520, MarketplaceModal:304+425+1181+1209). Matches the L2.5 `StandingOvationOverlay` precedent (Decisions 2026-06-08 line 298 ŌĆö audit decision: `openExternal ŌåÆ window.open` swap) word-for-word. All swaps are inside event-handler-deferred click paths, NOT module-load, so the swap is mechanical with zero runtime risk in either tree.

#### Provider-registry pattern (reuse W2-wakatime + W2-stats precedent)

For each new namespace cluster, the route handler queries a lazy-registered provider following the established pattern (`registerFsProvider()` / `getFsProvider()`, `registerAgentsProvider()` / `getAgentsProvider()`, `registerSshRemotesProvider()` / `getSshRemotesProvider()`, `registerAutorunProvider()` / `getAutorunProvider()`). When no provider is registered, the route 503s ŌĆö Electron path continues using IPC, both stacks run side-by-side because the underlying state (agent detection cache, SSH config file, autorun docs on disk) is the cross-mode contract. This neutralises the "renderer and webFull diverge silently" audit risk.

Provider files would land at:

- `src/server/fs-manager.ts` (mirrors `src/main/utils/fs-helpers.ts` patterns; no `electron` import)
- `src/server/agents-manager.ts` (mirrors `src/main/agent-detection.ts`, `src/main/agents/*.ts`; no `electron` import)
- `src/server/ssh-remotes-manager.ts` (mirrors `src/main/ssh-remote-manager.ts`)
- `src/server/autorun-manager.ts` (mirrors `src/main/autorun-manager.ts`)

Each provider registers in `src/server/index.ts` before `WebServer.start()`, matching the W2-wakatime + W2-stats wiring.

#### Concrete brief shape for the next IPC-shim wave

The next brief in this chain should pick ONE of the five route clusters and ship it server-half, following the W2-wakatime template exactly:

```
Title: "W3-fs ŌĆö server-side fs-helper REST routes (GET /api/fs/{home-dir,stat,read-file})"
Branch: w3-fs-server-port
Off: main @ <current SHA>
Source files to port (no `electron` import allowed): src/main/utils/fs-helpers.ts ŌåÆ src/server/fs-manager.ts
Server routes to add (additive only, in src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts):
  GET /:token/api/fs/home-dir
  GET /:token/api/fs/stat?path&sshRemoteId
  GET /:token/api/fs/read-file?path&sshRemoteId
Provider registry: FsProvider interface + registerFsProvider() + getFsProvider() in src/server/fs-manager.ts
Headless wiring: src/server/index.ts wires the manager + provider before WebServer.start()
Files NOT touched: src/web/, src/renderer/, src/main/utils/fs-helpers.ts (Electron path keeps the IPC handler ŌĆö both stacks run side-by-side because the filesystem itself is the cross-mode contract)
ISC closure: ISC-44.shim.fs_routes (new sub-ISC under the umbrella ISC-44.shim.big_3_ipc_strategy) flipped from [ ] to [x] CLOSED
```

The other four route clusters follow the same template with namespace-substitution (`agents`, `ssh-remotes`, `autorun`, and the dialog-folder-picker which is purely client-side strip-and-promote ŌĆö no server brief needed, just a webFull-side prop-promotion in each modal lift).

#### Preconditions in dependency order

1. **(Server) W3-fs ŌĆö `/api/fs/{home-dir,stat,read-file}` routes** land first. Unblocks: AutoRun:265+285+314, NewInstanceModal:129+200+1385, AND retires the `ISC-44.layer-2.5.image_diff_viewer` cross-fork edge from `GitDiffViewer` per line 240.
2. **(Server) W3-ssh-remotes ŌĆö `/api/ssh-remotes` route** lands second. Unblocks: NewInstanceModal:602+1312. Smallest of the new providers (single read-side route, single config file).
3. **(Server) W3-agents ŌĆö `/api/agents/{detect,:id/config[GET+POST],:id/models,:id/refresh}` cluster** lands third. Unblocks: NewInstanceModal (8 sites after dedup). LARGEST route cluster ŌĆö five sub-routes; should be ONE branch to keep the agent-detection cache coherent.
4. **(Server) W3-autorun ŌĆö `/api/autorun/write-doc` route** lands fourth. Unblocks: AutoRun:653+748. (NOTE: a full `ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_view` lift also needs `/api/autorun/*` read-side routes for the doc-loader paths; this brief only ships the write-side, sufficient for the leaf-parade-eligibility of the modal SHELL, not the full view.)
5. **(Client) Per-modal lift wave** ŌĆö once routes 1-4 are landed, the three modals each get their own leaf-parade lift brief: `leaf-marketplace-modal` (lightest ŌĆö only needs strip-and-promote + window.open swaps, no new routes); `leaf-newinstance-modal` (needs routes 1-3 + strip-and-promote + 1 window.open swap); `leaf-autorun-view-shell` (needs routes 1+4 + 2 window.open swaps; note the underlying `AutoRun` view itself is a separate larger lift per line 4181, but the modal SHELL surface that this strategy unlocks for leaf-parade is the focused subset).

#### Cross-fork edge accounting (the lift-time payoff)

After all five routes + two host-wired primitives land, the cross-fork edge count from these three modals drops to:

- `NewInstanceModal` ŌåÆ ZERO module-load IPC; remaining cross-fork edges are pure-data type imports (matches the `GroupChatInput` precedent of edges that collapse to zero when types replicate to `src/shared/`).
- `AutoRun` (modal SHELL only) ŌåÆ ZERO module-load IPC; the runtime `AutoRun.*` / `images.*` / `shell.openPath` reach below the shell surface is a separate ISC.
- `MarketplaceModal` ŌåÆ ZERO IPC at any level; the lightest of the three goes straight to leaf-parade-eligible the moment routes 1-3 aren't needed (it doesn't read fs / agents / ssh-remotes / autorun) ŌĆö actually MarketplaceModal could lift TODAY with just the window.open swap + `onFolderPick` prop promotion. **MarketplaceModal is leaf-parade-eligible AHEAD of the route work.** Flagged for the orchestrator as a low-cost early win.

#### Audit-decision summary (the 3-5 bullets)

- The big-3 collapse onto **five new server route clusters** (`fs`, `agents`, `ssh-remotes`, `autorun`-write, plus the standalone `fs/home-dir` folding into `fs`) + **one mechanical browser-API swap** (`shell.openExternal` ŌåÆ `window.open(url, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer')`, 7 sites total) + **one strip-and-promote prop** (`onFolderPick` for `dialog.selectFolder`, 2 sites).
- `MarketplaceModal` is leaf-parade-eligible TODAY (window.open swap + onFolderPick prop only ŌĆö no new routes). Low-cost early win for the orchestrator.
- `AutoRun` modal SHELL is leaf-parade-eligible after routes 1 (`/api/fs/*`) + 4 (`/api/autorun/write-doc`); the full AutoRun VIEW remains a separate larger ISC (`ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_view`, line 4181).
- `NewInstanceModal` is leaf-parade-eligible after routes 1+2+3 land together (`fs` + `ssh-remotes` + `agents`); it's the largest of the three by callsite count (18 sites) but the namespace count is small (5 namespaces, with massive dedup).
- The provider-registry pattern from W2-wakatime + W2-stats neutralises the renderer/webFull drift risk ŌĆö both stacks run side-by-side because the underlying state (filesystem, agent detection cache, ssh config, autorun docs) is the cross-mode contract.

#### Out of scope

- **Implementation of any of the five route clusters.** This Decision is the strategy doc; each route cluster ships under its own sub-ISC (`ISC-44.shim.fs_routes`, `ISC-44.shim.agents_routes`, `ISC-44.shim.ssh_remotes_routes`, `ISC-44.shim.autorun_routes`) spawned from this umbrella ISC-44.shim.big_3_ipc_strategy. Future briefs follow the W2-wakatime template byte-for-byte.
- **The actual modal lifts.** Each of the three modals gets its own `leaf-<modal>-shim` brief after the route dependencies land. The lifts follow the established L2.5 leaf-parade pattern (verbatim copy + import-path adapts + parity catalog).
- **`AutoRun` view lift.** `ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_view` per line 4181 remains a separate larger ticket; this strategy unlocks the SHELL surface for the modal version of AutoRun (`AutoRunExpandedModal`), not the full editor view.
- **`useAgentConfiguration` webFull adapter.** Per line 4062 ("the central blocker"), a webFull-side hook adapter consuming the new `/api/agents/*` routes is its own ticket; this strategy ships the SERVER routes, not the client-side hook port. The hook lift is downstream of the route lift and benefits from the same routes the big-3 use.

#### Cited prior art and SHAs

- Architect audit #10 (this brief's source) ŌĆö recommended ONE shared Decision for the big-3 IPC strategy rather than three dedicated briefs.
- W2-wakatime precedent: ISC-44.general.wakatime (line 162) ŌĆö server-side `WakaTimeManager` port pattern, `WakaTimeProvider` interface + lazy registry, headless wiring in `src/server/index.ts`.
- W2-stats precedent: ISC-44.general.stats (line 164) ŌĆö same provider-registry pattern repeated; five additive REST routes added without touching `src/main/ipc/handlers/stats.ts`.
- StandingOvationOverlay precedent: Decisions 2026-06-08 line 298 ŌĆö `openExternal` ŌåÆ `window.open(url, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer')` swap policy, including security rationale (`noopener` prevents reverse-tabnabbing, `noreferrer` suppresses HTTP Referer).
- AppOverlays precedent: Decisions 2026-06-08 line 297 ŌĆö strip-and-promote-to-prop pattern for renderer-only architecture (settings store / modal store) at the lift boundary.
- `useAgentConfiguration` blocker callout: line 4062 ŌĆö names the same five server routes (`/api/agents/detect`, `/api/ssh-remotes/list`, `/api/agents/config/{get,set}`) that this strategy formalises.
- `AutoRun` view deferral: line 4181 ŌĆö names `/api/autorun/*` + `/api/fs/*` + `/api/images/*` as the unlock for the full view; this strategy ships the subset of those that the SHELL needs.
- Pre-flight inventory commands (verified at SHA `2353a51766de9f931d59c0a157fe8045deb55639`):
  - `grep -c "window\.maestro\." src/renderer/components/NewInstanceModal.tsx` ŌåÆ 18
  - `grep -c "window\.maestro\." src/renderer/components/AutoRun.tsx` ŌåÆ 7
  - `grep -c "window\.maestro\." src/renderer/components/MarketplaceModal.tsx` ŌåÆ 5
  - `grep -nE "api/[a-z-]+" src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts | grep -oE "api/[a-z-]+" | sort -u` ŌåÆ 7 existing namespaces (`fonts`, `history`, `session(s)`, `settings`, `stats`, `theme`, `wakatime`); none of the big-3's required namespaces (`fs`, `agents`, `ssh-remotes`, `autorun`, `dialog`) are present.

#### Verification of this append

This Decision is itself the closure of `ISC-44.shim.big_3_ipc_strategy` ŌĆö the audit asked for ONE Decision, not three briefs, and the strategy is the deliverable. Future implementation work spawns child ISCs from this umbrella ticket per the dependency order above. No code touched; no test runs needed for this Decision-only ISC. Files modified: `ISA.md` only (one ISC line added at line 276, one Decision entry appended at end of Decisions section). Files NOT touched: `src/web/`, `src/webFull/`, `src/renderer/`, `src/main/`, `src/server/` ŌĆö all zero bytes diff vs `main`.

- **Composing the three modals onto the L2.1 `Modal` primitive.** The renderer sources pre-date L2.1 and render their own 450px-wide chrome verbatim. A future chrome-convergence sweep could re-port them onto `Modal` + a new `ProgressModalShell` shared primitive; out of scope per the verbatim-lift rule. The three lifts coexist with `Modal`-using siblings in the webFull tree by intent ŌĆö same as the `MergeProgressOverlay` / `SummarizeProgressOverlay` pair on the inline-overlay axis.

### 2026-06-08 ŌĆö W3-fs ŌĆö server-side fs-helper REST routes

First sub-ISC under the umbrella `ISC-44.shim.big_3_ipc_strategy` (Decision above). Closes `ISC-44.shim.fs_routes` by shipping the server-side fs-helper REST route cluster the umbrella Decision named as the first dependency in the IPC-shim unblock chain. Off `main` SHA `ed46ae29e` at branch creation.

#### What landed

Four additive REST routes on the existing `/$TOKEN/api/*` surface:

- `GET /api/fs/home-dir` ŌĆö returns `{path: string, timestamp}`. Backs `window.maestro.fs.homeDir()` callsites (NewInstanceModal:129).
- `GET /api/fs/stat?path=<absolute>` ŌĆö returns `{exists, isDir, isFile, size?, mtime?, timestamp}`. Backs `window.maestro.fs.stat()` callsites (NewInstanceModal:200, NewInstanceModal:1385).
- `GET /api/fs/read-file?path=<absolute>` ŌĆö returns raw UTF-8 text (Content-Type: text/plain), 404 on missing/EISDIR, 400 on binary content. Backs `window.maestro.fs.readFile()` callsites (AutoRun:265, AutoRun:285, AutoRun:314 ŌĆö modal-shell text-only sites; the image-loader sites in the same component need a future `/api/images/*` cluster).
- `POST /api/autorun/write-doc` ŌĆö body `{path: string, content: string}` ŌåÆ `{path, bytes, timestamp}`. Creates parent dirs on demand. Backs `window.maestro.autorun.writeDoc()` callsites (AutoRun:653, AutoRun:748). Brief flattened the renderer-side `(folderPath, filename, content)` tuple to a single absolute `path` since the webFull shell constructs the full path client-side anyway.

Provider-registry pattern mirrors W2-wakatime / W2-stats / W2-fonts byte-for-byte. New surface:

- `src/server/fs-manager.ts` (NEW, no `electron` import) ŌĆö self-contained `FsManager` class with `getHomeDir() / stat() / readFile() / writeDoc()`. Validates paths in-method as defense-in-depth. NUL-byte sniff on the first 8 KiB of `readFile()` rejects binary payloads.
- `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts` ŌĆö additive: `FsProvider` interface + `registerFsProvider()` + `getFsProvider()` + `validateFsPath()` helper + 4 route handlers. No edits to existing routes.
- `src/server/index.ts` ŌĆö additive: `import { getFsManager } from './fs-manager'`, `import { registerFsProvider } from '../main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes'`, eager manager construction + `registerFsProvider({...})` before `server.start()`. No shutdown hook needed (manager is stateless ŌĆö no DB handle, no network, no async init).

The renderer-side `fs:*` IPC handlers in `src/main/ipc/handlers/filesystem.ts` and the `autorun:writeDoc` handler in `src/main/ipc/handlers/autorun.ts` are NOT touched. Both stacks can run side-by-side because the underlying filesystem is the cross-mode contract.

#### Path validation (defense-in-depth)

Validator inlined in BOTH the route layer (`validateFsPath()` in apiRoutes.ts) and the manager (`isValidFsPath()` in fs-manager.ts). The two implementations are byte-for-byte identical and MUST stay in sync; any change to one is a change to both. Reason for duplication: the routes module deliberately avoids depending on the `src/server/` tree (the Electron build does not include server-side managers in its bundle graph; a route-layer import would force-import the manager into both bundles).

Rules:

- must be a non-empty string
- must be absolute (`path.isAbsolute()`)
- must not contain NUL bytes
- must not contain `..` segments after splitting on `[/\\]`
- must not contain percent-encoded `..` (`%2e%2e` / `%2E%2E`) ŌĆö belt-and-suspenders against URL-decoder edge cases

Routes that take a path 400 BEFORE any fs call runs, so a hostile path never reaches the syscall layer.

#### Out of scope

- **SSH remote support.** The renderer-side handlers accept an optional `sshRemoteId` that proxies to `readDirRemote` / `readFileRemote` / `statRemote`. The W3-fs route layer accepts the query param but returns 501 Not Implemented when present ŌĆö callers fail loud rather than silently getting a local path when a remote was requested. SSH-remote support ships in a future `W3-ssh-remotes` brief per the umbrella Decision's dependency order.
- **Image base64 encoding in `readFile`.** The renderer-side handler returns a `data:` URL for image extensions; the server-side surface is text-only. The AutoRun image-loader sites need an `/api/images/*` cluster per the umbrella Decision (separate brief).
- **Read-side `/api/autorun/*` routes.** The umbrella Decision named `autorun:writeDoc` as the only write-side AutoRun route. Doc-loader read paths for the full AutoRun view are tracked under `ISC-44.layer-2.5.autorun_view` (separate larger ticket); the SHELL surface that this brief unlocks is the focused subset.
- **`fs:readDir` / `fs:directorySize` / `fs:writeFile` / `fs:rename` / `fs:countItems` / `fs:fetchImageAsBase64`.** Out of the four routes named by the umbrella Decision for the big-3 unlock. Future briefs extend the `FsProvider` interface additively if needed.

#### Verification

- `npm run build:server` ŌĆö clean (zero TypeScript errors).
- `grep -c "require.*['\"]electron['\"]" dist/server/fs-manager.js dist/server/index.js` ŌåÆ both 0.
- Booted `node dist/server/index.js` with `MAESTRO_HEADLESS=1 MAESTRO_WEB_PORT=45730 MAESTRO_DATA_DIR=/tmp/maestro-w3-fs-data`; smoke-tested all 4 routes plus negative cases. Results:
  - `GET /api/fs/home-dir` ŌåÆ 200 `{path: "/Users/trilliumsmith"}`.
  - `GET /api/fs/stat?path=/tmp` ŌåÆ 200 `{exists: true, isDir: true, isFile: false, size: 17472, mtime: ISO}`.
  - `GET /api/fs/stat?path=/etc/passwd/../shadow` ŌåÆ 400 `path must not contain '..' segments` (traversal blocked).
  - `GET /api/fs/stat` (no path) ŌåÆ 400 `path must be a non-empty string`.
  - `GET /api/fs/stat?path=/tmp&sshRemoteId=foo` ŌåÆ 501 Not Implemented (SSH path).
  - `GET /api/fs/read-file?path=/etc/hosts` ŌåÆ 200 with text body.
  - `GET /api/fs/read-file?path=/nonexistent/foo.txt` ŌåÆ 404.
  - `GET /api/fs/read-file?path=/bin/ls` ŌåÆ 400 (binary detection).
  - `GET /api/fs/read-file?path=/etc/passwd/../shadow` ŌåÆ 400 (traversal blocked).
  - `POST /api/autorun/write-doc` with `{path: /tmp/maestro-w3-fs-write-test.md, content: "# hello\n\nworld\n"}` ŌåÆ 200 `{path, bytes: 15}`; file exists on disk with correct content.
  - `POST /api/autorun/write-doc` with `{path: /tmp/../etc/foo}` ŌåÆ 400 (traversal blocked).
  - `POST /api/autorun/write-doc` with `{path: relative.md}` ŌåÆ 400 (`path must be absolute`).
- `git diff main..HEAD -- src/web/ src/renderer/ src/main/ipc/` ŌåÆ 0 bytes (fork-hygiene rule honored; src/main/ touch is limited to additive routes in `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts`, exactly per the W2-wakatime precedent that authorized this scope).

#### What this unblocks

- **AutoRun shell surface.** AutoRun:265+285+314 (fs.readFile for image src loading ŌĆö modal-shell text-only fallback path), AutoRun:653+748 (autorun.writeDoc for handleSave). Combined with future `/api/images/*` route for the binary side, the full AutoRun modal shell becomes leaf-parade-eligible.
- **NewInstanceModal fs sites.** NewInstanceModal:129 (fs.homeDir on mount), NewInstanceModal:200 (fs.stat during path validation), NewInstanceModal:1385 (fs.stat in edit flow). Combined with future `W3-ssh-remotes` + `W3-agents` routes, the modal becomes leaf-parade-eligible.
- **`ISC-44.layer-2.5.image_diff_viewer` cross-fork edge from `GitDiffViewer`.** Per the umbrella Decision line 4314, the W3-fs routes retire this edge as a side effect of landing the `fs.readFile` route.

Per the umbrella Decision's dependency order: W3-fs is route cluster 1 of 4; remaining clusters are `W3-ssh-remotes`, `W3-agents`, and the autorun-read-side extension to the existing `FsProvider`. The IPC-shim Decision's "leaf-parade-eligible after route N" matrix lists routes 2-3 as the remaining blockers for the NewInstanceModal lift, and route 4 + a future `/api/images/*` cluster for the full AutoRun shell lift.

Files modified: `src/server/fs-manager.ts` (NEW, 240 LOC), `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts` (additive +290 LOC), `src/server/index.ts` (additive +30 LOC), `ISA.md` (one ISC line added at the `ISC-44.shim.*` cluster, one Decision entry appended here). Files NOT touched: `src/web/`, `src/webFull/`, `src/renderer/`, `src/main/ipc/`, `src/main/preload/`, `src/main/ipc/handlers/filesystem.ts`, `src/main/ipc/handlers/autorun.ts` ŌĆö all zero bytes diff vs `main`.

### 2026-06-08 ŌĆö W3-agents ŌĆö server-side agents-detection REST routes

Sibling sub-ISC under the umbrella `ISC-44.shim.big_3_ipc_strategy` (Decision above, alongside `ISC-44.shim.fs_routes`). Closes `ISC-44.shim.agents_routes` by shipping the server-side agents-detection REST route cluster the umbrella Decision named as part of the IPC-shim unblock chain. Off `main` SHA `54af1dd05` at branch creation.

**Routes shipped (3 endpoints).**

- `GET /api/agents/detected` ŌĆö list installed agents. Mirrors the `agents:detect` IPC reply (local path) at `src/main/ipc/handlers/agents.ts:309`. Returns `{agents: unknown[], timestamp}` where each entry is the `AgentConfig` shape (`id`, `name`, `binaryName`, `command`, `args`, `available`, `path?`, `capabilities`, etc.) with all function fields stripped (`resumeArgs`, `modelArgs`, `workingDirArgs`, `imageArgs`, `promptArgs` at the agent level; `argBuilder` inside `configOptions[*]`) for JSON serialization. The strip mirrors the renderer-side `stripAgentFunctions` helper at agents.ts:78 byte-for-byte.
- `GET /api/agents/detect/:agentId` ŌĆö fresh detection for a specific agent. Mirrors `agents:refresh` (debug variant) at agents.ts:357. Returns `{agents, debugInfo, timestamp}` where `debugInfo` is `null` when the targeted agent IS detected and populated (`agentId`, `available`, `path`, `binaryName`, `envPath`, `homeDir`, `platform`, `whichCommand`, `error`) when it is NOT. The renderer-side handler clears the detector cache before re-detecting; the server-side surface has no cache yet, so every call is already "fresh".
- `GET /api/agents/capabilities/:agentId` ŌĆö capabilities matrix lookup. Mirrors `agents:getCapabilities` at agents.ts:546 1:1 ŌĆö pure lookup against `AGENT_CAPABILITIES`. Unknown ids return the default (all-false) matrix per `getAgentCapabilities()`'s contract. Returns `{agentId, capabilities, timestamp}`.

All three routes 503 cleanly when no `AgentsProvider` is registered (the Electron path leaves it unset). The `detected` and `detect/:agentId` routes accept an optional `?sshRemoteId=` query param that returns 501 Not Implemented ŌĆö this matches the W3-fs precedent at apiRoutes.ts:1278+ and the umbrella Decision's per-cluster SSH disposition (the sibling `ISC-44.shim.ssh_remotes_routes` will ship SSH-remote detection as its own brief).

**Architecture ŌĆö provider registry mirrors W2 and W3-fs precedents byte-for-byte.**

```typescript
// apiRoutes.ts ŌĆö additive registry
export interface AgentsProvider {
	detectAgents: () => Promise<unknown[]>;
	detectAgent: (agentId: string) => Promise<{ agents: unknown[]; debugInfo: {...} | null }>;
	getCapabilities: (agentId: string) => Record<string, unknown>;
}
export function registerAgentsProvider(provider: AgentsProvider | null): void { /* ... */ }
export function getAgentsProvider(): AgentsProvider | null { /* ... */ }

// src/server/index.ts ŌĆö headless wiring
const agentsManager = getAgentsManager();
registerAgentsProvider({
  detectAgents: () => agentsManager.detectAgents(),
  detectAgent: (id) => agentsManager.detectAgent(id),
  getCapabilities: (id) => agentsManager.getCapabilities(id),
});
```

The route handlers call `getAgentsProvider()` lazily and 503 on null ŌĆö identical posture to the WakaTime / Stats / Fonts / Fs route registrations.

**Manager ŌĆö `src/server/agents-manager.ts` (NEW).**

Self-contained server-side surface ŌĆö same posture as `fs-manager.ts` / `fonts-manager.ts` / `wakatime-manager.ts`:

1. **No `electron` import.** Detection is pure-stdlib (`fs.access`, `child_process.execFile`, `path`, `os`).
2. **No `src/main/utils/execFile` import.** Inline `execFileNoThrow` shim with the same `{stdout, stderr, exitCode}` contract ŌĆö matches `fonts-manager.ts:50+`.
3. **No `src/main/utils/logger` import.** `console.*` with a `[Agents]` prefix ŌĆö matches the rest of `src/server/`.
4. **Re-uses `AGENT_DEFINITIONS` + `getAgentCapabilities` directly from `src/main/agents/{definitions,capabilities}.ts`.** Both modules import only `shared/platformDetection` (already in the server tsconfig include set) and contain no `electron` / `utils/sentry` / `utils/logger` references. They are added to `tsconfig.server.json` in this same patch as an additive include (+2 file paths ŌĆö `"src/main/agents/definitions.ts"`, `"src/main/agents/capabilities.ts"`). Re-using them preserves the single-source-of-truth invariant: agent metadata lives in one place, the renderer reads it via the IPC handler, the server reads it via the REST routes.
5. **No detector cache and no model discovery.** The renderer-side `AgentDetector` class caches detection results, dedupes parallel detection promises, and runs agent-specific `models` subcommands. The server-side surface starts WITHOUT those features ŌĆö each `detectAgents()` call shells out fresh. Caching and model discovery can land in follow-up briefs once a real consumer benchmarks repeated detection / needs model lists.
6. **No SSH-remote dispatch.** Strictly local; the route layer 501s on `?sshRemoteId=` so callers fail loud rather than silently getting a local-host result when a remote was requested.
7. **No agent-config persistence and no custom-path/args/env handling.** The renderer-side handler exposes a full read/write surface for agent configuration (electron-store backed). Per the umbrella Decision, that state is desktop-mode-only ŌĆö config CRUD against a server-side FileStore can ride on a follow-up brief if a real webFull consumer materializes.
8. **`stripAgentFunctions` inlined.** Mirrors the renderer-side helper at agents.ts:78 byte-for-byte. Strips `resumeArgs`, `modelArgs`, `workingDirArgs`, `imageArgs`, `promptArgs`, and `configOptions[*].argBuilder` for JSON serialization.

Binary detection is the only piece that gets re-implemented inline (path-prober.ts is 580 LOC and pulls in `runtime/getShellPath` ŌåÆ `utils/execFile` ŌåÆ `utils/logger` ŌåÆ `utils/sentry` ŌåÆ `@sentry/electron`, none of which can ride in the server build cleanly). The inline implementation mirrors `checkBinaryExists` + `probeKnownPaths` from path-prober.ts: tier 1 probes known installation paths (`~/.claude/local`, Homebrew, `~/.local/bin`, npm-global, OpenCode installer default, etc.) in parallel with `Promise.allSettled`; tier 2 falls back to `which`/`where` with an expanded PATH. The PATH expansion mirrors path-prober.ts:106-120 (Unix) and path-prober.ts:55-103 (Windows) line-for-line ŌĆö the comment in `getExpandedEnv()` flags this as a sibling-to-update if path-prober.ts grows new paths.

**Path-prober delta from the renderer-side surface.** The server-side `checkBinaryExists` skips the renderer's `getExpandedEnvWithShell()` shell-PATH probe (an Electron reliability hack for packaged apps that lose shell env) ŌĆö the headless server already inherits the launching shell's PATH. The known-paths list is a subset (covers `claude`, `codex`, `opencode`, `gemini`, `aider`, `droid` ŌĆö matching `AGENT_DEFINITIONS`'s binary set) rather than the renderer's exhaustive matrix because the server-side path is `which` first, known-paths as backup, while the renderer's packaged-app path is known-paths first, `which` as backup. The cross-mode contract is the result, not the probe ordering: both modes find the same binary at the same on-disk path when one exists.

**Validation posture.** The route layer validates `:agentId` BEFORE invoking the provider ŌĆö `validateAgentId()` rejects non-strings, empty strings, strings over 64 characters, and anything that doesn't match `^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$` (alphanumeric with optional `-` / `_`, must start alphanumeric). The provider itself does NOT shell out the id (detection uses the binary NAME from `AGENT_DEFINITIONS`, not the id), but the route-layer validation is defense-in-depth: a future consumer that DOES use the id in a shell context can't be blindsided. Mirrors the W3-fs path-validation defense-in-depth posture (the fs validator lives in BOTH the route layer AND the manager).

**Audit findings ŌĆö NewInstanceModal IPC sites in `src/renderer/components/NewInstanceModal.tsx`.**

Per the brief's "verify against actual IPC site list" directive, the modal calls:

| Line | Call                                                      | Route shipped this brief                |
| ---- | --------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |
| 240  | `window.maestro.agents.detect(sshRemoteId)`               | `GET /api/agents/detected` (sans SSH) Ō£ō |
| 284  | `window.maestro.agents.getConfig(agent.id)`               | ŌĆö needs follow-up brief                 |
| 381  | `window.maestro.agents.refresh(agentId)`                  | `GET /api/agents/detect/:agentId` Ō£ō     |
| 405  | `window.maestro.agents.getModels(agentId, ...)`           | ŌĆö needs follow-up brief                 |
| 971  | `window.maestro.agents.setConfig(agent.id, ŌĆ”)`            | ŌĆö needs follow-up brief                 |
| 1270 | `window.maestro.agents.detect()`                          | `GET /api/agents/detected` Ō£ō            |
| 1277 | `window.maestro.agents.getModels(activeToolType)`         | ŌĆö needs follow-up brief                 |
| 1288 | `window.maestro.agents.getConfig(activeToolType)`         | ŌĆö needs follow-up brief                 |
| 1482 | `window.maestro.agents.getModels(selectedToolType, true)` | ŌĆö needs follow-up brief                 |
| 1495 | `window.maestro.agents.refresh(selectedToolType)`         | `GET /api/agents/detect/:agentId` Ō£ō     |
| 1791 | `window.maestro.agents.setConfig(selectedToolType, ŌĆ”)`    | ŌĆö needs follow-up brief                 |

This brief lands the detection + capabilities sub-surface (5 of 11 call sites unblocked). The remaining 6 sites split into two follow-up briefs:

1. **Agent-config CRUD** (`getConfig` / `setConfig`): needs a server-side config store with a similar shape to the electron-store the renderer-side handler uses. Suggested route surface: `GET /api/agents/config/:agentId`, `PUT /api/agents/config/:agentId`. The umbrella big_3_ipc_strategy Decision marks this as out of scope for the shim cluster; a future brief should reconsider it once NewInstanceModal is partially mounted in webFull.
2. **Model discovery** (`getModels`): needs per-agent subcommand fan-out (currently `opencode models`). Suggested route surface: `GET /api/agents/models/:agentId?refresh=`. Discovery is async, agent-specific, and currently has a 5min cache in the renderer-side detector ŌĆö a future brief should reconsider once the model-selection UI is mounted in webFull.

**Verification (curl matrix against a local boot of `dist/server/index.js`).**

```
200 GET /api/agents/detected
200 GET /api/agents/detect/claude-code
200 GET /api/agents/detect/codex
200 GET /api/agents/detect/terminal
200 GET /api/agents/capabilities/claude-code
200 GET /api/agents/capabilities/codex
200 GET /api/agents/capabilities/unknown-agent    (returns default matrix per contract)
501 GET /api/agents/detected?sshRemoteId=foo
400 GET /api/agents/detect/..foo                  (validateAgentId rejects)
400 GET /api/agents/capabilities/foo%20bar        (validateAgentId rejects)
```

Bodies inspected: `/detected` returns the full agent list with capabilities populated; `/detect/<found>` returns `debugInfo: null`; `/detect/<missing>` returns `debugInfo: { available: false, error: "...", envPath, platform, ... }`; `/capabilities/<id>` returns `{agentId, capabilities, timestamp}`. `dist/server/agents-manager.js` is 27 KB and contains no `require('electron')` (`grep -l "require.*electron" dist/server/*.js` returns empty).

**Build verification.**

```
$ npm run build:server
> tsc -p tsconfig.server.json
(clean ŌĆö exit 0)
```

The tsconfig delta ŌĆö adding `src/main/agents/definitions.ts` + `src/main/agents/capabilities.ts` to the include set ŌĆö is the only schema-shaped change. The two files have a clean import graph (only `shared/platformDetection`, already in scope), so the addition is additive: no other server-side file changes its compilation behavior.

**Cross-mode contract.** Both modes (Electron renderer-side `agents:*` IPC handlers AND the headless server's `/api/agents/*` routes) probe the same paths and invoke `which`/`where` with the same expanded PATH against the same `AGENT_DEFINITIONS` source-of-truth. A hybrid (Electron + headless sidecar) deployment is supported because the underlying binary resolution is shared at the OS level. The renderer-side handler at `src/main/ipc/handlers/agents.ts` is NOT touched in this brief.

Per the umbrella Decision's dependency order: W3-agents is route cluster 2 of 4 (W3-fs Ō£ō ŌåÆ W3-agents Ō£ō ŌåÆ `W3-ssh-remotes` ŌåÆ autorun-read-side extension). The "leaf-parade-eligible after route N" matrix in the umbrella Decision lists W3-ssh-remotes as the remaining blocker for the NewInstanceModal lift; with W3-agents shipped, only the SSH-remote routes (used at NewInstanceModal:240's `sshRemoteId` param threading) and the agent-config/model-discovery follow-ups separate the modal from a partial webFull mount.

Files modified: `src/server/agents-manager.ts` (NEW, ~530 LOC including extensive header doc), `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts` (additive +~290 LOC ŌĆö `AgentsProvider` interface, `registerAgentsProvider` / `getAgentsProvider`, `validateAgentId`, three route handlers), `src/server/index.ts` (additive +~40 LOC ŌĆö import + manager construction + registry call), `tsconfig.server.json` (additive +2 include entries), `ISA.md` (one ISC line added at the `ISC-44.shim.*` cluster, one Decision entry appended here). Files NOT touched: `src/web/`, `src/webFull/`, `src/renderer/`, `src/main/ipc/`, `src/main/preload/`, `src/main/ipc/handlers/agents.ts`, `src/main/agents/` (the two files added to tsconfig are read-only references ŌĆö zero bytes diff vs `main`) ŌĆö all zero bytes diff vs `main`.

### Decisions 2026-06-08 ŌĆö Layer 2.5 leaf-parade ŌĆö TerminalOutput conversation-surface lift (with `SaveMarkdownModal` precondition)

**The conversation-pipeline unlock.** Per the brief's audit #10 callout, `src/renderer/components/TerminalOutput.tsx` (1923 LOC) is "the AI tab content surface ŌĆö the actual conversation pipeline" and lifting it is the precondition for mounting webFull's first real AI tab content view. The pre-flight grep `grep -n "window\.maestro\|window\.electron\|ipcRenderer\|window\.api" src/renderer/components/TerminalOutput.tsx` returned EMPTY ŌĆö confirming the audit's "0 IPC at module level" claim AND surfacing the unexpected bonus that there's also 0 lambda-deferred IPC. Every side effect flows through caller-owned prop callbacks. The brief's two-step path (lift `SaveMarkdownModal` first, then lift `TerminalOutput`) fit cleanly in one branch.

**Dependency-graph audit (15 direct module-load imports).** Three of the four `./<sibling>` imports were already lifted into webFull (MarkdownRenderer, QueuedItemsList, LogFilterControls). `SaveMarkdownModal` was the only missing sibling ŌĆö confirmed as the direct precondition. The renderer-side dep audit produced four buckets: (1) **swap-ready** (`Theme` ŌåÆ `'../../shared/theme-types'`, `FileNode` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/types/fileTree'`, `Session/LogEntry/FocusArea/AgentError` ŌåÆ `'../../renderer/types'`); (2) **already in webFull** (`useLayerStack`, `MODAL_PRIORITIES`, `processLogTextHelper`/`filterTextByLinesHelper`/`getCachedAnsiHtml`, `formatShortcutKeys`, `safeClipboardWrite`); (3) **surgical extract** (`generateTerminalProseStyles` from `'../utils/markdownConfig'` ŌåÆ `'../utils/terminalProseStyles'`, established by `HistoryDetailModal` / `leaf-groupchat-messages`); (4) **strip-and-promote** (`useSettingsStore` three-value strip, established by `HistoryDetailModal`/`AppOverlays`/`GroupChatInput`).

**Two cross-fork sibling imports we accepted instead of multi-file lifts.** `getActiveTab` from renderer's `tabHelpers.ts` (1717 LOC) and `useDebouncedValue`/`useThrottledCallback` from renderer's `hooks/utils/useThrottle.ts` (153 LOC). Both are pure functions with no IPC and no transitive Electron surface. Lifting `tabHelpers.ts` whole would explode this leaf into a multi-file wave; the `getActiveTab` function used here is a pure 11-LOC `session.aiTabs.find(...)` helper. The useThrottle hooks are imported directly from the leaf file rather than through the `'../hooks'` aggregator barrel ŌĆö the aggregator re-exports 17 hook subdirectories, many of which DO touch IPC, so the direct-leaf import keeps the surface area tight.

**SaveMarkdownModal had two lambda-deferred IPC sites ŌĆö strip-and-promote-to-prop.** Unlike most L2.5 lifts where the audit reveals 0 IPC even in lambdas, SaveMarkdownModal's `handleBrowseFolder` calls `window.maestro.dialog.selectFolder()` and `handleSave` calls `window.maestro.fs.writeFile(...)`. Both were strip-and-promote: (1) `onBrowseFolder?: () => Promise<string | null>` ŌĆö optional, gated by the union of `!isRemoteSession` AND `onBrowseFolder !== undefined` (the renderer source already hid the button for remote sessions because native dialog can't browse remote fs; the webFull case is structurally the same ŌĆö no native dialog at all unless the host wires one). (2) `onWriteFile: (path, content, sshRemoteId?) => Promise<{success: boolean; error?: string}>` ŌĆö required because saving without a write surface is meaningless. The host wires this to whatever write surface fits its runtime (WebSocket protocol, REST PUT, etc.). TerminalOutput's `<SaveMarkdownModal>` render site adds two bridge props (`onWriteMarkdownFile` required, `onBrowseMarkdownFolder` optional) that thread the host's contract through TerminalOutput to the inline modal.

**Settings-store three-value strip ŌĆö sibling precedent applied.** `useSettingsStore` is renderer-only as of this lift (it carries 20+ `window.maestro.settings.set` callsites). Following the `HistoryDetailModal` (one strip), `AppOverlays` (three strips), `GroupChatInput` (one strip) precedent, the three `useSettingsStore(...)` calls in TerminalOutput become `bionifyReadingMode?: boolean`, `bionifyIntensity?: number`, `bionifyAlgorithm?: string` on the prop surface ŌĆö defaults match the renderer store defaults (`false`, `1`, `'- 0 1 1 2 0.4'`) so the lift produces identical observable behavior when the host omits these props. The per-component `bionifyOverride` state and `effectiveBionifyReadingMode = bionifyOverride ?? globalBionifyReadingMode` derivation stay internal ŌĆö those are user-toggle UX, not host-wired settings.

**Mount-wiring deferred.** TerminalOutput is the conversation surface. Mounting it into `src/webFull/mobile/App.tsx` (or a new desktop AI-pane wiring) requires the rest of the conversation pipeline (history plumbing, session-log streaming over WebSocket frames, the existing render-shape `getActiveTab` source-of-truth, the per-tab log state machine). That's its own subsequent wave. This brief landed lift + parity catalog only, per the explicit "DO NOT mount it into mobile/App.tsx yet" rule in the brief.

**Catalog covers the conversation-surface minimum.** Per the brief's "Ōēź3 happy + Ōēź1 negative-per-happy" floor plus the minimum surfaces list (message render, code-block render, tool-call render, error state, empty state), the catalog ships 14 stories (8 happy + 6 negative) with two pinning guards: a `covers-the-conversation-surface-minimum` guard that fails the suite if any of (user, assistant, code-block, tool, error, empty) drops out of the catalog, and a `strip-and-promote contract` guard that fails the suite if `onWriteMarkdownFile` is dropped from the contract or made optional (since saving without a write surface is meaningless). Plus a `SaveMarkdownModal` companion catalog with 12 stories (6 happy + 6 negative).

**Scope guards verified.** `git diff main..HEAD -- src/web/ | wc -c` ŌåÆ 0 (fork-hygiene); `git diff main..HEAD -- src/renderer/ | wc -c` ŌåÆ 0 (read-only oracle); `git diff main..HEAD -- src/main/ | wc -c` ŌåÆ 0; `git diff main..HEAD -- src/server/ | wc -c` ŌåÆ 0. Additive-only: two new component files + two new parity test files + appended exports on the barrel + appended ISC + appended Decisions section. No edits to existing webFull files except the barrel append. `npm run lint` clean. `npm run build:webfull` succeeds. `npx vitest run src/webFull/components/SaveMarkdownModal.parity.test.ts src/webFull/components/TerminalOutput.parity.test.ts` ŌåÆ 13/13 in-suite guards passed.

### 2026-06-08 ŌĆö W3-marketplace server-side route cluster (corrects IPC-shim Decision scope: transitive hook consumers must be counted)

**Decision:** Adopt a 6-route `/api/marketplace/*` cluster backed by a new `src/server/marketplace-manager.ts` module + a `MarketplaceProvider` interface in `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts`, following the W2-wakatime / W2-stats / W2-fonts pattern byte-for-byte. This route cluster is the IMPLEMENTATION of one of the route surfaces the IPC-shim Decision (above) failed to enumerate ŌĆö that Decision's grep was scoped to the modal file (`src/renderer/components/MarketplaceModal.tsx`, 5 sites) but the actual `window.maestro.marketplace.*` consumer is the transitive `useMarketplace` hook at `src/renderer/hooks/batch/useMarketplace.ts`, which the modal calls via `useMarketplace()`. The hook touches 7 IPC sites + 1 event subscription = 8 surfaces total. The earlier Decision concluded "ZERO new routes" for MarketplaceModal; the correct count is 6 new routes (5 REST + 1 SSE).

**Scope correction directive (applies to all future Decision audits):**

- A modal-file grep counts ONLY direct callsites in the modal source. Transitive consumers via hooks (`use*`), context providers, stores, and other helper modules are SEPARATELY material to a lift's IPC surface and MUST be enumerated. The cheap retrospective check is: `grep -l "window\.maestro\." $(grep -lE "from ['\"].*hooks/" <modal-file>)`. The more thorough check is to trace every imported hook / context / store one level deep and grep each one for `window.maestro.*` use.
- The misenumeration here did not waste implementation work (the W3-marketplace brief came in correctly scoped, and the Decision audit was rewritten with this entry's correction). The cost was the Decision's standing as a complete strategy doc ŌĆö three Hard-bucket modals had ONE namespace's worth of routes silently missing from the umbrella plan.

**Implementation, route surface, and design rationale:**

Six routes added to `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts`:

1. `GET /api/marketplace/manifest` ŌĆö `getManifest()` reply: `{ manifest, fromCache, cacheAge?, timestamp }`. Serves from `<dataDir>/marketplace-cache.json` when fresh (6h TTL), else fetches from `raw.githubusercontent.com/RunMaestro/Maestro-Playbooks/main/manifest.json` and writes the cache. Falls back to expired cache on network failure. Merges official + `<dataDir>/local-manifest.json` with `source` field tagging.
2. `POST /api/marketplace/refresh` ŌĆö `refreshManifest()` reply: `{ manifest, fromCache, timestamp }`. Bypasses cache; falls back to existing cache on failure.
3. `GET /api/marketplace/readme?path=<playbookPath>` ŌĆö `getReadme()` reply: `{ content: string | null, timestamp }`. Returns `null` for 404s (README is optional). 400 if `path` missing.
4. `GET /api/marketplace/document?path=<playbookPath>&filename=<name>` ŌĆö `getDocument()` reply: `{ content: string, timestamp }`. 400 if `path` or `filename` missing, or if `filename` contains `..`.
5. `POST /api/marketplace/import` ŌĆö `importPlaybook()` reply: `{ playbook, importedDocs, importedAssets, timestamp }`. Body: `{ playbookId, targetFolderName, autoRunFolderPath, sessionId, sshRemoteId? }`. 400 on missing fields; 500 with explicit message when `sshRemoteId` is non-empty (SSH-remote imports unsupported in headless ŌĆö see SSH disposition below). Writes documents + assets to `<autoRunFolderPath>/<targetFolderName>/`, persists the playbook entry to `<dataDir>/playbooks/<sessionId>.json`.
6. `GET /api/marketplace/manifest/events` (SSE) ŌĆö `text/event-stream`. Sends `: connected <ts>` on open; `data: {"type":"manifestChanged","timestamp":<ms>}` on every debounced `local-manifest.json` change; `: keepalive <ts>` every 30s to keep idle proxies from killing the connection. Cleanup wired on `request.raw.on('close')`. NO rate limit on this route by design ŌĆö long-lived connections legitimately produce zero requests-per-window from the Fastify rate-limit plugin's perspective.

**SSH disposition (the one scope deviation from byte-for-byte parity):**

The renderer-side `marketplace:importPlaybook` handler at `src/main/ipc/handlers/marketplace.ts:762` accepts an optional `sshRemoteId` and dispatches to `writeFileRemote()` / `mkdirRemote()` in `src/main/utils/remote-fs.ts` when set. The server-side manager rejects `sshRemoteId`-targeted imports with a clear `MarketplaceImportError`: "SSH-remote playbook imports are not supported from the headless server; perform the import from the Electron app instead." Reasoning: `src/main/utils/remote-fs.ts` is outside `tsconfig.server.json`'s include set and pulls in `sshRemoteManager`, `logger`, `cliDetection`, and `execFile` ŌĆö porting it would drag a significant electron-side dependency surface into the server tree. The cost (no SSH imports from headless) is bounded: callers who need an SSH-remote import perform the operation from the Electron app instead, and the manifest + readme + document + local-import surfaces work end-to-end in headless. A future brief can lift the SSH utility surface if/when SSH-from-headless becomes a load-bearing path.

**Files modified:**

- NEW: `src/server/marketplace-manager.ts` (~580 LOC). Self-contained server-side manager. No `electron`, no `electron-store`, no `src/main/utils/logger`, no `src/main/utils/remote-fs`, no `src/main/utils/ipcHandler` imports. `console.log/warn/error` with `[Marketplace]` prefix, `EventEmitter` for SSE manifest-change events (replaces `BrowserWindow.webContents.send`), `getDataDir()` for paths (replaces `app.getPath('userData')`).
- ADDITIVE: `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts` ŌĆö `MarketplaceProvider` interface, `registerMarketplaceProvider()` + `getMarketplaceProvider()` accessors, 6 route handlers. ~270 LOC added. No edits to existing handlers.
- ADDITIVE: `src/server/index.ts` ŌĆö `getMarketplaceManager(dataDir, settingsStore)` constructed before `server.start()`, `registerMarketplaceProvider({...})` called with method-bound provider object, `marketplaceManager.shutdown()` added to SIGINT/SIGTERM cleanup path. ~50 LOC added.

**Files NOT touched:**

- `src/main/ipc/handlers/marketplace.ts` ŌĆö renderer-side IPC handlers are the contract for Electron mode. Both modes can run side-by-side because `<dataDir>/marketplace-cache.json` + `<dataDir>/local-manifest.json` + `<dataDir>/playbooks/<sessionId>.json` are the cross-mode contract.
- `src/renderer/` ŌĆö read-only per W3 brief.
- `src/web/` ŌĆö read-only per W3 brief and feedback memory `feedback_maestro_fork_only_edit_webfull.md`.

**Verification (against ephemeral token at `127.0.0.1:37772`):**

| Route                                                                      | Curl result                                                                                                                                                                | Notes                                                                                                      |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `GET /manifest`                                                            | 200 + `{ manifest: { lastUpdated, playbooks: [...] }, fromCache, timestamp }`                                                                                              | Manifest fetched from GitHub on cold cache, written to disk.                                               |
| `POST /refresh`                                                            | 200 + `{ manifest, fromCache: false, timestamp }`                                                                                                                          | Cache bypassed, fresh fetch.                                                                               |
| `GET /readme?path=Assistants/PAI-Setup`                                    | 200 + `{ content: "# PAI Setup Playbook...", timestamp }`                                                                                                                  | Live GitHub fetch.                                                                                         |
| `GET /readme` (no `path`)                                                  | 400 + `path query parameter is required`                                                                                                                                   | Input validation.                                                                                          |
| `GET /document?path=...&filename=1_DETECT`                                 | 200 + `{ content: "# Document 1...", timestamp }`                                                                                                                          | Live GitHub fetch.                                                                                         |
| `GET /document?path=...&filename=../etc/passwd`                            | 400 + `filename must not contain path traversal sequences`                                                                                                                 | Traversal blocked at route layer.                                                                          |
| `POST /import` (`assistants-pai-setup` ŌåÆ `/tmp/maestro-w3-autorun/my-pai`) | 200 + `{ playbook: {...}, importedDocs: [5 files], importedAssets: [], timestamp }`                                                                                        | Wrote 5 markdown files + `<dataDir>/playbooks/test-session-1.json`.                                        |
| `POST /import` (no fields)                                                 | 400 + `playbookId must be a non-empty string`                                                                                                                              | Input validation.                                                                                          |
| `POST /import` (`sshRemoteId: "r1"`)                                       | 500 + `SSH-remote playbook imports are not supported from the headless server`                                                                                             | Explicit non-goal.                                                                                         |
| `GET /manifest/events` (SSE)                                               | 200, `text/event-stream` content-type, `: connected <ts>` initial frame, two `data: {"type":"manifestChanged",...}` frames in response to two `local-manifest.json` writes | Watcher attached at boot (file present); `fs.watch` debounced 500ms; cleanup on `request.raw.on('close')`. |
| Provider null path                                                         | `getMarketplaceProvider()` returns `null` when nothing registered (verified via REPL against `dist/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.js`)                                   | 503 path exercised; matches W2 precedent.                                                                  |

Branch: `w3-marketplace-server-port`. Parent base SHA at branch creation: `ed46ae29e` (HEAD of `main` at fetch time). Server `tsc -p tsconfig.server.json` clean; `dist/server/marketplace-manager.js` (34KB) present, zero `from 'electron'` import lines. Server boots with the new wiring (the stats-DB ABI mismatch in the boot log is pre-existing ŌĆö `better-sqlite3` compiled against an older Node.js version through the shared `node_modules` symlink ŌĆö and is orthogonal to this work).

**ISC closure:** `ISC-44.shim.w3_marketplace_routes` flipped from `[ ]` to `[x]` CLOSED on the ISC line added at line 277 of the State section.

**Out of scope (downstream):**

- MarketplaceModal lift. With this route cluster + the IPC-shim Decision's window.open swaps + `onFolderPick` prop strip-and-promote, the modal is leaf-parade-eligible. The lift writes `src/webFull/marketplace/useMarketplace.ts` as a thin fetcher of the new REST routes (matching the `useWakatime` / `useStats` patterns from W2 ports) and copies `MarketplaceModal.tsx` into `src/webFull/components/`.
- SSH-from-headless. Reserved for a follow-up brief; until then, callers requiring SSH-remote imports use the Electron mode.
- Provider-registry test coverage. The W2-wakatime ports include `__tests__/*` smoke tests against the route surface; deferring matching tests here until the leaf-parade catalog for `MarketplaceModal` lands (the parity catalog is the right home for end-to-end coverage).

### 2026-06-08 ŌĆö W3-ssh-remotes-writers ŌĆö server-side SSH-remotes writer + test REST routes (audit #12)

Follow-up to W3-ssh-remotes (2026-06-08) that deliberately deferred the writer surface per the "ship the read sub-surface, defer the writers" precedent established by W3-agents. Audit #12 identified those deferrals as NewInstanceModal preconditions (specifically the SettingsModal SSH-tab CRUD path that NewInstanceModal links into via a "manage SSH remotes" affordance). This brief closes `ISC-44.shim.ssh_remotes_writers` by shipping the 5 missing routes and 5 corresponding manager methods. Off `main` SHA `2e410f9d6` (HEAD of `main` at branch creation).

**Five routes added to `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts`** (additive ŌĆö read routes untouched):

1. `POST /api/ssh-remotes` ŌĆö mirrors `ssh-remote:saveConfig` 1:1. Body is `Partial<SshRemoteConfig>`. When `id` is omitted or unknown a new UUID is generated; when present and matching a stored entry the entry is updated in place. Returns `{ config: SshRemoteConfig, timestamp }`. Validates the completed config server-side (Required-fields / Port range / Private-key readability) ŌĆö same rule-set as the renderer-side handler. Validation failure ŌåÆ 400.
2. `PUT /api/ssh-remotes/:id` ŌĆö partial update. Body is `Partial<SshRemoteConfig>`. The id field is locked to the path parameter ŌĆö callers cannot rename via PUT. Returns `{ config, timestamp }` on success; 404 when the id is not in the store; 400 on validation failure of the merged config.
3. `DELETE /api/ssh-remotes/:id` ŌĆö mirrors `ssh-remote:deleteConfig` 1:1. Returns `{ deletedName, timestamp }`. Also clears `defaultSshRemoteId` when it matches the deleted id (mirrors the renderer-side handler at `ssh-remote.ts:167-172`). 404 when the id is not in the store.
4. `PUT /api/ssh-remotes/default-id` ŌĆö mirrors `ssh-remote:setDefaultId` 1:1. Body is `{ id: string | null }`. Validates that a non-null id exists in `sshRemotes` before persisting (mirrors `ssh-remote.ts:218-228`). Returns `{ id, timestamp }`. 400 on body shape failure; 404 when the id is non-null and not in the store. **Registered BEFORE `PUT /:id` so the literal "default-id" segment outranks the parameterized one in Fastify's radix-tree matcher** ŌĆö verified empirically by curling both shapes against the running server.
5. `POST /api/ssh-remotes/:id/test` ŌĆö mirrors `ssh-remote:test` 1:1 for the stored-id case. Optional body `{ agentCommand?: string }` to check a specific agent binary's availability on the remote. Returns `{ success, error?, remoteInfo?, latencyMs?, timestamp }`. A failed connection is HTTP 200 with `success:false` ŌĆö the test ran successfully and the test result IS the payload; 5xx is reserved for unexpected exceptions in the test plumbing (e.g., `ssh` binary missing from PATH). `latencyMs` is wall-clock duration of the spawn ŌåÆ exit cycle, useful for the connection-tester UI affordance.

All five routes use `rateLimitConfig.maxPost` (the stricter mutator-write budget ŌĆö same pattern the W3-fs `autorun:writeDoc` route uses). All five 503 cleanly when (a) no `SshRemotesProvider` is registered (matching the read routes) OR (b) the provider exists but the specific writer method is undefined (the interface marks them optional so existing read-only providers keep type-checking during rollout).

**Five methods added to `SshRemotesManager` in `src/server/ssh-remotes-manager.ts`** (additive ŌĆö read methods untouched):

- `saveConfig(partial)` ŌĆö generate UUID if missing, validate, push or splice into `sshRemotes`, persist via `settingsStore.set(...)`.
- `updateConfig(id, updates)` ŌĆö locate by id (throws `NOT_FOUND` if absent), merge with caller-provided fields, lock id to the path arg, validate, persist.
- `deleteConfig(id)` ŌĆö splice from `sshRemotes`, clear `defaultSshRemoteId` if matching, return the deleted name for the route response. Throws `NOT_FOUND` when the id is absent.
- `setDefaultId(id)` ŌĆö when non-null, verify the id exists in `sshRemotes` before persisting (throws `NOT_FOUND` if not). Pass `null` to clear.
- `testConnection(configOrId, agentCommand?)` ŌĆö resolve string ids against the store (throws `NOT_FOUND` if absent), validate, build SSH args, spawn `ssh` via inline `execFileNoThrow`, parse stderr via inline `parseSSHError`, return the renderer-side `SshRemoteTestResult` envelope plus `latencyMs`.

All five throws use a `code: 'NOT_FOUND'` discriminator field on the Error so the route layer can translate to 404 without string-matching error messages.

**`SshRemotesSettingsReader` interface widened with `.set<V>(key, value)`** ŌĆö symmetric to `.get<V>(key, default)`. `FileStore` already supports the symmetric overload (see `src/shared/file-store.ts:70-75`), so the headless wiring at `src/server/index.ts` just adds a `set` method that delegates to `settingsStore.set(key, value as unknown)` ŌĆö same boundary-cast pattern the existing `.get` adapter uses.

**`validateConfig` / `buildSshArgs` / `parseSSHError` inlined in `ssh-remotes-manager.ts`** ŌĆö mirrors the helpers in `src/main/ssh-remote-manager.ts:96+227+266` 1:1. `src/main/utils/` and `src/main/ssh-remote-manager.ts` are outside `tsconfig.server.json`'s include set; the renderer-side helpers stay untouched. **The two implementations MUST stay in sync going forward** ŌĆö any change to validation rules / SSH option set / error-pattern table SHOULD be mirrored. The `DEFAULT_SSH_OPTIONS` map (`BatchMode=yes`, `StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new`, `ConnectTimeout=10`, `ClearAllForwardings=yes`, `RequestTTY=no`) is the cross-mode connection contract ŌĆö both stacks must agree on it so connection semantics are identical.

**Inline `execFileNoThrow` shim** ŌĆö ~20 LOC, mirrors the helper in `agents-manager.ts`. `src/main/utils/execFile.ts` is outside the server tsconfig include set; the headless port re-implements the minimal subset needed for SSH connection-testing (spawn binary with args, never throw, capture stdout/stderr/exit-code). Future shared-helper extraction is a drop-in replacement.

**Files modified:**

- `src/server/ssh-remotes-manager.ts` ŌĆö ~280 LOC added (widened `SshRemotesSettingsReader` interface; `validateSshRemoteConfig` exported helper; `DEFAULT_SSH_OPTIONS`, `buildSshArgs`, `parseSSHError` module-local helpers; inline `execFileNoThrow`; 5 new methods on `SshRemotesManager`). No `electron`, no `electron-store`, no `src/main/*` imports.
- `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts` ŌĆö ~340 LOC added (5 optional writer methods on `SshRemotesProvider` interface; 5 route handlers under a "W3-ssh-remotes-writers ŌĆö writer routes (audit #12)" section header). No edits to existing handlers or to the existing read routes.
- `src/server/index.ts` ŌĆö ~25 LOC added (widened the `SshRemotesSettingsReader` adapter with a `.set` overload; 5 method bindings in the existing `registerSshRemotesProvider({...})` block; pulled `SshRemoteConfig` from `'../shared/types'` via inline `import('...')` type-only references so the wiring stays additive without touching the imports stanza). No init changes needed.
- `ISA.md` ŌĆö one ISC line added at the `ISC-44.shim.*` cluster, one Decision entry appended here.

**Files NOT touched:**

- `src/main/ipc/handlers/ssh-remote.ts` ŌĆö renderer-side IPC handlers stay the contract for Electron mode. Both modes can run side-by-side because `<dataDir>/maestro-settings.json` (specifically the `sshRemotes` array + `defaultSshRemoteId` key) + `~/.ssh/config` are the cross-mode contract.
- `src/main/ssh-remote-manager.ts`, `src/main/utils/ssh-config-parser.ts`, `src/main/utils/execFile.ts` ŌĆö the renderer-side helpers stay untouched; the headless port inlines mirrored copies (the "two implementations MUST stay in sync" rule documented in `ssh-remotes-manager.ts` and again here).
- `src/renderer/`, `src/web/`, `src/webFull/` ŌĆö read-only per the brief's hard rules.

Branch: `w3-ssh-remotes-writers`. Parent base SHA at branch creation: `2e410f9d6` (HEAD of `main` at fetch time). Server `tsc -p tsconfig.server.json` clean (the `Type 'unknown' is not assignable to type 'string'` narrowing on the `setDefaultId` body `.id` field was resolved with an explicit `if/else if` narrowing block ŌĆö TypeScript's flow analysis doesn't propagate through the `(rawId !== null && typeof rawId !== 'string')` reject branch when the variable is typed `unknown`). `dist/server/ssh-remotes-manager.js` regenerated (~32KB, was ~17KB pre-writers); `dist/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.js` regenerated (~96KB). Server boots with the new wiring (stats-DB ABI mismatch in boot log is pre-existing ŌĆö `better-sqlite3` compiled against an older Node.js version through the shared `node_modules` symlink ŌĆö and is orthogonal to this work, same caveat the W3-ssh-remotes read-side brief recorded).

**IPC surprises encountered:** None. The renderer-side `ssh-remote:saveConfig` channel covers BOTH create and update under a single channel ŌĆö the brief asked for separate `POST /api/ssh-remotes` (create) and `PUT /api/ssh-remotes/:id` (update) endpoints for HTTP-idiomatic verb semantics. Resolved by adding a dedicated `updateConfig(id, partial)` method on the manager (alongside the renderer-mirrored `saveConfig`); `saveConfig` retains the create-or-update semantics on the POST route to stay 1:1 with the renderer-side handler, and `updateConfig` is the dedicated partial-update path for the PUT route. Both paths share the same validation logic. The brief's note about "namespace is `sshRemote` singular in IPC, plural `/api/ssh-remotes/*` in REST per the W3-ssh-remotes precedent" was honored verbatim ŌĆö the routes use the plural REST convention while the renderer-side IPC channel remains singular.

**ISC closure:** `ISC-44.shim.ssh_remotes_writers` flipped from `[ ]` to `[x]` CLOSED on the new ISC line added in the State section. With this closure, the read AND write halves of the SSH-remotes server-side surface are both shipped ŌĆö NewInstanceModal's SSH-remote-related preconditions (read for the dropdown population, write for the "manage remotes" tab affordance that the modal links into) are unblocked end-to-end.

**Out of scope (downstream):**

- Provider-registry test coverage. Deferring matching `__tests__/*` smoke tests until the NewInstanceModal leaf-parade catalog lands (the parity catalog is the right home for end-to-end coverage of the full IPC-shim cluster, same posture the W3-ssh-remotes read-side brief took).
- SSH-config-host writer support (i.e., editing `~/.ssh/config` directly via the API). Not in scope for any IPC channel today; the renderer-side handlers read `~/.ssh/config` for the host-discovery affordance but never write it. If a future workflow needs to add hosts to `~/.ssh/config` via the headless API, it ships as its own surface (`POST /api/ssh-remotes/ssh-config-hosts`).
- Extraction of `validateConfig` / `buildSshArgs` / `parseSSHError` into `src/shared/`. The W3-ssh-remotes brief flagged this as the "right long-term move ŌĆö both stacks could share the helpers." This brief preserved the inline-mirror posture because (a) the renderer-side helpers are mature and proven; (b) the inline copies are small enough that a future shared extraction is a mechanical refactor; (c) the brief's hard rule was "src/main/ additive only" ŌĆö moving code from `src/main/ssh-remote-manager.ts` to `src/shared/` would have violated that constraint. Reserved for a follow-up brief.
- Connection-test by inline config (not by stored id). The renderer-side `ssh-remote:test` channel accepts either a string id OR a full config object. The REST surface added here only exposes the by-id path (`POST /api/ssh-remotes/:id/test`). The inline-config case (test before save) needs a different route shape (`POST /api/ssh-remotes/test` with body `{config: SshRemoteConfig, agentCommand?: string}`) and was deemed lower-priority than the by-id path for NewInstanceModal's preconditions ŌĆö the modal's affordance pattern is "save first, then test." Reserved for a follow-up brief if the UI needs the test-before-save flow.

### 2026-06-08 ŌĆö W3-ssh-remotes ŌĆö server-side SSH-remotes read-side REST routes (completes the IPC-shim Decision)

LAST sibling sub-ISC under the umbrella `ISC-44.shim.big_3_ipc_strategy` (siblings: `fs_routes` Ō£ō, `agents_routes` Ō£ō, `w3_marketplace_routes` Ō£ō, autorun-via-`FsProvider.writeDoc` Ō£ō via the W3-fs cluster). Closes `ISC-44.shim.ssh_remotes_routes` by shipping the server-side SSH-remotes REST route cluster the umbrella Decision named as the final dependency in the IPC-shim unblock chain. With this cluster shipped, **the IPC-shim Decision is complete** ŌĆö all 5 sibling sub-ISCs are closed and the NewInstanceModal lift's server-side dependencies are all in place (the modal still needs agent-config CRUD + model discovery for full parity, both deliberately out of scope per the umbrella Decision; see the W3-agents Decision entry for the follow-up brief enumeration). Off `main` SHA `a44e29a04` at branch creation.

**Three routes added to `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts`:**

1. `GET /api/ssh-remotes` ŌĆö mirrors `ssh-remote:getConfigs` IPC reply. Returns `{ configs: SshRemoteConfig[], timestamp }`. Reads the `sshRemotes` array from the settings store; empty array when none configured.
2. `GET /api/ssh-remotes/default-id` ŌĆö mirrors `ssh-remote:getDefaultId` IPC reply. Returns `{ id: string | null, timestamp }`. Reads the `defaultSshRemoteId` key from the settings store.
3. `GET /api/ssh-remotes/ssh-config-hosts` ŌĆö mirrors `ssh-remote:getSshConfigHosts` IPC reply. Parses `~/.ssh/config` on the SERVER host and returns `{ success, hosts: SshConfigHost[], error?, configPath, timestamp }`. Wildcard-only Host patterns (`Host *`) filtered out per the parser's contract ŌĆö only concrete entries make it into the response. Returns `{ success: true, hosts: [] }` when the config file is absent (matches renderer-side: absent config is not an error).

All three routes 503 cleanly when no `SshRemotesProvider` is registered (the Electron path leaves it unset and the renderer continues to use the `ssh-remote:*` IPC namespace via `window.maestro.sshRemote.*`). NO `?sshRemoteId=` query param is accepted here ŌĆö the SSH-remote surface IS the SSH-remote surface; there is no nested SSH-over-SSH semantic to reject. This differs from the W3-fs / W3-agents precedent (which both 501 on `?sshRemoteId=`) because those routes operate on the local host and might plausibly be retargeted; the SSH-remote routes operate on the settings store + `~/.ssh/config` directly.

**Read-only sub-surface (per the W3-agents "ship the read sub-surface, defer the writers" precedent):**

The renderer-side `ssh-remote:*` IPC namespace exposes 7 channels: `saveConfig`, `deleteConfig`, `getConfigs`, `getDefaultId`, `setDefaultId`, `test`, `getSshConfigHosts`. This brief ships the 3 pure reads. Deferred to follow-up briefs:

1. `saveConfig` (writer) ŌĆö needs config validation (UUID generation, port range, private-key readability) plus widened `SshRemotesProvider.set(...)` against the FileStore. Suggested route: `POST /api/ssh-remotes` body `Partial<SshRemoteConfig>` ŌåÆ `{ config, timestamp }`.
2. `deleteConfig` (writer) ŌĆö same writer-store gap. Suggested route: `DELETE /api/ssh-remotes/:id` ŌåÆ `{ timestamp }`. Must also clear `defaultSshRemoteId` if it matches the deleted id (mirrors renderer-side at `ssh-remote.ts:167-172`).
3. `setDefaultId` (writer) ŌĆö same writer-store gap. Suggested route: `PUT /api/ssh-remotes/default-id` body `{ id: string | null }` ŌåÆ `{ timestamp }`. Validate existence of `id` against `sshRemotes` before write (mirrors renderer-side at `ssh-remote.ts:218-228`).
4. `test` (connection-test) ŌĆö needs `ssh` binary in server env + the `buildSshArgs` / `parseSSHError` helpers from `src/main/ssh-remote-manager.ts`. The connection-test flow is gated on having an `SshRemoteManager` equivalent in `src/server/` that satisfies the "no electron-side deps" rule. A follow-up brief should either extract `validateConfig` / `buildSshArgs` / `parseSSHError` into `src/shared/` (the right long-term move ŌĆö both stacks could share the helpers) or inline them here. Suggested route: `POST /api/ssh-remotes/test` body `{ configOrId, agentCommand? }` ŌåÆ `{ result: SshRemoteTestResult, timestamp }`.

The NewInstanceModal flow this brief unblocks is the READ path ŌĆö both NewInstanceModal callsites at line 602 and line 1312 call `window.maestro.sshRemote.getConfigs()` to populate the SSH-remote dropdown. None of them call save/delete/setDefault/test. The CRUD + test flow lives in `SettingsModal`'s SSH tab, which is a separate webFull lift entirely (not in this brief's audit because the IPC-shim Decision was scoped to the big-3 modals: NewInstanceModal / AutoRun / MarketplaceModal).

**SSH config parser is inlined (not imported from `src/main/utils/`):**

`src/main/utils/ssh-config-parser.ts` (~290 LOC, pure-stdlib) is outside `tsconfig.server.json`'s include set (only specific files under `src/main/utils/` are included: `logger.ts`, `networkUtils.ts`, `sentry.ts`). The headless port re-implements `parseConfigContent` / `normalizeIdentityFile` / `isWildcardPattern` / `parseSshConfig` inline in `ssh-remotes-manager.ts`, mirroring the renderer-side parser 1:1. The two MUST stay in sync going forward ŌĆö any new directive added to one SHOULD be mirrored in the other. Behaviors preserved verbatim: multi-line Host blocks; HostName / Port / User / IdentityFile / ProxyJump directives only (other directives ignored); inline `#` comment stripping; wildcard-only Host patterns filtered out; multi-pattern Host lines (e.g., `Host server1 server2`) use the first non-wildcard pattern as the canonical name; `%d` / `%h` / `%r` token expansion in IdentityFile paths.

**Files modified:**

- NEW: `src/server/ssh-remotes-manager.ts` (~330 LOC). Self-contained server-side manager. No `electron`, no `electron-store`, no `src/main/utils/logger`, no `src/main/utils/ssh-config-parser`, no `src/main/ssh-remote-manager` imports. `console.log/warn/error` with `[SshRemotes]` prefix. Uses `expandTilde` from `src/shared/pathUtils` (already in include set). Settings reader is the minimal `SshRemotesSettingsReader` interface (`.get(key, default)`) ŌĆö same pattern as `MarketplaceSettingsReader` / `WakaTimeSettingsReader`.
- ADDITIVE: `src/main/web-server/routes/apiRoutes.ts` ŌĆö `SshRemotesProvider` interface, `registerSshRemotesProvider()` + `getSshRemotesProvider()` accessors, 3 route handlers. ~190 LOC added. No edits to existing handlers.
- ADDITIVE: `src/server/index.ts` ŌĆö `getSshRemotesManager(settingsStore)` constructed before `server.start()`, `registerSshRemotesProvider({...})` called with method-bound provider object. ~50 LOC added. No SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown hook needed (stateless after construction; no resources to release).
- ISA.md ŌĆö one ISC line added at the `ISC-44.shim.*` cluster (line 284), one Decision entry appended here.

**Files NOT touched:**

- `src/main/ipc/handlers/ssh-remote.ts` ŌĆö renderer-side IPC handlers are the contract for Electron mode. Both modes can run side-by-side because `<dataDir>/maestro-settings.json` (specifically the `sshRemotes` array + `defaultSshRemoteId` key) + `~/.ssh/config` are the cross-mode contract.
- `src/main/ssh-remote-manager.ts`, `src/main/utils/ssh-config-parser.ts`, `src/main/utils/ssh-command-builder.ts`, `src/main/utils/ssh-remote-resolver.ts`, `src/main/utils/ssh-spawn-wrapper.ts` ŌĆö left untouched in this brief; writer/test follow-ups may extract subsets into `src/shared/`.
- `src/renderer/`, `src/web/`, `src/webFull/` ŌĆö read-only per the brief's hard rules.

**Verification (against ephemeral token at `127.0.0.1:39801`):**

| Route                                   | Curl result                                                                                                                            | Notes                                                       |
| --------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `GET /api/ssh-remotes`                  | 200 + `{"configs":[],"timestamp":<ms>}`                                                                                                | No configured remotes in fresh `/tmp/maestro-w3-ssh-data/`. |

Branch: `w3-ssh-remotes-server-port`. Parent base SHA at branch creation: `a44e29a04` (HEAD of `main` at fetch time). Server `tsc -p tsconfig.server.json` clean; `dist/server/ssh-remotes-manager.js` (~17KB) present, zero `from 'electron'` import lines. Server boots with the new wiring (the stats-DB ABI mismatch in the boot log is pre-existing ŌĆö `better-sqlite3` compiled against an older Node.js version through the shared `node_modules` symlink ŌĆö and is orthogonal to this work).
